en.title: Court stops MEC move from Lilongwe to Blantyre
en.body: A court has stopped President Peter Mutharika's plan to move the Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) from Lilongwe to Blantyre. Judge Kenyatta Nyirenda gave an order to block the move. The Malawi Congress Party (MCP) and three other people asked the court to stop it.
en.body_more:
The MCP, together with Emmanuel Chambulanyina Jere, Abraham Mwakhwawa, and Daniel Mwanyongo Chitonya, went to the High Court. They asked Judge Kenyatta Nyirenda to stop the relocation. The judge agreed and gave an injunction. This means the government cannot move MEC from Lilongwe to Blantyre or any other place right now. The court order also says the defendants—Chief Secretary Justin Saidi and Lands Minister Chimwemwe Chipungu—must not take any more steps toward the move.

The judge also gave the claimants leave to start Judicial Review and Constitutional Review cases. This means the court will look at whether the relocation is legal and whether it breaks the constitution. The lawyer for the three people and MCP, George Jivason Kadzipatike, said the court order stops any move of MEC. He said: "With this Order, MEC will not be relocated from Lilongwe to Blantyre or to any other place."

President Mutharika gave an executive order in October 2025 to move MEC to Blantyre. The order also said the Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority (MACRA) and the Malawi Housing Corporation (MHC) should move there too. But the court has now blocked this.

The government says MEC spends K100 million per month on rent in Lilongwe. Moving to Blantyre would cost only K5 million per month. This would save K95 million each month. The Attorney General, Frank Mbeta, said the government will fight the court order. He says the move is needed to save money. But the MCP says the government wants to move MEC away from Lilongwe to weaken the check on its power.

ny.title: Khoti laletsa MEC kusamuka kuchoka ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre
ny.body: Khothi laletsa dongosolo la Purezidenti Peter Mutharika losamutsa bungwe la Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) kuchoka ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre. Woweruza milandu Kenyatta Nyirenda adapereka lamulo loletsa izi. Chipani cha Malawi Congress (MCP) ndi anthu ena atatu adapempha khothi kuti liletse izi.
ny.body_more:
MCP, pamodzi ndi Emmanuel Chambulanyina Jere, Abraham Mwakhwawa, ndi Daniel Mwanyongo Chitonya, adapita ku Khoti Lalikulu. Adapempha Woweruza Kenyatta Nyirenda kuti aletse kusamutsa anthu. Woweruzayo adavomereza ndipo adapereka lamulo. Izi zikutanthauza kuti boma silingasunthe MEC kuchokera ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre kapena kwina kulikonse pakali pano. Lamulo la khothi likunenanso kuti otsutsawo—Mlembi Wamkulu Justin Saidi ndi Nduna ya Malo Chimwemwe Chipungu—sayenera kuchitapo kanthu kena pa kusamutsaku.

Woweruzayo adapatsanso odandaulawo chilolezo choyambitsa milandu ya Judicial Review ndi Constitutional Review. Izi zikutanthauza kuti khothi lidzayang'ana ngati kusamutsaku kuli kovomerezeka komanso ngati kukuphwanya malamulo. Loya wa anthu atatuwa ndi MCP, George Jivason Kadzipatike, adati lamulo la khothi likuletsa kusamutsa kulikonse kwa MEC. Iye anati: "Ndi Lamuloli, MEC sidzasamutsidwa kuchoka ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre kapena kwina kulikonse."

Purezidenti Mutharika adapereka lamulo la akuluakulu mu Okutobala 2025 kuti MEC isamutsidwe kupita ku Blantyre. Lamuloli linanenanso kuti bungwe la Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority (MACRA) ndi bungwe la Malawi Housing Corporation (MHC) nazonso ziyenera kusamukira kumeneko. Koma khoti tsopano laletsa izi.

Boma likunena kuti MEC imagwiritsa ntchito K100 miliyoni pamwezi pa lendi ku Lilongwe. Kusamukira ku Blantyre kungawononge K5 miliyoni yokha pamwezi. Izi zipulumutsa K95 miliyoni mwezi uliwonse. Loya Wamkulu, Frank Mbeta, anati boma lidzalimbana ndi lamulo la khoti. Iye akuti kusinthaku n'kofunika kuti ndalama zisungidwe. Koma MCP ikunena kuti boma likufuna kusuntha MEC kuchoka ku Lilongwe kuti ifooketse mphamvu zake.

en.title: FDH Bank holds back K7 billion dividend over debt worry
en.body: FDH Bank has decided not to pay out a K7 billion final dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025. The bank says the government's plan to restructure its domestic debt makes it unsafe to hand out the money now. FDH Bank is listed on the Malawi Stock Exchange.
en.body_more:
The government wants to restructure its debt by changing the terms with creditors. This means the government will pay money back more slowly. The bank says this creates risk, so it must keep the K7 billion instead.

Debt restructuring could change how much it costs businesses to borrow money. Banks may give less credit, or charge more for loans. Small and medium businesses that need loans may find it harder to get money. Businesses in farming, textiles, and building may be hit hard. The Malawi Stock Exchange has seen fewer trades, which shows that people are worried.

The bank's choice to hold back the dividend shows big worry about the economy. FDH Bank normally gives money to its shareholders. But now it thinks it is better to keep the money safe. People who own FDH Bank shares need to watch what happens next. The bank's health is tied to the whole economy. If the bank is worried, other people may be worried too.

Businesses now have a choice. The tight credit could push them to find new ways to get money, like partnerships or community loans. But if the debt plan works, it may lower the cost of borrowing later. Farmers could gain if the government spends more on water systems and roads. Companies must talk to the government to know what will happen next. Those that change their ways and find new ideas may do well even when money is tight.

ny.title: Banki ya FDH yaletsa phindu la K7 biliyoni chifukwa cha nkhawa ya ngongole
ny.body: Banki ya FDH yasankha kusapereka gawo lomaliza la K7 biliyoni chaka chomwe chinatha pa Disembala 31, 2025. Bankiyo ikunena kuti dongosolo la boma lokonzanso ngongole yake yamkati limapangitsa kuti kusapereka ndalamazo tsopano kukhale kosatetezeka. Banki ya FDH yalembedwa pa Malawi Stock Exchange.
ny.body_more:
Boma likufuna kusintha ngongole yake mwa kusintha malamulo ndi omwe ali ndi ngongole. Izi zikutanthauza kuti boma lidzabweza ndalama pang'onopang'ono. Bankiyo ikunena kuti izi zimabweretsa chiopsezo, kotero iyenera kusunga K7 biliyoni m'malo mwake.

Kukonzanso ngongole kungasinthe momwe zimawonongera mabizinesi kuti abwereke ndalama. Mabanki angapereke ngongole zochepa, kapena kulipiritsa ndalama zambiri pa ngongole. Mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono ndi apakatikati omwe amafunikira ngongole angavutike kupeza ndalama. Mabizinesi a ulimi, nsalu, ndi zomangamanga angakhumudwe kwambiri. Malawi Stock Exchange yawona malonda ochepa, zomwe zikusonyeza kuti anthu akuda nkhawa.

Kusankha kwa bankiyi kuletsa gawo la ndalama kukuwonetsa nkhawa yayikulu pa zachuma. FDH Bank nthawi zambiri imapereka ndalama kwa omwe ali ndi magawo ake. Koma tsopano ikuganiza kuti ndibwino kusunga ndalamazo mosamala. Anthu omwe ali ndi magawo a FDH Bank ayenera kuyang'anira zomwe zidzachitike pambuyo pake. Thanzi la bankiyo likugwirizana ndi chuma chonse. Ngati bankiyo ikuda nkhawa, anthu ena nawonso angadandaule.

Mabizinesi tsopano ali ndi chisankho. Ngongole yocheperako ikhoza kuwakakamiza kupeza njira zatsopano zopezera ndalama, monga mgwirizano kapena ngongole za anthu ammudzi. Koma ngati ndondomeko ya ngongole ikugwira ntchito, ikhoza kuchepetsa ndalama zobwereka pambuyo pake. Alimi angapeze phindu ngati boma litagwiritsa ntchito ndalama zambiri pamakina amadzi ndi misewu. Makampani ayenera kulankhula ndi boma kuti adziwe zomwe zidzachitike pambuyo pake. Iwo omwe asintha njira zawo ndikupeza malingaliro atsopano angachite bwino ngakhale ndalama zili zochepa.

en.title: Football Coaches Ask Sports Council to Stop FAM Expulsion
en.body: The National Football Coaches Association has asked the Malawi National Council of Sports to help stop the Football Association of Malawi (FAM) from expelling three groups. FAM removed the coaches, referees, and youth football groups in June last year. The coaches say FAM broke its own rules when it did this. They want the Sports Council to make FAM follow the correct steps.
en.body_more:
The coaches sent a letter to Sports Council chairperson Jim Kalua in October last year. They told him that FAM removed the three groups without proper talks or votes. The coaches say the vote was not done in secret, as the rules say it should be. They also say FAM did not talk about this with its main leaders first.

Coaches Association chairperson Aubrey Nankhuni said the groups were "ambushed." He said FAM did not tell them about the AGM agenda before the meeting. "We were not told what would be discussed," Nankhuni said. The coaches also sent a complaint to FIFA in October, saying FAM broke articles 30 and 36 of its own statutes.

Nankhuni asked FAM to show that it sent the new rules to FIFA and the Confederation of African Football (CAF) for approval. He said if FAM can remove groups now without following rules, other groups could be removed in the future just to help people with power and money. The coaches say they will use the law courts if the Sports Council does not help them.

FAM president Fleetwood Haiya said FAM followed the correct rules when it changed the statutes. He said FIFA requires all football groups to have rules that match FIFA's own rules. Sports Council chairperson Jim Kalua said he got the letter and the council's office is looking at it. FAM is planning to hold its Annual General Meeting on 27 June at Sunbird Livingstonia Beach in Salima, but the three groups will not be allowed to join or speak.

ny.title: Aphunzitsi a mpira apempha bungwe la masewera kuti lisiye kuchotsedwa kwa FAM
ny.body: Bungwe la National Football Coaches Association lapempha bungwe la Malawi National Council of Sports kuti lithandize kuletsa bungwe la Football Association of Malawi (FAM) kuchotsa magulu atatu. FAM inachotsa aphunzitsi, oweruza, ndi magulu a mpira wa achinyamata mu June chaka chatha. Aphunzitsiwo akuti FAM inaswa malamulo ake pamene inachita izi. Akufuna kuti bungwe la Sports Council lipangitse FAM kutsatira njira zoyenera.
ny.body_more:
Aphunzitsiwo adatumiza kalata kwa wapampando wa Sports Council Jim Kalua mu Okutobala chaka chatha. Adamuuza kuti FAM idachotsa magulu atatuwo popanda kukambirana kapena kuvota koyenera. Aphunzitsiwo akuti kuvota sikunachitike mwachinsinsi, monga momwe malamulo amanenera. Amanenanso kuti FAM sinayambe yalankhula za izi ndi atsogoleri ake akuluakulu.

Wapampando wa Coaches Association Aubrey Nankhuni adati maguluwo "adabisika." Adati FAM sinawauze za ndondomeko ya AGM msonkhano usanachitike. "Sitinauzidwe zomwe zikakambidwa," adatero Nankhuni. Aphunzitsiwo adatumizanso madandaulo ku FIFA mu Okutobala, ponena kuti FAM idaswa nkhani 30 ndi 36 za malamulo ake.

Nankhuni adapempha FAM kuti iwonetse kuti idatumiza malamulo atsopano ku FIFA ndi Confederation of African Football (CAF) kuti avomerezedwe. Adati ngati FAM ingachotse maguluwo tsopano popanda kutsatira malamulo, magulu ena akhoza kuchotsedwa mtsogolo kuti athandize anthu ndi mphamvu ndi ndalama. Aphunzitsiwo akuti adzagwiritsa ntchito makhothi azamalamulo ngati Sports Council siwathandiza.

Purezidenti wa FAM, Fleetwood Haiya, anati FAM inatsatira malamulo oyenera pamene inasintha malamulo. Iye anati FIFA imafuna kuti magulu onse a mpira akhale ndi malamulo ogwirizana ndi malamulo a FIFA. Wapampando wa Bungwe la Masewera, Jim Kalua, anati adalandira kalatayo ndipo ofesi ya bungweli ikuyang'ana nkhaniyi. FAM ikukonzekera kuchita Msonkhano Wapachaka pa 27 June ku Sunbird Livingstonia Beach ku Salima, koma magulu atatuwa sadzaloledwa kulowa nawo kapena kulankhula.

en.title: Economists warn of gaps in Malawi's recovery plan
en.body: The Economics Association of Malawi says the government's proposed National Economic Recovery Plan has weak points in growth, foreign exchange, spending and how it will be done. Ecama president Bertha Bangara-Chikadza spoke at a workshop in Lilongwe on Wednesday. She said the plan finds the problems but does not fix the deep causes.
en.body_more:
Bangara-Chikadza told the workshop that Malawi's economic problems come from more than outside shocks. She said the country has low productivity, weak export sales, bad governance, wasteful spending and weak private business. The government named four key sectors: Agriculture, Tourism, Mining and Manufacturing. But Ecama said public money goes mostly to agriculture only. The group wants more money for tourism, mining, horticulture and value-added manufacturing because these bring in foreign money.

Reserve Bank of Malawi Governor George Patridge also spoke at the workshop. He said Malawi's foreign exchange problems are deep. Money supply grew by 51 percent while the economy grew less than two percent, he said. This puts pressure on foreign exchange. Patridge said rapid borrowing and budget gaps have made demand for foreign currency too high.

Ecama warned that high debt, low money to spend and growing interest costs could stop the plan from working unless private business gives more money. Temwani Simwaka, chief of NBS Bank, agreed. He said the Stock Exchange should help pay for long-term projects when banks cannot. Civil society groups also told the government to add clearer yearly targets and learning from past plans that did not work well. They want spending limits on new promises and better checks on how money is used.

ny.title: Akatswiri azachuma akuchenjeza za mipata yomwe ili mu dongosolo lokonzanso zinthu ku Malawi
ny.body: Bungwe la zachuma la Malawi lati ndondomeko ya boma yokonzanso chuma cha dziko ili ndi zofooka pakukula kwachuma, ndalama zakunja, ndalama zomwe zimagwiritsidwa ntchito komanso momwe zidzachitikire. Purezidenti wa Ecama Bertha Bangara-Chikadza adalankhula pa msonkhano ku Lilongwe Lachitatu. Iye adati dongosololi lapeza mavuto koma silikukonza zomwe zimayambitsa mavutowo.
ny.body_more:
Bangara-Chikadza adauza msonkhanowo kuti mavuto azachuma ku Malawi amachokera ku zinthu zambiri osati ku zinthu zakunja. Iye adati dzikolo lili ndi zokolola zochepa, kugulitsa zinthu kunja kofooka, ulamuliro woipa, kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama molakwika komanso mabizinesi achinsinsi ofooka. Boma latchula magawo anayi ofunikira: Ulimi, Zokopa alendo, Migodi ndi Kupanga. Koma Ecama adati ndalama za boma zimapita makamaka ku ulimi wokha. Gululi likufuna ndalama zambiri zoyendera alendo, migodi, ulimi wa maluwa ndi kupanga zinthu zopindulitsa chifukwa izi zimabweretsa ndalama zakunja.

Bwanamkubwa wa Reserve Bank of Malawi George Patridge adalankhulanso pamsonkhanowo. Iye adati mavuto azachuma ku Malawi ndi akulu. Kupezeka kwa ndalama kudakula ndi 51 peresenti pomwe chuma chidakula pang'ono kuposa awiri peresenti, adatero. Izi zikuika mphamvu pakusinthana kwa ndalama zakunja. Patridge adati kubwereka mwachangu komanso kusiyana kwa bajeti kwapangitsa kuti kufunikira kwa ndalama zakunja kukhale kwakukulu kwambiri.

Ecama adachenjeza kuti ngongole yayikulu, ndalama zochepa zoti agwiritse ntchito komanso ndalama zomwe zikukula zitha kuletsa dongosololi kugwira ntchito pokhapokha ngati mabizinesi achinsinsi apereka ndalama zambiri. Temwani Simwaka, mkulu wa NBS Bank, adavomereza. Iye adati Stock Exchange iyenera kuthandiza kulipira mapulojekiti anthawi yayitali pomwe mabanki sangathe. Mabungwe a anthu wamba adauzanso boma kuti liwonjezere zolinga zomveka bwino pachaka ndikuphunzira kuchokera ku mapulani akale omwe sanagwire ntchito bwino. Akufuna malire ogwiritsira ntchito ndalama pa malonjezo atsopano komanso kuwunika bwino momwe ndalama zimagwiritsidwira ntchito.

en.title: Ex-PPDA Boss Takes Dismissal Case to Court
en.body: Eddington Chilapondwa, the former head of the Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority (PPDA), has gone to the Industrial Relations Court. He says his suspension in January 2026 and dismissal in April 2026 were illegal. Chilapondwa argues that the Treasury Secretary, not the PPDA Board, signed the suspension letter—but the law says only the board can do this.
en.body_more:
Chilapondwa told the court that Section 17(3) of the PPDA Act gives the board the power to suspend the director general. The Secretary to the Treasury, Cliff Chiunda, signed the suspension letter instead. Chilapondwa said his contract was only between him and the board, not with the Treasury.

The former board chairperson, Jacob Nyirongo, confirmed that the board had left office while Chilapondwa was still working. The current board chairperson, Kalekeni Kaphale, said in February 2026 that there was no board when the suspension happened. Kaphale said, "There was no board until last week." Chilapondwa argues that without a board in place, the suspension was not legal.

Chilapondwa also says he stayed suspended for over five months. The law says suspension can last only 60 days. He received a 21-day extension letter sent to his home at midnight. He asked the court to say his suspension and dismissal were illegal. He wants to go back to his job or get money to pay him. When a new board was made, it asked him to come to a hearing. He refused because he said the whole process was tainted from the start.

Stuart Ligomeka, the Comptroller for Statutory Corporations, would not comment. He said the case is now in court. Finance Minister Joseph Mwanamvekha said he had not been told about the matter yet.

ny.title: Bwana wakale wa PPDA Atenga Mlandu Ku Khoti
ny.body: Eddington Chilapondwa, yemwe kale anali mkulu wa Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority (PPDA), wapita ku Khoti Loona za Ubale wa Antchito. Iye akuti kuyimitsidwa kwake mu Januwale 2026 ndi kuchotsedwa ntchito mu Epulo 2026 kunali kosaloledwa. Chilapondwa akunena kuti Mlembi wa Zachuma, osati Bungwe la PPDA, ndiye adasaina kalata yoyimitsidwa—koma lamulo limati bungwe lokha ndi lomwe lingachite izi.
ny.body_more:
Chilapondwa adauza khoti kuti Gawo 17(3) la PPDA Act limapatsa bungweli mphamvu zoyimitsa director wamkulu. Mlembi wa Zachuma, Cliff Chiunda, adasaina kalata yoyimitsa ntchito m'malo mwake. Chilapondwa adati pangano lake linali pakati pa iye ndi bungweli, osati ndi Zachuma.

Wapampando wakale wa bungweli, Jacob Nyirongo, adatsimikiza kuti bungweli linasiya ntchito pomwe Chilapondwa anali kugwirabe ntchito. Wapampando wa bungweli, Kalekeni Kaphale, adati mu February 2026 kuti panalibe bungweli pamene kuyimitsa ntchito kunachitika. Kaphale adati, "Panalibe bungweli mpaka sabata yatha." Chilapondwa akunena kuti popanda bungweli, kuyimitsa ntchito sikunali kovomerezeka.

Chilapondwa akunenanso kuti adakhalabe woyimitsidwa kwa miyezi yoposa isanu. Lamulo limati kuyimitsidwa ntchito kumatha kukhala masiku 60 okha. Adalandira kalata yowonjezera ya masiku 21 yotumizidwa kunyumba kwake pakati pausiku. Adapempha khotilo kuti linene kuti kuyimitsidwa kwake ndi kuchotsedwa ntchito kwake kunali kosaloledwa. Akufuna kubwerera kuntchito yake kapena kupeza ndalama zomulipira. Bungwe latsopano litapangidwa, linamupempha kuti abwere kudzamvetsera. Iye anakana chifukwa anati ndondomeko yonseyi inali yoipa kuyambira pachiyambi.

Stuart Ligomeka, Woyang'anira Mabungwe Ovomerezeka, sananenepo kanthu. Iye anati nkhaniyi ili kukhoti tsopano. Nduna ya Zachuma Joseph Mwanamvekakha anati sanauzidwe za nkhaniyi.

en.title: Malawi Faces Environmental Crisis, Government Acts
en.body: Malawi is in danger. A new government report launched on Thursday warns that forests are dying, soils are breaking down, and the population is growing too fast. The 2026 National State of the Environment and Outlook Report says the country is using up its natural resources faster than they can grow back. Minister of Natural Resources Patricia Wiskes says the government will act to fix the damage.
en.body_more:
The report shows Malawi's problems are serious and growing. The population grew from 13.1 million people in 2008 to 20.7 million in 2024. If this trend continues, 40 million people will live in Malawi by 2050. More people need more food, so more forests are being cut down. About 90 percent of homes burn firewood and charcoal for energy, which kills more trees. Forests covered less land each year.

Soils are also getting worse. An average of 29 tonnes of soil is lost each year from each hectare of land. In some places, 39 tonnes are lost yearly. This is very bad because 85 percent of Malawi's workers farm the land. The soil is becoming too weak to grow good crops. Climate change makes everything worse. Since the late 1990s, Malawi has had 19 big floods and seven severe droughts. Scientists say temperatures will rise by more than one degree Celsius by 2040. This will bring more rain problems and hurt farming even more.

The government is taking steps to stop the damage. Minister Wiskes said the government will plant more trees, fix broken ecosystems, and teach farmers to manage land better. Rebecca Adda-Dontoh said Malawi is one of the ten countries most affected by climate change. She said protecting forests, water, and energy systems will help families and farmers. Norway's Deputy Ambassador Ranveig Rajendra said investing in the environment is not a choice—it must happen. Board Chairperson Yanira Ntupanyama said everyone must help: communities, businesses, schools, traditional leaders, and aid groups.

The report was made by 60 Malawian scientists with help from the United Nations Environment Programme and GRID-Arendal. The Government of Norway paid for the work.

ny.title: Malawi Ikukumana ndi Mavuto a Zachilengedwe, Malamulo a Boma
ny.body: Malawi ili pachiwopsezo. Lipoti latsopano la boma lomwe latulutsidwa Lachinayi likuchenjeza kuti nkhalango zikutha, dothi likuphwanyika, ndipo chiwerengero cha anthu chikukwera mofulumira kwambiri. Lipoti la 2026 National State of the Environment and Outlook Report likuti dzikolo likugwiritsa ntchito zachilengedwe zake mwachangu kuposa momwe zingamerenso. Nduna ya Zachilengedwe Patricia Wiskes akuti boma lichitapo kanthu kuti likonze kuwonongekako.
ny.body_more:
Lipotilo likuwonetsa kuti mavuto aku Malawi ndi aakulu komanso akukula. Chiwerengero cha anthu chinakula kuchoka pa anthu 13.1 miliyoni mu 2008 kufika pa 20.7 miliyoni mu 2024. Ngati izi zipitirira, anthu 40 miliyoni adzakhala ku Malawi pofika chaka cha 2050. Anthu ambiri amafunika chakudya chochulukirapo, kotero nkhalango zambiri zikudulidwa. Pafupifupi 90 peresenti ya nyumba zimayatsa nkhuni ndi makala kuti zipeze mphamvu, zomwe zimapha mitengo yambiri. Nkhalango zinali ndi malo ochepa chaka chilichonse.

Nthaka ikuipiraipiranso. Pa avareji ya matani 29 a nthaka imatayika chaka chilichonse kuchokera pa hekitala iliyonse ya nthaka. M'malo ena, matani 39 amatayika chaka chilichonse. Izi ndi zoyipa kwambiri chifukwa 85 peresenti ya ogwira ntchito ku Malawi amalima nthakayo. Nthaka ikufooka kwambiri kuti isabzale mbewu zabwino. Kusintha kwa nyengo kumapangitsa chilichonse kukhala choipa kwambiri. Kuyambira kumapeto kwa zaka za m'ma 1990, Malawi yakhala ndi kusefukira kwa madzi kwakukulu ka 19 ndi chilala chachikulu kasanu ndi kawiri. Asayansi amati kutentha kudzakwera ndi madigiri Celsius oposa limodzi pofika chaka cha 2040. Izi zibweretsa mavuto ambiri amvula ndikuvulaza ulimi kwambiri.

Boma likuchitapo kanthu kuti lithetse kuwonongeka kumeneku. Nduna Wiskes yati boma lidzabzala mitengo yambiri, kukonza zachilengedwe zomwe zawonongeka, ndikuphunzitsa alimi kusamalira bwino malo. Rebecca Adda-Dontoh yati Malawi ndi limodzi mwa mayiko khumi omwe akhudzidwa kwambiri ndi kusintha kwa nyengo. Yati kuteteza nkhalango, madzi, ndi mphamvu kungathandize mabanja ndi alimi. Wachiwiri kwa Kazembe wa Norway, Ranveig Rajendra, adati kuyika ndalama mu chilengedwe si chisankho—ziyenera kuchitika. Wapampando wa bungwe la Yanira Ntupanyama, adati aliyense ayenera kuthandiza: madera, mabizinesi, masukulu, atsogoleri achikhalidwe, ndi magulu othandizira.

Lipotilo linapangidwa ndi asayansi 60 aku Malawi mothandizidwa ndi United Nations Environment Programme ndi GRID-Arendal. Boma la Norway linalipira ntchitoyo.

en.title: Bank Lending Rates Fall To 20.4 Percent In June
en.body: Commercial banks in Malawi cut the reference lending rate to 20.4 percent in June, down from 20.6 percent in May. The rate has fallen nearly five percentage points since January, when it was 25.2 percent. Banks use this benchmark rate to set loan prices for businesses, farmers and families.
en.body_more:
The drop follows a steady downward trend over six months. The rate fell to 24.7 percent in February, 22.4 percent in March, 20.8 percent in April, and 20.6 percent in May before reaching 20.4 percent in June. The Reserve Bank of Malawi cut its policy rate from 26 percent to 24 percent in March, signaling confidence in the economy.

Financial Market Dealers Association Publicity Secretary Rupert Nkhono said the latest cut is due to high liquidity in the banking system. Banks and other financial institutions are holding large amounts of cash and short-term Treasury bills. The government's decision to limit borrowing and secure funds at lower yields has also helped push rates down, he said. "The drop is due to higher levels of liquidity in the market," Nkhono explained. "Excess cash and liquidity, plus conservative government borrowing, are the main driving factors."

Economist Marvin Banda said the reduction is small and most borrowers will not see big changes in monthly loan payments. Commercial lending rates in Malawi remain among the highest in the region. However, Banda said the move sends an important signal. "The encouraging aspect is the signal being sent," he said. "If inflation continues to decline and macroeconomic stability improves, this could be the continuation of a gradual easing cycle." Banda warned that Malawi still faces major challenges including foreign exchange shortages, high public debt, energy constraints, low productivity and weak export earnings.

The decline is being driven by improving liquidity, lower government borrowing and easing inflation. If inflation stays under control and economic stability continues, Malawi could see further rate cuts in the months ahead.

ny.title: Mitengo Yobwereketsa Mabanki Yatsika ndi 20.4 peresenti mu June
ny.body: Mabanki amalonda ku Malawi achepetsa chiwongola dzanja cha ngongole kufika pa 20.4 peresenti mu June, kuchokera pa 20.6 peresenti mu May. Chiwongola dzanja chatsika pafupifupi maperesenti asanu kuyambira Januwale, pomwe chinali 25.2 peresenti. Mabanki amagwiritsa ntchito chiwongola dzanja ichi poika mitengo ya ngongole kwa mabizinesi, alimi ndi mabanja.
ny.body_more:
Kutsika kumeneku kukutsatira kutsika pang'onopang'ono kwa miyezi isanu ndi umodzi. Chiwongola dzanjacho chinatsika kufika pa 24.7 peresenti mu February, 22.4 peresenti mu March, 20.8 peresenti mu April, ndi 20.6 peresenti mu May chisanafike pa 20.4 peresenti mu June. Reserve Bank of Malawi inachepetsa chiwongola dzanja chake cha ndondomeko kuchoka pa 26 peresenti kufika pa 24 peresenti mu March, zomwe zikusonyeza chidaliro mu chuma.

Mlembi wa Zamalonda wa Financial Market Dealers Association, Rupert Nkhono, anati kuchepetsa kwaposachedwaku chifukwa cha kuchuluka kwa ndalama zomwe zimalowa m'mabanki. Mabanki ndi mabungwe ena azachuma ali ndi ndalama zambiri komanso ma bilu a Treasury a nthawi yochepa. Chisankho cha boma chochepetsa kubwereka ndi kupeza ndalama zotsika chathandizanso kuchepetsa mitengo, adatero. "Kutsikaku kukuchitika chifukwa cha kuchuluka kwa ndalama zomwe zimalowa m'misika," Nkhono anafotokoza. "Ndalama zambiri komanso ndalama zomwe zimalowa m'misika, kuphatikiza kubwereka kwa boma kosasamala, ndizomwe zimayambitsa vutoli."

Wachuma Marvin Banda adati kuchepaku ndi kochepa ndipo obwereka ambiri sadzawona kusintha kwakukulu pakulipira ngongole pamwezi. Mitengo yobwereka yamalonda ku Malawi ikadali pakati pa okwera kwambiri m'chigawochi. Komabe, Banda anati izi zikupereka chizindikiro chofunika kwambiri. "Chinthu cholimbikitsa ndi chizindikiro chomwe chikutumizidwa," adatero. "Ngati kukwera kwa mitengo kukupitirira kutsika ndipo kukhazikika kwachuma kukukwera, izi zitha kukhala kupitiliza kwa kutsika pang'onopang'ono." Banda adachenjeza kuti Malawi ikadali ndi mavuto akulu kuphatikiza kusowa kwa ndalama zakunja, ngongole yayikulu ya boma, kusowa kwa mphamvu, kupanga kochepa komanso ndalama zochepa zogulitsa kunja.

Kutsika kumeneku kukuyendetsedwa ndi kusintha kwa ndalama, kubwereka ndalama kwa boma kutsika komanso kuchepetsa kukwera kwa mitengo. Ngati kukwera kwa mitengo kukupitirirabe kulamulidwa ndipo kukhazikika kwachuma kukupitirira, Malawi ikhoza kuwona kuchepa kwa mitengo m'miyezi ikubwerayi.

en.title: Ngoni King Mpezeni IV dies at 75 in Zambia
en.body: Inkosi ya Makhosi Mpezeni IV, leader of the Ngoni people, has died at age 75 in a hospital in Lusaka, Zambia on May 30, 2026. A delegation from Chipata, Zambia led by Inkosi Saili came to Mzimba on Monday to tell Inkosi ya Makhosi M'Mbelwa V of his brother's death. The M'Mbelwa Kingdom is now in mourning.
en.body_more:
The delegation travelled from Zambia to Edingeni Royal Palace in Mzimba to share the news face-to-face. In Ngoni custom, death of a senior royal must be announced in person, not by phone or letter. M'Mbelwa said Mpezeni IV was "a great leader who made peace, unity and harmony among his people". He thanked Inkosi Saili and Zambia's government "for honouring our shared heritage and for walking the path of our forefathers".

Mpezeni IV was born David Njengembaso Jere. He became king on June 19, 1982 and ruled for 44 years. The Ngoni people live across Malawi, Zambia and Tanzania. Mourners from all three countries are expected to come to his burial in Chipata. The Mzimba Heritage Association has postponed the Ingoma Sunset Soiree that was planned for Mzuzu. New dates for this event and other activities for the 2026 Umthetho Cultural Festival will be announced later. The Umthetho Festival is an annual celebration for Ngoni people from Malawi, Zambia, Tanzania and the diaspora.

In Zambia, the remains of Mpezeni IV have left Lusaka for Chipata. The body was escorted with tight security and followed Ngoni tradition. Thousands of mourners are expected to gather along the Great East Road. In Chipata, preparations are ready to receive the late chief with full cultural honours. Ngoni war dances and mourning rites will happen before the burial. Government has said the funeral will have state recognition because of the chief's importance to the nation.

ny.title: Mfumu Ngoni Mpezeni IV amwalira ali ndi zaka 75 ku Zambia
ny.body: Inkosi ya Makhosi Mpezeni IV, mtsogoleri wa anthu a Ngoni, yamwalira ali ndi zaka 75 m’chipatala ku Lusaka m’dziko la Zambia pa May 30, 2026. Nthumwi za ku Chipata m’dziko la Zambia motsogozedwa ndi Inkosi Saili zinafika ku Mzimba Lolemba kudzauza Inkosi ya Makhosi M’Mbelwa V za imfa ya m’bale wake. Ufumu wa M’Mbelwa tsopano uli pachisoni.
ny.body_more:
Gulu la a Ngoni linachoka ku Zambia kupita ku Nyumba Yachifumu ya Edingeni ku Mzimba kuti likagawane nkhaniyi maso ndi maso. Mwa mwambo wa a Ngoni, imfa ya mfumu wamkulu iyenera kulengezedwa pamaso pa anthu, osati pafoni kapena kalata. M'Mbelwa anati Mpezeni IV anali "mtsogoleri wamkulu amene adapanga mtendere, mgwirizano ndi mgwirizano pakati pa anthu ake". Anayamikira Inkosi Saili ndi boma la Zambia "chifukwa cholemekeza cholowa chathu chogawana komanso poyenda m'njira ya makolo athu".

Mpezeni IV anabadwa David Njengambembaso Jere. Anakhala mfumu pa June 19, 1982 ndipo analamulira kwa zaka 44. Anthu a Ngoni amakhala ku Malawi, Zambia ndi Tanzania. Anthu olira maliro ochokera m'maiko onse atatu akuyembekezeka kubwera ku maliro ake ku Chipata. Bungwe la Mzimba Heritage Association layimitsa mwambo wa Ingoma Sunset Soiree womwe unakonzedwa ku Mzuzu. Masiku atsopano a chochitikachi ndi zochitika zina za 2026 Umthetho Cultural Festival adzalengezedwa pambuyo pake. Umthetho Festival ndi chikondwerero cha pachaka cha anthu a Ngoni ochokera ku Malawi, Zambia, Tanzania ndi anthu ochokera kunja kwa dzikolo.

Ku Zambia, mtembo wa Mpezeni Wachinayi wachoka ku Lusaka kupita ku Chipata. Mtembowo unaperekezedwa ndi chitetezo cholimba ndipo unatsatira mwambo wa Ngoni. Anthu ambirimbiri olira akuyembekezeka kusonkhana mumsewu wa Great East Road. Ku Chipata, kukonzekera kuli kokonzeka kulandira mfumu yomwalirayo ndi ulemu wonse wa chikhalidwe. Magule ankhondo a Ngoni ndi miyambo ya maliro idzachitika maliro asanachitike. Boma lati malirowo adzavomerezedwa ndi boma chifukwa cha kufunika kwa mfumuyo kudziko.

en.title: Malawi urged to act now on climate change
en.body: Malawi must take urgent action on climate change, says the Civil Society Network on Climate Change (CISONECC). The group warns that droughts, floods and damaged land now threaten food, jobs and the economy. CISONECC calls on government, businesses and communities to do more to fight climate change and help vulnerable people.
en.body_more:
CISONECC National Coordinator Julius Ng'oma and Board Chairperson Gift Numeri made the call during World Environment Day, marked on June 5 yearly. They said climate change is no longer a future problem but a present crisis affecting millions of Malawians right now. The group says the rising frequency of climate-related disasters is hurting national development and making poverty worse, especially among communities that depend on natural resources.

CISEONECC called on the Government of Malawi to strengthen climate policies, improve adaptation and mitigation programmes, and increase investment in environmental protection and resilience building. The network urged development partners to support climate action that helps local communities and promotes sustainable development. Communities and citizens should take part in environmental conservation through tree planting, sustainable land management, biodiversity protection, and careful use of natural resources.

The private sector must adopt environmentally friendly practices that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and protect ecosystems, CISONECC said. The organization stressed that protecting forests, wetlands, water bodies and other natural resources is essential for building resilient communities and securing a sustainable future for present and future generations. This year's World Environment Day uses the theme "Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future." CISONECC urged all stakeholders to move beyond commitments and translate climate ambitions into tangible actions that deliver real benefits for people and the planet. "Together, we can build a climate-resilient Malawi and ensure a healthier, safer, and more sustainable environment for all," the statement said.

ny.title: Malawi yalimbikitsidwa kuchitapo kanthu tsopano pankhani yokhudza kusintha kwa nyengo
ny.body: Bungwe la Civil Society Network on Climate Change (CISONECC) lati dziko la Malawi liyenera kuchitapo kanthu mwachangu pankhani ya kusintha kwa nyengo. Gululi likuchenjeza kuti chilala, kusefukira kwa madzi ndi malo owonongeka tsopano zikuopseza chakudya, ntchito ndi chuma. CISONECC ikupempha boma, mabizinesi ndi madera kuti achite zambiri polimbana ndi kusintha kwa nyengo ndi kuthandiza anthu ovutika.
ny.body_more:
Wogwirizanitsa Dziko Lonse wa CISONECC Julius Ng'oma ndi Wapampando wa Bungwe Gift Numeri adapereka pempholi pa Tsiku la Zachilengedwe Padziko Lonse, lomwe limachitika pa June 5 chaka chilichonse. Iwo adati kusintha kwa nyengo sikulinso vuto la mtsogolo koma vuto lomwe likukhudza anthu mamiliyoni ambiri aku Malawi pakadali pano. Gululi likuti kukwera kwa masoka okhudzana ndi nyengo kukuwononga chitukuko cha dziko ndikupangitsa umphawi kukhala woipitsitsa, makamaka pakati pa anthu omwe amadalira zachilengedwe.

CISEONECC idapempha Boma la Malawi kuti lilimbikitse mfundo za nyengo, kukonza mapulogalamu osinthira ndi kuchepetsa mavuto, ndikuwonjezera ndalama pakuteteza zachilengedwe ndi kumanga kulimba mtima. Netiwekiyi idalimbikitsa ogwira nawo ntchito zachitukuko kuti athandizire kuchitapo kanthu pa nyengo komwe kumathandiza anthu am'deralo ndikulimbikitsa chitukuko chokhazikika. Anthu ammudzi ndi nzika ayenera kutenga nawo mbali pakusunga chilengedwe kudzera mu kubzala mitengo, kusamalira nthaka kokhazikika, kuteteza zamoyo zosiyanasiyana, komanso kugwiritsa ntchito mosamala zachilengedwe.

Mabungwe achinsinsi ayenera kutsatira njira zoteteza chilengedwe zomwe zimachepetsa mpweya wowononga chilengedwe ndikuteteza zachilengedwe, CISONECC idatero. Bungweli lidagogomezera kuti kuteteza nkhalango, madambo, madzi ndi zinthu zina zachilengedwe ndikofunikira kwambiri popanga madera olimba mtima ndikuteteza tsogolo lokhazikika la mibadwo yamakono ndi yamtsogolo. Tsiku la Zachilengedwe Padziko Lonse la chaka chino limagwiritsa ntchito mutu wakuti "Kuuziridwa ndi Chilengedwe. Kwa Nyengo. Kwa Tsogolo Lathu." CISONECC yapempha onse okhudzidwa kuti apitirire kupitirira zomwe adalonjeza ndikusandutsa zolinga zokhudzana ndi nyengo kukhala zochita zenizeni zomwe zimabweretsa phindu lenileni kwa anthu ndi dziko lapansi. "Pamodzi, titha kumanga Malawi yolimbana ndi nyengo ndikuwonetsetsa kuti chilengedwe chathanzi, chotetezeka, komanso chokhazikika kwa onse," idatero chikalatacho.

en.title: USA gives Malawi $792 million for health
en.body: The United States will give Malawi $792 million over the next five years for health work. US Chargé d'Affaires Jonathan Fischer said this in Lilongwe. The two countries marked 62 years as friends and America's 250 years of freedom. The money comes from a health agreement they signed in January.
en.body_more:
Fischer said the $792 million builds on more than $6 billion the USA has given Malawi since it became free. Since 2013 alone, America has put $2.5 billion into Malawi's health work. This money has helped hospitals, stopped disease, and paid for health workers and medicines.

The partnership does more than health. The USA also helps Malawi with food, learning, and bringing people together. Nearly 1,400 Malawians have been in US exchange programmes since 1953. More than 3,000 Peace Corps helpers have worked in Malawi since 1963. A new US Embassy is being built in Lilongwe. It has put $90 million into the city and given jobs to more than 1,800 Malawians. The embassy will open in 2027.

Minister of Foreign Affairs George Chaponda said Malawi likes the help. But he said the country wants more trade and business with the USA, not just aid. Malawi wants to buy and sell more with America. Chaponda also asked the USA to put money into mining, making things, and farming in Malawi. He said Malawi is not poor but the people are poor. The government wants African Growth and Opportunity Act help. This lets African countries sell things to America more easily.

ny.title: USA yapereka ndalama zokwana $792 miliyoni ku Malawi pa nkhani zaumoyo
ny.body: Dziko la United States lipereka ndalama zokwana $792 miliyoni ku Malawi pazaka zisanu zikubwerazi kuti lithandizire pa ntchito zaumoyo. Jonathan Fischer, yemwe ndi mkulu wa bungwe la US, wanena izi ku Lilongwe. Mayiko awiriwa akondwerera zaka 62 monga mabwenzi komanso zaka 250 za ufulu wa America. Ndalamazi zimachokera ku mgwirizano wa zaumoyo womwe adasaina mu Januwale.
ny.body_more:
Fischer anati ndalama zokwana $792 miliyoni zikuwonjezeka pa ndalama zoposa $6 biliyoni zomwe USA yapereka ku Malawi kuyambira pomwe idayamba kukhala yaulere. Kuyambira mu 2013 yokha, America yagwiritsa ntchito $2.5 biliyoni pantchito yazaumoyo ku Malawi. Ndalamazi zathandiza zipatala, zaletsa matenda, komanso zalipira ogwira ntchito zachipatala ndi mankhwala.

Mgwirizanowu umachita zambiri kuposa zaumoyo. USA imathandizanso Malawi ndi chakudya, kuphunzira, komanso kusonkhanitsa anthu. Anthu aku Malawi pafupifupi 1,400 akhala mu mapulogalamu osinthana ndalama aku US kuyambira mu 1953. Othandizira a Peace Corps oposa 3,000 agwira ntchito ku Malawi kuyambira mu 1963. Kazembe watsopano wa US akumangidwa ku Lilongwe. Wagwiritsa ntchito $90 miliyoni mumzindawu ndipo wapereka ntchito kwa anthu aku Malawi oposa 1,800. Kazembeyo idzatsegulidwa mu 2027.

Nduna ya Zachilendo George Chaponda adati Malawi imakonda thandizoli. Koma adati dzikolo likufuna malonda ndi bizinesi yambiri ndi USA, osati thandizo lokha. Malawi ikufuna kugula ndikugulitsa zambiri ndi America. Chaponda adapemphanso USA kuti igwiritse ntchito ndalama mu migodi, kupanga zinthu, ndi ulimi ku Malawi. Iye adati Malawi si osauka koma anthu ndi osauka. Boma likufuna thandizo la African Growth and Opportunity Act. Izi zimathandiza mayiko aku Africa kugulitsa zinthu ku America mosavuta.

en.title: US Visa Rules Make It Hard for Malawians to Travel
en.body: Malawi's Foreign Affairs Minister George Chaponda has said new US visa rules are causing big problems. Students, sick people, and business people from Malawi cannot easily go to America now. The US made these new rules on 1 January 2026. Chaponda spoke about this during celebrations of 250 years of US freedom.
en.body_more:
The US government has made it harder for people from Malawi to get visas. Chaponda told people that students who got into American universities now face problems. Sick people who need medical help in America cannot travel easily. Business people who want to trade with America also face new difficulties.

The US is also changing where people can apply for visas. Right now, there are about 50 visa offices across Africa south of the Sahara. The US plans to cut this to only 20 offices. If this happens, Malawians will have to travel to other countries like Tanzania, South Africa or Lesotho just to apply for a visa. This will cost families many millions of kwacha in travel money before they even get an answer.

The US Embassy in Lilongwe says it has no new news to share about what it will do. The embassy says the US State Department always looks at its offices to make sure they work well. It says it will tell people directly if anything changes.

Experts say these rules show that Malawi may have problems with how it works as a country. Visa rules like these often come when other countries worry about how safe a place is, or how good its papers and systems are. People are asking why Malawi, which needs help from other countries for business, school and money, is now having more trouble with America. They say the government must find out why this happened and fix the real problems.

ny.title: Malamulo a Visa aku US Amapangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta kwa a Malawi kuyenda
ny.body: Nduna ya Zachilendo ku Malawi, George Chaponda, yati malamulo atsopano a visa aku US akubweretsa mavuto akulu. Ophunzira, odwala, ndi amalonda ochokera ku Malawi sangathe kupita ku America mosavuta tsopano. US idapanga malamulo atsopanowa pa 1 Januwale 2026. Chaponda adalankhula za izi panthawi yokondwerera zaka 250 za ufulu wa US.
ny.body_more:
Boma la US lapangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta kwa anthu ochokera ku Malawi kupeza ma visa. Chaponda adauza anthu kuti ophunzira omwe adalowa m'mayunivesite aku America tsopano akukumana ndi mavuto. Anthu odwala omwe akufunika thandizo lachipatala ku America sangathe kuyenda mosavuta. Amalonda omwe akufuna kuchita malonda ndi America akukumananso ndi mavuto atsopano.

US ikusinthanso komwe anthu angapemphe ma visa. Pakadali pano, pali maofesi pafupifupi 50 a ma visa ku Africa kum'mwera kwa Sahara. US ikukonzekera kuchepetsa izi kukhala maofesi 20 okha. Ngati izi zitachitika, anthu aku Malawi adzayenera kupita kumayiko ena monga Tanzania, South Africa kapena Lesotho kuti akapemphe visa. Izi zidzawonongera mabanja ndalama zambiri zoyendera asanapeze yankho.

Embassy ya US ku Lilongwe ikunena kuti ilibe nkhani yatsopano yoti igawane za zomwe idzachita. Embassy ikunena kuti Dipatimenti ya Zachilendo ya US nthawi zonse imayang'ana maofesi ake kuti atsimikizire kuti akugwira ntchito bwino. Ikuti idzauza anthu mwachindunji ngati chilichonse chasintha.

Akatswiri akuti malamulo awa akuwonetsa kuti Malawi ikhoza kukhala ndi mavuto ndi momwe imagwirira ntchito ngati dziko. Malamulo a Visa ngati awa nthawi zambiri amabwera pamene mayiko ena akuda nkhawa ndi chitetezo cha malo, kapena momwe mapepala ndi machitidwe ake alili abwino. Anthu akufunsa chifukwa chake Malawi, yomwe ikufunika thandizo kuchokera kumayiko ena pazamalonda, masukulu ndi ndalama, tsopano ikukumana ndi mavuto ambiri ndi America. Akuti boma liyenera kupeza chifukwa chake izi zachitika ndikukonza mavuto enieni.

en.title: Hotel Inquiry Stalls, Answers Still Missing
en.body: A big investigation into the Amaryllis Hotel has stopped moving forward. The hotel was bought by the Public Service Pension Trust Fund for K128 billion. The Parliamentary Public Accounts Committee started to look at the deal but has not finished. Many people want to know what happened.
en.body_more:
The committee first said it had finished its work. Then it stopped. Some government officials said key people had not been heard yet. The committee asked many people to come and answer questions. These people were Colleen Zamba, the old Secretary to the President, Justin Saidi, the Chief Secretary to the Government, people from Yusuf Investment Limited, and Macdonald Mwale, the old head of the Reserve Bank of Malawi.

Colleen Zamba did not come. Her lawyer George Kadzipatike said she will not come because there is a court case about the hotel. She is also away from Malawi getting help for her health. The people from Yusuf Investment Limited also did not come. The committee leader Steven Malondera did not answer calls or messages. His helper Tulinje Muluzi said to ask Malondera instead.

The K128 billion price is very big. In 2024, the hotel was valued at only K47 billion. A report that leaked said that the deal was wrong and broke rules. The report said some government leaders should face criminal charges. The Financial Intelligence Authority and the Anti-Corruption Bureau froze bank accounts tied to the deal. They are looking at money laundering and corruption.

Political analyst George Chaima said the long wait is making people lose faith. The Registrar of Financial Institutions took away the jobs of 11 trustees for the fund because they would not stop the hotel buy. The government must pick new trustees. But the Ministry of Finance has not said if new trustees are in place. Many people worry the whole thing will be forgotten.

ny.title: Malo Ofufuzira a Hotelo, Mayankho Akusowekabe
ny.body: Kafukufuku wamkulu wokhudza hotelo ya Amaryllis wasiya kupita patsogolo. Hoteloyi idagulidwa ndi Public Service Pension Trust Fund pa mtengo wa K128 biliyoni. Komiti ya Nyumba Yamalamulo Yoona za Maakaunti a Anthu inayamba kuyang'ana mgwirizanowu koma sinathe. Anthu ambiri akufuna kudziwa zomwe zinachitika.
ny.body_more:
Komitiyi inayamba yanena kuti yamaliza ntchito yake. Kenako inasiya. Akuluakulu ena aboma anati anthu ofunikira sanamvedwebe. Komitiyi inapempha anthu ambiri kuti abwere kudzayankha mafunso. Anthu awa anali Colleen Zamba, Mlembi wakale wa Purezidenti, Justin Saidi, Mlembi Wamkulu wa Boma, anthu ochokera ku Yusuf Investment Limited, ndi Macdonald Mwale, mtsogoleri wakale wa Reserve Bank of Malawi.

Colleen Zamba sanabwere. Loya wake George Kadzipatike anati sabwera chifukwa pali mlandu wokhudza hoteloyi. Ali kutali ndi Malawi kukalandira thandizo la thanzi lake. Anthu ochokera ku Yusuf Investment Limited nawonso sanabwere. Mtsogoleri wa komitiyi Steven Malondera sanayankhe mafoni kapena mauthenga. Wothandizira wake Tulinje Muluzi anati apemphe Malondera m'malo mwake.

Mtengo wa K128 biliyoni ndi waukulu kwambiri. Mu 2024, hoteloyi inali ndi mtengo wa K47 biliyoni yokha. Lipoti lomwe linatuluka linati mgwirizanowu unali wolakwika ndipo unaswa malamulo. Lipotilo linati atsogoleri ena aboma ayenera kuimbidwa milandu. Bungwe la Financial Intelligence Authority ndi bungwe lolimbana ndi ziphuphu latseka maakaunti akubanki okhudzana ndi mgwirizanowu. Akuyang'ana kutsuka ndalama ndi ziphuphu.

Katswiri wa ndale George Chaima adati kudikira kwa nthawi yayitali kukupangitsa anthu kutaya chikhulupiriro. Registrar of Financial Institutions adachotsa ntchito za matrasti 11 a thumba la ndalama chifukwa sakanaletsa kugula hotelo. Boma liyenera kusankha matrasti atsopano. Koma Unduna wa Zachuma sunanene ngati matrasti atsopano alipo. Anthu ambiri akuda nkhawa kuti nkhaniyi iiwalika.

en.title: Government Removes Journalist From Tanzania Diplomat Post
en.body: The government has withdrawn journalist Aubrey Sumbuleta from his appointment as High Commissioner-designate to Tanzania. Sumbuleta was scheduled to appear before Parliament's Public Appointments Committee in Lilongwe for confirmation hearings, but the appointment was cancelled at the last moment. No reason was given for the withdrawal.
en.body_more:
PAC chairperson Felix Njawala confirmed that lawmakers received official communication about the withdrawal while conducting interviews for diplomatic nominees. "We received communication regarding the withdrawal of Mr Sumbuleta who was supposed to appear before this committee for confirmation hearings," Njawala said. The committee was not told why the decision was made.

Sumbuleta is a well-known journalist and public relations specialist. He worked as public relations manager at the Malawi Electoral Commission and later became director general of the Malawi Broadcasting Corporation. His experience in media and government made him a strong choice for the Tanzania posting. Tanzania is one of Malawi's closest neighbours and an important trade and security partner.

The removal is unusual because diplomatic nominees normally go through extensive background checks long before they face Parliament. Last-minute withdrawals are rare and often raise questions. While other diplomats nominated on the same day — including Robert Dafter Salama for South Africa, Patrick Makina for Zambia, David Nungu for Zimbabwe, Reynold Mangisa for Mozambique, and Spoon Phiri as Permanent Representative to the United Nations — all kept their appointments and went through confirmation hearings without problems.

When journalists asked for answers, Office of the President and Cabinet spokesperson Focus Maganga asked for more time. Chief Secretary Justin Saidi said he first needed to find out the facts before responding. The government has not yet explained why Sumbuleta's name was removed from the list. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs is expected to announce next steps on the Tanzania post soon.

ny.title: Boma Lachotsa Mtolankhani ku Udindo wa Diplomat ku Tanzania
ny.body: Boma lachotsa mtolankhani Aubrey Sumbuleta pa udindo wake monga High Commissioner ku Tanzania. Sumbuleta anayenera kukaonekera pamaso pa Komiti Yoona za Kusankhidwa kwa Anthu ku Nyumba Yamalamulo ku Lilongwe kuti akamve maganizo ake, koma udindo wake unathetsedwa nthawi yomaliza. Palibe chifukwa chomwe chinaperekedwa chochotsera udindowu.
ny.body_more:
Wapampando wa PAC, Felix Njawala, watsimikiza kuti opanga malamulo adalandira mauthenga ovomerezeka okhudza kuchoka kwa a Sumbuleta omwe amayenera kuonekera pamaso pa komitiyi kuti akamve zotsimikizira za chisankho chawo, "Tinalandira mauthenga okhudza kuchoka kwa a Sumbuleta omwe amayenera kuonekera pamaso pa komitiyi kuti akamve zotsimikizira za chisankho chawo," adatero Njawala. Komitiyo sinadziwitsidwe chifukwa chake chisankhocho chinapangidwa.

Sumbuleta ndi mtolankhani wodziwika bwino komanso katswiri wodziwa za ubale ndi anthu. Anagwira ntchito ngati manejala wa ubale ndi anthu ku Malawi Electoral Commission ndipo pambuyo pake anakhala director wamkulu wa Malawi Broadcasting Corporation. Chidziwitso chake muzofalitsa nkhani ndi boma chinamupangitsa kukhala chisankho chabwino kwambiri pa ntchito ya ku Tanzania. Tanzania ndi imodzi mwa mayiko oyandikana kwambiri ndi Malawi komanso bwenzi lofunika kwambiri pazamalonda ndi chitetezo.

Kuchotsedwa kumeneku sikwachilendo chifukwa osankhidwa a diplomatic nthawi zambiri amafufuzidwa kwambiri mbiri yawo asanakumane ndi Nyumba Yamalamulo. Kuchoka komaliza kumakhala kochepa ndipo nthawi zambiri kumabweretsa mafunso. Pomwe oimira boma ena omwe adasankhidwa tsiku lomwelo - kuphatikizapo Robert Dafter Salama wa ku South Africa, Patrick Makina wa ku Zambia, David Nungu wa ku Zimbabwe, Reynold Mangisa wa ku Mozambique, ndi Spoon Phiri monga Woyimira Wosatha ku United Nations - onse adasunga maudindo awo ndipo adapita kumisonkhano yotsimikizira popanda mavuto.

Atolankhani atapempha mayankho, Ofesi ya Purezidenti komanso wolankhulira nduna Focus Maganga anapempha nthawi yochulukirapo. Mlembi Wamkulu Justin Saidi anati choyamba ayenera kupeza zoona zake asanayankhe. Boma silinafotokoze chifukwa chake dzina la Sumbuleta linachotsedwa pamndandanda. Unduna wa Zachilendo ukuyembekezeka kulengeza zomwe zichitike paudindo wa ku Tanzania posachedwa.

en.title: WHO says Malawi made good progress in health care
en.body: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has praised Malawi for making progress in health care. The WHO report came out on June 1, 2026. It says Malawi did well with polio, HIV treatment, maternal health, newborn health, disease checks and cholera control. Malawi faced money problems and disease outbreaks but still improved.
en.body_more:
The report shows that mothers and babies died less often. Maternal mortality fell from 439 deaths per 100,000 live births in 2015-16 to 224 deaths per 100,000 live births in 2024. Neonatal mortality dropped from 27 to 24 deaths per 1,000 live births over the same time. Child mortality and fertility rates also went down.

Malawi kept high rates of child vaccination. DPT3 coverage was 94 percent and measles-rubella first dose coverage was 89 percent. The country ran a National Human Papillomavirus vaccination campaign that reached over two million adolescent girls. WHO said this was a historic milestone that will help stop cervical cancer. Malawi also met the UNAIDS 95-95-95 HIV treatment targets even though the United States Government gave less money.

Malawi improved its ability to respond to health emergencies. The country made its first fully costed five-year National Action Plan for Health Security. It stopped a cholera outbreak in six months. Malawi set up its first Mpox Treatment Centre at Kamuzu Central Hospital in 48 hours after the first case was found. The country tested 99.6 percent of Mpox cases, the highest rate in the African region, and became the first African country to do this.

Meanwhile, Israel is helping Malawi reduce newborn deaths. Secretary for Health Dr Dan Namarika said Israel gave equipment and training to 70 healthcare workers at Bwaila, Kabudula, Mchinji, Dowa and Mponela health facilities in Lilongwe. The goal is to cut neonatal mortality from 24 to 12 deaths per 1,000 live births by 2030. Bwaila Hospital handles 65 to 80 births every 24 hours. The Neonatal Resuscitation Programme is led by Neonatologists for Africa, an Israeli group. The training teaches workers how to help newborns with breathing problems at birth.

ny.title: WHO yati Malawi yapita patsogolo kwambiri pankhani ya chisamaliro chaumoyo
ny.body: Bungwe la World Health Organization (WHO) layamikira dziko la Malawi chifukwa chopita patsogolo pa nkhani ya chisamaliro chaumoyo. Lipoti la WHO linatuluka pa June 1, 2026. Limati dziko la Malawi linachita bwino ndi matenda a polio, chithandizo cha HIV, thanzi la amayi, thanzi la makanda, kuyezetsa matenda komanso kuchepetsa kolera. Dziko la Malawi linakumana ndi mavuto azachuma komanso kufalikira kwa matenda koma linasinthabe.
ny.body_more:
Lipotilo likuwonetsa kuti amayi ndi makanda amafa pang'onopang'ono. Imfa za amayi zinachepa kuchoka pa imfa 439 pa ana 100,000 obadwa amoyo mu 2015-16 kufika pa imfa 224 pa ana 100,000 obadwa amoyo mu 2024. Imfa za makanda zinachepa kuchoka pa imfa 27 kufika pa imfa 24 pa ana 1,000 obadwa amoyo nthawi yomweyo. Chiwerengero cha imfa za ana ndi kubereka chinatsikanso.

Malawi inasungabe kuchuluka kwa katemera wa ana. Kufalikira kwa DPT3 kunali 94 peresenti ndipo kuchuluka koyamba kwa katemera wa chikuku-rubella kunali 89 peresenti. Dzikoli linayendetsa kampeni ya National Human Papillomavirus yomwe inafikira atsikana oposa 2 miliyoni achichepere. WHO inati iyi ndi mbiri yakale yomwe ingathandize kuthetsa khansa ya pachibelekero. Malawi idakwaniritsanso zolinga za UNAIDS 95-95-95 ngakhale Boma la United States linapereka ndalama zochepa.

Malawi yasintha luso lake loyankha pazadzidzidzi zaumoyo. Dzikoli linapanga Ndondomeko Yake Yoyamba Yadziko Lonse Yogwira Ntchito Yokwanira ya Zaka zisanu Yokhudza Chitetezo cha Zaumoyo. Inaletsa kufalikira kwa kolera m'miyezi isanu ndi umodzi. Malawi idakhazikitsa Mpox Treatment Centre yoyamba ku Kamuzu Central Hospital patatha maola 48 kuchokera pamene wodwala woyamba adapezeka. Dzikoli linayesa 99.6 peresenti ya milandu ya Mpox, yomwe ndi chiŵerengero chachikulu kwambiri m'chigawo cha Africa, ndipo linakhala dziko loyamba ku Africa kuchita izi.

Pakadali pano, Israeli ikuthandiza Malawi kuchepetsa imfa za makanda. Nduna ya Zaumoyo Dr Dan Namarika adati Israeli yapereka zida ndi maphunziro kwa ogwira ntchito zachipatala 70 ku Bwaila, Kabudula, Mchinji, Dowa ndi Mponela ku Lilongwe. Cholinga chake ndikuchepetsa imfa za makanda kuyambira 24 mpaka 12 pa ana 1,000 obadwa amoyo pofika chaka cha 2030. Chipatala cha Bwaila chimasamalira ana 65 mpaka 80 obadwa maola 24 aliwonse. Pulogalamu Yotsitsimutsa Makanda Amabadwa imatsogozedwa ndi Akatswiri a Makanda ku Africa, gulu la Israeli. Maphunzirowa amaphunzitsa ogwira ntchito momwe angathandizire makanda obadwa kumene omwe ali ndi vuto la kupuma akabadwa.

en.title: JCE exams start well, minister says pass rate will rise
en.body: Bright Msaka, Minister of Education, Science and Technology, said Junior Certificate of Education (JCE) examinations started well on Monday this week. He visited Lilongwe Girls Secondary School on Tuesday to check the exams. Msaka said no big problems happened. He said this year's pass rate will be better than last year's 77 percent.
en.body_more:
A total of 162,967 candidates sit for the JCE exams across Malawi. The exams will run until 10 June 2026. Maneb, the Malawi National Examinations Board, gives the tests.

Msaka said monitoring teams found the process smooth and orderly. He said no bad incident came to his office since the exams started on Monday. "These are the best and most organised examinations ever," Msaka said. He also said this is the start of free secondary school for most candidates, as government paid their exam fees.

At Lilongwe Girls Secondary School, 160 candidates sit the JCE. Head teacher Loyce Kumtamula said the school is fully ready. She said learners follow exam rules and supervisors work well. "The process has been orderly. There is good teamwork between students and supervisors," Kumtamula said. Last year, Lilongwe Girls Secondary School had a 100 percent pass rate. This year, Kumtamula said the school expects the same results again.

Msaka also gave thanks to teachers, school leaders and exam workers. He said they helped learners prepare and made sure exam papers came to schools on time. In total, 614,915 candidates sit for all exams this year — JCE, MSCE and PSLCE. Of these, 316,764 are female and 309,428 are male. The JCE exams are a key step in Malawi's schools, as they say if learners can go to senior secondary school.

ny.title: Mayeso a JCE ayamba bwino, nduna yati chiwerengero cha anthu opambana chidzakwera
ny.body: Bright Msaka, Nduna ya Maphunziro, Sayansi ndi Ukadaulo, anati mayeso a Junior Certificate of Education (JCE) adayamba bwino Lolemba sabata ino. Anapita ku Lilongwe Girls Secondary School Lachiwiri kuti akaone mayesowo. Msaka anati palibe vuto lalikulu lomwe lachitika. Anati chiwerengero cha ophunzira omwe apambana chaka chino chidzakhala chabwino kuposa 77 peresenti chaka chatha.
ny.body_more:
Ophunzira okwana 162,967 alemba mayeso a JCE ku Malawi konse. Mayesowa apitirira mpaka pa 10 June 2026. Maneb, bungwe la Malawi National Examinations Board, apereka mayesowo.

Msaka anati magulu owunikira apeza kuti ntchitoyi yayenda bwino komanso mwadongosolo. Iye anati palibe vuto lalikulu lomwe labwera ku ofesi yake kuyambira pomwe mayeso adayamba Lolemba. "Awa ndi mayeso abwino kwambiri komanso okonzedwa bwino kwambiri," adatero Msaka. Anatinso uku ndi kuyamba kwa sukulu ya sekondale yaulere kwa ophunzira ambiri, chifukwa boma limalipira ndalama zawo zolipirira mayeso.

Ku Lilongwe Girls Secondary School, ophunzira 160 alemba JCE. Mphunzitsi wamkulu Loyce Kumtamula adati sukuluyo yakonzeka mokwanira. Iye anati ophunzira amatsatira malamulo a mayeso ndipo oyang'anira amagwira ntchito bwino. "Njirayo yakhala yolongosoka. Pali mgwirizano wabwino pakati pa ophunzira ndi oyang'anira," adatero Kumtamula. Chaka chatha, Lilongwe Girls Secondary School inali ndi chiŵerengero chopambana 100%. Chaka chino, Kumtamula adati sukuluyo ikuyembekezeranso zotsatira zomwezo.

Msaka adayamikiranso aphunzitsi, atsogoleri a masukulu ndi ogwira ntchito za mayeso. Iye anati anathandiza ophunzira kukonzekera ndikuonetsetsa kuti mapepala a mayeso afika m'masukulu panthawi yake. Onse, ophunzira 614,915 alemba mayeso onse chaka chino — JCE, MSCE ndi PSLCE. Mwa awa, 316,764 ndi akazi ndipo 309,428 ndi amuna. Mayeso a JCE ndi gawo lofunika kwambiri m'masukulu aku Malawi, monga momwe amanenera ngati ophunzira angapite kusukulu ya sekondale.

en.title: Malawi Treasury Stops Payments for Contracts Signed in 2023-2025
en.body: The Ministry of Finance has frozen all payments for government contracts signed between September 1, 2023 and September 1, 2025. Secretary to the Treasury Cliff Chiunda ordered the review in a memo on May 29, 2026. All government offices must give details of these contracts to the National Audit Office and Attorney General by June 20, 2026.
en.body_more:
The frozen contracts include all agreements, guarantees, consents and employment-related deals signed in that period. The government will not pay for any work or services linked to these contracts until the review is done. Chiunda asked for the original contract amounts, any changes to the costs, reasons for the price increases, and proof that the correct approval process was followed.

Public finance expert Dalitso Kubalasa said the review is important. He told The Nation newspaper that many of Malawi's money problems come from contracts signed quietly with guarantees or consent clauses. "When these agreements are reviewed properly, we are not just checking for legal mistakes, but rather protecting the money meant for hospitals, for roads, for schools," Kubalasa said. Scotland-based economist Velli Nyirongo agreed that the review can help identify contracts that put the government at risk or have unfair terms. He said other countries have lost large sums of money from poorly negotiated contracts.

Corporate governance expert Jimmy Lipunga said the exercise will help Treasury understand what hidden costs and guarantees the government owes. Lawyer Benedicto Kondowe warned that the government must follow the law when reviewing contracts. "Any decision must follow due process and be supported by evidence to avoid exposing the State to legal liability," he said.

The contract review follows a payroll check that started in December 2025. That check aimed to find ghost workers and stop overspending on government salaries, which rose from K479.6 billion in 2021-22 to a projected K1.6 trillion in 2025-26.

ny.title: Boma la Malawi Laletsa Kulipira Mapangano Osainidwa mu 2023-2025
ny.body: Unduna wa Zachuma waletsa kulipira konse kwa mapangano a boma omwe adasainidwa pakati pa Seputembala 1, 2023 ndi Seputembala 1, 2025. Mlembi wa Zachuma Cliff Chiunda adalamula kuti kuwunikiraku kubwerezedwenso mu chikalata cholembedwa pa Meyi 29, 2026. Maofesi onse aboma ayenera kupereka tsatanetsatane wa mapanganowa ku Ofesi Yoyang'anira Maakaunti Yadziko Lonse ndi Attorney General pofika pa Juni 20, 2026.
ny.body_more:
Mapangano oletsedwawa akuphatikizapo mapangano onse, zitsimikizo, mapangano ndi mapangano okhudzana ndi ntchito omwe adasainidwa nthawi imeneyo. Boma silidzalipira ntchito iliyonse kapena ntchito zokhudzana ndi mapanganowa mpaka kuwunikaku kutatha. Chiunda adapempha ndalama zoyambirira za mapangano, kusintha kulikonse kwa ndalama, zifukwa zomwe mitengo idakwera, komanso umboni woti njira yolondola yovomerezera idatsatiridwa.

Katswiri wazachuma cha boma Dalitso Kubalasa adati kuwunikaku ndikofunikira. Adauza nyuzipepala ya The Nation kuti mavuto ambiri azachuma ku Malawi amachokera ku mapangano omwe adasainidwa mwakachetechete ndi zitsimikizo kapena zigawo zovomerezeka. "Mapanganowa akawunikidwa bwino, sitikungoyang'ana zolakwika zamalamulo, koma m'malo mwake tikuteteza ndalama zomwe zimapangidwira zipatala, misewu, masukulu," adatero Kubalasa. Velli Nyirongo, katswiri wazachuma wochokera ku Scotland, adavomereza kuti kuwunikaku kungathandize kuzindikira mapangano omwe amaika boma pachiwopsezo kapena omwe ali ndi malamulo osalungama. Adati mayiko ena ataya ndalama zambiri kuchokera ku mapangano omwe sanagwirizane bwino.

Katswiri wa kayendetsedwe ka makampani Jimmy Lipunga adati ntchitoyi ithandiza Treasury kumvetsetsa ndalama zobisika komanso chitsimikizo chomwe boma liyenera kulipira. Loya Benedicto Kondowe adachenjeza kuti boma liyenera kutsatira lamulo powunikira mapangano. "Chisankho chilichonse chiyenera kutsatira njira yoyenera ndikuthandizidwa ndi umboni kuti Boma lisapatsidwe mlandu wovomerezeka," adatero.

Kuwunikanso kwa mgwirizanowu kukutsatira kuwunika kwa malipiro komwe kunayamba mu Disembala 2025. Cholinga cha cheke chimenecho chinali kupeza antchito osagwira ntchito ndikusiya kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama zambiri pa malipiro a boma, zomwe zidakwera kuchoka pa K479.6 biliyoni mu 2021-22 kufika pa K1.6 trilioni yomwe ikuyembekezeredwa mu 2025-26.

en.title: New Tool to Measure Malawi's Social Progress Launched
en.body: Mary Navicha, the Minister of Gender, Community Development and Social Welfare, launched the Umunthu Social Index on Tuesday in Lilongwe. The new tool will measure how well Malawi is making social progress and development. The launch took place at the Umunthu Social Impact Forum, where government leaders, businesses and aid groups met to plan for the future.
en.body_more:
Navicha told the forum that Malawi must find new ways to pay for development work at home. She said many aid groups rely too much on money from other countries. "Some donors are withdrawing support from projects," she said. "Organisations must rethink their funding models and innovate beyond traditional donor dependence." The minister said the forum helps people brainstorm ideas on how to improve lives using local resources.

Wilson Chivhanga, the chief executive of Thrive Afrika, said the new index is not meant to shame organisations. "It is a mirror," he said. "It helps us understand where we are performing well and where improvements are needed." The index will track how well Malawi's social impact ecosystem works. Early results show that communities participate well in projects, but the country faces challenges in using data and raising money locally.

Navicha said the index comes at a critical time as Malawi works towards Malawi 2063, a plan for the nation to become wealthy and self-reliant. She noted that President Arthur Peter Mutharika has stressed the importance of Umunthu values, which teach that people must work together. She gave the example of the K5 billion Constituency Development Fund, which brings development money to all parts of the country. Ruth Kulaisi, the Conference Organising Chairperson, said the index will help track progress in health, education, civic participation and media access using evidence-based numbers.

ny.title: Chida Chatsopano Choyesera Kupita Patsogolo kwa Anthu ku Malawi Chatsegulidwa
ny.body: Mary Navicha, Nduna ya za Jenda, Chitukuko cha Anthu ndi Ubwino wa Anthu, adayambitsa Umunthu Social Index Lachiwiri ku Lilongwe. Chida chatsopanochi chidzayesa momwe dziko la Malawi likuyendera bwino komanso chitukuko cha anthu. Kuyambitsaku kunachitika ku Umunthu Social Impact Forum, komwe atsogoleri a boma, mabizinesi ndi magulu othandizira adakumana kuti akonzekere tsogolo.
ny.body_more:
Navicha adauza msonkhanowo kuti Malawi iyenera kupeza njira zatsopano zolipirira ntchito zachitukuko kunyumba. Iye adati magulu ambiri othandizira amadalira kwambiri ndalama zochokera kumayiko ena. "Opereka ena akuchotsa thandizo kuchokera ku mapulojekiti," adatero. "Mabungwe ayenera kuganiziranso njira zawo zopezera ndalama ndikusintha zinthu kupitirira kudalira opereka chithandizo." Ndunayo idati msonkhanowu umathandiza anthu kuganizira malingaliro amomwe angakulitsire miyoyo pogwiritsa ntchito zinthu zakomweko.

Wilson Chivhanga, mkulu wa Thrive Afrika, adati mndandanda watsopanowu sunapangidwe kuti uchititse manyazi mabungwe. "Ndi galasi," adatero. "Umatithandiza kumvetsetsa komwe tikuchita bwino komanso komwe kusintha kukufunika." Mndandandawu udzatsatira momwe chilengedwe cha Malawi chimakhudzira anthu. Zotsatira zoyambirira zikuwonetsa kuti madera akutenga nawo mbali bwino m'mapulojekiti, koma dzikolo likukumana ndi zovuta pakugwiritsa ntchito deta ndikusonkhanitsa ndalama zakomweko.

Navicha adati mndandandawu ukubwera panthawi yofunika kwambiri pamene Malawi ikugwira ntchito ku Malawi 2063, dongosolo loti dzikolo likhale lolemera komanso lodzidalira. Adanenanso kuti Purezidenti Arthur Peter Mutharika wagogomezera kufunika kwa mfundo za Umunthu, zomwe zimaphunzitsa kuti anthu ayenera kugwira ntchito limodzi. Iye anapereka chitsanzo cha ndalama zokwana K5 biliyoni za Constituency Development Fund, zomwe zimabweretsa ndalama zachitukuko m'madera onse a dzikolo. Ruth Kulaisi, Wapampando Wokonzekera Msonkhano, anati mndandandawu uthandiza kutsata kupita patsogolo kwa zaumoyo, maphunziro, kutenga nawo mbali kwa anthu wamba komanso kupeza mwayi wofalitsa nkhani pogwiritsa ntchito manambala ozikidwa pa umboni.

en.title: Parliament checks new diplomats for Malawi
en.body: Parliament's Public Appointments Committee is interviewing people chosen to be ambassadors and high commissioners for Malawi. The hearings started in Lilongwe on June 1 and will run until June 7, 2026. About 20 people are being checked to see if they can do the job well.
en.body_more:
The committee chair Felix Njawala said lawmakers want to understand how the nominees will help Malawi abroad. He said some people have worked as diplomats for many different governments over 15 to 20 years, and the committee is learning from their success.

Right now, Malawi only asks that diplomats be Malawian citizens. There are no rules about school qualifications or work skills needed. Njawala said this may need to change because diplomacy is hard work today. The Foreign Ministry said it is writing new rules and will bring them to Parliament. The ministry also wants to start checking how well diplomats do their jobs, and to have clear rules about when to call them home.

On the first day, the committee spoke to four people: Benedicto Wokomaatani Malunga for the United Kingdom, Edward Sawerengera for the United States, Evans Matabwa for Germany, and Yusuf Aufi for Kenya. Sawerengera said he wants to bring American business and money to Malawi if the Parliament says yes. He said Malawi needs help to grow, and America has money and skill to share.

Experts say Malawi has 25 diplomatic offices around the world. Some say these missions do not bring back enough trade, money, or visitors to pay for them. They say diplomats should focus on getting investment and trade, not just having nice parties. President Peter Mutharika has chosen 13 ambassadors, nine high commissioners, and four consul-generals. The committee will keep talking to the rest of the people chosen for places like South Africa, Tanzania, Egypt, China, and other countries.

ny.title: Nyumba Yamalamulo ikuyang'ana akazembe atsopano ku Malawi
ny.body: Komiti Yoona za Kusankhidwa kwa Anthu ku Nyumba Yamalamulo ikukambirana ndi anthu omwe asankhidwa kukhala akazembe ndi makomishina akuluakulu a Malawi. Misonkhanoyi inayamba ku Lilongwe pa June 1 ndipo ipitilira mpaka June 7, 2026. Anthu pafupifupi 20 akuwunikidwa kuti awone ngati angathe kugwira ntchitoyo bwino.
ny.body_more:
Wapampando wa komiti Felix Njawala anati opanga malamulo akufuna kumvetsetsa momwe osankhidwawo angathandizire Malawi kunja. Anati anthu ena agwira ntchito ngati ma diplomat m'maboma osiyanasiyana kwa zaka 15 mpaka 20, ndipo komitiyi ikuphunzira kuchokera ku kupambana kwawo.

Pakadali pano, Malawi ikupempha kuti ma diplomat akhale nzika za ku Malawi. Palibe malamulo okhudza ziyeneretso za kusukulu kapena luso la ntchito lomwe likufunika. Njawala anati izi zingafunike kusintha chifukwa ma diplomat ndi ntchito yovuta masiku ano. Unduna wa Zakunja unati ukulemba malamulo atsopano ndipo udzawabweretsa ku Nyumba Yamalamulo. Undunawu ukufunanso kuyamba kuwona momwe ma diplomat amachitira ntchito zawo bwino, komanso kukhala ndi malamulo omveka bwino okhudza nthawi yoti awaitanire kwawo.

Pa tsiku loyamba, komitiyi idalankhula ndi anthu anayi: Benedicto Wokomaatani Malunga wa ku United Kingdom, Edward Sawerengera wa ku United States, Evans Matabwa wa ku Germany, ndi Yusuf Aufi wa ku Kenya. Sawerengera adati akufuna kubweretsa mabizinesi aku America ndi ndalama ku Malawi ngati Nyumba Yamalamulo ivomereza. Anati Malawi ikufunika thandizo kuti ikule, ndipo America ili ndi ndalama ndi luso logawana.

Akatswiri akuti Malawi ili ndi maofesi 25 a zandale padziko lonse lapansi. Ena amati mautumikiwa sabweretsa malonda okwanira, ndalama, kapena alendo okwanira kuti awalipire. Amati andale ayenera kuyang'ana kwambiri pakupeza ndalama ndi malonda, osati kungokhala ndi maphwando abwino. Purezidenti Peter Mutharika wasankha akazembe 13, makomishinala asanu ndi anayi, ndi akazembe anayi. Komitiyi ipitiliza kulankhula ndi anthu ena onse omwe asankhidwa m'malo monga South Africa, Tanzania, Egypt, China, ndi mayiko ena.

en.title: Rare earth minerals found at Kasiya mine in Lilongwe
en.body: Sovereign Metals Limited says high-value rare earth minerals were found at the Kasiya Rutile-Graphite Project in Lilongwe. Tests show dysprosium, terbium and yttrium are present. These minerals are used in electric vehicles, wind turbines and defence systems. Kasiya's rare earth content is higher than the world's top five producers.
en.body_more:
The company's Managing Director Frank Eagar said the minerals are in areas planned for early mining. He added that some minerals may be recovered from waste material already made during mining, so extra mining may not be needed. An independent pricing report says Kasiya's concentrate could sell for between $16,000 and $19,000 per tonne. The current market price is about $6,142 per tonne.

Geoscientist Ignatius Kamwanje said the discovery will raise Malawi's value in the global critical minerals industry. He asked the government to help local people take part in mining, mineral work and refining in Malawi. Mining policy expert Paul Mvula warned that Malawi could face the resource curse if corruption and cartels are not stopped in the mining sector.

Sovereign Metals plans to start Phase One construction in mid-2027. Mineral extraction and production are set to begin in 2029. The company says it will make about $16.2 billion (roughly K28.37 trillion) over 25 years from the mine. However, Malawi does not own Kasiya. Foreign companies also control other major mining projects in Malawi, including uranium at Kayelekera and rare earths at Kangankunde and Songwe Hill. This raises questions about how much of the wealth stays in Malawi.

ny.title: Rare earth minerals kupezeka ku Kasiya mine ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Kampani ya Sovereign Metals Limited yati miyala yamtengo wapatali ya rare earth yapezeka ku Kasiya Rutile-Graphite Project ku Lilongwe. Mayeso akusonyeza kuti pali dysprosium, terbium ndi yttrium. Miyala iyi imagwiritsidwa ntchito m'magalimoto amagetsi, ma wind turbines ndi machitidwe oteteza. Kuchuluka kwa nthaka ya rare earth ku Kasiya ndikokwera kuposa opanga asanu apamwamba padziko lonse lapansi.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa kampaniyo, Frank Eagar, anati mcherewu uli m'madera omwe akukonzekera kukumba koyambirira. Anawonjezera kuti mchere wina ukhoza kutengedwa kuchokera ku zinyalala zomwe zapangidwa kale panthawi ya migodi, kotero migodi yowonjezera singafunike. Lipoti lodziyimira pawokha la mitengo likunena kuti mchere wa Kasiya ukhoza kugulitsidwa pakati pa $16,000 ndi $19,000 pa tani. Mtengo wa msika wamakono ndi pafupifupi $6,142 pa tani.

Katswiri wa sayansi ya za nthaka, Ignatius Kamwanje, adati kupezeka kumeneku kudzakweza mtengo wa Malawi mumakampani ofunikira padziko lonse lapansi amchere. Anapempha boma kuti lithandize anthu am'deralo kutenga nawo mbali mu migodi, ntchito zamigodi ndi kuyenga ku Malawi. Katswiri wa mfundo za migodi, Paul Mvula, anachenjeza kuti Malawi ikhoza kukumana ndi temberero la chuma ngati ziphuphu ndi magulu ankhondo sadzayimitsidwa mu gawo la migodi.

Sovereign Metals ikukonzekera kuyamba kumanga Gawo Loyamba pakati pa 2027. Kutulutsa ndi kupanga mchere kukuyembekezeka kuyamba mu 2029. Kampaniyo ikunena kuti ipanga pafupifupi $16.2 biliyoni (pafupifupi K28.37 trillion) pazaka 25 kuchokera ku mgodiwu. Komabe, Malawi ilibe Kasiya. Makampani akunja amayang'aniranso mapulojekiti ena akuluakulu a migodi ku Malawi, kuphatikizapo uranium ku Kayelekera ndi rare earth ku Kangankunde ndi Songwe Hill. Izi zikubweretsa mafunso okhudza kuchuluka kwa chuma chomwe chili ku Malawi.

en.title: Malawi insurance firms back stronger new rules
en.body: Insurance companies in Malawi say they support new, tougher rules under the Insurance Act of 2024. The Registrar of Financial Institutions is now enforcing key parts of the law. These include mandatory insurance, advance payment of premiums, and heavy penalties for fraud. The Insurance Association of Malawi praised the move.
en.body_more:
Dorothy Chapeyama, President of the Insurance Association of Malawi, said the new rules are a big step forward. "We support measures that strengthen regulation, enhance consumer protection, improve risk management and promote confidence in the insurance sector," she said. The law came into effect on May 1, 2025, and replaced the old Insurance Act of 2010.

Under the new rules, people must pay their insurance premium before a policy starts to work. This does not apply to some life insurance policies. The law also brings tough punishments for fraud. Anyone who commits insurance fraud will pay a fine of K100 million or three times the amount they stole, whichever is higher. They may also go to prison for up to 10 years. Chapeyama said these harsh penalties send a clear message to people who might try to cheat the system.

Economist Marvin Banda said the stronger rules will help the insurance sector work better and stay stable. He called the requirement for mandatory insurance on public buildings and construction projects long overdue. However, he warned that the real challenge is making sure the rules are followed. "Many Malawians remain uninsured not because insurance is unavailable but because they perceive it as expensive and doubt that claims will be paid promptly and fairly," Banda said.

The new law also requires contractors who work on construction projects to have insurance. This must cover risks like bodily injury, death and property damage during construction work. The Registrar said the 12-month period for businesses to get ready for the new rules ended on May 1, 2026.

ny.title: Makampani a inshuwaransi ku Malawi avomereza malamulo atsopano olimba
ny.body: Makampani a inshuwalansi ku Malawi akuti akuchirikiza malamulo atsopano komanso olimba kwambiri motsatira lamulo la Inshuwalansi la 2024.
