Registrar of Financial Institutions tsopano akukakamiza zigawo zofunika kwambiri za lamuloli. Izi zikuphatikizapo inshuwalansi yokakamiza, kulipira ndalama zolipirira pasadakhale, ndi zilango zazikulu zachinyengo. Bungwe la Inshuwalansi la Malawi layamikira izi.
ny.body_more:
Dorothy Chapeyama, Purezidenti wa Inshuwalansi Association of Malawi, anati malamulo atsopanowa ndi sitepe yaikulu. "Timathandizira njira zolimbikitsira malamulo, kulimbikitsa chitetezo cha ogula, kukonza kayendetsedwe ka zoopsa ndikulimbikitsa chidaliro mu gawo la inshuwaransi," adatero. Lamuloli linayamba kugwira ntchito pa Meyi 1, 2025, ndipo linalowa m'malo mwa Inshuwalansi yakale ya 2010.

Motsatira malamulo atsopanowa, anthu ayenera kulipira ndalama zawo za inshuwaransi asanayambe kugwira ntchito. Izi sizikugwira ntchito pa mfundo zina za inshuwaransi ya moyo. Lamuloli limaperekanso zilango zovuta kwa achinyengo. Aliyense amene amachita chinyengo cha inshuwaransi adzalipira chindapusa cha K100 miliyoni kapena kuwirikiza katatu ndalama zomwe adaba, chilichonse chomwe chili chokwera. Akhozanso kupita kundende mpaka zaka 10. Chapeyama adati zilango zovutazi zimatumiza uthenga womveka bwino kwa anthu omwe angayese kubera dongosololi.

Wachuma Marvin Banda adati malamulo olimba athandiza gawo la inshuwaransi kugwira ntchito bwino ndikukhalabe okhazikika. Adati kufunika kwa inshuwaransi yokakamiza pa nyumba za anthu onse ndi ntchito zomanga zomwe zachedwa kwambiri. Komabe, adachenjeza kuti vuto lenileni ndikuwonetsetsa kuti malamulowo akutsatiridwa. "Anthu ambiri aku Malawi alibe inshuwaransi osati chifukwa chakuti inshuwaransi siilipo koma chifukwa amaona kuti ndi yokwera mtengo ndipo akukayikira kuti zopemphazo zidzalipidwa mwachangu komanso mwachilungamo," adatero Banda.

Lamulo latsopanoli likufunanso kuti makontrakitala omwe amagwira ntchito yomanga akhale ndi inshuwaransi. Izi ziyenera kuphimba zoopsa monga kuvulala kwa thupi, imfa ndi kuwonongeka kwa katundu panthawi yomanga. Wolembetsa adati nthawi ya miyezi 12 kuti mabizinesi akonzekere malamulo atsopano inatha pa Meyi 1, 2026.

en.title: Football fans get refunds for cancelled match after 219 days
en.body: The Football Association of Malawi said it will give money back to fans who bought tickets for a cancelled World Cup match. Equatorial Guinea told the association five hours before the game that the team would not come to Malawi. The match was set for October 19 at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
After the game was stopped, the Football Association first asked fans to use their tickets for other games. Now the association has decided to give the money back instead. Fans who bought tickets online will get their money back to the phone wallets they used to buy the tickets. Fans who have paper tickets must go to the Football Association offices at Bingu National Stadium between June 2 and June 4, 2026. The offices will check the tickets before giving the money back.

Fans who do not want their money back can use their tickets to watch Malawi play in the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations games in September 2026. Fredrick Thom, who works for the Spectators Rights Coalition, said the refunds come very late. He said the way the Football Association is doing this is not fair. He worried that fans from other towns will have trouble getting to Lilongwe to check their tickets. He also said the Football Association should have told fans to keep their tickets safe when the game was first stopped.

Sports analyst Enock Banda said it is good that the Football Association will give the money back. He told the Football Association to make a new system for tickets on computers. He said this would make it easier and faster to give money back when games are stopped or cancelled.

ny.title: Okonda mpira amalandira ndalama chifukwa cha kuthetsedwa kwa masewerawa patatha masiku 219
ny.body: Bungwe la Mpira wa Miguu la Malawi lati lidzabwezera ndalama kwa mafani omwe adagula matikiti a masewera a World Cup omwe adathetsedwa. Equatorial Guinea idauza bungweli maola asanu masewerawa asanachitike kuti timuyi isabwere ku Malawi. Masewerawa adakonzedwa pa 19 Okutobala ku Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Pambuyo poti masewerawa ayimitsidwa, bungwe la Football Association linapempha mafani kuti agwiritse ntchito matikiti awo pamasewera ena. Tsopano bungweli laganiza zobwezera ndalamazo m'malo mwake. Mafani omwe adagula matikiti pa intaneti adzabweza ndalama zawo m'ma wallet a foni omwe adagwiritsa ntchito pogula matikitiwo. Mafani omwe ali ndi matikiti apepala ayenera kupita ku maofesi a Football Association ku Bingu National Stadium pakati pa June 2 ndi June 4, 2026. Maofesiwa adzayang'ana matikitiwo asanabweze ndalamazo.

Mafani omwe sakufuna kubweza ndalama zawo angagwiritse ntchito matikiti awo kuwonera Malawi ikusewera masewera a 2027 Africa Cup of Nations mu September 2026. Fredrick Thom, yemwe amagwira ntchito ku Spectators Rights Coalition, adati kubweza ndalamazo kumabwera mochedwa kwambiri. Anati momwe bungwe la Football Association likuchitira izi sichilungamo. Anada nkhawa kuti mafani ochokera m'matauni ena adzavutika kufika ku Lilongwe kukayang'ana matikiti awo. Anatinso bungwe la Football Association liyenera kuuza mafani kuti asunge matikiti awo otetezeka pamene masewerawa adayimitsidwa koyamba.

Katswiri wa zamasewera Enock Banda adati ndibwino kuti bungwe la Football Association libweze ndalamazo. Iye anauza bungwe la Football Association kuti lipange njira yatsopano yopezera matikiti pa makompyuta. Iye anati izi zipangitsa kuti kubweza ndalama kukhale kosavuta komanso mwachangu ngati masewera aimitsidwa kapena aletsedwa.

en.title: ESCOM board and staff union join forces to fix power company
en.body: The Electricity Supply Corporation of Malawi (ESCOM) board and the ESCOM Staff Union have agreed to work together to improve the company. Board Chairperson Alfred Nhlema, CEO William Kaipa, and Union President Alex Chatsira met to discuss how to make ESCOM stronger and give better service to Malawians.
en.body_more:
At the meeting, Nhlema told the group that the board, management, and staff must be one team. "The union, management, and the Board must work as one united force to restore the glory of ESCOM. When we align our efforts toward this common goal, everything else will fall into place," he said. Kaipa also spoke about the need to work together. "During challenging times, organizations are not broken—they are strengthened by collaboration," Kaipa said. He told staff that management will keep the door open to listen to workers.

The meeting gave the union a chance to raise problems about how the company works, how it is led, and how staff are treated. Both the board and the union said they will solve these problems by talking to each other. They said that if workers are happy and treated well, the company will do better.

In a special moment, the union gave Nhlema the honour of "Comrade." This is a rare sign that the workers trust him and believe he will help them. The board, management, and all the workers are now working in the same direction to help fix ESCOM and keep electricity flowing to the nation.

ny.title: Bungwe la ESCOM ndi bungwe la ogwira ntchito agwirizana kuti akonze kampani yamagetsi
ny.body: Bungwe la Electricity Supply Corporation of Malawi (ESCOM) ndi bungwe la ESCOM Staff Union agwirizana kuti agwire ntchito limodzi kuti akonze kampaniyo. Wapampando wa bungweli Alfred Nhlema, CEO William Kaipa, ndi Purezidenti wa bungweli Alex Chatsira adakumana kuti akambirane momwe angapangire ESCOM kukhala yolimba komanso kupereka chithandizo chabwino kwa anthu aku Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Pamsonkhanowo, Nhlema anauza gululo kuti bungwe, oyang'anira, ndi antchito ayenera kukhala gulu limodzi. "Mgwirizano, oyang'anira, ndi Bungwe ayenera kugwira ntchito limodzi ngati gulu limodzi logwirizana kuti abwezeretse ulemerero wa ESCOM. Tikagwirizanitsa khama lathu kuti tikwaniritse cholinga chimodzichi, zina zonse zidzayenda bwino," adatero. Kaipa adalankhulanso za kufunika kogwirira ntchito limodzi. "Panthawi zovuta, mabungwe sasweka—amalimbikitsidwa ndi mgwirizano," adatero Kaipa. Adauza antchito kuti oyang'anira adzatsegula chitseko kuti amvere antchito.

Msonkhanowo unapatsa bungweli mwayi woti liwuze mavuto okhudza momwe kampaniyo imagwirira ntchito, momwe imatsogoleredwera, komanso momwe antchito amachitiridwira. Bungweli ndi bungweli onse adati athetsa mavutowa polankhulana. Adati ngati antchito ali okondwa komanso ochitiridwa bwino, kampaniyo idzachita bwino.

Munthawi yapadera, bungweli linapatsa Nhlema ulemu wa "Comrade." Ichi ndi chizindikiro chosowa kuti antchito amamukhulupirira ndipo akukhulupirira kuti adzawathandiza. Bungwe la oyang'anira, ndi antchito onse tsopano akugwira ntchito limodzi kuti athandize kukonza ESCOM ndikupangitsa kuti magetsi aziyenda m'dziko lonselo.

en.title: Malawi tobacco sales earn K120 billion in six weeks
en.body: Six weeks into the tobacco marketing season, Malawi has earned K120 billion (US$68 million) from tobacco sales. The market opened on April 20. Farmers sold 32.7 million kilogrammes at an average price of K3,799 per kilogramme. This is 32 percent less money than last year at the same time.
en.body_more:
Last year during the same six weeks, the country earned K175 billion (US$100 million) from 42 million kilogrammes sold at K4,079 per kilogramme. This year's prices are 10 percent lower than last year. The amount of tobacco sold is 22 percent less.

When the market started, buyers rejected between 90 and 100 percent of the tobacco at some floors. Farmers had to take their bales home. The rejection rate has now fallen to around 55 percent. This is good news, but still more than double last year's 26 percent rejection rate. Tama Farmers Trust president Abiel Kalima Banda said high rejection levels are the main problem. "Prices remain very low," he said. "The situation is worse on the auction floors compared with contract markets." The rejection rate fell after Minister of Agriculture, Irrigation and Water Development Roza Mbilizi met with tobacco buyers in the third week. Tobacco Commission spokesperson Telephorus Chigwenembe said the market is progressing well and the downward trend in rejections is encouraging.

Malawi is expected to grow 197 million kilogrammes of tobacco this year. Buyers want only 170 million kilogrammes. This means supply is 27 million kilogrammes more than demand. Only eight buying companies are working this season, down from 11 last year. This puts more downward pressure on prices. The Tobacco Commission warned farmers that poor quality leaf will face higher rejection and lower returns.

Last season, Malawi sold 221 million kilogrammes and earned a record K944.66 billion (US$542 million) at K4,307 per kilogramme. Industry players believe this season can still improve if rejection rates continue to fall and farmers present better-quality tobacco.

ny.title: Kugulitsa fodya ku Malawi kwapeza ndalama zokwana K120 biliyoni m'masabata asanu ndi limodzi
ny.body: Patatha milungu isanu ndi umodzi kuchokera pamene malonda a fodya ayamba, dziko la Malawi lapeza ndalama zokwana K120 biliyoni (US$68 miliyoni) kuchokera ku malonda a fodya. Msika unatsegulidwa pa Epulo 20. Alimi anagulitsa makilogalamu 32.7 miliyoni pamtengo wapakati wa K3,799 pa kilogalamu. Izi ndi ndalama zochepa ndi 32 peresenti poyerekeza ndi chaka chatha panthawi yomweyi.
ny.body_more:
Chaka chatha m'masabata asanu ndi limodzi omwewo, dzikolo linapeza K175 biliyoni (US$100 miliyoni) kuchokera ku makilogalamu 42 miliyoni omwe anagulitsidwa pa K4,079 pa kilogalamu. Mitengo ya chaka chino ndi yotsika ndi 10 peresenti poyerekeza ndi chaka chatha. Kuchuluka kwa fodya wogulitsidwa ndi 22 peresenti.

Msika utayamba, ogula anakana pakati pa 90 ndi 100 peresenti ya fodya m'zipinda zina. Alimi anayenera kutenga mabale awo kunyumba. Kuchuluka kwa fodya wokanidwa tsopano kwatsika kufika pafupifupi 55 peresenti. Iyi ndi nkhani yabwino, koma kupitirira kawiri kuchuluka kwa fodya wokanidwa ndi 26 peresenti chaka chatha. Purezidenti wa Tama Farmers Trust, Abiel Kalima Banda, anati vuto lalikulu ndi kukanidwa kwakukulu. "Mitengo ikadali yotsika kwambiri," adatero. "Mkhalidwe uli woipa kwambiri m'zipinda zogulitsira poyerekeza ndi misika yogwirizana." Kuchuluka kwa fodya wokanidwa kunatsika pambuyo poti Nduna ya Zaulimi, Ulimi Wothirira ndi Chitukuko cha Madzi, Roza Mbilizi, adakumana ndi ogula fodya m'sabata lachitatu. Mneneri wa Tobacco Commission, Telephorus Chigwenembe, adati msika ukuyenda bwino ndipo kutsika kwa fodya wokanidwa kukulimbikitsa.

Dziko la Malawi likuyembekezeka kulima makilogalamu 197 miliyoni a fodya chaka chino. Ogula akufuna makilogalamu 170 miliyoni okha. Izi zikutanthauza kuti kupezeka ndi makilogalamu 27 miliyoni kuposa momwe akufunira. Makampani asanu ndi atatu okha ndi omwe akugwira ntchito nyengo ino, kuchokera pa 11 chaka chatha. Izi zikuchepetsa mitengo. Bungwe la Tobacco Commission linachenjeza alimi kuti masamba osakhwima adzakanidwa kwambiri komanso phindu lochepa.

Nyengo yatha, dziko la Malawi linagulitsa makilogalamu 221 miliyoni ndipo linapeza K944.66 biliyoni (US$542 miliyoni) pa K4,307 pa kilogalamu. Ogwira ntchito m'makampani akukhulupirira kuti nyengo ino ikhoza kusintha ngati kuchuluka kwa fodya wokanidwa kukupitirira kutsika ndipo alimi akupereka fodya wabwino kwambiri.

en.title: Standard Bank marks Africa Day with week of Malawi culture
en.body: Standard Bank Malawi marked Africa Day with a week-long celebration of Malawi's culture and identity. The campaign, with the theme "We're in Malawi and Malawi is in Us," used five symbols to tell Malawi's story: Lake Malawi, Chambo fish, a canoe, Mount Mulanje and the Kwacha Sunrise. The bank worked with radio stations across the country to help people share what these symbols mean to them.
en.body_more:
Lake Malawi represents connection and livelihoods for many people. The Chambo fish stands for family, belonging and shared food. The canoe shows courage and how communities move forward together. Mount Mulanje is a sign of strength and endurance. The Kwacha Sunrise represents hope and new beginnings. Standard Bank took the five symbols and created a special Chitenje fabric design. A Chitenje is a cloth that Malawians wear at weddings and ceremonies, use to carry children, and give as gifts during celebrations.

The bank worked with Radio Lilanguka in Mangochi, Radio Tigabane in Mzuzu, Mulanje FM and YONECO FM in Zomba. Listeners called in to talk about what the symbols mean in their homes and communities. Tamanda Ng'ombe, Head of Brand and Marketing at Standard Bank Malawi, said that Africa's story is too big to tell in one day. "These symbols reflect our homes, our journeys, our resilience, and our shared hopes for the future. Through Africa Week, we wanted to celebrate the stories and spirit that continue to move Malawi forward," Ng'ombe said.

The bank will continue the campaign through the Be More Race 2026 on June 20, 2026 at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. People will run in three groups: 5 kilometres, 10 kilometres and 21 kilometres. The race kits will have the same five symbols from the Africa Week campaign. Standard Bank said the campaign shows it cares about Malawi's culture and growth. The bank also introduced Kwacha Point to bring banking services to communities that do not have easy access to banks.

ny.title: Banki ya Standard ikukondwerera Tsiku la Africa ndi sabata ya chikhalidwe cha ku Malawi
ny.body: Banki ya Standard Malawi idakondwerera Tsiku la Africa ndi chikondwerero cha sabata yonse cha chikhalidwe ndi umunthu wa Malawi. Kampeniyi, yokhala ndi mutu wakuti "Tili ku Malawi ndipo Malawi ili mu Ife," idagwiritsa ntchito zizindikiro zisanu pofotokoza nkhani ya Malawi: Nyanja ya Malawi, nsomba ya Chambo, bwato, Phiri la Mulanje ndi Kutuluka kwa Dzuwa kwa Kwacha. Bankiyi idagwira ntchito ndi malo owulutsira wailesi mdziko lonselo kuti ithandize anthu kugawana tanthauzo la zizindikirozi kwa iwo.
ny.body_more:
Nyanja ya Malawi ikuyimira kulumikizana ndi moyo wa anthu ambiri. Nsomba ya Chambo ikuyimira banja, kukhala m'banja komanso chakudya chogawana. Bwatoli limasonyeza kulimba mtima ndi momwe madera amayendera limodzi. Phiri la Mulanje ndi chizindikiro cha mphamvu ndi kupirira. Kwacha Sunrise ikuyimira chiyembekezo ndi chiyambi chatsopano. Standard Bank idatenga zizindikiro zisanu ndikupanga kapangidwe kapadera ka nsalu ya Chitenje. Chitenje ndi nsalu yomwe anthu aku Malawi amavala paukwati ndi miyambo, amagwiritsa ntchito kunyamula ana, komanso amapereka ngati mphatso pa zikondwerero.

Bankiyi idagwira ntchito ndi Radio Lilanguka ku Mangochi, Radio Tigabane ku Mzuzu, Mulanje FM ndi YONECO FM ku Zomba. Omvera adayimba kuti akambirane za tanthauzo la zizindikirozo m'nyumba zawo ndi m'madera awo. Tamanda Ng'ombe, Mtsogoleri wa Brand and Marketing ku Standard Bank Malawi, adati nkhani ya Africa ndi yayikulu kwambiri moti sitingathe kuifotokoza tsiku limodzi. "Zizindikirozi zikuwonetsa nyumba zathu, maulendo athu, kulimba mtima kwathu, komanso ziyembekezo zathu zamtsogolo. Kudzera mu Africa Week, tinkafuna kukondwerera nkhani ndi mzimu womwe ukupitiliza kupititsa patsogolo Malawi," adatero Ng'ombe.

Banki ipitiliza kampeniyi kudzera mu mpikisano wa Be More Race 2026 pa June 20, 2026 ku Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Anthu adzathamanga m'magulu atatu: makilomita 5, makilomita 10 ndi makilomita 21. Zida za mpikisanowu zidzakhala ndi zizindikiro zisanu zomwezo kuchokera ku kampeni ya Africa Week. Standard Bank yati kampeniyi ikuwonetsa kuti imasamala za chikhalidwe ndi kukula kwa dziko la Malawi. Bankiyi idayambitsanso Kwacha Point kuti ibweretse ntchito zamabanki kumadera omwe alibe mwayi wopeza mabanki mosavuta.

en.title: Workers ask for help to stop fight at water board
en.body: Workers at Blantyre Water Board want a government official to help stop a fight between the chief executive and the board. The union wrote a letter on May 29 asking the Comptroller of Statutory Corporations to help. The workers say the fight is making work hard and people are sad and tired.
en.body_more:
The union's general secretary Davie Maluwa and president Victor Munishi wrote that the chief executive officer Yeremia Chihana and the board can no longer work well together. They said this harms the water board, the staff, and the people who need clean water. The workers said they will meet again in 14 days. If nothing changes, they will take action.

The fight started when the board stopped Chihana in April, just weeks after he began work. The board said he did wrong things. In May, the High Court said Chihana could go back to work. But then the board said no. Now Chihana is fighting in the Supreme Court.

The workers say the fight is breaking the water board. Staff are tired and sad. Work is not good. The union also says the board paid three chief executives and eight directors at the same time in April. This costs too much money. The union says if this does not stop, the water board will break. People will not get clean water.

The Comptroller of Statutory Corporations Stuart Ligomeka did not answer calls or messages about this. The water board serves many people in Blantyre and the areas near it. The workers want help fast to make things right again.

ny.title: Ogwira ntchito akupempha thandizo kuti athetse ndewu ku bwalo la madzi
ny.body: Ogwira ntchito ku Blantyre Water Board akufuna mkulu wa boma kuti athandize kuthetsa mkangano pakati pa mkulu wa bungweli ndi bungweli. Bungweli linalemba kalata pa Meyi 29 kupempha Woyang'anira Mabungwe Ovomerezeka kuti athandize. Ogwira ntchitowo akuti mkanganowu ukupangitsa kuti ntchito ikhale yovuta ndipo anthu ali achisoni komanso otopa.
ny.body_more:
Mlembi wamkulu wa bungweli, Davie Maluwa, ndi purezidenti Victor Munishi, analemba kuti mkulu wa bungweli, Yeremia Chihana, ndi bungweli, sangagwirizanenso bwino. Anati izi zikuvulaza bungwe la madzi, antchito, ndi anthu omwe amafunikira madzi oyera. Ogwira ntchitowo anati adzakumananso patatha masiku 14. Ngati palibe chomwe chasintha, adzachitapo kanthu.

Nkhondoyi inayamba pamene bungweli linaletsa Chihana mu Epulo, milungu ingapo atangoyamba ntchito. Bungweli linati adachita zinthu zolakwika. Mu Meyi, Khothi Lalikulu linati Chihana akhoza kubwerera kuntchito. Koma kenako bungweli linati ayi. Tsopano Chihana akumenyana ku Khothi Lalikulu.

Ogwira ntchitowo akuti nkhondoyi ikuphwanya bungwe la madzi. Ogwira ntchito atopa komanso achisoni. Ntchito si yabwino. Bungweli limanenanso kuti bungweli linalipira akuluakulu atatu ndi oyang'anira asanu ndi atatu nthawi imodzi mu Epulo. Izi zimawononga ndalama zambiri. Bungweli likuti ngati izi sizisiya, bungwe la madzi lidzasweka. Anthu sadzapeza madzi oyera.

Woyang'anira Mabungwe Ovomerezeka, Stuart Ligomeka, sanayankhe mafoni kapena mauthenga okhudza izi. Bodi la madzi limathandiza anthu ambiri ku Blantyre ndi madera oyandikana nalo. Antchito akufuna thandizo mwachangu kuti zinthu zikonzedwenso.

en.title: Admarc gets K60 billion for maize purchase this year
en.body: Admarc, the government maize buyer, has been given K60 billion to buy 65,000 metric tonnes of maize during the farm season. Admarc spokesperson Theresa Chapulapula said they will start buying maize from 1st June at K900 per kilogramme. Markets will open across the country. The maize must be dry and clean to meet quality standards.
en.body_more:
Chapulapula said Admarc has made ready the transport and storage needed to buy and store maize fairly. The corporation will also buy other crops such as rice, pigeon peas, cotton and groundnuts. She told farmers to bring their maize to Admarc depots instead of selling to vendors in the market.

But reports show that only K5 billion of the K60 billion has been given to Admarc so far. This is less than 10 percent of the full amount. Admarc CEO Ben Botolo said the organisation needs K144 billion to work well in the 2026-27 season. Admarc also owes CDH Bank K39.5 billion, which limits what money it can get from other places.

Francis Njanje, head of Malawi Volunteers Organisation, said the delay is a problem. Most maize is now in the hands of vendors, not farmers. Vendors are buying maize for as little as K600 per kilogramme, below the official price. This means farmers may lose money. Njanje said Admarc should have come to the market much earlier to buy straight from farmers and stop vendors from paying too little.

Experts worry that with so little money released, Admarc cannot buy all the maize it needs or protect farmers from low prices. The question of where the rest of the K60 billion is remains unclear.

ny.title: Admarc yapeza K60 biliyoni yogulira chimanga chaka chino
ny.body: Admarc, yomwe ndi yogula chimanga cha boma, yapatsidwa K60 biliyoni kuti igule matani 65,000 a chimanga nthawi ya ulimi. Mneneri wa Admarc, Theresa Chapulapula, adati ayamba kugula chimanga kuyambira pa 1 Juni pa K900 pa kilogalamu imodzi. Misika idzatsegulidwa mdziko lonselo. Chimanga chiyenera kukhala chouma komanso choyera kuti chikwaniritse miyezo yabwino.
ny.body_more:
Chapulapula anati Admarc yakonza zoyendera ndi kusungira zomwe zikufunika kuti igule ndikusunga chimanga moyenera. Kampaniyo idzagulanso mbewu zina monga mpunga, nandolo, thonje ndi mtedza. Iye adauza alimi kuti abweretse chimanga chawo ku malo osungiramo zinthu a Admarc m'malo mogulitsa kwa ogulitsa pamsika.

Koma malipoti akusonyeza kuti K5 biliyoni yokha mwa K60 biliyoni yaperekedwa ku Admarc mpaka pano. Izi ndi zosakwana 10 peresenti ya ndalama zonse. CEO wa Admarc Ben Botolo adati bungweli likufunika K144 biliyoni kuti ligwire ntchito bwino mu nyengo ya 2026-27. Admarc ilinso ndi ngongole ya CDH Bank K39.5 biliyoni, zomwe zimaletsa ndalama zomwe ingapeze kuchokera kwina.

Francis Njanje, mkulu wa Malawi Volunteers Organisation, adati kuchedwaku ndi vuto. Chimanga chambiri tsopano chili m'manja mwa ogulitsa, osati alimi. Ogulitsa akugula chimanga pamtengo wotsika ngati K600 pa kilogalamu, pansi pa mtengo wovomerezeka. Izi zikutanthauza kuti alimi angataye ndalama. Njanje anati Admarc iyenera kuti inabwera pamsika kale kwambiri kuti igule mwachindunji kwa alimi ndikuletsa ogulitsa kuti asapereke ndalama zambiri.

Akatswiri akuda nkhawa kuti chifukwa cha ndalama zochepa zomwe zatulutsidwa, Admarc singagule chimanga chonse chomwe ikufunikira kapena kuteteza alimi ku mitengo yotsika. Funso loti ndalama zina zonse za K60 biliyoni zili kuti silikudziwika bwino.

en.title: World Netball backs Malawi girls netball project
en.body: The Netball Association of Malawi (Nam) has won support from World Netball for a new project. The project is called 'Grassroots to Queens'. It aims to help young girls in Malawi learn netball and stay in school. World Netball approved the project on 26 March 2026. Money for the project has not yet been paid, but World Netball said it will pay when it has the money.
en.body_more:
The project will work with schools across Malawi. Girls who do very well at netball will get money to pay for secondary school and university. The best players may be picked for Malawi's Under-19, Under-21 and senior national teams. Old and new Malawi Queens players will coach and teach the girls.

Former Malawi Queens star Mary Waya will help lead the project with her foundation. She said netball gave her many good things in life. Now she wants to help young girls get the same chances. The project will teach girls more than just netball. They will learn life skills and how to be leaders. They will also talk about school, health and how to stay safe.

Nam President Vitumbiko Gubuduza said World Netball's approval is a big win for Malawi netball. She said the project shows how sport can help girls get good education and good chances. World Netball Head of Governance Paul Smith said Nam was ready to do good work. Nam is now waiting for World Netball to send the money. The team is ready to start as soon as the funding comes.

ny.title: World Netball yathandizira polojekiti ya atsikana ya Malawi
ny.body: Bungwe la Netball Association of Malawi (Nam) lapeza thandizo kuchokera ku World Netball pa ntchito yatsopano. Ntchitoyi imatchedwa 'Grassroots to Queens'. Cholinga chake ndi kuthandiza atsikana achichepere ku Malawi kuphunzira netball ndikupitilizabe kusukulu. World Netball idavomereza ntchitoyi pa 26 Marichi 2026. Ndalama za polojekitiyi sizinalipidwebe, koma World Netball idati idzalipira ikadzakhala ndi ndalamazo.
ny.body_more:
Pulojekitiyi igwira ntchito ndi masukulu ku Malawi konse. Atsikana omwe amachita bwino kwambiri pa netball adzapeza ndalama zolipirira sukulu ya sekondale ndi yunivesite. Osewera abwino kwambiri angasankhidwe m'magulu adziko lonse a Malawi a Under-19, Under-21 ndi akuluakulu. Osewera akale ndi atsopano a Malawi Queens adzaphunzitsa ndi kuphunzitsa atsikanawo.

Nyenyezi wakale wa Malawi Queens Mary Waya adzathandiza kutsogolera pulojekitiyi ndi maziko ake. Anati netball inamupatsa zinthu zambiri zabwino m'moyo. Tsopano akufuna kuthandiza atsikana achichepere kupeza mwayi womwewo. Pulojekitiyi iphunzitsa atsikana zambiri osati netball yokha. Adzaphunzira maluso a moyo komanso momwe angakhalire atsogoleri. Adzalankhulanso za sukulu, thanzi komanso momwe angakhalire otetezeka.

Purezidenti wa Nam Vitumbiko Gubuduza adati kuvomerezedwa kwa World Netball ndi kupambana kwakukulu kwa netball ya Malawi. Anati pulojekitiyi ikuwonetsa momwe masewera angathandizire atsikana kupeza maphunziro abwino komanso mwayi wabwino. Mtsogoleri wa Utsogoleri wa World Netball Paul Smith adati Nam anali wokonzeka kuchita ntchito yabwino. Tsopano Nam akuyembekezera World Netball kuti atumize ndalamazo. Gululo lakonzeka kuyamba ndalamazo zikangobwera.

en.title: MP gives food to students before exams
en.body: Martha Mzomera-Ngwira is a Member of Parliament for Mzimba Hora and Deputy Minister of Gender, Children, Disability and Social Welfare. She gave beans, eggs, cooking oil, and maize flour to Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education (PSLCE) candidates in her area. The food was given to help students study well before their exams.
en.body_more:
Mzomera-Ngwira visited many PSLCE candidate camps across Mzimba Hora. She told the students that education is very important for the country. She said, "Nothing is easy. You need to sacrifice yourself by studying hard and listening to your teachers." She also said that Malawi needs educated people to help the nation grow.

The MP told the students not to be sad because they come from villages. She said many successful people in Malawi also started from small towns. "Dream big, work hard and believe in your abilities," she told them. She said that where you come from does not matter. What matters is how hard you work. She asked the students to stay focused and confident as they prepare for their exams.

Mzomera-Ngwira also said thank you to the teachers. She said teachers do very important work teaching the students. She told the teachers, "May God bless you." She said the food she gave may not be enough, but it will help the students during this important time. She promised that her office will help more students in the future, especially those who do well and go to national secondary schools.

Nine camps received the food: Bulala, Mzalangwe, Malangazi, Chiseng'ezi, Malinyete, Chanyama, Endindeni, Visenthe, and Lukwelukwe. Teachers and community leaders praised the MP for helping the students. They said this gift will give the students hope and help them do better in their exams.

ny.title: MP apereka chakudya kwa ophunzira mayeso asanayambe
ny.body: Martha Mzomera-Ngwira ndi phungu wa Nyumba Yamalamulo wa Mzimba Hora komanso Wachiwiri kwa Nduna ya za Jenda, Ana, Olumala ndi Ubwino wa Anthu. Iye anapereka nyemba, mazira, mafuta ophikira, ndi ufa wa chimanga kwa ophunzira a pulayimale omwe akufuna kulembetsa ku Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education (PSLCE) m'dera lake. Chakudyacho chinaperekedwa kuti chithandize ophunzira kuphunzira bwino mayeso awo asanayambe.
ny.body_more:
Mzomera-Ngwira adayendera misasa yambiri ya ofuna kulowa nawo PSLCE ku Mzimba Hora. Adauza ophunzirawo kuti maphunziro ndi ofunika kwambiri mdzikolo. Adati, "Palibe chophweka. Muyenera kudzimana nokha pophunzira mwakhama ndikumvera aphunzitsi anu." Adatinso Malawi ikufunika anthu ophunzira kuti athandize dziko kukula.

Nduna ya Nyumba Yamalamulo idauza ophunzirawo kuti asakhale achisoni chifukwa amachokera kumidzi. Adati anthu ambiri opambana ku Malawi adayambanso kuchokera m'matauni ang'onoang'ono. "Lota zazikulu, gwirani ntchito mwakhama ndipo khulupirirani luso lanu," adawauza. Adati komwe mumachokera sikofunika. Chofunika ndi momwe mumagwirira ntchito molimbika. Adapempha ophunzirawo kuti akhalebe okhazikika komanso odzidalira pamene akukonzekera mayeso awo.

Mzomera-Ngwira adatinso zikomo kwa aphunzitsi. Adati aphunzitsi amachita ntchito yofunika kwambiri yophunzitsa ophunzirawo. Adauza aphunzitsiwo kuti, "Mulungu akudalitseni." Adati chakudya chomwe adapereka sichingakhale chokwanira, koma chithandiza ophunzirawo panthawi yofunikayi. Adalonjeza kuti ofesi yake ithandiza ophunzira ambiri mtsogolo, makamaka omwe amachita bwino ndikupita ku masukulu apamwamba adziko lonse.

Misasa isanu ndi inayi yalandira chakudya: Bulala, Mzalangwe, Malangazi, Chiseng'ezi, Malinyete, Chanyama, Endindeni, Visenthe, ndi Lukwelukwe. Aphunzitsi ndi atsogoleri ammudzi adayamika MP chifukwa chothandiza ophunzirawo. Iwo adati mphatso iyi ipatsa ophunzirawo chiyembekezo ndikuwathandiza kuchita bwino mayeso awo.

en.title: Architects meet to fix Malawi's buildings and design
en.body: Malawi's architects met for three days in Blantyre to talk about problems in the country's buildings and design. Steve Musopole, the head of the Malawi Institute of Architects, said the quality of buildings needs to be better. He said architects want to build a stronger Malawi. The meeting brought together architects from the government and private companies.
en.body_more:
Thandeka Palai, the head of the Botswana Architects Association, told people to learn new technology and digital tools. "We cannot fall behind in artificial intelligence and technology," she said. The world is moving fast, and Malawi must keep up with these new ways of building and designing.

Young architects also spoke at the meeting. Tamanda Banda, a fourth-year student at Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences, said she was happy to learn from older, more skilled architects. The young voices at the meeting showed that new people are ready to work on Malawi's buildings and design.

Fergus Gondwe, the Principal Secretary for administration in the Ministry of Transport and Public Works, opened the meeting. He told architects to think about long-term plans, not just quick fixes. The meeting's theme was "Architecture at a crossroads: Resilient construction in a volatile economic environment." This means architects must design buildings that are strong and can handle hard times. Steve Musopole said that buildings show who we are as a country. Good design and strong construction can help Malawi's economy grow and give people more trust in the future.

ny.title: Akatswiri a zomangamanga akumana kuti akonze nyumba ndi mapangidwe a Malawi
ny.body: Akatswiri a zomangamanga ku Malawi anakumana kwa masiku atatu ku Blantyre kuti akambirane za mavuto omwe ali m'nyumba ndi kapangidwe ka nyumba mdzikolo. Steve Musopole, mkulu wa bungwe la Malawi Institute of Architects, anati ubwino wa nyumba uyenera kukhala wabwino. Iye anati akatswiri a zomangamanga akufuna kumanga Malawi yolimba. Msonkhanowu unasonkhanitsa akatswiri a zomangamanga ochokera ku boma ndi makampani achinsinsi.
ny.body_more:
Thandeka Palai, mtsogoleri wa Botswana Architects Association, adauza anthu kuti aphunzire ukadaulo watsopano ndi zida za digito. "Sitingathe kutsalira mu luntha lochita kupanga ndi ukadaulo," adatero. Dziko lapansi likuyenda mwachangu, ndipo Malawi iyenera kutsatira njira zatsopano zomangira ndi kupanga mapulani.

Akatswiri a zomangamanga achichepere adalankhulanso pamsonkhanowo. Tamanda Banda, wophunzira wa chaka chachinayi ku Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences, adati anali wokondwa kuphunzira kuchokera kwa akatswiri a zomangamanga achikulire komanso aluso. Mawu a achinyamata pamsonkhanowo adawonetsa kuti anthu atsopano ali okonzeka kugwira ntchito pa nyumba ndi mapangidwe aku Malawi.

Fergus Gondwe, Mlembi Wamkulu wa Unduna wa Zoyendera ndi Ntchito Zapagulu, adatsegula msonkhanowo. Adauza akatswiri a zomangamanga kuti aganizire za mapulani anthawi yayitali, osati kungokonza mwachangu. Mutu wa msonkhanowo unali "Zomangamanga pamphambano: Kumanga kolimba m'malo osakhazikika azachuma." Izi zikutanthauza kuti akatswiri a zomangamanga ayenera kupanga nyumba zolimba komanso zomwe zingathe kuthana ndi mavuto. Steve Musopole adati nyumba zimasonyeza kuti ndife ndani monga dziko. Kapangidwe kabwino ndi zomangamanga zolimba zingathandize chuma cha Malawi kukula ndikupatsa anthu chidaliro chambiri mtsogolo.

en.title: Admarc starts buying maize but gets only K5 billion
en.body: Admarc will begin buying maize from farmers tomorrow at K900 per kilogramme. But the government has given only K5 billion of the K60 billion budget. Admarc chief Ben Botolo said the corporation will buy 65,000 metric tonnes during 2026/27. He asked farmers to sell through official depots and bring clean, dry maize.
en.body_more:
The K5 billion is the first payment, said Admarc spokesperson Theresa Chapulapula. More money must come for buying to continue all season. This is a worry for farmers and traders.

Grain Traders Association president Grace Mijiga-Mhango said Admarc's entry is good news but K5 billion is too small. "Coming at a time when its entry into the market was expected earlier, we hope it will quickly mobilise additional resources and continue buying the commodity so that farmers are not exploited," she said. Right now, some traders buy maize for only K600 per kilogramme. This is far below the fair price of K900. Innocent Pangapanga, director of the Centre for Agriculture Research and Development at Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources, said the K5 billion helps but is not enough. He told the government to release all K60 billion and to also give more money to the National Food Reserve Agency. "Given the looming threat of the upcoming 2026/27 El Niño and the heightened risk of food insecurity, the government should urgently scale up financing to both Admarc and the National Food Reserve Agency to aggressively procure maize, support farmer incomes, stabilise markets and rebuild the Strategic Grain Reserve before supply conditions deteriorate," he said.

Agriculture expert Leonard Chimwaza said Admarc brings fair prices and stops bad vendors. "Many vendors were offering low prices across commodity markets in Malawi. Therefore, the entry of a competitor offering prices above the minimum farm-gate price is a positive development," he said. Mwapata Institute leader William Chadza said Admarc came late. In March, Botolo said Admarc needs K144 billion to work well. The government gave only K60 billion. Admarc must borrow K80 billion from banks, which costs K10 billion in interest. Botolo also asked the government to pay K39.5 billion owed to CDH Bank.

ny.title: Admarc yayamba kugula chimanga koma yapeza K5 biliyoni yokha
ny.body: Admarc iyamba kugula chimanga kwa alimi mawa pa K900 pa kilogalamu imodzi. Koma boma lapereka K5 biliyoni yokha pa bajeti ya K60 biliyoni. Mkulu wa Admarc, Ben Botolo, anati kampaniyo idzagula matani 65,000 mu 2026/27. Iye anapempha alimi kuti agulitse kudzera m'malo osungiramo mbewu ovomerezeka ndikubweretsa chimanga choyera komanso chouma.
ny.body_more:
K5 biliyoni ndi malipiro oyamba, anatero wolankhulira Admarc Theresa Chapulapula. Ndalama zambiri ziyenera kuperekedwa kuti kugula kupitirire nyengo yonse. Izi ndi nkhawa kwa alimi ndi amalonda.

Purezidenti wa Grain Traders Association, Grace Mijiga-Mhango, anati kulowa kwa Admarc ndi nkhani yabwino koma K5 biliyoni ndi yochepa kwambiri. "Pofika nthawi yomwe kulowa kwake pamsika kunali koyembekezeredwa kale, tikukhulupirira kuti idzasonkhanitsa mwachangu zinthu zina ndikupitiliza kugula zinthuzo kuti alimi asagwiritsidwe ntchito molakwika," adatero. Pakadali pano, amalonda ena amagula chimanga pa K600 yokha pa kilogalamu. Izi ndi zotsika kwambiri pamtengo woyenera wa K900. Innocent Pangapanga, mkulu wa Centre for Agriculture Research and Development ku Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources, anati K5 biliyoni imathandiza koma sikokwanira. Anauza boma kuti litulutse K60 biliyoni yonse ndikuperekanso ndalama zambiri ku National Food Reserve Agency. "Popeza kuti pali chiopsezo cha El Niño ya 2026/27 yomwe ikubwera komanso chiopsezo chachikulu cha kusowa kwa chakudya, boma liyenera kuwonjezera ndalama mwachangu ku Admarc ndi National Food Reserve Agency kuti agule chimanga mwachangu, kuthandizira ndalama za alimi, kukhazikitsa misika yokhazikika ndikumanganso Strategic Grain Reserve isanafike poipa," adatero.

Katswiri wa zaulimi Leonard Chimwaza adati Admarc imabweretsa mitengo yoyenera ndikuletsa ogulitsa oipa. "Ogulitsa ambiri anali kupereka mitengo yotsika m'misika yonse yazinthu ku Malawi. Chifukwa chake, kulowa kwa mpikisano wopereka mitengo yoposa mtengo wocheperako wa famu ndi chitukuko chabwino," adatero. Mtsogoleri wa Mwapata Institute, William Chadza, adati Admarc idabwera mochedwa. Mu Marichi, Botolo adati Admarc ikufunika K144 biliyoni kuti igwire ntchito bwino. Boma lidapereka K60 biliyoni yokha. Admarc iyenera kubwereka K80 biliyoni kuchokera ku mabanki, zomwe zimawononga K10 biliyoni ngati chiwongola dzanja. Botolo adapemphanso boma kuti lipereke K39.5 biliyoni yomwe ili ndi ngongole ku CDH Bank.

en.title: PSG wins Champions League, Paris streets turn violent
en.body: Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League final against Arsenal on Saturday night. Hundreds of people were arrested after fans took to the streets to celebrate. Police say 416 people were detained across France, including 283 in Paris. Seven police officers were hurt. Shops were damaged and cars were set on fire.
en.body_more:
The French government sent 22,000 police officers across the country before the match. In Paris alone, 8,000 officers were ready. But the celebrations became chaotic. Nearly 20,000 supporters gathered on the Champs-Élysées near the Arc de Triomphe. Fans set off flares, lit fireworks, and blared car horns. Some groups broke shop windows and set cars on fire. In the 8th Arrondissement, supporters tried to storm a police station. Police stopped them.

Interior Minister Laurent Nunez said the trouble had been expected because of the big match and the feelings of the fans. He called the violence "absolutely unacceptable". Near PSG's Parc des Princes stadium in the 16th Arrondissement, police contained about 1,000 people. A bakery and a restaurant were damaged. Violence also broke out near the stadium where fans were watching the match on big screens.

This was not the first time. In May 2025, PSG's first Champions League win led to the same kind of trouble. That time, 201 people were hurt and more than 500 were arrested. Two people died, including a 17-year-old boy. Political leader Marine Le Pen said it was wrong that a football win could cause riots and make people fear for their safety.

PSG planned to hold an official celebration on Sunday at the Champ de Mars near the Eiffel Tower. About 85,000 to 90,000 people were expected to come. French President Emmanuel Macron was to meet the players at the Élysée Palace. Later that evening at Parc des Princes, fans and players were to gather from 7.30pm local time to mark a second straight European title.

ny.title: PSG yapambana Champions League, misewu ya Paris yakhala yachiwawa
ny.body: Paris Saint-Germain idapambana mpikisano wa Champions League motsutsana ndi Arsenal Loweruka usiku. Anthu mazana ambiri adamangidwa pambuyo poti mafani adapita m'misewu kukasangalala. Apolisi ati anthu 416 adamangidwa ku France konse, kuphatikiza 283 ku Paris. Apolisi asanu ndi awiri adavulala. Masitolo adawonongeka ndipo magalimoto adawotchedwa.
ny.body_more:
Boma la France linatumiza apolisi 22,000 m'dziko lonselo masewerawa asanachitike. Ku Paris kokha, apolisi 8,000 anali okonzeka. Koma chikondwererocho chinasanduka chisokonezo. Otsatira pafupifupi 20,000 anasonkhana pa Champs-Élysées pafupi ndi Arc de Triomphe. Otsatira anayatsa moto, anayatsa zozimitsa moto, ndipo anafuwula malipenga a magalimoto. Magulu ena anaswa mawindo a masitolo ndi kuyatsa magalimoto. Mu Arrondissement ya 8, otsatira anayesa kulowa m'malo mwa apolisi. Apolisi anawaletsa.

Nduna ya Zamkati Laurent Nunez anati vutoli linkayembekezeredwa chifukwa cha masewerawa akuluakulu komanso momwe mafaniwo amamvera. Iye anati chiwawacho "sichovomerezeka konse". Pafupi ndi bwalo lamasewera la Parc des Princes la PSG ku Arrondissement ya 16, apolisi anali ndi anthu pafupifupi 1,000. Buledi ndi lesitilanti zinawonongeka. Chiwawa chinayambanso pafupi ndi bwalo lamasewera pomwe mafani anali kuonera masewerawa pa zikwangwani zazikulu.

Iyi sinali nthawi yoyamba. Mu Meyi 2025, kupambana koyamba kwa PSG mu Champions League kunabweretsa mavuto omwewo. Nthawi imeneyo, anthu 201 anavulala ndipo oposa 500 anamangidwa. Anthu awiri anamwalira, kuphatikizapo mnyamata wazaka 17. Mtsogoleri wa ndale Marine Le Pen anati n’kulakwa kuti kupambana mpira kungayambitse zipolowe ndikupangitsa anthu kuopa chitetezo chawo.

PSG inakonza zoti ichite chikondwerero chovomerezeka Lamlungu ku Champ de Mars pafupi ndi Eiffel Tower. Anthu pafupifupi 85,000 mpaka 90,000 ankayembekezeredwa kubwera. Purezidenti wa ku France Emmanuel Macron anali kukakumana ndi osewerawo ku Élysée Palace. Madzulo amenewo ku Parc des Princes, mafani ndi osewera anali kusonkhana kuyambira 7:30pm nthawi yakomweko kuti akumbukire chikho chachiwiri motsatizana cha ku Europe.

en.title: Tobacco Farmers Blamed for Poor Handling and Low Prices
en.body: The Tobacco Commission says many farmers are damaging Malawi's tobacco by mixing grades, adding water to bales, and not caring for quality. The commission warned on Wednesday in Lilongwe that this breaks the law and makes buyers reject tobacco. When tobacco is rejected, farmers get less money. An expert said farmers must stop complaining and focus on quality instead.
en.body_more:
The Tobacco Commission Public Relations Officer Telephorus Chigwenembe said farmers are mixing tobacco grades and tampering with moisture levels in their bales. This violates the Tobacco Industry Act of 2019. He said that if farmers want good prices, they must improve their grading and curing methods, or Malawi will lose business on the world market.

Governance expert Dr. Ben Dzolowere agreed strongly. He said some farmers hide mold in tobacco and mix poor leaf with good leaf to trick buyers. He said farmers control the grade they bring to market, even if they cannot control the world price. When tobacco is clean and well cured, buyers compete for it and pay more money. When tobacco is wet, mixed, or dirty, buyers pay less. "Factories do not compete for trash — they compete for quality," Dzolowere said.

One observation at the Lilongwe Auction Floor showed the problem clearly. On May 19, 2026, only 142 bales of tobacco from independent farmers were for sale. Of these, 126 were rejected — a rate of 88.7 percent. Most were rejected because moisture inside the bales was too high. Temperature tests showed these bales had mold. Contracted farmers, who have company support, had far fewer rejections. Many independent farmers lack proper barns and storage, so their tobacco gets wet and fails the test.

The Tobacco Commission is now running campaigns to teach farmers how to dry, grade, and pack tobacco correctly. Officials say better quality is the only way to get better prices and bring confidence back to Malawi's tobacco business.

ny.title: Alimi a Fodya Aimbidwa Mlandu Chifukwa cha Kusagwiritsa Ntchito Moyenera ndi Mitengo Yotsika
ny.body: Bungwe la Tobacco Commission lati alimi ambiri akuwononga fodya wa ku Malawi mwa kusakaniza magiredi, kuwonjezera madzi m'mabale, komanso kusasamalira mtundu wake. Bungweli linachenjeza Lachitatu ku Lilongwe kuti izi zikuphwanya lamulo ndipo zimapangitsa ogula kukana fodya. Fodya ikakanidwa, alimi amapeza ndalama zochepa. Katswiri wina anati alimi ayenera kusiya kudandaula ndikuyang'ana kwambiri pa mtundu wake.
ny.body_more:
Mkulu wa Ubale wa Anthu ku Tobacco Commission, Telephorus Chigwenembe, anati alimi akusakaniza mitundu ya fodya ndi kusokoneza chinyezi m'mabale awo. Izi zikuphwanya lamulo la makampani opanga fodya la 2019. Anati ngati alimi akufuna mitengo yabwino, ayenera kusintha njira zawo zowerengera ndi kuyeretsa, apo ayi Malawi idzataya bizinesi pamsika wapadziko lonse.

Katswiri wa zaulamuliro Dr. Ben Dzolowere adavomereza mwamphamvu. Anati alimi ena amabisa nkhungu mu fodya ndikusakaniza masamba osalimba ndi masamba abwino kuti anyenge ogula. Anati alimi amalamulira mitundu yomwe amabweretsa pamsika, ngakhale atakhala kuti sangathe kuwongolera mtengo wapadziko lonse. Fodya ikayera komanso yochiritsidwa bwino, ogula amapikisana nayo ndipo amalipira ndalama zambiri. Fodya ikanyowa, yosakanikirana, kapena yodetsedwa, ogula amalipira ndalama zochepa. "Mafakitale sapikisana ndi zinyalala - amapikisana ndi mtundu," adatero Dzolowere.

Kuwona kwina ku Lilongwe Auction Floor kunawonetsa vutoli momveka bwino. Pa Meyi 19, 2026, mabale 142 okha a fodya ochokera kwa alimi odziyimira pawokha anali ogulitsidwa. Mwa awa, 126 adakanidwa - chiŵerengero cha 88.7 peresenti. Ambiri anakanidwa chifukwa chinyezi mkati mwa mabule chinali chokwera kwambiri. Mayeso a kutentha adawonetsa kuti mabule awa anali ndi nkhungu. Alimi omwe adagwirizana, omwe ali ndi chithandizo cha kampani, anali ndi ochepa kwambiri omwe anakanidwa. Alimi ambiri odziyimira pawokha alibe malo osungiramo ndi malo abwino, kotero fodya wawo umanyowa ndipo umalephera mayesowo.

Bungwe la Tobacco Commission tsopano likuyendetsa kampeni yophunzitsa alimi momwe angaumitsire, kuyika maginito, ndi kulongedza fodya moyenera. Akuluakulu aboma akuti ubwino wabwino ndiyo njira yokhayo yopezera mitengo yabwino ndikubwezeretsa chidaliro ku bizinesi ya fodya ku Malawi.

en.title: Bullets part with coach Chirwa, name Munthali interim leader
en.body: FC Nyasa Big Bullets have ended Gilbert Chirwa's time as head coach. The club and Chirwa agreed to part ways on Friday. Heston Munthali, a club legend, will lead the team on an interim basis. Munthali was Chirwa's assistant. He will lead Bullets in Sunday's match against Moyale Barracks. The club will search for a new head coach after the international window.
en.body_more:
Chirwa took charge in December on a temporary basis after Peter Mponda left. He stayed on after winning the Castel Challenge Cup and finishing second in last season's league. This season, however, Bullets have not done well. After five matches, they sit sixth with eight or nine points. They have two wins and three draws. The club said it thanks Chirwa for his work and wishes him well in the future.

Munthali is a well-known figure at Bullets. He was a striker for the club and has worked in coaching roles before. Club officials said Munthali will pick the team and make decisions during matches. He will have help from the other coaches. The club believes he will keep the team ready while they find a new coach. The club said the new coach will come from abroad.

Bullets have had a mixed start to the 2026 season. They have not lost a match in five games, but they have drawn too many times. This has cost them points. They are now only five points above the teams that could go down from the league. A football expert said Chirwa did not do a bad job because he took over quickly and the team is still unbeaten. The club is looking for a coach with at least a CAF A Licence and proven experience. The new head coach will be named in the second week of June.

ny.title: Bullets isiyanirana ndi coach Chirwa yemwe wasankha Munthali kukhala mtsogoleri
ny.body: Timu ya FC Nyasa Big Bullets yathetsa nthawi ya Gilbert Chirwa monga mphunzitsi wamkulu. Kalabuyi ndi Chirwa adagwirizana kuti asiyane Lachisanu. Heston Munthali, katswiri wa timuyi, adzatsogolera timuyi kwakanthawi. Munthali anali wothandizira wa Chirwa. Adzatsogolera Bullets pamasewera a Lamlungu motsutsana ndi Moyale Barracks. Kalabuyi idzafunafuna mphunzitsi watsopano pambuyo pa zenera lapadziko lonse lapansi.
ny.body_more:
Chirwa adayamba kulamulira mu Disembala kwakanthawi Peter Mponda atachoka. Anakhalabe atapambana Castel Challenge Cup ndikumaliza pa nambala yachiwiri mu ligi ya nyengo yatha. Komabe, nyengo ino, Bullets sinachite bwino. Pambuyo pa masewera asanu, ali pa nambala 6 ndi mapointi asanu ndi atatu kapena asanu ndi anayi. Ali ndi zigonjetso ziwiri ndi ma draw atatu. Kalabuyo inati ikuthokoza Chirwa chifukwa cha ntchito yake ndipo ikumufunira zabwino mtsogolo.

Munthali ndi munthu wodziwika bwino ku Bullets. Anali womenya mpira wa kalabuyo ndipo wakhala akugwira ntchito yophunzitsa kale. Akuluakulu a kalabuyo anati Munthali adzasankha timuyo ndikupanga zisankho pamasewera. Adzathandizidwa ndi aphunzitsi ena. Kalabuyo ikukhulupirira kuti asunga timuyo okonzeka pamene ikupeza mphunzitsi watsopano. Kalabuyo inati mphunzitsi watsopanoyo adzachokera kunja.

Bullets yayamba mosiyanasiyana nyengo ya 2026. Sanataye masewera aliwonse m'masewera asanu, koma asewera ma draw kangapo. Izi zawatayitsa mapointi. Tsopano ali ndi mapointi asanu okha kuposa magulu omwe angatsike kuchokera mu ligi. Katswiri wina wa mpira anati Chirwa sanachite bwino chifukwa adatenga udindo mwachangu ndipo timuyi sinagonjetsedwebe. Kalabuyi ikufuna mphunzitsi wokhala ndi CAF A License komanso wodziwa bwino ntchito yake. Mphunzitsi wamkulu watsopanoyu adzasankhidwa sabata yachiwiri ya mwezi wa June.

en.title: President bypasses vice presidents, uses ministers instead
en.body: President Peter Mutharika is giving key State duties to Cabinet ministers instead of his two vice presidents. First Vice President Jane Ansah and Second Vice President Enoch Chihana have been absent from major national events since the start of the year. The President's office says this saves money, because ministers cost less to pay than vice presidents. Ansah also lost two of her job titles in recent weeks.
en.body_more:
In January, Minister of Finance Joseph Mwanamvekha went to John Chilembwe Day on behalf of the President, even though the Second Vice President was in the country. In March, Minister of Transport Jappie Mhango spoke at Martyr's Day in Nkhata Bay instead of a vice president. Minister of Sports Alfred Gangata went to Kamuzu Day in May. The President did not send a vice president. When the President flew to South Africa in March, neither vice president was there to say goodbye.

The President's office says this is allowed by the Constitution. A person named Focus Maganga, who speaks for the President, said the President can choose to give jobs to whoever he wants. He said this does not mean there is bad feeling between the President and Ansah. He said the jobs are still being done, just in a different way.

But experts and human rights leaders are worried. They say the vice presidents look weak now. Lawyer Benedicto Kondowe said taking away jobs without giving new ones is wrong. He said it makes people think the Constitution does not matter. A human rights leader named Robert Mkwezalamba said people who pay taxes want to see vice presidents doing real work, not sitting at home. Another leader, Michael Kaiyatsa, said the country should ask if the vice president offices are being made useless on purpose.

ny.title: Purezidenti amadutsa ma vice prezidenti, koma m'malo mwake amagwiritsa ntchito nduna
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika akupatsa nduna za boma ntchito zofunika kwambiri m'malo mwa apulezidenti ake awiri. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Jane Ansah ndi Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enoch Chihana akhala akusowa pazochitika zazikulu zadziko kuyambira chaka chino. Ofesi ya Purezidenti ikunena kuti izi zimapulumutsa ndalama, chifukwa nduna zimalipira ndalama zochepa kuposa apulezidenti. Ansah adatayanso maudindo ake awiri a ntchito m'masabata aposachedwa.
ny.body_more:
Mu Januwale, Nduna ya Zachuma Joseph Mwanamvekha adapita ku John Chilembwe Day m'malo mwa Purezidenti, ngakhale kuti Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti anali mdzikolo. Mu Marichi, Nduna ya Zoyendera Jappie Mhango adalankhula pa Tsiku la Martyr ku Nkhata Bay m'malo mwa wachiwiri kwa purezidenti. Nduna ya Zamasewera Alfred Gangata adapita ku Tsiku la Kamuzu mu Meyi. Purezidenti sanatumize wachiwiri kwa purezidenti. Purezidenti atapita ku South Africa mu Marichi, palibe wachiwiri kwa purezidenti amene adalipo kuti atsanzikane.

Ofesi ya Purezidenti ikunena kuti izi zaloledwa ndi Malamulo. Munthu wotchedwa Focus Maganga, yemwe amalankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti, adati Purezidenti akhoza kusankha kupereka ntchito kwa aliyense amene akufuna. Anati izi sizikutanthauza kuti pali kusamvana pakati pa Purezidenti ndi Ansah. Anati ntchitozo zikupitirirabe kuchitika, m'njira yosiyana.

Koma akatswiri ndi atsogoleri a ufulu wachibadwidwe akuda nkhawa. Akuti achiwiri kwa purezidenti akuwoneka ofooka tsopano. Loya Benedicto Kondowe adati kuchotsa ntchito popanda kupereka zatsopano n'kolakwika. Anati zimapangitsa anthu kuganiza kuti Malamulo sali ofunika. Mtsogoleri wa ufulu wachibadwidwe wotchedwa Robert Mkwezalamba anati anthu omwe amalipira misonkho amafuna kuona apulezidenti akugwira ntchito yeniyeni, osati kukhala panyumba. Mtsogoleri wina, Michael Kaiyatsa, anati dzikolo liyenera kufunsa ngati maofesi a apulezidenti akupangidwa kukhala opanda ntchito mwadala.

en.title: Malawi can earn big money from Europe's green change
en.body: Malawi has minerals that Europe needs for green energy. Europe is looking for graphite, uranium, and rare earth minerals. These are used to make electric cars and solar power systems. The World Bank says Malawi could earn $30 billion from mining by 2040. But right now, mining gives less than 1% of Malawi's money. Experts say Malawi must use this chance well.
en.body_more:
Paul Mvula is a mining expert. He says Malawi must not just dig and sell raw minerals. Instead, the country should build factories to process the minerals here. Mvula says Malawi needs better roads, power, and skills training for workers. This will help Malawi earn more money from its minerals.

Marvin Banda is an economist. He says Europe's need for green minerals makes Africa important again. But he warns that Malawi must make strong deals. The deals must protect Malawi's people and land, not just help big foreign companies. Banda says Malawi needs smart contracts that are fair.

A German group called PowerShift also spoke about this. Michael Reckordt works there. He said Europe has only 6% of the world's people, but uses 25 to 30% of all metals. This is unfair, he said. Reckordt asked Europe to use less. He also asked for fair deals that help miners and their families. He said mining must not harm the land or hurt workers' rights.

Sixteen journalists from Africa came to a workshop in Malawi to talk about these ideas. They discussed how Africa's minerals could change the world. Experts say Malawi must move fast to get ready. The country must build trust with fair and open deals. If Malawi does this well, mining could change the whole economy.

ny.title: Malawi ingapeze ndalama zambiri kuchokera ku kusintha kwa zachilengedwe ku Europe
ny.body: Malawi ili ndi mchere womwe Europe imafuna kuti ipange mphamvu zobiriwira. Europe ikufuna miyala ya graphite, uranium, ndi miyala yamtengo wapatali. Izi zimagwiritsidwa ntchito popanga magalimoto amagetsi ndi makina amagetsi a dzuwa. Banki Yapadziko Lonse ikunena kuti Malawi ikhoza kupeza $30 biliyoni kuchokera ku migodi pofika chaka cha 2040. Koma pakadali pano, migodi imapereka ndalama zosakwana 1% ya ndalama za Malawi. Akatswiri akuti Malawi iyenera kugwiritsa ntchito mwayiwu bwino.
ny.body_more:
Paul Mvula ndi katswiri wa migodi. Iye akuti Malawi sayenera kungokumba ndi kugulitsa miyala yaiwisi. M'malo mwake, dzikolo liyenera kumanga mafakitale kuti ligwiritse ntchito miyala yaiwisi kuno. Mvula akuti Malawi ikufunika misewu yabwino, mphamvu, ndi maphunziro aluso kwa ogwira ntchito. Izi zithandiza Malawi kupeza ndalama zambiri kuchokera ku miyala yake yaiwisi.

Marvin Banda ndi katswiri wazachuma. Iye akuti kufunikira kwa mchere wobiriwira ku Europe kumapangitsa Africa kukhala yofunika kwambiri. Koma akuchenjeza kuti Malawi iyenera kupanga mapangano amphamvu. Mapanganowa ayenera kuteteza anthu aku Malawi ndi malo, osati kuthandiza makampani akuluakulu akunja okha. Banda akuti Malawi ikufunika mapangano anzeru omwe ndi achilungamo.

Gulu la ku Germany lotchedwa PowerShift nalonso linalankhula za izi. Michael Reckordt amagwira ntchito kumeneko. Iye anati Europe ili ndi 6% yokha ya anthu padziko lonse lapansi, koma imagwiritsa ntchito 25 mpaka 30% ya zitsulo zonse. Izi sizolungama, adatero. Reckordt anapempha Europe kuti igwiritse ntchito zochepa. Anapemphanso mapangano oyenera omwe amathandiza ogwira ntchito m'migodi ndi mabanja awo. Anati migodi siyenera kuvulaza nthaka kapena kuvulaza ufulu wa ogwira ntchito.

Atolankhani khumi ndi asanu ndi mmodzi ochokera ku Africa anabwera ku msonkhano ku Malawi kuti akambirane za malingaliro awa. Anakambirana momwe mchere wa ku Africa ungasinthire dziko lapansi. Akatswiri amati Malawi iyenera kuchitapo kanthu mwachangu kuti ikonzekere. Dzikoli liyenera kumanga chidaliro ndi mgwirizano wolungama komanso wotseguka. Ngati Malawi ichita izi bwino, migodi ingasinthe chuma chonse.

en.title: Young man builds business from plastic waste after foundation help
en.body: A young entrepreneur in Lilongwe is turning plastic waste into useful products and making good money. Alinafe Velemu got K1 million and help from the Paul Katema Foundation's Young Builders Challenge. He came third in the competition. Now his business, Green Innovations Center, makes tables, baskets, and knife handles from old plastic. The work is growing fast.
en.body_more:
Alinafe is a student at Phwezi Technical College. He learns general fitting skills at Level 2. He started his business in 2024 at Nalikule, near Lilongwe. The foundation gave him money and a teacher to help him grow his business. This is what the Young Builders Challenge does—it gives young people seed money, training, and advice.

At his workshop, Alinafe and his team take plastic that people throw away. They clean it and cut it and shape it into new things. Tables and baskets sell well. Knife handles do too. More people want to buy these things now. His workshop makes more products every week. Alinafe says the money and teaching from the foundation have changed everything. He says his work helps in two ways: it stops plastic from going to the dump, and it gives him and others jobs.

Ashley Chingoma works for the Paul Katema Foundation as a business teacher. He says the Young Builders Challenge does more than give money. The programme also teaches discipline and how to run a business the right way. This helps young people build businesses that last. The foundation sees many young people in the programme growing their businesses and making more money.

Alinafe says his work is not just about making money for himself. He says it helps young people find jobs when work is hard to find. It also helps the earth by using old things again instead of throwing them away.

ny.title: Mnyamata akumanga bizinesi pogwiritsa ntchito zinyalala za pulasitiki pambuyo pothandizidwa ndi maziko
ny.body: Wamalonda wachinyamata ku Lilongwe akusintha zinyalala za pulasitiki kukhala zinthu zothandiza ndipo akupeza ndalama zambiri. Alinafe Velemu adapeza K1 miliyoni ndi thandizo kuchokera ku Paul Katema Foundation's Young Builders Challenge. Iye adakhala wachitatu pampikisano. Tsopano bizinesi yake, Green Innovations Center, imapanga matebulo, mabasiketi, ndi zogwirira za mpeni kuchokera ku pulasitiki yakale. Ntchitoyi ikukula mofulumira.
ny.body_more:
Alinafe ndi wophunzira ku Phwezi Technical College. Amaphunzira luso lomanga zinthu pa Level 2. Anayamba bizinesi yake mu 2024 ku Nalikule, pafupi ndi Lilongwe. Bungweli linamupatsa ndalama ndi mphunzitsi kuti amuthandize kukulitsa bizinesi yake. Izi ndi zomwe Young Builders Challenge imachita—imapatsa achinyamata ndalama, maphunziro, ndi upangiri.

Pa workshop yake, Alinafe ndi gulu lake amatenga pulasitiki yomwe anthu amataya. Amaiyeretsa ndikuidula ndikuipanga kukhala zinthu zatsopano. Matebulo ndi madengu amagulitsidwa bwino. Zogwirira za mipeni nazonso zimatero. Anthu ambiri akufuna kugula zinthuzi tsopano. Workshop yake imapanga zinthu zambiri sabata iliyonse. Alinafe akuti ndalama ndi maphunziro ochokera ku bungweli zasintha chilichonse. Akuti ntchito yake imathandiza m'njira ziwiri: imaletsa pulasitiki kupita ku zinyalala, ndipo imapatsa iye ndi ena ntchito.

Ashley Chingoma amagwira ntchito ku Paul Katema Foundation ngati mphunzitsi wa bizinesi. Akuti Young Builders Challenge imachita zambiri kuposa kungopereka ndalama. Pulogalamuyi imaphunzitsanso kudziletsa komanso momwe angayendetsere bizinesi moyenera. Izi zimathandiza achinyamata kumanga mabizinesi okhalitsa. Bungweli likuwona achinyamata ambiri mu pulogalamuyi akukula mabizinesi awo ndikupanga ndalama zambiri.

Alinafe akuti ntchito yake si yongodzipangira ndalama zokha. Iye akuti imathandiza achinyamata kupeza ntchito pamene ntchito ikuvuta kupeza. Imathandizanso dziko lapansi pogwiritsa ntchito zinthu zakale m'malo mozitaya.

en.title: Church leader gives hope to man sick for 29 years
en.body: Apostle Clifford Kawinga visited Yotamu Nkokoko in Dedza District on Friday. Nkokoko is 55 years old and has been in bed for 29 years. He cannot move. Kawinga brought gifts to help the family. He gave a sewing machine, food, blankets, and a solar light. Nkokoko's mother said the gifts give the family hope.
en.body_more:
Nkokoko became ill in 1997 when he was in Form Three at William Murray Secondary School. The illness left him unable to move. For nearly 30 years, his mother Emily Chaima has cared for him at their home in Thundudzi Village. The family had no money and no light. Life was very hard.

Kawinga leads Salvation For All Ministries International. Through his Hope Field Initiative, he gives help to poor and sick people. In 2021, his group built a new house for Nkokoko's family. On Friday's visit, Kawinga brought more gifts. He gave K2 million in money, a radio, rice, soya, soap, and oil. The solar light will give the home power for the first time. The sewing machine will help the family make money.

Kawinga said every person has value and needs love. He said the church must care for people who suffer and feel alone. He told the community that helping others shows what real faith means. Chaima said the gifts bring big change. She said her son suffered alone for so long. Now she feels hope again.

Kawinga said this visit is not the end. He plans to help more poor families across Malawi. He said acts of care can give back hope to people who have lost it.

ny.title: Mtsogoleri wa tchalitchi amapereka chiyembekezo kwa munthu wodwala kwa zaka 29
ny.body: Mtumwi Clifford Kawinga adapita ku Yotamu Nkokoko ku Dedza District Lachisanu. Nkokoko ali ndi zaka 55 ndipo wakhala pabedi kwa zaka 29. Sangathe kuyenda. Kawinga adabweretsa mphatso zothandizira banjali. Adapereka makina osokera, chakudya, mabulangete, ndi nyali ya dzuwa. Amayi ake a Nkokoko adati mphatsozo zimapatsa banjali chiyembekezo.
ny.body_more:
Nkokoko anadwala mu 1997 pamene anali mu Fomu Yachitatu ku William Murray Secondary School. Matendawa anamupangitsa kuti asathe kuyenda. Kwa zaka pafupifupi 30, amayi ake Emily Chaima akhala akumusamalira kunyumba kwawo ku Thundudzi Village. Banjali linalibe ndalama komanso kuwala. Moyo unali wovuta kwambiri.

Kawinga akutsogolera Salvation For All Ministries International. Kudzera mu Hope Field Initiative yake, amapereka thandizo kwa anthu osauka ndi odwala. Mu 2021, gulu lake linamanga nyumba yatsopano ya banja la Nkokoko. Paulendo wa Lachisanu, Kawinga anabweretsa mphatso zambiri. Anapereka ndalama zokwana K2 miliyoni, wailesi, mpunga, soya, sopo, ndi mafuta. Kuwala kwa dzuwa kudzapereka mphamvu kunyumba koyamba. Makina osokera athandiza banjali kupeza ndalama.

Kawinga anati munthu aliyense ali ndi phindu ndipo akufunika chikondi. Anati mpingo uyenera kusamalira anthu omwe akuvutika komanso omwe akumva kuti ali okha. Anauza anthu ammudzi kuti kuthandiza ena kumasonyeza tanthauzo la chikhulupiriro chenicheni. Chaima anati mphatsozo zimabweretsa kusintha kwakukulu. Anati mwana wake wamwamuna anavutika yekha kwa nthawi yayitali. Tsopano akumvanso chiyembekezo.

Kawinga anati ulendowu si mapeto. Akukonzekera kuthandiza mabanja ambiri osauka ku Malawi konse. Anati ntchito zosamalira anthu zitha kubwezera chiyembekezo kwa anthu omwe ataya.

en.title: Patients Pay Bribes at Public Hospitals, Officials Admit
en.body: Malawi's public hospitals have a serious corruption problem. Patients are being forced to pay money for free treatment. Some pay bribes to jump queues. Others pay for drugs that should be free. The government's Health Secretary, Dan Namarika, told parliament this is true. He said the government has started to take action against the workers who take bribes.
en.body_more:
At Kamuzu Central Hospital, one patient paid 90,000 kwacha to use the Endoscopy Department. Another patient paid 18,500 kwacha before a tooth was pulled out. Hospital staff steal drugs and sell them in private shops. Two pharmacists and one hospital worker were found to have stolen medicine.

At Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital, patients pay money to skip the queue and get help faster. Some patients fear to tell anyone about the bribes. They worry the doctors will not help them next time. The hospital also said that theatre clothes, gauze, and cotton rolls were stolen.

At Mzuzu Central Hospital, staff ask for money before they help patients. Some workers give fake receipts. Sick people are afraid to report these bad workers because they fear being punished. A news team made a film called "Pay Up or Die." They found this problem at many hospitals. The film was made by seven news groups in Malawi. After the film, President Peter Mutharika told the Health Ministry and the Anti-Corruption Bureau to investigate and punish the workers who took bribes.

Parliament's Health Committee chief, Anthony Masamba, said this is very bad. He said the government must act fast. The hospitals have started to fight corruption. They have made complaint boxes, phone lines to report crimes, cameras, and teams to check honesty. They also work with the Anti-Corruption Bureau to stop this.

ny.title: Akuluakulu a boma avomereza kuti odwala amapereka ziphuphu m'zipatala za boma.
ny.body: Zipatala za boma ku Malawi zili ndi vuto lalikulu la ziphuphu. Odwala akukakamizidwa kulipira ndalama kuti alandire chithandizo chaulere. Ena amapereka ziphuphu kuti adutse pamzere. Ena amalipira mankhwala omwe ayenera kukhala aulere. Nduna ya zaumoyo ya boma, Dan Namarika, adauza nyumba yamalamulo kuti izi ndi zoona. Iye adati boma layamba kuchitapo kanthu motsutsana ndi ogwira ntchito omwe amalandira ziphuphu.
ny.body_more:
Ku Chipatala cha Kamuzu Central, wodwala m'modzi adalipira 90,000 kwacha kuti agwiritse ntchito Dipatimenti ya Endoscopy. Wodwala wina adalipira 18,500 kwacha asanachotse dzino. Ogwira ntchito kuchipatala amaba mankhwala osokoneza bongo ndikuwagulitsa m'masitolo achinsinsi. Akatswiri awiri a zamankhwala ndi wogwira ntchito m'chipatala m'modzi adapezeka kuti aba mankhwala.

Ku Chipatala cha Queen Elizabeth Central, odwala amalipira ndalama kuti adutse pamzere ndikupeza thandizo mwachangu. Odwala ena amaopa kuuza aliyense za ziphuphu. Amada nkhawa kuti madokotala sadzawathandiza nthawi ina. Chipatalacho chinanenanso kuti zovala za m'chipinda chochezera, gauze, ndi thonje zabedwa.

Ku Chipatala cha Mzuzu Central, ogwira ntchito amapempha ndalama asanathandize odwala. Ogwira ntchito ena amapereka malisiti abodza. Odwala amaopa kunena za ogwira ntchito oipa awa chifukwa amaopa kulangidwa. Gulu la atolankhani linapanga filimu yotchedwa "Pay Up or Die." Anapeza vutoli m'zipatala zambiri. Filimuyi idapangidwa ndi magulu asanu ndi awiri a nkhani ku Malawi. Pambuyo pa filimuyi, Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adauza Unduna wa Zaumoyo ndi Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu kuti afufuze ndikulanga ogwira ntchito omwe adalandira ziphuphu.

Mtsogoleri wa Komiti ya Zaumoyo ku Nyumba Yamalamulo, Anthony Masamba, anati izi ndi zoyipa kwambiri. Anati boma liyenera kuchitapo kanthu mwachangu. Zipatala zayamba kulimbana ndi ziphuphu. Zapanga mabokosi odandaula, mafoni kuti zifotokoze milandu, makamera, ndi magulu kuti aone ngati anthu ndi oona mtima. Amagwiranso ntchito ndi Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu kuti athetse vutoli.

en.title: Drug Shipment Worth K106 Billion Stopped at Border
en.body: South African police have stopped a truck at the Beitbridge Border Post. The truck came from Malawi and had drugs inside. Police found about 713,000 grams of a drug used to make mandrax. The drugs are worth around K106 billion. Two Malawians and one Zambian were arrested. Police used scanners and took eight hours to search the truck.
en.body_more:
The truck was stopped after scanners found something wrong. Border officials looked very carefully at the vehicle and found hidden drugs inside. The South African Border Management Authority said they used advanced tools to check the truck. A hidden part of the truck held the drugs. The three people arrested are now in a police station in Musina, South Africa. They will go to court soon.

The South African Home Affairs Minister Leon Schreiber said this is one of the biggest drug catches in South Africa. He said police knew about the truck before it came. They waited for it at the border. Police worked for eight hours to take the truck apart and find the drugs.

In Malawi, the police are waiting to hear more news from Interpol. Malawi Police Service said they do not have full details yet. A security expert said the catch shows that bad people use roads in the region to move drugs. He said some of these people may work for the police or the government. The Malawi customs office has not yet said how the drugs left the country.

ny.title: Kutumiza Mankhwala Osokoneza Bongo Okwana K106 Biliyoni Kwayimitsidwa Pamalire
ny.body: Apolisi aku South Africa ayimitsa galimoto yaikulu ku Beitbridge Border Post. Galimotoyo inachokera ku Malawi ndipo inali ndi mankhwala osokoneza bongo mkati mwake. Apolisi adapeza pafupifupi magalamu 713,000 a mankhwala osokoneza bongo omwe amagwiritsidwa ntchito popanga mandrax. Mankhwalawa ndi ofunika pafupifupi K106 biliyoni. Awiri aku Malawi ndi m'modzi waku Zambia adamangidwa. Apolisi adagwiritsa ntchito makina ojambulira ndipo adatenga maola asanu ndi atatu kuti afufuze galimotoyo.
ny.body_more:
Galimotoyo inaimitsidwa pambuyo poti ma scanner apeza cholakwika. Akuluakulu a m'malire anayang'ana galimotoyo mosamala kwambiri ndipo anapeza mankhwala obisika mkati. Bungwe Loona za Malire a South Africa linati linagwiritsa ntchito zipangizo zamakono kuti liyang'ane galimotoyo. Gawo lobisika la galimotoyo linali ndi mankhwalawo. Anthu atatu omwe amangidwa tsopano ali ku siteshoni ya apolisi ku Musina, South Africa. Apita kukhoti posachedwa.

Nduna ya Zamkati ku South Africa, Leon Schreiber, anati iyi ndi imodzi mwa mankhwala ogwidwa kwambiri ku South Africa. Anati apolisi ankadziwa za galimotoyo isanabwere. Anaiyembekezera kumalire. Apolisi anagwira ntchito kwa maola asanu ndi atatu kuti awononge galimotoyo ndikupeza mankhwalawo.

Ku Malawi, apolisi akuyembekezera kumva nkhani zambiri kuchokera ku Interpol. Apolisi a Malawi anati alibe tsatanetsatane wathunthu. Katswiri wa zachitetezo anati kugwidwaku kukusonyeza kuti anthu oipa amagwiritsa ntchito misewu m'derali kusuntha mankhwala osokoneza bongo. Anati ena mwa anthuwa akhoza kugwira ntchito ku apolisi kapena boma. Ofesi ya kasitomu ku Malawi sinanenebe momwe mankhwalawo adachokera mdzikolo.

en.title: Malawi's factory jobs drop as manufacturing shrinks
en.body: Malawi's factory jobs are falling. In 2010, 4.3 percent of workers had jobs in factories. In 2024, only 3.9 percent do. A new report from the African Development Bank warns this is bad news. The country makes fewer things now, and what it makes has low value. A bank report says Malawi must grow its factories fast or jobs will get worse.
en.body_more:
Two big reasons hurt Malawi's factories. Power cuts stop work. Money for new machines and ideas is hard to find. The report says Malawi is weaker than its neighbours. Mozambique has 4.8 percent factory workers. Zambia has 5.7 percent. But even these are far below the world average of 13.74 percent.

Malawi's factories make mostly cheap things. Food and drinks are 38.4 percent of what Malawi sells. Basic metals are 37.4 percent. The country does not make high-value goods. The bank says this must change. Malawi must make more from farm goods. It must join trade deals with other African countries.

The government says it is doing work to help. Patrick Botha, a government speaker, said they are making new rules. They stopped the sale of raw materials abroad so people will process them at home. But a work expert, Bright Limani, said the government must do more. He said taxes on factories are too high. He asked for help so young people can find jobs.

Every year, 270,000 young people look for work in Malawi. But only 40,000 new formal jobs open. The bank says if Malawi does not grow its factories soon, more people will stay poor and out of work.

ny.title: Ntchito m'mafakitale ku Malawi zachepa chifukwa cha kuchepa kwa ntchito zopanga zinthu
ny.body: Ntchito za mafakitale ku Malawi zikuchepa. Mu 2010, 4.3 peresenti ya ogwira ntchito anali ndi ntchito m'mafakitale. Mu 2024, 3.9 peresenti yokha ndi yomwe ili ndi ntchito. Lipoti latsopano lochokera ku African Development Bank likuchenjeza kuti iyi ndi nkhani yoipa. Dzikoli likupanga zinthu zochepa tsopano, ndipo zomwe limapanga zili ndi mtengo wotsika. Lipoti la banki likuti Malawi iyenera kukulitsa mafakitale ake mwachangu apo ayi ntchito zidzaipiraipira.
ny.body_more:
Zifukwa ziwiri zazikulu zikuvulaza mafakitale aku Malawi. Kuchepa kwa magetsi kumayimitsa ntchito. Ndalama zogulira makina atsopano ndi malingaliro n'zovuta kupeza. Lipotilo likuti Malawi ndi yofooka kuposa mayiko oyandikana nawo. Mozambique ili ndi antchito a mafakitale 4.8 peresenti. Zambia ili ndi 5.7 peresenti. Koma ngakhale izi zili pansi kwambiri pa avareji yapadziko lonse ya 13.74 peresenti.

Mafakitale aku Malawi amapanga zinthu zotsika mtengo kwambiri. Chakudya ndi zakumwa ndi 38.4 peresenti ya zomwe Malawi imagulitsa. Zitsulo zoyambira ndi 37.4 peresenti. Dzikoli silipanga zinthu zamtengo wapatali. Banki ikunena kuti izi ziyenera kusintha. Malawi iyenera kupanga zambiri kuchokera ku zinthu zaulimi. Iyenera kulumikizana ndi mapangano amalonda ndi mayiko ena aku Africa.

Boma likunena kuti likugwira ntchito yothandiza. Patrick Botha, sipikala wa boma, adati akupanga malamulo atsopano. Anasiya kugulitsa zinthu zopangira kunja kuti anthu azikonza kunyumba. Koma katswiri wa ntchito, Bright Limani, adati boma liyenera kuchita zambiri. Anati misonkho pamafakitale ndi yokwera kwambiri. Anapempha thandizo kuti achinyamata athe kupeza ntchito.

Chaka chilichonse, achinyamata 270,000 amafunafuna ntchito ku Malawi. Koma ntchito zatsopano 40,000 zokha ndi zomwe zatsegulidwa. Bankiyo ikunena kuti ngati Malawi siikulitsa mafakitale ake posachedwa, anthu ambiri adzakhala osauka komanso opanda ntchito.

en.title: No forex means cannabis factory still closed
en.body: A big cannabis factory in Malawi cannot open. The factory cost $10 million. It needs money from other countries to buy machine parts. The company, MMC Laboratories, has waited almost two years. Eve Mwase works there. She said the factory can start in eight months if the country gets more foreign money. The factory will make medicine from cannabis. It will sell to Europe.
en.body_more:
MMC Laboratories already has most of the machines it needs. The drying machine came to Malawi. But some parts are still missing. Without foreign money, the company cannot buy them. Mwase said this is the main problem: "We are almost two years behind. The lack of forex is our main issue."

When the factory works, it will make 700 tonnes of cannabis products each year. This will bring $100 million to Malawi every year. Europe wants to buy these products. But right now, nothing is made. The money does not come.

Economist Velli Nyirongo said the government must help. He said the country must give foreign money to factories that sell goods to other countries. "The authorities need to give forex to factories that make money for Malawi," he said. He also said the government must make business easier and faster. Banks must help factories buy machines.

The Reserve Bank of Malawi said it will help. Boston Maliketi works there. He said the bank will look for ways to help factories like MMC. But many other small businesses have the same problem. They cannot buy what they need from other countries.

ny.title: Palibe forex kutanthauza kuti fakitale ya chamba idatsekedwabe
ny.body: Fakitale yaikulu ya chamba ku Malawi singathe kutsegulidwa. Fakitaleyi yagula $10 miliyoni. Ikufunika ndalama kuchokera kumayiko ena kuti igule zida za makina. Kampaniyo, MMC Laboratories, yadikira pafupifupi zaka ziwiri. Eve Mwase amagwira ntchito kumeneko.
