Iye anati fakitaleyi ikhoza kuyamba m'miyezi isanu ndi itatu ngati dzikolo lipeza ndalama zambiri zakunja. Fakitaleyi ipanga mankhwala kuchokera ku chamba. Idzagulitsidwa ku Europe.
ny.body_more:
MMC Laboratories ili kale ndi makina ambiri omwe ikufuna. Makina owumitsira anafika ku Malawi. Koma zida zina zikusowabe. Popanda ndalama zakunja, kampaniyo singathe kuzigula. Mwase anati vuto lalikulu ndi ili: "Tatsala pang'ono zaka ziwiri. Kusowa kwa ndalama zakunja ndiye vuto lathu lalikulu."

Fakitale ikagwira ntchito, ipanga matani 700 a chamba chaka chilichonse. Izi zidzabweretsa $100 miliyoni ku Malawi chaka chilichonse. Europe ikufuna kugula zinthuzi. Koma pakadali pano, palibe chomwe chimachitika. Ndalama sizibwera.

Katswiri wazachuma Velli Nyirongo anati boma liyenera kuthandiza. Anati dzikolo liyenera kupereka ndalama zakunja ku mafakitale ogulitsa katundu kumayiko ena. "Akuluakulu ayenera kupereka ndalama zakunja ku mafakitale omwe amapanga ndalama ku Malawi," adatero. Anatinso boma liyenera kupangitsa bizinesi kukhala yosavuta komanso yachangu. Mabanki ayenera kuthandiza mafakitale kugula makina.

Banki Yaikulu ya Malawi inati ithandiza. Boston Maliketi imagwira ntchito kumeneko. Anati bankiyo idzafunafuna njira zothandizira mafakitale monga MMC. Koma mabizinesi ena ambiri ang'onoang'ono ali ndi vuto lomwelo. Sangagule zomwe akufuna kuchokera kumayiko ena.

en.title: Malawi football clubs welcome reserve league, want money help
en.body: Malawi's top football clubs have said yes to a new plan by the Football Association. From 2027, each big club will have its own reserve team in a new league. The clubs like the idea. But they say they need money to pay for it. The Football Association said this plan on Tuesday. It will help young players grow and learn the game better.
en.body_more:
Big Bullets CEO Albert Chigoga said the new league is good. But he said money is a big problem. Bullets have a main team, a women's team, and now a reserve team. All three teams must travel around the country to play matches. This costs a lot. Other club leaders said the same thing. They all want the Football Association to find sponsors to give money.

Civil Service United's leader Rashid Ntelera also said the new league is good. But he asked the Football Association to check the ages of reserve players. He said clubs need good sponsors to help pay for everything. A sports person named Parry Chinyama said the league can work. But first, the Football Association must find a big company to give money.

The Football Association leader Fleetwood Haiya said this reserve league will help find new talent for Malawi. Young players will move up from the reserve team to the main team. This is good for the national team too. Before the full league starts in 2027, the Football Association will run a reserve cup in 2026. This will give reserve teams matches to play. If a club goes down from the top league, its reserve team also goes down. The Football Association is now looking for sponsors to pay for the new league.

ny.title: Magulu a mpira wa ku Malawi alandila ligi yosungiramo zinthu zakale, akufuna thandizo la ndalama
ny.body: Magulu akuluakulu a mpira ku Malawi avomereza dongosolo latsopano la Football Association. Kuyambira mu 2027, kilabu iliyonse yayikulu idzakhala ndi timu yake yosungira mu ligi yatsopano. Maguluwa amakonda lingaliroli. Koma akuti akufunika ndalama kuti alipire. Bungwe la Football Association linanena dongosololi Lachiwiri. Lithandiza osewera achichepere kukula ndikuphunzira bwino masewerawa.
ny.body_more:
Mkulu wa Big Bullets, Albert Chigoga, anati ligi yatsopanoyi ndi yabwino. Koma anati ndalama ndi vuto lalikulu. Bullets ili ndi timu yayikulu, timu ya akazi, ndipo tsopano ndi timu yosungira. Magulu onse atatu ayenera kuyenda mdziko lonselo kukasewera masewera. Izi zimawononga ndalama zambiri. Atsogoleri ena a makalabu adanenanso chimodzimodzi. Onse akufuna kuti Football Association ipeze othandizira kuti apereke ndalama.

Mtsogoleri wa Civil Service United, Rashid Ntelera, adatinso ligi yatsopanoyi ndi yabwino. Koma adapempha Football Association kuti iwunike zaka za osewera osungira. Anati makalabu amafunika othandizira abwino kuti athandize kulipira chilichonse. Munthu wina wamasewera dzina lake Parry Chinyama adati ligiyo ingagwire ntchito. Koma choyamba, Football Association iyenera kupeza kampani yayikulu kuti ipereke ndalama.

Mtsogoleri wa Football Association, Fleetwood Haiya, adati ligi yosungira iyi ithandiza kupeza aluso atsopano ku Malawi. Osewera achichepere adzasamuka kuchoka ku timu yosungira kupita ku timu yayikulu. Izi ndi zabwino kwa timu yadziko lonse. Ligi yonse isanayambe mu 2027, Football Association idzayendetsa chikho chosungira mu 2026. Izi zipatsa magulu osungira masewera masewera. Ngati kilabu yatsika kuchokera ku ligi yayikulu, timu yake yosungira nayonso yatsika. Bungwe la Mpira wa Miguu tsopano likufuna othandizira kuti alipire ligi yatsopano.

en.title: Airtel Top 8 football cup starts June 13 in Lilongwe
en.body: The Airtel Top 8 football cup will start on Saturday, June 13, 2026. Two games will play at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. Blue Eagles will face Civil Service United at 14:00. After that, FCB Nyasa Big Bullets will play Ekhaya. Bullets are the holders. The Football Association of Malawi said the news on Wednesday.
en.body_more:
On Sunday, June 14, two more games will happen. Mighty Wanderers will play Creck Sporting at Zomba Stadium. Silver Strikers will play Karonga United at Silver Stadium. The top four teams have home games first.

The second games will be on July 11 and 12. Ekhaya will host Bullets. Civil will host Eagles. Creck will host Wanderers. Karonga will host Silver. The teams with the higher total score will move on to the semifinals. If the score is the same, the away goals rule will count.

The semifinals will play on August 1 and 2. The final will play on August 29. The place for the final is not yet known. The winners will get K40 million. The second place team will get K14 million. All eight teams will get K4 million each. The best player will get K2 million. The top goal scorer will get K1.35 million. Each best player of each game will get K140,000.

Big Bullets won the cup three times in a row: 2023, 2024, and 2025. Last year they beat Silver 4-3 on penalties after the game was 1-1. Big Bullets have won four cups in total. Silver have won two. Wanderers and Eagles have one each.

ny.title: Chikho cha mpira cha Airtel Top 8 chikuyamba pa 13 June ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Chikho cha mpira cha Airtel Top 8 chidzayamba Loweruka, pa 13 June, 2026. Masewera awiri adzaseweredwa ku Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Blue Eagles idzakumana ndi Civil Service United nthawi ya 14:00. Pambuyo pake, FCB Nyasa Big Bullets idzasewera ndi Ekhaya. Bullets ndiye omwe ali ndi mpikisano. Bungwe la Football Association of Malawi lanena izi Lachitatu.
ny.body_more:
Lamlungu, pa 14 Juni, masewera ena awiri adzachitika. Mighty Wanderers idzasewera ndi Creck Sporting pa Zomba Stadium. Silver Strikers idzasewera ndi Karonga United pa Silver Stadium. Magulu anayi apamwamba adzakhala ndi masewera apakhomo poyamba.

Masewera achiwiri adzakhala pa 11 ndi 12 Julayi. Ekhaya idzalandira Bullets. Civil idzalandira Eagles. Creck idzalandira Wanderers. Karonga idzalandira Silver. Magulu omwe ali ndi zigoli zambiri adzapita ku semifinals. Ngati zigolizo zili zofanana, lamulo la zigoli zakunja lidzawerengedwa.

Masemifinals adzasewera pa 1 ndi 2 Ogasiti. Final idzasewera pa 29 Ogasiti. Malo oti achite finals sakudziwikabe. Opambana adzalandira K40 miliyoni. Gulu lachiwiri lidzalandira K14 miliyoni. Magulu onse asanu ndi atatu adzalandira K4 miliyoni iliyonse. Wosewera wabwino kwambiri adzalandira K2 miliyoni. Wogoletsa zigoli wapamwamba adzalandira K1.35 miliyoni. Wosewera wabwino kwambiri pamasewera aliwonse adzalandira K140,000.

Big Bullets idapambana chikho katatu motsatizana: 2023, 2024, ndi 2025. Chaka chatha idagonjetsa Silver 4-3 pa ma penalty pambuyo poti masewerawa anali 1-1. Big Bullets yapambana makapu anayi onse. Silver yapambana awiri. Wanderers ndi Eagles ali ndi chimodzi chilichonse.

en.title: Maize farmers lose billions as government delays purchases
en.body: Maize farmers in Malawi could lose between 40 and 48 billion kwacha in sales money. The problem: the government has not yet given money to buy their crops. Two state agencies need 120 billion kwacha to buy maize from farmers. Instead, traders are buying maize at only 500 kwacha per kilogramme. The right price should be 900 kwacha per kg. Farmers are selling cheap because they need money fast.
en.body_more:
Three weeks ago, the government said it would release the money soon. But the National Food Reserve Agency and Agricultural Development and Marketing Corporation have not yet bought any maize. Each agency got 60 billion kwacha to spend. They want to buy between 100,000 and 120,000 metric tonnes of maize at the fair price of 900 kwacha per kg.

Policy expert Tamani Nkhono-Mvula said the delay is very bad. Low prices make farmers desperate. They sell because they need money for their families right now, not because they want to. This will make farmers poor and hungry in the future. Betty Chinyamunyamu, chief of the farmers group, said traders are breaking the law. They buy maize below 900 kwacha even though that is the lowest price allowed. Many farmers cannot even get back the money they spent on seeds and work. If prices stay so low, farmers may plant less maize next year, use less good seed, or stop growing maize altogether.

The head of the Food Reserve Agency, Bruce Munthali, said his group wants to buy more maize. They want to store 150,000 metric tonnes for the country, up from 82,000 now. This will help keep food cheap and safe. The grain traders group also asked the government to move fast. This year's harvest is about 3.3 million metric tonnes, which is good. But the country needs 3.5 million metric tonnes to feed all people.

ny.title: Alimi a chimanga ataya mabiliyoni ambiri chifukwa boma lachedwetsa kugula
ny.body: Alimi a chimanga ku Malawi akhoza kutaya ndalama zogulira pakati pa 40 ndi 48 biliyoni kwacha. Vuto ndilakuti boma silinapereke ndalama zogulira mbewu zawo. Mabungwe awiri aboma akufunika 120 biliyoni kwacha kuti agule chimanga kwa alimi. M'malo mwake, amalonda akugula chimanga pa 500 kwacha yokha pa kilogalamu. Mtengo woyenera uyenera kukhala 900 kwacha pa kilogalamu. Alimi akugulitsa pamtengo wotsika chifukwa amafunikira ndalama mwachangu.
ny.body_more:
Masabata atatu apitawo, boma linati lipereka ndalamazo posachedwa. Koma bungwe la National Food Reserve Agency ndi Agricultural Development and Marketing Corporation sanagule chimanga chilichonse. Bungwe lililonse linapeza ma kwacha 60 biliyoni kuti agwiritse ntchito. Akufuna kugula pakati pa matani 100,000 ndi 120,000 a chimanga pamtengo woyenera wa 900 kwacha pa kg.

Katswiri wa mfundo Tamani Nkhono-Mvula anati kuchedwaku n'koipa kwambiri. Mitengo yotsika imapangitsa alimi kukhala ndi nkhawa. Amagulitsa chifukwa akufuna ndalama zothandizira mabanja awo pakali pano, osati chifukwa choti akufuna. Izi zipangitsa alimi kukhala osauka komanso anjala mtsogolo. Betty Chinyamunyamu, mkulu wa gulu la alimi, anati amalonda akuswa lamulo. Amagula chimanga chochepera 900 kwacha ngakhale kuti mtengo wake ndi wotsika kwambiri. Alimi ambiri sangabweze ndalama zomwe adagwiritsa ntchito pa mbewu ndi ntchito. Ngati mitengo ikhala yotsika kwambiri, alimi angabzale chimanga chochepa chaka chamawa, kugwiritsa ntchito mbewu zabwino zochepa, kapena kusiya kulima chimanga konse.

Mtsogoleri wa bungwe losungira chakudya, Bruce Munthali, anati gulu lake likufuna kugula chimanga china. Akufuna kusunga matani 150,000 mdziko muno, kuchokera pa 82,000 tsopano. Izi zithandiza kuti chakudya chikhale chotsika mtengo komanso chotetezeka. Gulu la ogulitsa tirigu linapemphanso boma kuti lichitepo kanthu mwachangu. Chaka chino zokolola zili pafupifupi matani 3.3 miliyoni, zomwe ndi zabwino. Koma dzikolo likufunika matani 3.5 miliyoni kuti lidyetse anthu onse.

en.title: Malawi export plan is not working as hoped
en.body: Malawi's plan to sell more goods to other countries is failing. The plan started in 2021. It wanted exports to grow to 20 percent of the economy by 2026. But exports are getting smaller, not bigger. In 2021, Malawi sold $1.02 billion of goods. In 2025, this fell to $913.8 million. The government now says the 2026 goal will not happen.
en.body_more:
The plan had big goals. It wanted to raise exports from 14.6 percent of all the goods Malawi makes to 20 percent by 2026. But five years have passed and exports are going down, not up. In 2023, exports were at their best at $1.03 billion. Then they fell to $958.5 million in 2024 and $913.8 million in 2025. This is the lowest amount since the plan began.

The real problem is that Malawi sells the same things to the same countries. Most exports are farm goods and low-value products. Very few new things are sold. The plan itself said this was a big weakness. Malawi needs to sell more kinds of things and to more countries. Without this change, the country will not make the $6.4 billion target for 2026.

Economist Murry Siyasiya said the news is very bad. He said that when exports go down and imports go up, the country has less money. This makes problems like fuel shortages worse. He said the government must do more to help make better products and build factories. Minister Simon Itaye agreed the 2026 goal will not be reached. He said the country has many problems: poor roads, weak factories, and less aid from other countries. The government is now looking at a new plan.

Itaye said the private business sector must help more. He said companies must make and sell more goods if Malawi wants to grow. The country's long-term plan for 2063 puts export growth at the center of creating jobs and bringing in money from abroad. Without a real change in how things are made and sold, this goal will be very hard to reach.

ny.title: Dongosolo la kutumiza kunja ku Malawi silikugwira ntchito monga momwe amayembekezera
ny.body: Dongosolo la Malawi logulitsa katundu wambiri kumayiko ena likulephera. Dongosololi linayamba mu 2021. Linkafuna kuti katundu wotumizidwa kunja ukule kufika pa 20 peresenti ya chuma pofika mu 2026. Koma katundu wotumizidwa kunja akuchepa, osati kukula. Mu 2021, Malawi idagulitsa katundu wokwana $1.02 biliyoni. Mu 2025, izi zinatsika kufika pa $913.8 miliyoni. Boma tsopano likunena kuti cholinga cha 2026 sichidzachitika.
ny.body_more:
Dongosololi linali ndi zolinga zazikulu. Linkafuna kukweza katundu wochokera kunja kuchokera pa 14.6 peresenti ya katundu yense amene Malawi imapanga kufika pa 20 peresenti pofika chaka cha 2026. Koma zaka zisanu zapita ndipo katundu wochokera kunja akutsika, osati kukwera. Mu 2023, katundu wochokera kunja anali pamtengo wapamwamba kwambiri pa $1.03 biliyoni. Kenako anatsika kufika pa $958.5 miliyoni mu 2024 ndi $913.8 miliyoni mu 2025. Izi ndi ndalama zochepa kwambiri kuyambira pamene dongosololi linayamba.

Vuto lenileni ndilakuti Malawi imagulitsa zinthu zomwezo kumayiko omwewo. Zinthu zambiri zotumizidwa kunja ndi zaulimi ndi zinthu zotsika mtengo. Zinthu zatsopano zochepa kwambiri zimagulitsidwa. Dongosololi linati izi ndi zofooka zazikulu. Malawi ikufunika kugulitsa zinthu zambiri komanso kumayiko ambiri. Popanda kusintha kumeneku, dzikolo silidzafika pa cholinga cha $6.4 biliyoni cha 2026.

Katswiri wa zachuma Murry Siyasiya anati nkhaniyo ndi yoipa kwambiri. Anati katundu wochokera kunja akatsika ndipo katundu wochokera kunja akakwera, dzikolo limakhala ndi ndalama zochepa. Izi zimapangitsa mavuto monga kusowa kwa mafuta kukhala ovuta. Iye anati boma liyenera kuchita zambiri kuti lithandize kupanga zinthu zabwino ndikumanga mafakitale. Nduna Simon Itaye adavomereza kuti cholinga cha 2026 sichidzakwaniritsidwa. Iye anati dzikolo lili ndi mavuto ambiri: misewu yoipa, mafakitale ofooka, komanso thandizo lochepa kuchokera kumayiko ena. Boma tsopano likuyang'ana dongosolo latsopano.

Itaye adati makampani achinsinsi ayenera kuthandiza kwambiri. Iye adati makampani ayenera kupanga ndikugulitsa katundu wambiri ngati Malawi ikufuna kukula. Dongosolo la nthawi yayitali la dzikolo la 2063 limayika kukula kwa kutumiza kunja pakati pa kupanga ntchito ndikubweretsa ndalama kuchokera kunja. Popanda kusintha kwenikweni momwe zinthu zimapangidwira ndikugulitsidwa, cholinga ichi chidzakhala chovuta kwambiri kuchikwaniritsa.

en.title: Questions Grow Over Secret Plan To Free Wildlife Trafficker
en.body: People are asking hard questions about a plan to secretly free and send home Lin Yunhua, a Chinese man convicted of illegal wildlife trade. Lin was sent to prison for 14 years but left after only four years when the former president pardoned him. Now he is back in prison on new charges. People say he tried to pay judges and prison workers for help while in jail. Reports now say some government people want to free and deport him before his trial ends.
en.body_more:
Lin Yunhua was first arrested for buying and selling wild animals illegally. His early pardon caused anger from groups that protect wild animals. They said the pardon was wrong and made Malawi look bad to the world. When Lin left prison, police from the Anti-Corruption Bureau caught him again. They say he paid money to judges and prison staff while locked up. The court said he was a flight risk and refused to let him go free while waiting for trial.

Now secret sources tell journalists that people in the government want to quietly send Lin back to China before the corruption case finishes. One person with knowledge of the plan said: "People are working day and night to free him and deport him before the next court date. The Director of Public Prosecutions and the Ministry of Justice should know what is happening." If this is true, many people will be very angry. It would raise big questions about whether the courts are free to work without help from politicians.

When asked about this, Director of Public Prosecutions Fostino Maere said he knew nothing about a plan to free or deport Lin. He said people could ask the Ministry of Justice for more answers. Lin last came to court early this month. Two state workers told the court what they knew. The trial was stopped and set for a later date. Legal experts say sending a person away before a trial ends would break the law and the constitution.

ny.title: Mafunso Akukula Pankhani ya Dongosolo Lachinsinsi Lopezera Ogulitsa Zinyama Zakuthengo Mwaulere
ny.body: Anthu akufunsa mafunso ovuta okhudza dongosolo lomasula mwachinsinsi ndikutumiza kunyumba Lin Yunhua, mwamuna waku China yemwe adapezeka ndi mlandu wogulitsa nyama zakuthengo mosaloledwa. Lin adatumizidwa kundende kwa zaka 14 koma adachoka patatha zaka zinayi zokha pomwe purezidenti wakale adamukhululukira. Tsopano wabwerera kundende pamilandu yatsopano. Anthu akuti adayesa kulipira oweruza ndi ogwira ntchito kundende kuti amuthandize ali kundende. Malipoti tsopano akuti anthu ena aboma akufuna kumumasula ndikumuchotsa m'ndende mlandu wake usanathe.
ny.body_more:
Lin Yunhua anamangidwa koyamba chifukwa chogula ndi kugulitsa nyama zakuthengo mopanda chilolezo. Chikhululukiro chake choyambirira chinakwiyitsa magulu omwe amateteza nyama zakuthengo. Anati chikhululukirocho chinali cholakwika ndipo chinapangitsa kuti dziko la Malawi liwoneke loipa. Lin atatuluka m'ndende, apolisi ochokera ku Anti-Corruption Bureau adamugwiranso. Akuti adalipira ndalama kwa oweruza ndi ogwira ntchito kundende ali m'ndende. Khothi linati anali pachiwopsezo chothawa ndipo linakana kumumasula akuyembekezera kuzengedwa mlandu.

Magwero achinsinsi tsopano akuuza atolankhani kuti anthu m'boma akufuna kutumiza Lin ku China mwakachetechete mlandu wa ziphuphu usanathe. Munthu wina wodziwa bwino za dongosololi anati: "Anthu akugwira ntchito usana ndi usiku kuti amumasule ndikumutulutsa m'ndende asanafike tsiku lotsatira la khothi. Mtsogoleri wa Makhothi ndi Unduna wa Zachilungamo ayenera kudziwa zomwe zikuchitika." Ngati izi ndi zoona, anthu ambiri adzakwiya kwambiri. Zingabweretse mafunso akuluakulu okhudza ngati makhothi ali ndi ufulu wogwira ntchito popanda thandizo la andale.

Atafunsidwa za izi, Mtsogoleri wa Makhothi Fostino Maere adati sakudziwa chilichonse chokhudza dongosolo lomasula kapena kuthamangitsa Lin. Anati anthu akhoza kufunsa Unduna wa Zachilungamo kuti awapatse mayankho ambiri. Lin adabwera kukhothi komaliza koyambirira kwa mwezi uno. Antchito awiri aboma adauza khothi zomwe amadziwa. Mlanduwu unayimitsidwa ndipo unakonzedwa kuti uchitike tsiku lina. Akatswiri azamalamulo amati kutumiza munthu kutali mlandu usanathe kungaphwanye lamulo ndi malamulo.

en.title: Malawi Fuel Drivers Say Tanzania Trucks Get Better Work
en.body: Fuel drivers in Malawi say the government gives more work to trucks from Tanzania. Groups of Malawian drivers say this is not fair. They say Tanzania drivers make many trips and get paid in dollars. Malawian drivers wait weeks to load fuel. They get paid in kwacha. This costs the country money when Malawi needs foreign currency.
en.body_more:
Frank Banda speaks for the Transporters Association of Malawi. He said one Tanzania truck costs about $13,500 to bring fuel into Malawi. That is about 23.4 million kwacha. He said if Malawi used local drivers paid in kwacha, the money would stay in the country. Banda said they talked to Mera and Nocma about this. They have not had good answers yet.

The fight over fuel trucks is not new. From 2020 to 2024, Tanzanian trucks used a system called DDU. Local drivers said this was not fair. They did not have the same chance to work. In August 2024, the High Court of Malawi said no to DDU. It said this system was wrong.

Francis Mkandawire works for the Truck Drivers Union. He said they have talked to the government many times. They have been to many meetings. But things have not changed. Mera said in April 2026 it gave 85 percent of fuel work to Malawian drivers. But drivers say this is not what happens. Tanzanian trucks still get the most work.

Mera spokesperson Fitina Khonje said she will keep talking with the drivers. She said they will tell the news what they agree on. Nocma did not say anything about this. Banda said if the talks do not work, he will ask members what to do next.

ny.title: Oyendetsa Mafuta ku Malawi Akuti Magalimoto Oyendetsa Mafuta ku Tanzania Apeza Ntchito Yabwino
ny.body: Oyendetsa mafuta ku Malawi akuti boma lapereka ntchito yowonjezereka kwa magalimoto ochokera ku Tanzania. Magulu a oyendetsa mafuta aku Malawi akuti izi si chilungamo. Amati oyendetsa mafuta aku Tanzania amapita maulendo ambiri ndipo amalipidwa ndi ndalama. Oyendetsa mafuta aku Malawi amadikira kwa milungu ingapo kuti akweze mafuta. Amalipidwa ndi kwacha. Izi zimawononga ndalama mdziko muno pamene Malawi ikufuna ndalama zakunja.
ny.body_more:
Frank Banda amalankhula m'malo mwa Transporters Association of Malawi. Anati galimoto imodzi ya ku Tanzania imadula pafupifupi $13,500 kuti ibweretse mafuta ku Malawi. Ndalamazo ndi pafupifupi 23.4 miliyoni kwacha. Anati ngati Malawi igwiritsa ntchito madalaivala am'deralo omwe amalipidwa mu kwacha, ndalamazo zikanakhalabe mdzikolo. Banda anati adalankhula ndi Mera ndi Nocma za izi. Sanapeze mayankho abwino pakadali pano.

Mkangano wokhudza magalimoto amafuta si watsopano. Kuyambira 2020 mpaka 2024, magalimoto a ku Tanzania adagwiritsa ntchito njira yotchedwa DDU. Madalaivala am'deralo adati izi sizinali zachilungamo. Sanali ndi mwayi wofanana wogwira ntchito. Mu Ogasiti 2024, Khothi Lalikulu la Malawi linati ayi kwa DDU. Linati njira iyi ndi yolakwika.

Francis Mkandawire amagwira ntchito ku Truck Drivers Union. Anati adalankhula ndi boma kangapo. Apita ku misonkhano yambiri. Koma zinthu sizinasinthe. Mera adati mu Epulo 2026 adapereka 85 peresenti ya ntchito yamafuta kwa madalaivala aku Malawi. Koma madalaivala akuti izi sizomwe zimachitika. Magalimoto a ku Tanzania akadali ndi ntchito yambiri.

Mneneri wa Mera, Fitina Khonje, anati apitiliza kulankhula ndi madalaivala. Iye anati adzauza nkhani zomwe akugwirizana nazo. Nocma sananene chilichonse pankhaniyi. Banda anati ngati zokambiranazo sizigwira ntchito, adzafunsa mamembala zomwe achite kenako.

en.title: Senegal Prime Minister Sacked, Becomes Parliament Speaker
en.body: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye fired his Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko on May 22, 2026. Weeks later, Sonko became the new Speaker of Parliament. Sonko's PASTEF party has nearly 80% of seats in parliament. He said he will not cause problems for the government. The move shows tension between the President and his party.
en.body_more:
Sonko was one of the top leaders who brought President Faye to power in 2024. The two men wore the same suit and tie to their first cabinet meeting and looked like close allies. But months of stress built up between them. Now Faye has removed Sonko from his job as Prime Minister.

Sonko's new job as Speaker of Parliament is powerful because his party holds most seats in the government. This gives him big control over laws and checks on the President. Some say this move balances power inside the party during a hard time for Senegal.

Sonko said publicly that he will not use his new job to cause chaos or break the government. His words are meant to calm people and show that Senegal is still stable. The world is watching to see if the two men can work together or if more problems will come.

This story is like what has happened in other African countries. In Kenya, President Uhuru Kenyatta and his Deputy William Ruto were close allies but then fought badly. In Malawi, President Lazarus Chakwera and Vice President Saulos Chilima also had a big split. Political teams often start strong after winning but then break apart when leaders want more power for themselves.

ny.title: Nduna Yaikulu ya Senegal Yachotsedwa Ntchito, Yakhala Sipikala wa Nyumba Yamalamulo
ny.body: Purezidenti wa Senegal Bassirou Diomaye Faye adachotsa Prime Minister wake Ousmane Sonko pa Meyi 22, 2026. Patatha milungu ingapo, Sonko adakhala Sipikala watsopano wa Nyumba Yamalamulo. Chipani cha Sonko cha PASTEF chili ndi mipando pafupifupi 80% mu nyumba yamalamulo. Iye adati sadzayambitsa mavuto ku boma. Izi zikusonyeza kusamvana pakati pa Purezidenti ndi chipani chake.
ny.body_more:
Sonko anali m'modzi mwa atsogoleri akuluakulu omwe adabweretsa Purezidenti Faye ku ulamuliro mu 2024. Amuna awiriwa adavala suti ndi tayi yomweyo pamsonkhano wawo woyamba wa nduna ndipo ankaoneka ngati mabwenzi apamtima. Koma miyezi yambiri ya nkhawa idakula pakati pawo. Tsopano Faye wachotsa Sonko paudindo wake monga Prime Minister.

Ntchito yatsopano ya Sonko monga Sipika wa Nyumba Yamalamulo ndi yamphamvu chifukwa chipani chake chili ndi mipando yambiri m'boma. Izi zimamupatsa ulamuliro waukulu pa malamulo ndikuwunika Purezidenti. Ena amati izi zikugwirizana ndi mphamvu mkati mwa chipani panthawi yovuta ku Senegal.

Sonko adanena poyera kuti sadzagwiritsa ntchito ntchito yake yatsopano kuyambitsa chisokonezo kapena kuswa boma. Mawu ake cholinga chake ndi kutonthoza anthu ndikuwonetsa kuti Senegal ikadali yokhazikika. Dziko lapansi likuyang'ana kuti lione ngati amuna awiriwa angagwire ntchito limodzi kapena ngati mavuto ena angabwere.

Nkhaniyi ili ngati zomwe zachitika m'maiko ena aku Africa. Ku Kenya, Purezidenti Uhuru Kenyatta ndi Wachiwiri wake William Ruto anali mabwenzi apamtima koma kenako adamenyana moyipa. Ku Malawi, Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera ndi Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima nawonso adagawanika kwambiri. Magulu andale nthawi zambiri amayamba mwamphamvu akapambana koma kenako amasweka atsogoleri akamafuna mphamvu zambiri.

en.title: Court cuts coach's pay claim from 916 million to 53 million
en.body: A court in Malawi has cut former football coach Patrick Mabedi's money demand from MK916 million to MK53.7 million. The Industrial Relations Court said Mabedi was fired in an unfair way. The Football Association of Malawi must pay him the money in seven days. Mabedi was the coach of the Malawi national team. He was fired in October 2024 after just one year of work.
en.body_more:
Mabedi started his job as coach in November 2023. He signed a two-year deal with the football body. But in October 2024, he was fired because of poor game results. The court said the firing was wrong. It said Mabedi was never told what he did wrong. He was never called to a meeting to talk about it. He was never asked to say his side of the story before he lost his job.

The court found two big problems with how the football body acted. It said the firing was unfair. It also said the football body broke the work rules. The judge worked out what Mabedi should get. His pay was MK8.4 million each month. He also got fuel — 350 litres each month. When the court added this up, his pay was MK10.7 million a month. The court gave him three months of pay for the unfair firing. This was MK32.2 million. The court also gave him MK21.4 million for breaking the work rules.

Mabedi had asked for much more money. He wanted MK917 million. He asked for a new car, plane tickets, a bonus, and other things. The court said no to almost all of this. The judge said many of these things were not real or were already paid in other ways. The football body now has seven days to pay the money. Both sides can ask a higher court to look at this choice again. They have 30 days to do this.

ny.title: Khothi lachepetsa malipiro a mphunzitsi kuchoka pa 916 miliyoni kufika pa 53 miliyoni
ny.body: Khothi ku Malawi lachepetsa ndalama zomwe mphunzitsi wakale wa mpira Patrick Mabedi amafuna kuchokera pa MK916 miliyoni kufika pa MK53.7 miliyoni. Khothi la Industrial Relations linati Mabedi adachotsedwa ntchito mopanda chilungamo. Bungwe la Football Association of Malawi liyenera kumulipira ndalamazo m'masiku asanu ndi awiri. Mabedi anali mphunzitsi wa timu ya dziko la Malawi. Anachotsedwa ntchito mu Okutobala 2024 atangogwira ntchito chaka chimodzi chokha.
ny.body_more:
Mabedi anayamba ntchito yake monga mphunzitsi mu Novembala 2023. Anasaina mgwirizano wa zaka ziwiri ndi bungwe la mpira. Koma mu Okutobala 2024, anachotsedwa ntchito chifukwa cha zotsatira zoipa pamasewera. Khothi linati kuchotsedwa ntchito kunali kolakwika. Linati Mabedi sanauzidwepo zomwe adachita molakwika. Sanayitanidwe pamsonkhano kuti akambirane za izi. Sanafunsidwepo kuti anene mbali yake asanataye ntchito yake.

Khothi linapeza mavuto awiri akuluakulu ndi momwe bungwe la mpira linkachitira zinthu. Linati kuchotsedwa ntchito kunali kosayenera. Linatinso bungwe la mpira linaswa malamulo a ntchito. Woweruza milandu adaganiza zomwe Mabedi ayenera kupeza. Malipiro ake anali MK8.4 miliyoni mwezi uliwonse. Amalandiranso mafuta - malita 350 mwezi uliwonse. Khothi litawonjezera izi, malipiro ake anali MK10.7 miliyoni pamwezi. Khothi linamupatsa malipiro a miyezi itatu chifukwa chochotsedwa ntchito mosayenera. Izi zinali MK32.2 miliyoni. Khothi linamupatsanso MK21.4 miliyoni chifukwa choswa malamulo a ntchito.

Mabedi adapempha ndalama zambiri. Iye ankafuna MK917 miliyoni. Anapempha galimoto yatsopano, matikiti a ndege, bonasi, ndi zina. Khotilo linakana pafupifupi zonsezi. Woweruzayo anati zambiri mwa izi sizinali zenizeni kapena zinali zitalipidwa kale m'njira zina. Bungwe la mpira tsopano lili ndi masiku asanu ndi awiri oti lilipire ndalamazo. Magulu onse awiri akhoza kupempha khoti lalikulu kuti liwonenso chisankhochi. Ali ndi masiku 30 oti achite izi.

en.title: MAGLA gives K40 million to help FDH Bank Premiership awards
en.body: The Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority (MAGLA) has given K40 million to help pay for monthly awards in the FDH Bank Premiership. The money will reward the best goal and best save each month during the 2026-27 season. MAGLA and the Super League of Malawi (SULOM) signed an agreement on Monday in Blantyre. The plan will make the league stronger and give players more reason to play well.
en.body_more:
The prize for the goal of the month and the save of the month is K1 million each. The rest of the money will help pay for the work that runs these awards. MAGLA Director General Muchanakhwaye Mpuluka said the money helps football grow in Malawi. He also said the awards will help MAGLA tell people about the right way to use games of chance.

Mpuluka said football reaches many people in the country. This makes it a good way to share messages about safe gaming. He said the plan does two things at once: it helps find the best players and it helps teach the public.

SULOM Vice President Ronald Chiwaula was happy with the deal. He said the awards will push players to do better. Chiwaula also said thank you to FDH Bank. FDH Bank is the main sponsor of the league and agreed to let SULOM bring in MAGLA as a new partner. The FDH Bank Premiership is the top football league in Malawi and many people watch it.

ny.title: MAGLA yapereka K40 miliyoni kuti ithandize mphoto za FDH Bank Premiership
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority (MAGLA) lapereka K40 miliyoni kuti lithandizire kulipira mphoto za mwezi uliwonse mu FDH Bank Premiership. Ndalamazi zipereka mphoto kwa chigoli chabwino kwambiri komanso ndalama zabwino kwambiri mwezi uliwonse mu nyengo ya 2026-27. MAGLA ndi Super League of Malawi (SULOM) adasaina pangano Lolemba ku Blantyre. Dongosololi lipangitsa ligi kukhala yolimba ndikupatsa osewera zifukwa zambiri zosewerera bwino.
ny.body_more:
Mphoto ya cholinga cha mwezi ndi ndalama zomwe mweziwo wapulumutsidwa ndi K1 miliyoni iliyonse. Ndalama zotsalazo zithandiza kulipira ntchito yomwe ikuyendetsa mphotozi. Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa MAGLA Muchanakhwaye Mpuluka adati ndalamazo zimathandiza kuti mpira ukule ku Malawi. Anatinso mphotozi zithandiza MAGLA kuuza anthu za njira yoyenera yogwiritsira ntchito masewera amwayi.

Mpuluka adati mpira umafikira anthu ambiri mdziko muno. Izi zimapangitsa kuti ikhale njira yabwino yogawana mauthenga okhudza masewera otetezeka. Anati dongosololi limachita zinthu ziwiri nthawi imodzi: limathandiza kupeza osewera abwino kwambiri ndipo limathandiza kuphunzitsa anthu.

Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wa SULOM Ronald Chiwaula adakondwera ndi mgwirizanowu. Anati mphotozi zilimbikitsa osewera kuti achite bwino. Chiwaula adatinso zikomo ku FDH Bank. FDH Bank ndiye wothandizira wamkulu wa ligi ndipo adavomereza kuti SULOM ibweretse MAGLA ngati mnzake watsopano. FDH Bank Premiership ndiye ligi yapamwamba kwambiri ya mpira ku Malawi ndipo anthu ambiri amaionera.

en.title: MPICO gives money to architects' big meeting
en.body: MPICO, a big company in Malawi, gave six million five hundred thousand kwacha to help pay for a big meeting. The Malawi Institute of Architects will hold this meeting in Blantyre from May 27 to 29, 2026. MPICO said it gave the money because architects are very important for building and making the country grow. The money was given on Monday, May 25, in Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
Stella Sokosa is the boss of MPICO. She said that architects help to make buildings and cities. She said MPICO works with architects every day, so the company wanted to help them. She said the building work in Malawi is hard now because money is short and fuel costs a lot. But she said when companies like MPICO build new things, it shows people that Malawi will be okay.

Dorothy Nyong'onya is the deputy boss of the Malawi Institute of Architects. She said the money from MPICO came at a very good time. She said the meeting will talk about how to build strong buildings when the country has money problems. She said over 120 architects will come to this meeting. They will talk about how to make buildings that are good and will last a long time, even when money is hard to find.

Nyong'onya said MPICO is a good partner because the company builds things in Malawi. She said MPICO's building work will be used as an example at the meeting. She said architects will learn from what MPICO does. The big meeting will use a name: "Architecture at the Crossroads: Resilient Construction in Volatile Economic Environments." This name means that building in Malawi right now is hard but architects can find ways to make it work.

ny.title: MPICO yapereka ndalama ku msonkhano waukulu wa akatswiri omanga nyumba
ny.body: MPICO, kampani yayikulu ku Malawi, yapereka ndalama zokwana makwacha mamiliyoni asanu ndi limodzi mphambu mazana asanu kuti ithandize kulipira msonkhano waukulu. Bungwe la Malawi Institute of Architects lidzachita msonkhanowu ku Blantyre kuyambira pa 27 mpaka 29 Meyi, 2026. MPICO yati yapereka ndalamazo chifukwa akatswiri omanga nyumba ndi ofunikira kwambiri pakumanga ndikukula kwa dzikolo. Ndalamazo zinaperekedwa Lolemba, pa 25 Meyi, ku Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Stella Sokosa ndi mkulu wa MPICO. Anati akatswiri omanga nyumba amathandiza kupanga nyumba ndi mizinda. Anati MPICO imagwira ntchito ndi akatswiri omanga nyumba tsiku lililonse, kotero kampaniyo ikufuna kuwathandiza. Anati ntchito yomanga ku Malawi ndi yovuta tsopano chifukwa ndalama ndi zochepa ndipo mafuta amawononga ndalama zambiri. Koma anati makampani ngati MPICO akamanga zinthu zatsopano, zimawonetsa anthu kuti Malawi idzakhala bwino.

Dorothy Nyong'onya ndi wachiwiri kwa mkulu wa Malawi Institute of Architects. Anati ndalama zochokera ku MPICO zabwera panthawi yabwino kwambiri. Anati msonkhanowu udzakambirana za momwe tingamangire nyumba zolimba pamene dzikolo lili ndi mavuto azachuma. Anati akatswiri omanga nyumba oposa 120 adzabwera pamsonkhanowu. Adzakambirana za momwe angapangire nyumba zabwino komanso zomwe zidzakhalapo nthawi yayitali, ngakhale ndalama zitavuta kupeza.

Nyong'onya anati MPICO ndi mnzake wabwino chifukwa kampaniyo imamanga zinthu ku Malawi. Anati ntchito yomanga ya MPICO idzagwiritsidwa ntchito ngati chitsanzo pamsonkhanowu. Anati akatswiri omanga nyumba adzaphunzira kuchokera ku zomwe MPICO imachita. Msonkhano waukuluwu udzagwiritsa ntchito dzina lakuti: "Zomangamanga pa Crossroads: Kumanga Kolimba M'malo Osakhazikika Azachuma." Dzinali limatanthauza kuti kumanga ku Malawi pakadali pano n'kovuta koma akatswiri omanga nyumba angapeze njira zogwirira ntchito.

en.title: Court Orders Lawyer Kamangila to Pay Judge Manda Damages
en.body: A High Court judge has ruled that lawyer Alexious Kamangila must pay money to Judge Kenan Manda. Judge Howard Pemba said Kamangila did not follow court rules. Manda had asked the court for help in March 2025. Kamangila did not send papers that the court asked for. Now the court will decide how much money Kamangila must pay.
en.body_more:
Judge Manda sent a letter to Kamangila on 7 October 2024. In that letter, Manda asked for 250 million kwacha in damages. He also asked Kamangila to say sorry in public and to stop making bad statements about him. Kamangila did not follow the court's directions. The court said Kamangila had to send witness statements and other papers within 21 days. He did not do this.

Judge Pemba said the case could go ahead without Kamangila being there first. The court has now told a court officer, called the Registrar, to decide how much money Kamangila must give to Manda. This meeting has not yet been set.

The court also gave an order to stop Kamangila from publishing the same words or words like them. Kamangila must delete the posts within 24 hours of getting the court's order. Kamangila has said he does not agree with what the court decided. The case started because of statements Kamangila made in October 2024.

ny.title: Khoti Lalamula Loya Kamangila Kuti Alipire Chiwongola dzanja cha Woweruza Manda
ny.body: Woweruza milandu wa Khothi Lalikulu wagamula kuti loya Alexious Kamangila ayenera kulipira ndalama kwa Woweruza Kenan Manda. Woweruza milandu Howard Pemba anati Kamangila sanatsatire malamulo a khothi. Manda anapempha thandizo ku khothi mu Marichi 2025. Kamangila sanatumize mapepala omwe khothi linapempha. Tsopano khothi lidzasankha ndalama zomwe Kamangila ayenera kulipira.
ny.body_more:
Woweruza Manda adatumiza kalata kwa Kamangila pa 7 Okutobala 2024. M'kalatayo, Manda adapempha chiwongola dzanja cha 250 miliyoni kwacha. Adapemphanso Kamangila kuti apepese pagulu ndikusiya kunena mawu oipa okhudza iye. Kamangila sanatsatire malangizo a khothi. Khothi linati Kamangila ayenera kutumiza mawu a mboni ndi mapepala ena mkati mwa masiku 21. Sanachite izi.

Woweruza Pemba adati mlanduwu ukhoza kupitilira popanda Kamangila kukhalapo kaye. Khothi tsopano lauza mkulu wa khothi, yemwe adayimbira Registrar, kuti asankhe ndalama zomwe Kamangila ayenera kupereka kwa Manda. Msonkhanowu sunakhazikitsidwebe.

Khothi linaperekanso lamulo loletsa Kamangila kufalitsa mawu kapena mawu ofanana nawo. Kamangila ayenera kuchotsa zolembazo mkati mwa maola 24 kuchokera pamene khothi lalandira lamulo. Kamangila wanena kuti sakugwirizana ndi zomwe khothi linagamula. Mlanduwu unayamba chifukwa cha mawu omwe Kamangila adapereka mu Okutobala 2024.

en.title: 10.8 Million Malawians Live in Deep Poverty, UN Report Shows
en.body: A new United Nations report says 55.9 percent of Malawians are very poor. This means about 10.8 million people do not have enough food, clean water, electricity, schools or doctors. The report is called "A Matter of Choice: People and Possibilities in the Age of AI." It measures poverty in a new way. It does not just look at money. It looks at ten things: food, water, toilets, power, houses, schools and more.
en.body_more:
The report uses a score called the Global Multidimensional Poverty Index. Malawi's score is 0.231. This score comes from surveys done in 2019 and 2020. A person is counted as very poor if they lack one-third of these ten things at the same time. For most Malawian families, this means children go to school hungry. Homes have no electricity or clean water. People cannot easily get to doctors.

The report also says that poor people with little school do not use the internet. This is a big problem now. Digital tools are used for trade, jobs and money services all over the world. Many Malawians cannot use these tools. This leaves them out of new ways to earn money. Malawi's income per person is only 1,634 US dollars. This is very low. A workforce that is sick, uneducated and without basic services cannot help the economy grow.

Malawi ranks 172nd out of 193 countries on the Human Development Index. This shows how hard life is for most people. Economist Marvin Banda said the economy cannot grow when people are hungry and uneducated. "We cannot celebrate small growth when most people live without basic needs," he said. Francis Thauzeni from the Malawi School of Government said these facts must make leaders change their plans for health, schools and services. Without big changes, Malawi will not reach its goal to become a strong, modern country by 2063.

ny.title: Lipoti la UN lasonyeza kuti anthu 10.8 miliyoni aku Malawi ali mu umphawi waukulu.
ny.body: Lipoti latsopano la United Nations likuti 55.9 peresenti ya anthu aku Malawi ndi osauka kwambiri. Izi zikutanthauza kuti anthu pafupifupi 10.8 miliyoni alibe chakudya chokwanira, madzi oyera, magetsi, masukulu kapena madokotala. Lipotilo limatchedwa "Nkhani Yosankha: Anthu ndi Mwayi mu Nthawi ya AI." Limayesa umphawi mwanjira yatsopano. Silingoyang'ana ndalama zokha. Limayang'ana zinthu khumi: chakudya, madzi, zimbudzi, magetsi, nyumba, masukulu ndi zina zambiri.
ny.body_more:
Lipotilo likugwiritsa ntchito chigoli chotchedwa Global Multidimensional Poverty Index. Chigoli cha Malawi ndi 0.231. Chigoli ichi chikuchokera ku kafukufuku wochitidwa mu 2019 ndi 2020. Munthu amawerengedwa kuti ndi wosauka kwambiri ngati alibe gawo limodzi mwa magawo atatu a zinthu khumi izi nthawi imodzi. Kwa mabanja ambiri aku Malawi, izi zikutanthauza kuti ana amapita kusukulu ali ndi njala. Nyumba zilibe magetsi kapena madzi oyera. Anthu sangathe kufika kwa madokotala mosavuta.

Lipotilo likunenanso kuti anthu osauka omwe ali ndi sukulu yochepa sagwiritsa ntchito intaneti. Vuto lalikulu tsopano. Zida zamagetsi zimagwiritsidwa ntchito pa malonda, ntchito ndi ntchito zandalama padziko lonse lapansi. Anthu ambiri aku Malawi sangagwiritse ntchito zidazi. Izi zimawasiya opanda njira zatsopano zopezera ndalama. Ndalama zomwe Malawi amapeza pa munthu aliyense ndi madola 1,634 aku US okha. Izi ndi zochepa kwambiri. Anthu ogwira ntchito omwe ndi odwala, osaphunzira komanso opanda ntchito zoyambira sangathandize chuma kukula.

Malawi ali pa nambala 172 mwa mayiko 193 omwe ali pa Human Development Index. Izi zikusonyeza momwe moyo ulili wovuta kwa anthu ambiri. Katswiri wazachuma Marvin Banda adati chuma sichingakule pamene anthu ali ndi njala komanso osaphunzira. "Sitingakondwerere kukula pang'ono pamene anthu ambiri amakhala opanda zosowa zofunika," adatero. Francis Thauzeni wochokera ku Malawi School of Government adati mfundo izi ziyenera kupangitsa atsogoleri kusintha mapulani awo azaumoyo, masukulu ndi mautumiki. Popanda kusintha kwakukulu, Malawi sidzafika pacholinga chake chokhala dziko lolimba komanso lamakono pofika chaka cha 2063.

en.title: More students can get government loans starting today
en.body: The government will give student loans to 38,000 young people in the 2026–27 school year. This is 9% more than last year, when 34,863 students got loans. Applications open today, May 25, and close on June 24. For the first time, students who study online or by post (called ODeL students) can also apply for loans. The loans board says the money is for students who need help and are good at school.
en.body_more:
ODeL stands for Open, Distance and e-Learning. These students take classes by post, online, or a mix of both. Before now, only students in regular classrooms could get government loans. Many ODeL students had to stop school because they did not have money. Now they can apply under the same rules as other students.

Vitumbiko Mzumara is a student leader at Mzuzu University. He said many young people come from secondary school and choose ODeL study. Some do this because the government tells them to. But many stop their studies because they have no money. He said the loan will now help these students finish their degree. He also said ODeL students are just like other university students. They do the same subjects. The only difference is how they learn.

Education expert Lexon Ndalama said giving loans to ODeL students is good. It helps more people go to university. He said it will not make the school work worse if the university checks that teachers are good and students do their work.

Benedicto Kondowe works to help education in Malawi. He said the 9% rise is good, but many more students need loans. He asked old students who got loans to pay them back. This will free up money for new students. He also said the government must check that only real students get the loans.

ny.title: Ophunzira ambiri akhoza kulandira ngongole za boma kuyambira lero
ny.body: Boma lipereka ngongole za ophunzira kwa achinyamata 38,000 m'chaka cha sukulu cha 2026-27. Izi ndi 9% kuposa chaka chatha, pomwe ophunzira 34,863 adalandira ngongole. Mafomu ofunsira atsegulidwa lero, Meyi 25, ndipo atsekedwa pa Juni 24. Kwa nthawi yoyamba, ophunzira omwe amaphunzira pa intaneti kapena positi (otchedwa ophunzira a ODeL) nawonso akhoza kulembetsa ngongole. Bungwe la ngongole limati ndalamazo ndi za ophunzira omwe akufunika thandizo ndipo ndi abwino kusukulu.
ny.body_more:
ODeL imayimira Open, Distance ndi e-Learning. Ophunzira awa amaphunzira kudzera pa positi, pa intaneti, kapena kuphatikiza zonse ziwiri. Kale, ophunzira okha omwe ali m'makalasi wamba ndi omwe ankalandira ngongole za boma. Ophunzira ambiri a ODeL anayenera kusiya sukulu chifukwa analibe ndalama. Tsopano akhoza kulembetsa motsatira malamulo omwewo monga ophunzira ena.

Vitumbiko Mzumara ndi mtsogoleri wa ophunzira ku Mzuzu University. Anati achinyamata ambiri amachokera ku sekondale ndipo amasankha kuphunzira za ODeL. Ena amachita izi chifukwa boma limawauza kuti atero. Koma ambiri amasiya maphunziro awo chifukwa alibe ndalama. Anati ngongoleyo tsopano ithandiza ophunzira awa kumaliza digiri yawo. Anatinso ophunzira a ODeL ali ngati ophunzira ena aku yunivesite. Amachita maphunziro omwewo. Kusiyana kokha ndi momwe amaphunzirira.

Katswiri wa maphunziro Lexon Ndalama anati kupereka ngongole kwa ophunzira a ODeL ndi kwabwino. Kumathandiza anthu ambiri kupita ku yunivesite. Anati sizingapangitse kuti sukulu igwire ntchito moyipa ngati yunivesite ikafufuza kuti aphunzitsi ndi abwino ndipo ophunzira agwira ntchito yawo.

Benedicto Kondowe amagwira ntchito yothandizira maphunziro ku Malawi. Iye anati kukwera kwa 9% ndi kwabwino, koma ophunzira ambiri akufunika ngongole. Anapempha ophunzira akale omwe adalandira ngongole kuti azibweze. Izi zithandiza ophunzira atsopano kupeza ndalama. Anatinso boma liyenera kuwonetsetsa kuti ophunzira enieni okha ndi omwe amalandira ngongolezo.

en.title: Union Says New Wages Are Not Enough for Workers
en.body: Malawi's government announced new minimum wages that will start on June 1, 2026. But the Civil Servants Trade Union says the pay rises are too small. Union leader Lameck Magawa said the new wages do not match the high cost of living. Domestic workers will earn K83,720 per month, up from K72,800. Commercial workers will get K157,500, up from K126,000. Magawa said even the highest wage of K400,000 for truck drivers is not enough.
en.body_more:
Magawa was very clear about his anger. He called the commercial workers' raise of K157,500 "just an assault." He said it does not help workers who are struggling to buy food, pay rent, and pay for school. The government thinks the new wages are good, but workers say they will lose the money to high prices very fast.

Magawa said the government made a mistake. He said they should have cut taxes for employers and workers first. Then they could have given the new wages. He said "The best thing government could have done was to reduce taxes on employers and revise Pay As You Earn for employees before coming up with these new rates."

Workers in Malawi have big problems right now. Food prices are very high. Transport costs a lot of money. The money is worth less and less each month. Many workers have jobs but still cannot pay for basic things. Even when wages go up, prices go up more. This is called inflation. Many workers across the country feel they earn more money on paper but are actually poorer than before.

Labour leaders have asked the government for years to link wages to real living costs. They say thousands of workers in Malawi work full time but still cannot meet basic needs.

ny.title: Bungwe Lalikulu Lanena Kuti Malipiro Atsopano Sali Okwanira kwa Ogwira Ntchito
ny.body: Boma la Malawi lalengeza malipiro atsopano ochepera omwe adzayamba pa June 1, 2026. Koma bungwe la Civil Servants Trade Union likuti kukwezedwa kwa malipiro ndi kochepa kwambiri. Mtsogoleri wa bungweli Lameck Magawa adati malipiro atsopanowa sakugwirizana ndi kukwera mtengo kwa zinthu pa moyo. Ogwira ntchito zapakhomo azilandira K83,720 pamwezi, kuchokera pa K72,800. Ogwira ntchito zamalonda azilandira K157,500, kuchokera pa K126,000. Magawa adati ngakhale malipiro apamwamba kwambiri a K400,000 kwa oyendetsa magalimoto akuluakulu sikokwanira.
ny.body_more:
Magawa analankhula momveka bwino za mkwiyo wake. Anati kukweza kwa ogwira ntchito zamalonda kwa K157,500 "ndi kuukira chabe." Anati sikuthandiza antchito omwe akuvutika kugula chakudya, kulipira lendi, ndi kulipira sukulu. Boma likuganiza kuti malipiro atsopano ndi abwino, koma ogwira ntchito akunena kuti adzataya ndalamazo chifukwa cha mitengo yokwera mwachangu kwambiri.

Magawa anati boma lalakwitsa. Anati liyenera kuchepetsa misonkho kwa olemba ntchito ndi antchito kaye. Kenako akanatha kupereka malipiro atsopano. Anati "Chinthu chabwino chomwe boma likanachita chinali kuchepetsa misonkho kwa olemba ntchito ndikusintha Malipiro Monga Mumalandira kwa antchito asanabwere ndi mitengo yatsopanoyi."

Ogwira ntchito ku Malawi ali ndi mavuto akulu pakali pano. Mitengo ya chakudya ndi yokwera kwambiri. Mayendedwe amawononga ndalama zambiri. Ndalamazo ndizochepa mwezi uliwonse. Ogwira ntchito ambiri ali ndi ntchito koma sangathe kulipira zinthu zofunika. Ngakhale malipiro akakwera, mitengo imakwera kwambiri. Izi zimatchedwa kukwera kwa mitengo. Ogwira ntchito ambiri mdziko lonselo amaona kuti amapeza ndalama zambiri papepala koma kwenikweni ndi osauka kuposa kale.

Atsogoleri a Labour apempha boma kwa zaka zambiri kuti ligwirizanitse malipiro ndi ndalama zenizeni zogulira zinthu. Amati antchito zikwizikwi ku Malawi amagwira ntchito nthawi zonse koma sangakwanitsebe zosowa zawo zofunika.

en.title: UTM Party Does Not Invite MCP to Chilima Memorial
en.body: The UTM Party will not ask the MCP to attend a memorial service for former Vice-President Saulos Chilima on June 13, 2026. UTM spokesperson Felix Njawala said all other political parties are invited, but not MCP. Chilima died in a military plane crash in June 2024. The memorial will be at Ntcheu Stadium, about 15 kilometres from where Chilima was buried.
en.body_more:
Njawala said UTM wants to avoid trouble at the event. He claimed that MCP members were booed at a similar memorial last year, so organizers do not want that to happen again. However, he did not say which MCP members were booed or give more details.

MCP publicity secretary Jessie Kabwila said this was not true. She said she went to last year's memorial and nothing bad happened. She said MCP would have wanted to be at the new memorial to honour Chilima, who was Vice-President when MCP led the government. Kabwila said if UTM does not want them there, that is UTM's choice.

Bad feelings between the two parties started after the plane crash in June 2024. Some UTM people have said they think foul play happened, but two official inquiries found the crash was an accident. In 2025, on the first anniversary, President Lazarus Chakwera held one memorial at Lunjika Turn-off near the crash site, and Chilima's family held another one in Nsipe. UTM then organized a candlelight vigil that was attended by other opposition parties like the Democratic Progressive Party and United Democratic Front, but not MCP.

Political analyst Chimwemwe Tsitsi said it is bad for a political party to organize a national memorial and leave out other political groups. He said a memorial for a former Vice-President should not exclude people based on what party they belong to.

ny.title: Chipani cha UTM Sichikuitana MCP ku Chikumbutso cha Chilima
ny.body: Chipani cha UTM sichidzapempha chipani cha MCP kuti chikhale nawo pa mwambo wokumbukira wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima pa June 13, 2026. Mneneri wa UTM Felix Njawala adati zipani zina zonse zandale ndizo ziyitanidwa, koma osati MCP. Chilima anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndege ya asilikali mu June 2024. Chikumbutsochi chidzakhala ku Ntcheu Stadium, pafupifupi makilomita 15 kuchokera komwe Chilima adayikidwa.
ny.body_more:
Njawala anati UTM ikufuna kupewa mavuto pamwambowu. Iye anati mamembala a MCP ananyozedwa pamwambo wofanana ndi umenewu chaka chatha, kotero okonza sakufuna kuti zimenezo zichitikenso. Komabe, sananene kuti ndi mamembala ati a MCP omwe ananyozedwa kapena kupereka zambiri.

Mlembi wa MCP, Jessie Kabwila, anati izi si zoona. Iye anati anapita kumwambo wokumbukira chaka chatha ndipo palibe choipa chomwe chinachitika. Iye anati MCP ikanafuna kukhala pamwambo watsopano wokumbukira Chilima, yemwe anali Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti pamene MCP inkatsogolera boma. Kabwila anati ngati UTM siikufuna kuti apiteko, ndicho chisankho cha UTM.

Mkangano pakati pa magulu awiriwa unayamba pambuyo pa ngozi ya ndege mu June 2024. Anthu ena a UTM anena kuti akuganiza kuti kuchita zinthu molakwika kunachitika, koma kafukufuku awiri ovomerezeka adapeza kuti ngoziyi inali ngozi. Mu 2025, pa chikondwerero choyamba, Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera adachita chikumbutso chimodzi ku Lunjika Turn-off pafupi ndi malo omwe ngoziyo inachitikira, ndipo banja la Chilima linachita china ku Nsipe. Kenako UTM inakonza mwambo wowunikira makandulo womwe unachitikira ndi zipani zina zotsutsa monga Democratic Progressive Party ndi United Democratic Front, koma osati MCP.

Katswiri wa ndale Chimwemwe Tsitsi anati n’koipa kuti chipani cha ndale chikonze chikumbutso cha dziko lonse ndikusiya magulu ena andale. Iye anati chikumbutso cha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wakale sichiyenera kuletsa anthu kutengera chipani chomwe ali.

en.title: Masters win big, Silver draw with Wanderers
en.body: Masters FC moved to the top of the FDH Bank Premiership last night with a 4-0 win over Goshen City Dedza Dynamos. The match took place at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. Hastings Ndau scored three goals for Masters. At Zomba Stadium, Silver Strikers drew 0-0 with Mighty Wanderers and now sit in second place with 10 points.
en.body_more:
Ndau opened the score in the 21st minute for Masters, and Enock Kaonga doubled the lead nine minutes later. The first half ended 2-0. Ndau added a third goal in the 54th minute and completed his hat-trick in the 81st minute. Masters coach Peter Mponda said his team played very well. "I am happy with how my charges have played today. They played according to plan," he said. This was Masters' fourth win in five matches, giving them 12 points at the top of the table.

Silver Strikers had the chance to move top with a win at Zomba, but the goalless draw left them in second place on 10 points, the same as third-placed Blue Eagles. However, Silver have a better goal difference than Blue Eagles. Silver played well in the second half but could not score past Wanderers goalkeeper Dalitso Khungwa. Silver assistant coach Mapopa Msukwa said the draw was acceptable. "We dominated in the second half and we were unlucky not to get a goal. All the same, most of our fixtures against Wanderers end in stalemates and plucking a point from this match is not bad," he said.

Wanderers climbed two places to seventh with their point. Coach Bob Mpinganjira said his team struggled in the middle of the field. "It was a difficult game. We didn't play our game, especially in the midfield. We lost a lot of balls and it was difficult to play our game," he said. In other matches, Mafco FC beat Chitipa United 2-0 at Champion Stadium in Mponela, with Clifford Msowoya scoring twice. Karonga United won 1-0 against Civil Service United at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe, with Godfrey Suwale scoring the only goal.

ny.title: Masters apambana mpikisano waukulu, Silver draw ndi Wanderers
ny.body: Masters FC yakwera pamwamba pa FDH Bank Premiership usiku watha ndi chigonjetso cha 4-0 motsutsana ndi Goshen City Dedza Dynamos. Masewerowa adachitikira ku Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Hastings Ndau adagoletsa zigoli zitatu za Masters. Pa Zomba Stadium, Silver Strikers adagoletsa 0-0 ndi Mighty Wanderers ndipo tsopano ali pa nambala yachiwiri ndi mapointi 10.
ny.body_more:
Ndau ndiye anayambitsa chigoli mu mphindi ya 21 kwa Masters, ndipo Enock Kaonga anawonjezera chigoli chachiwiri patatha mphindi zisanu ndi zinayi. Gawo loyamba linatha 2-0. Ndau anawonjezera chigoli chachitatu mu mphindi ya 54 ndipo anamaliza hat-trick yake mu mphindi ya 81. Mphunzitsi wa Masters Peter Mponda anati timu yake idasewera bwino kwambiri. "Ndikusangalala ndi momwe osewera anga asewera lero. Adasewera motsatira dongosolo," adatero. Uku kunali kupambana kwachinayi kwa Masters mu masewera asanu, kuwapatsa mapointi 12 pamwamba pa tebulo.

Silver Strikers inali ndi mwayi wopita pamwamba ndi chigonjetso ku Zomba, koma chigoli chopanda zigoli chinawasiya pamalo achiwiri ndi mapointi 10, mofanana ndi Blue Eagles yomwe ili pamalo achitatu. Komabe, Silver ili ndi kusiyana kwa zigoli kuposa Blue Eagles. Silver idasewera bwino mu theka lachiwiri koma sinathe kugoletsa chigoli cha Wanderers Dalitso Khungwa. Mphunzitsi wothandizira wa Silver, Mapopa Msukwa, adati chigolicho chinali chovomerezeka. "Tinalamulira mu theka lachiwiri ndipo sitinapeze chigoli. Komabe, masewera ambiri omwe tidasewera motsutsana ndi Wanderers amathera pamlingo wofanana ndipo kupeza mfundo imodzi pamasewerawa sikoipa," adatero.

Wanderers adakwera malo awiri kufika pa nambala 7 ndi mfundo yawo. Mphunzitsi Bob Mpinganjira adati timu yake idavutika pakati pa bwalo. "Unali masewera ovuta. Sitinasewere masewera athu, makamaka pakati pa bwalo. Tinataya mipira yambiri ndipo zinali zovuta kusewera masewera athu," adatero. M'masewera ena, Mafco FC idagonjetsa Chitipa United 2-0 ku Champion Stadium ku Mponela, pomwe Clifford Msowoya adagoletsa kawiri. Karonga United idapambana 1-0 motsutsana ndi Civil Service United ku Civo Stadium ku Lilongwe, pomwe Godfrey Suwale adagoletsa chigoli chokhacho.

en.title: Mozambique invites Malawi businesses to use new port
en.body: The Government of Mozambique has asked Malawian business people to invest in Zambezia Province. On Saturday in Blantyre, Governor Pio Augusto Matos met with Malawi business leaders. He said Malawi can use Quelimane Port on the ocean. The port is 306 kilometres from Malawi. This will help Malawi trade more easily. Matos said Zambezia Province is a safe place to do business.
en.body_more:
Lucky Mfungwe is the business director of the Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry. He said using Quelimane Port will help Malawi. It will cost less money to ship goods. He asked Malawi businesses to take this chance. The meeting was called "Zambezia open for business: Trade and investment opportunities for Malawi through the Port of Quelimane."

Rachel Mijiga works for HS Winehouse Beverages. Her company makes Capestars liquor with Ethanol Company Limited. She said her company is talking with Mozambique about starting work there. She said they want to begin in Mozambique by the end of this year. Her business is not the only one interested. Shoprite, a big shop from South Africa, is also watching what happens. A Shoprite person said their company can bring more jobs. They can help with storage and moving goods.

The port is also the capital of Zambezia Province. This means the rules are clear for new businesses. Malawi businesses will pay less to move their goods. They can sell to other countries more easily. This can help many Malawi companies—not just food and drink makers. Small businesses in packing and moving goods can also grow. Lower costs mean Malawi goods are cheaper for people in Mozambique and other countries. Malawi people will also buy cheaper goods from other places. Business people say this is a good time to start work in Mozambique.

ny.title: Mozambique ikuyitanitsa mabizinesi aku Malawi kuti agwiritse ntchito doko latsopano
ny.body: Boma la Mozambique lapempha amalonda aku Malawi kuti agule ndalama ku Zambezia Province. Loweruka ku Blantyre, Bwanamkubwa Pio Augusto Matos anakumana ndi atsogoleri amalonda aku Malawi. Iye anati Malawi ikhoza kugwiritsa ntchito doko la Quelimane panyanja. Dokoli lili pamtunda wa makilomita 306 kuchokera ku Malawi. Izi zithandiza Malawi kuchita malonda mosavuta. Matos adati chigawo cha Zambezia ndi malo otetezeka ochitira bizinesi.
ny.body_more:
Lucky Mfungwe ndi mkulu wa bizinesi wa Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry. Iye anati kugwiritsa ntchito Quelimane Port kudzathandiza Malawi. Kutumiza katundu kudzawononga ndalama zochepa. Iye anapempha mabizinesi aku Malawi kuti agwiritse ntchito mwayi umenewu. Msonkhanowu unatchedwa "Zambezia yotseguka kwa bizinesi: Malonda ndi mwayi woyika ndalama ku Malawi kudzera pa Doko la Quelimane."

Rachel Mijiga amagwira ntchito ku HS Winehouse Beverages. Kampani yake imapanga zakumwa za Capestars ndi Ethanol Company Limited. Iye anati kampani yake ikulankhula ndi Mozambique za kuyamba ntchito kumeneko. Iye anati akufuna kuyamba ku Mozambique pofika kumapeto kwa chaka chino. Bizinesi yake si yokhayo yomwe ili ndi chidwi. Shoprite, shopu yayikulu yochokera ku South Africa, ikuyang'ananso zomwe zikuchitika. Munthu wina wa ku Shoprite anati kampani yawo ikhoza kubweretsa ntchito zambiri. Angathandize posungira ndi kusuntha katundu.

Dokoli ndi likulu la Zambezia Province. Izi zikutanthauza kuti malamulo ndi omveka bwino kwa mabizinesi atsopano. Mabizinesi aku Malawi azilipira ndalama zochepa kuti asunthire katundu wawo. Angagulitse kumayiko ena mosavuta. Izi zingathandize makampani ambiri aku Malawi—osati opanga chakudya ndi zakumwa okha. Mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono onyamula katundu ndi kunyamula katundu nawonso angakule. Mitengo yotsika ikutanthauza kuti katundu wa ku Malawi ndi wotsika mtengo kwa anthu aku Mozambique ndi mayiko ena. Anthu aku Malawi adzagulanso katundu wotsika mtengo kuchokera kumadera ena. Amalonda amati ino ndi nthawi yabwino yoyambira ntchito ku Mozambique.

en.title: Football Legends Give Clothes to Elderly People
en.body: Football legends in Malawi gave clothes to old people on Saturday. The Football Legends Association brought sweaters and other clothes to Mai Mbambande Foundation in Dzama Village, Lilongwe District. More than 70 old people live at the centre. The clothes came from Finish Line Ministries. Leaders said this is important work for the community.
en.body_more:
The Football Legends Association leader, Gomezgani Solomon Mchawi, said that old people need help from everyone. He said: "These old people are like our parents. They raised us when we were young. So we must care for them now." Two other leaders, Rashid Ntelera and Shadreck Chikhosi, came with him to give the clothes.

The head of the foundation, Richard Masina, said thank you to the football legends. He said the old people need food, clothes and medicine every day. Winter is coming soon, so the sweaters are very good. Masina said the centre needs more help from people who want to give.

One old man, Kapiseni Ching'ombe, was very happy. He said: "May God bless the Football Legends Association. They show us that we are not alone." He said that his life got much better when he came to the centre. But the centre needs many things every day to help all the old people.

The football legends first got the clothes from Finish Line Ministries on Friday. They gave some to old football players at Bingu National Stadium on the same day. But they chose to give clothes to the old people at Mai Mbambande too. The leaders said this is what real football legends do—they help people off the ground as well as on it.

ny.title: Nthano za Mpira Zimapatsa Okalamba Zovala
ny.body: Akatswiri a mpira ku Malawi adapereka zovala kwa okalamba Loweruka. Bungwe la Football Legends Association linabweretsa ma sweta ndi zovala zina ku Mai Mbambande Foundation ku Dzima Village, m'boma la Lilongwe. Okalamba oposa 70 amakhala pamalopo. Zovalazo zinachokera ku Finish Line Ministries. Atsogoleri adati iyi ndi ntchito yofunika kwambiri kwa anthu ammudzi.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri wa Football Legends Association, Gomezgani Solomon Mchawi, anati okalamba amafunika thandizo kuchokera kwa aliyense. Iye anati: "Okalamba awa ali ngati makolo athu. Anatilera tili ana. Choncho tiyenera kuwasamalira tsopano." Atsogoleri ena awiri, Rashid Ntelera ndi Shadreck Chikhosi, anabwera naye kudzapereka zovalazo.

Mkulu wa mazikowo, Richard Masina, anati zikomo kwa akatswiri a mpira. Iye anati okalamba amafunika chakudya, zovala ndi mankhwala tsiku lililonse. Nyengo yozizira ikubwera posachedwa, kotero majekete ndi abwino kwambiri. Masina anati malo ochitira masewerawa akufunika thandizo lochulukirapo kuchokera kwa anthu omwe akufuna kupereka.

Mkulu wina, Kapiseni Ching'ombe, anali wokondwa kwambiri. Iye anati: "Mulungu adalitse Football Legends Association. Akutisonyeza kuti sitili tokha." Iye anati moyo wake unakhala bwino kwambiri atabwera ku malo ochitira masewerawa. Koma malo ochitira masewerawa amafunika zinthu zambiri tsiku lililonse kuti athandize okalamba onse.

Atsogoleri a mpira adalandira zovalazo koyamba kuchokera ku Finish Line Ministries Lachisanu. Anapereka zina kwa osewera mpira akale ku Bingu National Stadium tsiku lomwelo. Koma anasankha kupatsanso zovala anthu okalamba a ku Mai Mbambande. Atsogoleriwo anati izi ndi zomwe akatswiri enieni a mpira amachita—amathandiza anthu ochokera pansi komanso omwe ali pamwamba pake.

en.title: Government calls back all diplomats, plans new team
en.body: The government has called back all diplomats who work at Malawi's embassies abroad. A new team will be sent to these jobs soon. The government says this is part of a planned change to match new priorities. Minister Shadric Namalomba said the government will tell people who the new diplomats will be at the right time. Some names have already appeared on social media.
en.body_more:
Three people have already said they have new diplomat jobs. Linda Kunje will work in New York as deputy high commissioner. Kondwani Munthali will work in the United Kingdom, also as deputy high commissioner. Leonard Chimbanga will work in South Africa as first secretary. They told people about their new jobs on Facebook.

The Foreign Affairs Ministry says it needs more time before it speaks about who will get which jobs. A list is moving around on social media, but the government has not yet said if it is correct. Parliament's Public Appointments Committee will check all the new diplomats when the government sends them the names. The committee must make sure all new diplomats are good enough for the work.

Benedicto Kondowe from the National Advocacy Platform says the government must choose people who are good at their work, not people who are political friends. He says if the government picks diplomats for political reasons, it may cost more money and hurt Malawi's work around the world. He says Malawi's embassies should help bring trade, jobs, and money to the country. They should also help Malawians who live in other countries and help local business people make links with other countries.

Kondowe says the government must choose diplomats who have the skills and knowledge to help Malawi grow. The new diplomatic team is one of the biggest changes in Malawi's foreign service in many years. It comes at a time when Malawi faces money problems and needs strong friends in other countries.

ny.title: Boma likuyimitsanso akazembe onse, likukonzekera gulu latsopano
ny.body: Boma layimitsanso akazembe onse omwe amagwira ntchito ku ma ambassade a Malawi kunja. Gulu latsopano lidzatumizidwa kuntchitozi posachedwa. Boma lati izi ndi gawo la kusintha komwe kukukonzekera kuti kugwirizane ndi zinthu zatsopano zofunika. Nduna Shadric Namalomba yati boma lidzauza anthu omwe akazembe atsopanowa adzakhala panthawi yoyenera. Mayina ena awonekera kale pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti.
ny.body_more:
Anthu atatu anena kale kuti ali ndi ntchito zatsopano za diplomat. Linda Kunje adzagwira ntchito ku New York ngati wachiwiri kwa komishonala wamkulu. Kondwani Munthali adzagwiranso ntchito ku United Kingdom, komanso ngati wachiwiri kwa komishonala wamkulu. Leonard Chimbanga adzagwira ntchito ku South Africa ngati mlembi woyamba. Anauza anthu za ntchito zawo zatsopano pa Facebook.

Unduna wa Zakunja ukunena kuti ukufunika nthawi yochulukirapo usanalankhule za omwe adzapeza ntchito ziti. Mndandanda ukuyendayenda pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti, koma boma silinanenebe ngati ndi lolondola. Komiti Yoyang'anira Kusankhidwa kwa Anthu ku Nyumba Yamalamulo idzayang'ana ma diplomat atsopano onse boma likawatumizira mayina. Komitiyi iyenera kuwonetsetsa kuti ma diplomat atsopano onse ndi abwino pantchitoyi.

Benedicto Kondowe wochokera ku National Advocacy Platform akuti boma liyenera kusankha anthu omwe ali ndi luso pantchito yawo, osati anthu omwe ndi mabwenzi andale. Iye akuti ngati boma lisankha ma diplomat pazifukwa zandale, zitha kuwononga ndalama zambiri ndikuvulaza ntchito ya Malawi padziko lonse lapansi. Iye akuti ma officer a Malawi ayenera kuthandiza kubweretsa malonda, ntchito, ndi ndalama mdziko muno. Ayeneranso kuthandiza anthu aku Malawi omwe amakhala m'maiko ena ndikuthandizira amalonda am'deralo kupanga maubwenzi ndi mayiko ena.

Kondowe akuti boma liyenera kusankha a diplomat omwe ali ndi luso komanso chidziwitso kuti athandize Malawi kukula. Gulu latsopano la a diplomat ndi limodzi mwa kusintha kwakukulu muutumiki wakunja wa Malawi m'zaka zambiri. Izi zikubwera panthawi yomwe Malawi ikukumana ndi mavuto azachuma ndipo ikufunika mabwenzi olimba m'maiko ena.

en.title: Eagles go top after Bullets draw one-one
en.body: Blue Eagles moved to the top of the FDH Bank Premiership after a 1-1 draw with FCB Nyasa Bullets at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe. Bullets came from behind to get a point. Tonic Viyuyi scored first for Eagles in the 38th minute. Ephraim Kondowe levelled for Bullets five minutes into the second half. Eagles now have 10 points. Bullets stay on 9 points.
en.body_more:
Viyuyi's goal came from a header after Eagles started the match very well. In the second minute, a corner from Mecium Mhone almost gave Eagles the lead, but Innocent Nyangulu cleared the ball off the line to save Bullets. Eagles kept their lead at half-time, but Bullets came out strong after the break. Kondowe came on as a substitute and scored the equaliser when Eagles keeper Precious Masamba failed to hold the ball.

Bullets pushed hard for a winner after levelling. Hassan Kajoke played a fine pass to Madalitso Safuli in the 57th minute, but Safuli's shot went wide. One minute later, Andrew Jovinala's free-kick hit the Eagles wall. Wongani Lungu also came close but shot wide. Paul Master played for Bullets and was named player of the match. Eagles coach Deklerk Msakakuona said after the game that he wanted to win. Bullets coach Gilbert Chirwa said the draw was fair.

Eagles have now moved to the top with 10 points from their matches. Bullets are on 9 points, level with Silver and Masters FC, who play today. Eagles won back-to-back games before this draw, beating Silver Strikers 1-0 and Goshen City Dedza Dynamos 2-0. Bullets had drawn with Red Lions the week before without scoring.

ny.title: Eagles apambana pambuyo poti Bullets yapambana chimodzi ndi chimodzi
ny.body: Blue Eagles yafika pamwamba pa FDH Bank Premiership itatha kusewera 1-1 ndi FCB Nyasa Bullets pa Civo Stadium ku Lilongwe. Bullets idachokera kumbuyo kuti ipeze mfundo imodzi. Tonic Viyuyi adagoletsa chigoli choyamba cha Eagles mu mphindi ya 38. Ephraim Kondowe adafanana ndi Bullets mphindi zisanu mu theka lachiwiri. Eagles tsopano ali ndi mfundo 10. Bullets ikupitilizabe ndi mfundo 9.
ny.body_more:
Chigoli cha Viyuyi chinachokera ku mutu pambuyo poti Eagles yayamba bwino kwambiri masewerawa. Mphindi yachiwiri, kona yochokera kwa Mecium Mhone inatsala pang'ono kupatsa Eagles chitsogozo, koma Innocent Nyangulu anachotsa mpirawo pamzere kuti apulumutse Bullets. Eagles inapitirizabe kutsogola nthawi yopuma, koma Bullets inatuluka mwamphamvu pambuyo pa nthawi yopuma. Kondowe anabwera ngati wolowa m'malo ndipo anagoletsa chigoli chofanana pamene Precious Masamba, yemwe anali mlonda wa Eagles, analephera kugwira mpirawo.

Bullets inakankhira mwamphamvu kuti ipambane pambuyo pofanana. Hassan Kajoke adasewera bwino kwa Madalitso Safuli mu mphindi ya 57, koma chigoli cha Safuli chinapita kutali. Mphindi imodzi pambuyo pake, free-kick ya Andrew Jovinala inagunda khoma la Eagles. Wongani Lungu nayenso anayandikira koma anawombera kutali. Paul Master adasewera ku Bullets ndipo adasankhidwa kukhala wosewera wamasewerawa. Mphunzitsi wa Eagles Deklerk Msakakuona adati masewerawa atatha kuti akufuna kupambana. Mphunzitsi wa Bullets Gilbert Chirwa adati masewerowo anali abwino.

Eagles tsopano yapita pamwamba ndi mapointi 10 kuchokera pamasewera awo. Bullets ili ndi mapointi 9, ofanana ndi Silver ndi Masters FC, omwe akusewera lero. Eagles adapambana masewera awiri otsatizana asanafike masewerowa, akugonjetsa Silver Strikers 1-0 ndi Goshen City Dedza Dynamos 2-0. Bullets idasewera ndi Red Lions sabata yatha osagoletsa zigoli.

en.title: MEC evicted from Lilongwe office over unpaid lease
en.body: The Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) has been evicted from its office in Lilongwe. The property owner, Capital Developments Limited, sent a letter on May 21 saying MEC must leave right away. The Ministry of Lands did not renew the lease. MEC was given 14 days to respond to a new offer, but did not reply. The deadline was May 10, 2026. The government wants MEC to move to Blantyre.
en.body_more:
The property owner, through its manager Mpico Plc, sent the eviction letter to MEC Chief Elections Officer Andrew Mpesi. Stella Sokosa, the General Manager, wrote that MEC must leave by May 21 and give back the empty office. She said MEC did not answer two letters from the company. A new tenant is ready to move in on June 1.

The eviction follows an order by President Peter Mutharika from October 2025. The President said MEC, along with three other government bodies, must move from Lilongwe to Blantyre. Two other groups and one have already moved. MEC has stayed in Lilongwe because of court cases. All the court cases have been decided against MEC. A High Court judge threw out three people who asked the court to stop the move. Another judge said MEC was too late to ask for a review.

The Attorney General said MEC must move right now. The Office of President and Cabinet said the same thing. They said the government gave MEC three months, which has now passed. A person from MEC said the group has not yet decided what to do. An expert said the long wait creates worry and may take attention away from MEC's main job of running fair elections. The government said it will pay rent for a new tenant at the office in Lilongwe.

ny.title: MEC yachotsedwa mu ofesi ya ku Lilongwe chifukwa chosalipira lendi
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) lachotsedwa mu ofesi yake ku Lilongwe. Mwini malo, Capital Developments Limited, adatumiza kalata pa Meyi 21 yonena kuti MEC iyenera kuchoka nthawi yomweyo. Unduna wa Zamalo sunasinthenso lendi. MEC idapatsidwa masiku 14 kuti ayankhe pempho latsopano, koma sinayankhe. Tsiku lomaliza linali pa Meyi 10, 2026. Boma likufuna kuti MEC isamukire ku Blantyre.
ny.body_more:
Mwini malo, kudzera mwa manejala wake Mpico Plc, adatumiza kalata yochotsa anthu m'nyumba kwa MEC Chief Elections Officer Andrew Mpesi. Stella Sokosa, General Manager, adalemba kuti MEC iyenera kuchoka pofika pa Meyi 21 ndikubwezera ofesi yopanda anthu. Adati MEC sinayankhe makalata awiri ochokera ku kampaniyo. Wobwereka watsopano ali wokonzeka kusamukira pa June 1.

Kuchotsedwa kumeneku kukutsatira lamulo la Purezidenti Peter Mutharika kuyambira Okutobala 2025. Purezidenti adati MEC, pamodzi ndi mabungwe ena atatu aboma, ayenera kusamukira ku Blantyre kuchokera ku Lilongwe. Magulu ena awiri ndi limodzi asamukira kale. MEC yakhala ku Lilongwe chifukwa cha milandu ya khothi. Milandu yonse ya khothi yagamulidwa motsutsana ndi MEC. Woweruza wa Khothi Lalikulu adathamangitsa anthu atatu omwe adapempha khothi kuti lisiye kusunthaku. Woweruza wina adati MEC yachedwa kwambiri kupempha kuti iwunikenso.

Loya Wamkulu adati MEC iyenera kusuntha pakadali pano. Ofesi ya Purezidenti ndi Kabinati idanenanso chimodzimodzi. Adati boma lapatsa MEC miyezi itatu, yomwe tsopano yadutsa. Munthu wochokera ku MEC adati gululo silinasankhe chochita. Katswiri wina anati kudikira kwa nthawi yayitali kumabweretsa nkhawa ndipo kungachotse chidwi pa ntchito yaikulu ya MEC yoyendetsa zisankho zoyenera. Boma lati lidzalipira lendi ya munthu watsopano wobwereka ku ofesi ku Lilongwe.

en.title: Malawi tobacco faces big problems with buyers and markets
en.body: Malawi's tobacco sales are in trouble. About ninety percent of tobacco was rejected at the auction floors this year. Tobacco is very important to Malawi.
