It brings money from other countries. Farmers need this money to buy food, fuel, and medicine. But now many buyers say no to Malawi's tobacco. The main problem is quality. Some farmers add sand and stones to make the tobacco heavier. Buyers want good tobacco, not bad tobacco.
en.body_more:
The Tobacco Commission works to help farmers grow better tobacco. But maybe it is not doing enough work. One idea is to sell tobacco to China instead of only to the West. China bought two hundred thirty-six million dollars of tobacco from Zimbabwe this year. Malawi sold only two point one four million dollars to China. This shows China may be a new market for Malawi's tobacco if the quality is good.

Other countries are also moving away from tobacco. People say smoking is bad for health. Many countries now have laws to stop smoking. This means fewer people want to buy tobacco in the future. Malawi's farmers will have big problems if fewer people want tobacco.

Malawi needs to grow other crops too. Soybeans, groundnuts, and nuts can make money for farmers. These crops do not have the same problems as tobacco. But farmers need help to grow new crops. They need to know the new crops will sell and make money. If the government and the markets support other crops, farmers may try them. This will help Malawi not depend only on tobacco.

Right now, farmers have no good choice. Tobacco is still the easiest crop to sell. Other crops do not have good markets or good prices. Farmers must feed their families. So they keep growing tobacco even if it is hard. Malawi must fix this problem fast.

ny.title: Fodya ku Malawi ikukumana ndi mavuto aakulu ndi ogula komanso misika
ny.body: Kugulitsa fodya ku Malawi kuli m'mavuto. Pafupifupi 90 peresenti ya fodya inakanidwa pamalo ogulitsira malonda chaka chino. Fodya ndi wofunika kwambiri ku Malawi. Imabweretsa ndalama kuchokera kumayiko ena. Alimi amafunikira ndalamazi kuti agule chakudya, mafuta, ndi mankhwala. Koma tsopano ogula ambiri akuti ayi ku fodya wa ku Malawi. Vuto lalikulu ndi labwino. Alimi ena amawonjezera mchenga ndi miyala kuti fodya ikhale yolemera. Ogula amafuna fodya wabwino, osati fodya woipa.
ny.body_more:
Bungwe la Tobacco Commission limagwira ntchito yothandiza alimi kulima fodya wabwino. Koma mwina silikugwira ntchito yokwanira. Lingaliro limodzi ndi kugulitsa fodya ku China m'malo mogulitsa kumayiko akumadzulo okha. China idagula fodya wa madola 236 miliyoni kuchokera ku Zimbabwe chaka chino. Malawi idagulitsa madola awiri okha point one four million ku China. Izi zikusonyeza kuti China ikhoza kukhala msika watsopano wa fodya wa ku Malawi ngati khalidwe lake lili labwino.

Mayiko ena akusiyanso kugwiritsa ntchito fodya. Anthu amati kusuta fodya n'koipa pa thanzi. Mayiko ambiri tsopano ali ndi malamulo oletsa kusuta fodya. Izi zikutanthauza kuti anthu ochepa akufuna kugula fodya mtsogolo. Alimi aku Malawi adzakhala ndi mavuto akulu ngati anthu ochepa akufuna fodya.

Malawi iyeneranso kulima mbewu zina. Soya, mtedza, ndi mtedza zimatha kupangira alimi ndalama. Mbewu izi sizili ndi mavuto ofanana ndi fodya. Koma alimi amafunika thandizo kuti alime mbewu zatsopano. Ayenera kudziwa kuti mbewu zatsopano zidzagulitsidwa ndikupanga ndalama. Ngati boma ndi misika zithandizira mbewu zina, alimi angayese. Izi zithandiza Malawi osati kudalira fodya wokha.

Pakadali pano, alimi alibe njira yabwino. Fodya akadali mbewu yosavuta kugulitsa. Mbewu zina sizili ndi misika yabwino kapena mitengo yabwino. Alimi ayenera kudyetsa mabanja awo. Choncho akupitiriza kulima fodya ngakhale zitavuta. Dziko la Malawi liyenera kukonza vutoli mwachangu.

en.title: Pension Fund Report Shows Big Money Problems
en.body: A new report shows the Public Service Pension Trust Fund has serious money problems. The report says 1,144 workers are missing from the records. It also says K11.5 billion in workers' pension money was never added to their accounts. The fund bought a big hotel for K128.75 billion, and people say the price was too high. The report was made in July and August 2025.
en.body_more:
The fund looks after retirement money for about 139,000 civil servants. But the report found many problems. The fund did not have audited accounts since 2021. For nine months, the fund had no board to check what the leaders did. During that time, the leaders gave out K4.9 billion without board approval. This is against the rules.

The report also found six people listed twice in the records. It says K11.5 billion in worker money was not put in their accounts from 2023 to August 2025. The rules say the government must put money in within 14 days after each month ends. But this did not happen. Workers cannot see how much money they have saved for retirement.

The fund also lost money on other things. It paid K1.4 billion for land in Lilongwe. But the land was only worth K937 million. That is a loss of K463 million. The fund also lost K169 million on a computer system that did not work. One more problem: the government owes the fund K90.4 billion. The fund also put too much money in hotels. Hotels make up 37.2 percent of all the money the fund put in. Records were kept on old computer sheets that are not safe.

The Reserve Bank of Malawi now says the fund must clean up all its work. The bank said it will stop some payments and may fine the fund K100 million. A person from the fund said they are doing audits and will tell the public what they find.

ny.title: Lipoti la Ndalama za Pensheni Likusonyeza Mavuto Aakulu a Ndalama
ny.body: Lipoti latsopano likusonyeza kuti Public Service Pension Trust Fund ili ndi mavuto aakulu azachuma. Lipotilo likuti antchito 1,144 akusowa m'mabuku. Limanenanso kuti K11.5 biliyoni ya ndalama za penshoni za ogwira ntchito sizinawonjezedwepo ku akaunti zawo. Thumbalo linagula hotelo yayikulu pa K128.75 biliyoni, ndipo anthu akuti mtengo wake unali wokwera kwambiri. Lipotilo linapangidwa mu Julayi ndi Ogasiti 2025.
ny.body_more:
Ndalama zolipirira penshoni za antchito aboma pafupifupi 139,000. Koma lipotilo linapeza mavuto ambiri. Ndalamazo sizinali ndi maakaunti owunikidwa kuyambira mu 2021. Kwa miyezi isanu ndi inayi, ndalamazo zinalibe bungwe loyang'anira zomwe atsogoleriwo ankachita. Panthawiyo, atsogoleriwo adapereka K4.9 biliyoni popanda chilolezo cha bungwe. Izi zikutsutsana ndi malamulo.

Lipotilo linapezanso anthu asanu ndi mmodzi omwe adalembedwa kawiri m'mabuku. Limati K11.5 biliyoni ya ndalama za ogwira ntchito sizinayikidwe m'maakaunti awo kuyambira 2023 mpaka Ogasiti 2025. Malamulowo amati boma liyenera kuyika ndalama mkati mwa masiku 14 mwezi uliwonse ukatha. Koma izi sizinachitike. Ogwira ntchito sangaone kuchuluka kwa ndalama zomwe asunga kuti apume pantchito.

Nkhokweyi idatayanso ndalama pazinthu zina. Idalipira K1.4 biliyoni ya malo ku Lilongwe. Koma malowo anali ofunika K937 miliyoni okha. Kumeneko ndi kutayika kwa K463 miliyoni. Ndalamazo zidatayanso K169 miliyoni pa kompyuta yomwe sinagwire ntchito. Vuto linanso: boma lili ndi ngongole ya K90.4 biliyoni. Ndalamayi inaikanso ndalama zambiri m'mahotela. Mahotela amapanga 37.2 peresenti ya ndalama zonse zomwe ndalamayi inayika. Zolemba zinasungidwa pamakompyuta akale omwe si otetezeka.

Banki Yaikulu ya Malawi tsopano ikunena kuti ndalamayi iyenera kuyeretsa ntchito zake zonse. Bankiyo inati iyimitsa kulipira kwina ndipo ikhoza kulipira ndalama zokwana K100 miliyoni. Munthu wochokera ku ndalamayi anati akuchita kafukufuku ndipo adzauza anthu zomwe apeza.

en.title: Electoral Commission Ordered to Leave Lilongwe Offices
en.body: Malawi's Electoral Commission must leave its offices in Lilongwe. The landlord, a company called Capital Developments Limited, sent an eviction letter on May 20, 2026. The company said MEC did not reply to an offer sent on April 27. The offer was only good for 14 days and expired on May 10. MEC must leave by May 21.
en.body_more:
The eviction comes after the President ordered MEC to move to Blantyre last year. The government said it would not pay rent for MEC offices in Lilongwe any more. The government also said another institution will use the building instead. A high court ruled that the President's order was lawful and MEC must obey it.

MEC has resisted the move for months. The commission said the move would cause problems with its work. But other government bodies have already moved to Blantyre without complaint. MEC is now the only one still in Lilongwe. A government spokesperson, Focus Maganga, said the move does not harm MEC's freedom to work. He said moving offices to a new city does not change MEC's job or power.

MEC's spokesperson Sangwani Mwafulirwa said he did not even know about the eviction letter. This shows poor communication inside the commission. A political expert said MEC made a mistake by ignoring the landlord's letter and losing the court case. He said the commission should have responded faster and made better plans.

MEC now faces a hard choice. The commission must find new offices or move to Blantyre very soon. The timing is serious because MEC runs elections. Any delay or confusion could harm the country's democratic work. MEC has lost both its legal fight and now its physical space. The next few weeks will show how the commission moves forward.

ny.title: Komiti Yosankha Yalamulidwa Kutuluka M'maofesi a Lilongwe
ny.body: Bungwe Loona za Chisankho ku Malawi liyenera kuchoka m'maofesi ake ku Lilongwe. Mwininyumba, kampani yotchedwa Capital Developments Limited, adatumiza kalata yoti achotsedwe m'nyumba pa Meyi 20, 2026. Kampaniyo idati MEC sinayankhe pempho lotumizidwa pa Epulo 27. Pempholo linali labwino kwa masiku 14 okha ndipo linatha pa Meyi 10. MEC iyenera kuchoka pofika pa Meyi 21.
ny.body_more:
Kuchotsedwa kwa anthu kumeneku kunachitika Pulezidenti atalamula kuti bungwe la MEC lisamukire ku Blantyre chaka chatha. Boma linati sililipiranso lendi ya maofesi a MEC ku Lilongwe. Boma linanenanso kuti bungwe lina lidzagwiritsa ntchito nyumbayo m'malo mwake. Khoti lalikulu linagamula kuti lamulo la Purezidenti linali lovomerezeka ndipo bungwe la MEC liyenera kulitsatira.

Bungwe la MEC lakana izi kwa miyezi ingapo. Bungweli linati izi zibweretsa mavuto pa ntchito yake. Koma mabungwe ena aboma asamukira kale ku Blantyre popanda kudandaula. Bungwe la MEC tsopano ndi lokhalo lomwe lidakali ku Lilongwe. Mneneri wa boma, Focus Maganga, anati izi sizikuwononga ufulu wa MEC wogwira ntchito. Anati kusamutsa maofesi kupita kumzinda watsopano sikusintha ntchito kapena mphamvu ya MEC.

Mneneri wa bungwe la MEC Sangwani Mwafulirwa anati sakudziwa ngakhale za kalata yochotsa anthu. Izi zikusonyeza kusalankhulana bwino mkati mwa bungweli. Katswiri wandale anati bungwe la MEC linalakwitsa ponyalanyaza kalata ya mwini nyumbayo ndikutaya mlandu kukhothi. Anati bungweli liyenera kuyankha mwachangu ndikupanga mapulani abwino.

Tsopano bungwe la MEC likukumana ndi chisankho chovuta. Bungweli liyenera kupeza maofesi atsopano kapena kusamukira ku Blantyre posachedwa. Nthawi yake ndi yofunika kwambiri chifukwa MEC ikuyendetsa zisankho. Kuchedwa kulikonse kapena chisokonezo chilichonse chingawononge ntchito ya demokalase ya dzikolo. MEC yataya nkhondo yake yamilandu komanso tsopano malo ake enieni. Masabata angapo otsatira adzawonetsa momwe komishoni ikuyendera.

en.title: Army arrests five Russians over illegal mining in Malawi
en.body: The Malawi Defence Force arrested five Russian nationals in Chikwawa on Wednesday, May 20, 2026. They are accused of entering the country without permission and searching for minerals illegally. The men are named Badalyan, Rushan Nersisyan, Andreir Sklyarchuk, Mark Sklyarchuk, and Aleksel Latcyk. They worked for two companies: Baze International Limited and Avro International Limited. A local group called CDEDI said Malawians must protect the country's mineral wealth.
en.body_more:
The arrests came after the Ministry of Mining and Energy met with artisanal miners. Mining Minister Jean Mathanga and artisanal miner leader James Kudazala agreed to work together. The miners promised to tell the government about illegal mining activities. One week after this meeting, CDEDI got a tip. Someone said six Russian men were staying at a lodge in Chikwawa. They had been there for three weeks. They did not let the lodge staff clean their rooms. This strange behavior worried people. CDEDI told the government. The army then arrested the five men.

CDEDI's leader Sylvester Namiwa said hotels and lodges must report suspicious guests. He said this is important for the country. He also said the government must make the Mining and Minerals Regulatory Authority stronger. This authority does not have enough workers. Many jobs are empty. Namiwa asked the government to stop giving mining licenses to foreign people. He said all licenses that are more than three years old must be cancelled.

"The minerals in our soil are our wealth," Namiwa said. "Let us protect them to benefit us and generations to come." The case shows how ordinary people and the government can work as a team. Citizens who report suspicious activity help catch criminals who steal Malawi's resources.

ny.title: Asilikali agwira anthu asanu aku Russia chifukwa cha migodi yosaloledwa ku Malawi
ny.body: Asilikali a Malawi Defense Force amanga nzika zisanu za ku Russia ku Chikwawa Lachitatu, pa 20 Meyi, 2026. Akuimbidwa mlandu wolowa mdziko muno popanda chilolezo ndikufufuza miyala yamtengo wapatali mozemba. Amunawa ndi Badalyan, Rushan Nersisyan, Andreir Sklyarchuk, Mark Sklyarchuk, ndi Aleksel Latcyk. Ankagwira ntchito m'makampani awiri: Baze International Limited ndi Avro International Limited. Gulu la anthu am'deralo lotchedwa CDEDI linati anthu aku Malawi ayenera kuteteza chuma cha miyala yamtengo wapatali mdzikolo.
ny.body_more:
Kumangidwa kwa anthuwa kunachitika pambuyo poti Unduna wa Migodi ndi Mphamvu wakumana ndi ogwira ntchito zamigodi. Nduna ya Migodi Jean Mathanga ndi mtsogoleri wa ogwira ntchito zamigodi James Kudazala adagwirizana kuti agwire ntchito limodzi. Ogwira ntchito zamigodiwo adalonjeza kuuza boma za ntchito zochotsa migodi zosaloledwa. Patatha sabata imodzi msonkhanowu utatha, CDEDI idalandira malangizo. Winawake adati amuna asanu ndi mmodzi aku Russia akukhala ku lodge ku Chikwawa. Adakhala kumeneko kwa milungu itatu. Sanalole ogwira ntchito ku lodge kuyeretsa zipinda zawo. Khalidwe lachilendoli linadetsa nkhawa anthu. CDEDI idauza boma. Kenako asilikali adagwira amuna asanuwo.

Mtsogoleri wa CDEDI Sylvester Namiwa adati mahotela ndi malo osungira migodi ayenera kupereka malipoti kwa alendo okayikitsa. Anati izi ndizofunikira mdziko muno. Anatinso boma liyenera kulimbikitsa Bungwe Lolamulira Migodi ndi Migodi. Bungweli lilibe antchito okwanira. Ntchito zambiri zilibe kanthu. Namiwa adapempha boma kuti lisiye kupereka zilolezo za migodi kwa anthu akunja. Anati zilolezo zonse zomwe zili ndi zaka zoposa zitatu ziyenera kuthetsedwa.

"Migodi yomwe ili m'nthaka yathu ndi chuma chathu," adatero Namiwa. "Tiyeni titeteze kuti tipindule ife ndi mibadwo ikubwerayi." Nkhaniyi ikuwonetsa momwe anthu wamba ndi boma angagwirire ntchito limodzi. Nzika zomwe zimanena za zochitika zokayikitsa zimathandiza kugwira zigawenga zomwe zimaba chuma cha Malawi.

en.title: Malawi Trade Fair Opens with Focus on Making and Selling
en.body: The 36th Malawi International Trade Fair opened on Thursday in Blantyre. Trade Minister Simon Itaye said Malawi must make more goods and sell them abroad. He said the country buys too many things from other countries. Instead, Malawi should use its own goods to make products for export. This will bring money into the country and create jobs. The fair runs from 21 to 29 May at Chichiri Trade Fair Grounds. About 200 companies are taking part from Malawi and five other countries.
en.body_more:
Minister Itaye told businesses to add value to Malawi's natural goods. He said this will help grow the economy and make the country stronger. The trade fair is run by the Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry with help from the Trade Ministry. This year's theme is "Promoting Economic Stability through Manufacturing and Industrialization."

The head of the chambers, Ronald Ngwira, said fewer foreign companies are coming to the fair now because of money problems in Malawi. But he said local businesses still gain from meeting other traders and finding new markets. Companies from Mozambique, India, South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Tanzania are showing their goods at the fair.

The Ministry of Transport and Public Works is also at the fair. They show roads, trains, airports, and water transport projects. They want private businesses to work with government on these big projects. They also show new technology for paying road fees and keeping drivers safe. The Ministry says this fair helps people see where money is going and where private companies can help.

The fair gives local businesses a chance to learn new ways of doing business and meet people who give money for projects. Business owners can talk to government workers and find new work. The fair helps bring buyers and sellers together in one place.

ny.title: Chiwonetsero cha Zamalonda ku Malawi Chatsegulidwa ndi Kuyang'ana Kwambiri pa Kupanga ndi Kugulitsa
ny.body: Chiwonetsero cha 36 cha Zamalonda Padziko Lonse ku Malawi chinatsegulidwa Lachinayi ku Blantyre. Nduna ya Zamalonda Simon Itaye anati Malawi iyenera kupanga zinthu zambiri ndikugulitsa kunja. Iye anati dzikolo limagula zinthu zambiri kuchokera kumayiko ena. M'malo mwake, Malawi iyenera kugwiritsa ntchito katundu wake popanga zinthu zotumizidwa kunja. Izi zibweretsa ndalama mdzikolo ndikupanga ntchito. Chiwonetserochi chikuchitika kuyambira pa 21 mpaka 29 Meyi ku Chichiri Trade Fair Grounds. Makampani pafupifupi 200 akutenga nawo mbali kuchokera ku Malawi ndi mayiko ena asanu.
ny.body_more:
Nduna Itaye yauza mabizinesi kuti awonjezere phindu ku zinthu zachilengedwe za ku Malawi. Iye anati izi zithandiza kukulitsa chuma ndikulimbitsa dzikolo. Chiwonetsero chamalonda chikuyendetsedwa ndi Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry mothandizidwa ndi Unduna wa Zamalonda. Mutu wa chaka chino ndi "Kulimbikitsa Kukhazikika kwa Zachuma Kudzera mu Kupanga ndi Kukulitsa Mafakitale."

Mkulu wa makomiti, Ronald Ngwira, adati makampani ochepa akunja akubwera ku chiwonetserochi tsopano chifukwa cha mavuto azachuma ku Malawi. Koma adati mabizinesi am'deralo akupindulabe pokumana ndi amalonda ena ndikupeza misika yatsopano. Makampani ochokera ku Mozambique, India, South Africa, Zimbabwe, ndi Tanzania akuwonetsa katundu wawo pachiwonetserochi.

Unduna wa Zamayendedwe ndi Ntchito za Anthu ulinso pachiwonetserochi. Akuwonetsa misewu, sitima, ma eyapoti, ndi mapulojekiti oyendera madzi. Akufuna mabizinesi achinsinsi kuti agwire ntchito ndi boma pa mapulojekiti akuluakulu awa. Akuwonetsanso ukadaulo watsopano wolipira ndalama zamisewu ndikusunga madalaivala otetezeka. Undunawu ukuti chiwonetserochi chimathandiza anthu kuwona komwe ndalama zikupita komanso komwe makampani achinsinsi angathandize.

Chiwonetserochi chimapatsa mabizinesi am'deralo mwayi wophunzira njira zatsopano zochitira bizinesi ndikukumana ndi anthu omwe amapereka ndalama pamapulojekiti. Eni mabizinesi amatha kulankhula ndi ogwira ntchito m'boma ndikupeza ntchito zatsopano. Chiwonetserochi chimathandiza kusonkhanitsa ogula ndi ogulitsa pamalo amodzi.

en.title: Court lets election case against Gangata go forward
en.body: The High Court of Malawi said no to Alfred Gangata on Wednesday. Gangata is a member of parliament for Lilongwe. He wanted the court to throw out a case against him. But Justice Yakuwawa Msiska said no. Now George Zulu can move forward with his case. Zulu says the election result was wrong. He wants a new election in that area.
en.body_more:
Gangata is from the Democratic Progressive Party. He is also a minister for youth, sports and culture. In September 2025, there was a general election in Lilongwe Mtandire-Mtsiriza. Gangata won. But George Zulu, from the Malawi Congress Party, said the result was not right. Zulu asked the court to say Gangata did not win.

Gangata tried to stop Zulu's case. He said there was an earlier court case about the same thing. The judge would not agree. He said the earlier case was different. The earlier case was about the Malawi Electoral Commission's decision to cancel the first election. The new case is about whether Gangata really won after new votes were counted.

Zulu's lawyer said the problem was about null and void votes. He said the results sheet showed 445 votes that could not be used. But the commission said it got only 345 such votes. No one said why the numbers were different. This is what Zulu wants the court to look at.

After the judge said no to Gangata, Gangata's lawyer said his team will read the full court order. Then they will decide what to do next. The court has not set a date yet for the full hearing of Zulu's case. If Zulu wins, Gangata will lose his seat and there will be a new election.

ny.title: Khoti lalola kuti mlandu wotsutsana ndi Gangata upitirire patsogolo
ny.body: Khothi Lalikulu la Malawi lakana Alfred Gangata Lachitatu. Gangata ndi phungu wa nyumba yamalamulo ku Lilongwe. Ankafuna kuti khothi lichotse mlandu womutsutsa. Koma Woweruza Yakuwawa Msiska adati ayi. Tsopano George Zulu akhoza kupitiliza ndi mlandu wake. Zulu akuti zotsatira za chisankho zinali zolakwika. Akufuna chisankho chatsopano m'derali.
ny.body_more:
Gangata ndi wa chipani cha Democratic Progressive Party. Iyenso ndi nduna ya achinyamata, masewera ndi chikhalidwe. Mu Seputembala 2025, panali chisankho chachikulu ku Lilongwe Mtandire-Mtsitsira. Gangata adapambana. Koma George Zulu, wa chipani cha Malawi Congress, adati zotsatira zake sizinali zolondola. Zulu adapempha khothi kuti linene kuti Gangata sanapambane.

Gangata anayesa kuletsa mlandu wa Zulu. Anati panali mlandu wakale wa khothi wokhudza chinthu chomwecho. Woweruza sanavomereze. Anati mlandu woyamba unali wosiyana. Mlanduwu woyamba unali wokhudza chisankho cha Malawi Electoral Commission choletsa chisankho choyamba. Mlanduwu watsopano ndi wokhudza ngati Gangata adapambanadi pambuyo poti mavoti atsopano awerengedwa.

Loya wa Zulu adati vuto linali la mavoti opanda pake ndi opanda pake. Anati pepala la zotsatira likuwonetsa mavoti 445 omwe sangagwiritsidwe ntchito. Koma bungweli linati linapeza mavoti 345 okha. Palibe amene ananena chifukwa chake manambalawo anali osiyana. Ichi ndi chomwe Zulu akufuna kuti khothi liwone.

Woweruza milandu atakana Gangata, loya wa Gangata adati gulu lake liwerenga chigamulo chonse cha khothi. Kenako adzasankha chochita kenako. Khothi silinakhazikitse tsiku loti mlandu wa Zulu umvedwe mokwanira. Ngati Zulu apambana, Gangata adzataya mpando wake ndipo padzakhala chisankho chatsopano.

en.title: 45 students go to hospital after vaccine at school
en.body: On Monday, 45 students from Dowa Secondary School felt sick after they took a vaccine against bilharzia. They had stomach pain, headache, and were sick. The school sent them to Mponela Rural Hospital. The hospital checked them and found they had mild side effects from the vaccine. All the students went home after one day.
en.body_more:
The students took the vaccine around 3pm on Monday afternoon. By 6pm the same day, some started to feel ill. The head teacher, Saulos Mdalamba, said the students were rushed to hospital for care. The hospital's in-charge, Owen Chipewa, said the side effects were not serious. He said doctors watched the students at the hospital and all of them left in good health.

One report says the students may have taken the medicine without eating food first. The District Commissioner for Dowa, James Kanyangalazi, said this can cause problems. He said the medicine, called Praziquantel, is safe and works well. But when people take it with empty stomachs, they may feel pain, feel sick, or be dizzy. He said only this one school had this problem. Other schools in the area had no big issues.

Health workers say this vaccine stops a sickness called bilharzia. The government is giving it to all children in Dowa district now. Kanyangalazi told parents to make sure their children eat before they take the medicine. He said health workers will watch more carefully when they give the vaccine. He said thank you to the doctors, nurses, and teachers who helped the students fast.

ny.title: Ophunzira 45 apita kuchipatala atalandira katemera kusukulu
ny.body: Lolemba, ophunzira 45 ochokera ku Dowa Secondary School adadwala atalandira katemera wa bilharzia. Anali ndi ululu m'mimba, mutu, komanso anali odwala. Sukuluyo inawatumiza ku Mponela Rural Hospital. Chipatala chinawayang'ana ndipo chinapeza kuti anali ndi zotsatirapo zochepa chifukwa cha katemerayo. Ophunzira onse adabwerera kunyumba patatha tsiku limodzi.
ny.body_more:
Ophunzirawo adamwa katemera pafupifupi 3 koloko masana Lolemba. Pofika 6 koloko masana tsiku lomwelo, ena adayamba kudwala. Mphunzitsi wamkulu, Saulos Mdalamba, adati ophunzirawo adathamangira kuchipatala kuti akalandire chithandizo. Woyang'anira chipatalacho, Owen Chipewa, adati zotsatira zake sizinali zoyipa kwambiri. Anati madokotala adayang'anira ophunzirawo kuchipatala ndipo onse adachoka ali ndi thanzi labwino.

Lipoti lina likuti ophunzirawo mwina adamwa mankhwalawo osadya chakudya kaye. Woyang'anira Chigawo cha Dowa, James Kanyangalazi, adati izi zitha kuyambitsa mavuto. Anati mankhwalawo, otchedwa Praziquantel, ndi otetezeka ndipo amagwira ntchito bwino. Koma anthu akamamwa ali ndi njala m'mimba, amatha kumva kupweteka, kudwala, kapena kukhala ndi chizungulire. Anati sukulu imodzi yokhayi ndi yomwe ili ndi vutoli. Masukulu ena m'derali alibe mavuto akulu.

Ogwira ntchito zachipatala akuti katemerayu amaletsa matenda otchedwa bilharzia. Boma likupereka kwa ana onse m'boma la Dowa tsopano. Kanyangalazi adauza makolo kuti awonetsetse kuti ana awo adya asanamwe mankhwalawo. Anati ogwira ntchito zachipatala aziyang'anira mosamala akamapereka katemerayo. Anati zikomo kwa madokotala, anamwino, ndi aphunzitsi omwe anathandiza ophunzirawo kudya mofulumira.

en.title: MDF soldiers learn smart money skills in new workshops
en.body: Old Mutual Malawi has run money classes for soldiers and other workers across the country. The classes teach people how to save, budget, and plan for the future. Soldiers from the Navy, Cobbe Barracks, and other bases joined the workshops. Old Mutual says people need good money skills to stay safe when money is hard.
en.body_more:
Benard Chiluzi works as a Financial Education Manager for Old Mutual. He said the workshops cover many topics. People learn about changing bad money habits, how to save, how to invest, and how to plan for when they stop work. They also learn about emergency money and how to handle debt. Chiluzi said these things are very important right now because money is hard to find and prices are high.

The workshops reached soldiers in seven places. These were the Navy Battalion in Mangochi, Cobbe Barracks and Changalume Barracks in Zomba, MAFCO in Salima, Mvera Battalion in Dowa, Moyale Barracks in Mzuzu, and Chilumba Barracks in Karonga. Old Mutual also taught classes for other groups. These groups included Africa Global Logistics, Beit Cure, Malawi Parliament, and St Andrews Primary School.

Catherine Makokola works for Africa Global Logistics. She said the workshops are very good and very much needed. Many people have money problems, she said. People do not know how to manage money well, how to invest, or how to get ready for when they are old. Without help, people will have big money stress and worry. Makokola said Old Mutual is doing good work by giving these classes for free.

ny.title: Asilikali a MDF akuphunzira luso lanzeru la ndalama m'ma workshop atsopano
ny.body: Old Mutual Malawi yakhala ikuyendetsa makalasi andalama kwa asilikali ndi antchito ena mdziko lonse. Makalasiwa amaphunzitsa anthu momwe angasungire ndalama, bajeti, komanso kukonzekera tsogolo. Asilikali ochokera ku Navy, Cobbe Barracks, ndi malo ena ogwirira ntchito adalowa nawo m'ma workshop. Old Mutual imati anthu amafunika luso labwino landalama kuti akhale otetezeka ndalama zikavuta.
ny.body_more:
Benard Chiluzi amagwira ntchito ngati Woyang'anira Maphunziro a Zachuma ku Old Mutual. Iye anati misonkhanoyi ikufotokoza mitu yambiri. Anthu amaphunzira za kusintha zizolowezi zoipa zandalama, momwe angasungire ndalama, momwe angasungire ndalama, komanso momwe angakonzekere nthawi yomwe ayimitsa ntchito. Amaphunziranso za ndalama zadzidzidzi komanso momwe angasamalire ngongole. Chiluzi adati zinthuzi ndizofunikira kwambiri pakali pano chifukwa ndalama zimakhala zovuta kupeza ndipo mitengo yake ndi yokwera.

Misonkhanoyi idafikira asilikali m'malo asanu ndi awiri. Awa anali Navy Battalion ku Mangochi, Cobbe Barracks ndi Changalume Barracks ku Zomba, MAFCO ku Salima, Mvera Battalion ku Dowa, Moyale Barracks ku Mzuzu, ndi Chilumba Barracks ku Karonga. Old Mutual idaphunzitsanso makalasi a magulu ena. Maguluwa akuphatikizapo Africa Global Logistics, Beit Cure, Malawi Parliament, ndi St Andrews Primary School.

Catherine Makokola amagwira ntchito ku Africa Global Logistics. Iye adati misonkhanoyi ndi yabwino kwambiri ndipo ikufunika kwambiri. Anthu ambiri ali ndi mavuto azandalama, adatero. Anthu sadziwa momwe angasamalire ndalama bwino, momwe angasungire ndalama, kapena momwe angakonzekere akakalamba. Popanda thandizo, anthu adzakhala ndi nkhawa komanso ndalama zambiri. Makokola anati Old Mutual ikugwira ntchito yabwino popereka makalasi awa kwaulere.

en.title: Court stops Blantyre Water Board chief's suspension
en.body: A High Court judge in Malawi has stopped the suspension of Yeremiah Chihana. Chihana is the chief of the Blantyre Water Board. The court gave him leave to appeal. This means Chihana can stay in his job until a higher court makes a final choice. The board had suspended him for making decisions without asking them first.
en.body_more:
Chihana went to court and asked for help. He said the board should not suspend him. The board had said he made choices on his own, without talking to the board. Chihana then got an injunction, a court order that stopped the board from suspending him. But the board said the court was wrong. A court ruled against Chihana. This would have made his suspension real.

Now the High Court has stopped that ruling from going forward. The judge said the case has hard legal questions. The judge said it is fair to wait and let Chihana stay in his job while the case goes to a higher court. The board cannot suspend him or fire him while the appeal is happening. Chihana stays in his office.

The court also said the board and all its members must not do anything to make the suspension real. They cannot put it on paper. They cannot tell anyone he is fired. They must wait. The case will now go to the Supreme Court of Appeal. This is the highest court in Malawi. The Supreme Court will make the final choice about who is right.

ny.title: Khoti laletsa kuyimitsidwa kwa mkulu wa bungwe la Blantyre Water Board
ny.body: Woweruza milandu wa Khoti Lalikulu ku Malawi waletsa kuyimitsidwa kwa Yeremiah Chihana. Chihana ndiye mkulu wa Blantyre Water Board. Khotilo linamupatsa chilolezo choti apilo. Izi zikutanthauza kuti Chihana akhoza kukhalabe pantchito yake mpaka khoti lalikulu litapanga chisankho chomaliza. Bungwelo linamuyimitsa ntchito chifukwa chopanga zisankho popanda kuwafunsa kaye.
ny.body_more:
Chihana anapita kukhoti kukapempha thandizo. Anati bungweli siliyenera kumuyimitsa ntchito. Bungweli linanena kuti anasankha yekha, popanda kulankhula ndi bungweli. Kenako Chihana analandira chilolezo cha khoti chomwe chinaletsa bungweli kumuyimitsa ntchito. Koma bungweli linati khotilo linali lolakwika. Khotilo linagamula motsutsana ndi Chihana. Izi zikanapangitsa kuti kuyimitsidwa kwake kuchitike.

Tsopano Khoti Lalikulu laletsa chigamulocho kuti chisapitirire. Woweruzayo anati mlanduwu uli ndi mafunso ovuta azamalamulo. Woweruzayo anati ndi bwino kudikira ndikulola Chihana kukhalabe pantchito yake pamene mlanduwo ukupita kukhoti lalikulu. Bungweli silingamuyimitse ntchito kapena kumuchotsa ntchito pamene apilo ikupitirira. Chihana akukhalabe mu ofesi yake.

Khotilo linanenanso kuti bungweli ndi mamembala ake onse sayenera kuchita chilichonse kuti kuyimitsidwako kuchitike. Sangathe kulemba papepala. Sangauze aliyense kuti wachotsedwa ntchito. Ayenera kudikira. Mlanduwu tsopano upita ku Khoti Lalikulu la Apilo. Ili ndi khoti lalikulu kwambiri ku Malawi. Khoti Lalikulu lidzapanga chisankho chomaliza chokhudza yemwe ali wolondola.

en.title: Tobacco Buyers Reject Crops from Independent Farmers at High Rate
en.body: Tobacco buyers in Malawi are rejecting tobacco from independent farmers at a rate of 93 percent. Members of Parliament visited auction floors on Wednesday and heard this from Alison Kambiri, the Tobacco Commission manager in Lilongwe. Independent farmers grow tobacco without contracts. Many are facing big losses after months of work and spending money on seeds and tools.
en.body_more:
The Tobacco Commission board was shocked by what they found. Daniel Gunya, the board chairperson, said the high rejection rates surprised them. He said the board will meet with buyers and farmers to find a way forward. But members of Parliament who saw the auction floors were angry and disappointed. They said the situation is unfair.

Anthony Kamoto, a lawmaker, said it is wrong that tobacco is bought at low prices and independent farmers cannot sell their crops. He said Malawi does not produce too much tobacco. He said blaming low prices on too much supply is not fair. Abel Kalima Banda, a farmer leader, agreed. He said farmers need to sell their tobacco because farming is how they live. He called for talks to fix the rejection problem.

Since the market opened, about 22 million kilograms of tobacco have been sold. But only 8,000 kilograms came from independent farmers. This shows how little room there is for farmers who do not have contracts. Kambiri said many things cause the rejections, but the commission will help solve this. If this trend continues, independent farmers may be pushed out of tobacco farming completely. This is bad for rural families who depend on tobacco for food, school fees, and medical help.

ny.title: Ogula Fodya Akukana Mbewu Zochokera kwa Alimi Odziyimira Pawokha Pamtengo Wapamwamba
ny.body: Ogula fodya ku Malawi akukana fodya kuchokera kwa alimi odziyimira pawokha pamlingo wa 93 peresenti. Aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo adapita kumalo ogulitsira malonda Lachitatu ndipo adamva izi kuchokera kwa Alison Kambiri, manejala wa Tobacco Commission ku Lilongwe. Alimi odziyimira pawokha amalima fodya popanda mgwirizano. Ambiri akukumana ndi kutayika kwakukulu pambuyo pa miyezi yambiri yogwira ntchito komanso kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama pa mbewu ndi zida.
ny.body_more:
Bungwe la Tobacco Commission linadabwa ndi zomwe adapeza. Daniel Gunya, wapampando wa bungweli, anati kuchuluka kwa anthu omwe anakana kukana kunawadabwitsa. Anati bungweli lidzakumana ndi ogula ndi alimi kuti apeze njira yopitira patsogolo. Koma mamembala a Nyumba Yamalamulo omwe adawona malo ogulitsira malonda adakwiya komanso kukhumudwa. Anati zinthu sizili bwino.

Anthony Kamoto, wopanga malamulo, adati n'kulakwa kuti fodya igulidwe pamitengo yotsika ndipo alimi odziyimira pawokha sangagulitse mbewu zawo. Anati Malawi sipanga fodya wochuluka kwambiri. Anati kunena kuti mitengo yotsika ndi yotsika chifukwa cha kuchuluka kwa zinthu sikolungama. Abel Kalima Banda, mtsogoleri wa alimi, adavomereza. Anati alimi ayenera kugulitsa fodya wawo chifukwa ulimi ndi momwe amakhalira. Anapempha kuti pakhale zokambirana kuti akonze vuto la kukana.

Kuyambira pomwe msika unatsegulidwa, pafupifupi makilogalamu 22 miliyoni a fodya agulitsidwa. Koma makilogalamu 8,000 okha ndi omwe achokera kwa alimi odziyimira pawokha. Izi zikusonyeza kuti pali malo ochepa kwa alimi omwe alibe mapangano. Kambiri adati zinthu zambiri zimayambitsa kukana, koma bungweli lithandiza kuthetsa vutoli. Ngati izi zipitirira, alimi odziyimira pawokha akhoza kuchotsedwa kwathunthu mu ulimi wa fodya. Izi ndi zoyipa kwa mabanja akumidzi omwe amadalira fodya kuti apeze chakudya, ndalama zolipirira sukulu, komanso thandizo lachipatala.

en.title: Malawi must cut prices to reach 15 percent inflation by 2027
en.body: Malawi's inflation is now 23.4 percent and must fall to 15 percent by March 2027, says the Economics Association of Malawi. Food prices are the biggest problem. They make up more than half of all prices in the country. Food also adds 13.5 points to the inflation number. The central bank keeps interest rates high at 24 percent to slow prices down.
en.body_more:
The group says food prices must fall by 1.5 points every three months to reach the goal. But many things make this hard. Fertiliser costs are high. Transport is costly. The kwacha is weak. These things all push prices up.

Housing, water and electricity now cost much more. In 2023, these made up 18 percent of inflation. Now they make up 23.7 percent. This is because of high power bills. Transport costs also went up. In one quarter, transport rose by two points.

Economists say the path forward is fragile. One expert says the cost of living may stay high even if the inflation number goes down. He says real help for poor people may not come. The central bank says it will keep using its money tools to fight high prices. But experts warn that tight money may make it hard for people to get loans and start work.

Fix the problem, the group says, will need many actions at once. Prices that the state controls must be managed carefully. Money costs in business must fall. The kwacha must get stronger. Food supply must get better. Money policy alone cannot do all this work.

ny.title: Malawi iyenera kuchepetsa mitengo kuti ifike pa 15 peresenti pofika chaka cha 2027
ny.body: Kukwera kwa mitengo ku Malawi tsopano kuli pa 23.4 peresenti ndipo kuyenera kutsika kufika pa 15 peresenti pofika mu Marichi 2027, ikutero bungwe la Economics Association of Malawi. Mitengo ya chakudya ndiyo vuto lalikulu. Ndiwo omwe amapanga theka la mitengo yonse mdzikolo. Chakudya chimawonjezeranso mapointi 13.5 pa chiwerengero cha kukwera kwa mitengo. Banki yayikulu imasunga chiwongola dzanja chokwera pa 24 peresenti kuti mitengo ichepe.
ny.body_more:
Gululi likunena kuti mitengo ya chakudya iyenera kutsika ndi mapointi 1.5 miyezi itatu iliyonse kuti likwaniritse cholinga chake. Koma zinthu zambiri zimapangitsa izi kukhala zovuta. Mitengo ya feteleza ndi yokwera. Mayendedwe ndi okwera mtengo. Kwacha ndi yofooka. Zinthu zonsezi zimakweza mitengo.

Nyumba, madzi ndi magetsi tsopano zimadula kwambiri. Mu 2023, izi zidapanga 18 peresenti ya kukwera kwa mitengo. Tsopano zipanga 23.7 peresenti. Izi zili choncho chifukwa cha mabilu okwera amagetsi. Mitengo ya mayendedwe idakweranso. Mu kotala limodzi, mayendedwe adakwera ndi mapointi awiri.

Akatswiri azachuma akuti njira yopita patsogolo ndi yofooka. Katswiri wina akuti mtengo wa moyo ungakhalebe wokwera ngakhale chiwerengero cha kukwera kwa mitengo chikatsika. Iye akuti thandizo lenileni kwa anthu osauka silingabwere. Banki yayikulu ikunena kuti ipitiliza kugwiritsa ntchito zida zake zandalama kuti ithane ndi mitengo yokwera. Koma akatswiri akuchenjeza kuti ndalama zochepa zitha kupangitsa kuti anthu azivutika kupeza ngongole ndikuyamba ntchito.

Gululi likuti konzani vutoli, gululo likuti, lidzafunika kuchitapo kanthu nthawi imodzi. Mitengo yomwe boma limayang'anira iyenera kuyang'aniridwa mosamala. Mitengo yandalama mu bizinesi iyenera kutsika. Kwacha iyenera kulimba. Kupereka chakudya kuyenera kukhala bwino. Ndondomeko ya ndalama yokha singathe kugwira ntchito yonseyi.

en.title: Old Mutual Fund Gives Members 94% Return in 2025
en.body: Old Mutual Life Assurance Company said its Guaranteed Fund gave members a 94% return in 2025. This is much higher than the 37% return in 2024. Mark Mikwamba is the Managing Director. He said the company made good choices with money and wants to help members save for old age.
en.body_more:
Mikwamba said the 94% return is not just a number. It shows that the company's plan works well. He said the return came from good choices, a strong fund, and focus on keeping members' money safe. This matters because it helps people who are saving for retirement.

The Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund is the biggest retirement investment fund in Malawi. It has three parts: Segregated Funds, the Unrestricted Fund, and Preservation Fund, which is also called Protektor. These give members different ways to get their money.

Mikwamba said many funds do well when markets are good. But the real test is what happens when markets go bad. The Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund has a protection system. This system keeps members safe from big changes in markets, both up and down. This means members get steady returns over many years. That is what makes Old Mutual different from other funds.

Mikwamba said the company will keep working to give returns that are higher than inflation. Inflation means prices go up. If returns beat inflation, members' money grows and stays strong. The company says it will keep protecting members' savings in the future.

ny.title: Ndalama za Old Mutual Fund Zapatsa Mamembala 94% Phindu mu 2025
ny.body: Kampani ya Old Mutual Life Assurance inati Guaranteed Fund yake inapatsa mamembala phindu la 94% mu 2025. Izi ndi zokwera kwambiri kuposa phindu la 37% mu 2024. Mark Mikwamba ndi Managing Director. Iye anati kampaniyo idasankha bwino ndalama ndipo ikufuna kuthandiza mamembala kusunga ndalama zawo kuti apulumuke.
ny.body_more:
Mikwamba anati phindu la 94% si chiwerengero chokha. Zikusonyeza kuti dongosolo la kampaniyo likugwira ntchito bwino. Anati phinduli linachokera ku zisankho zabwino, thumba lamphamvu, komanso kuyang'ana kwambiri pakusunga ndalama za mamembala. Izi ndizofunikira chifukwa zimathandiza anthu omwe akusunga ndalama zopuma pantchito.

Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund ndi thumba lalikulu kwambiri losungira ndalama za penshoni ku Malawi. Lili ndi magawo atatu: Segregated Funds, Unrestricted Fund, ndi Preservation Fund, yomwe imatchedwanso Protektor. Izi zimapatsa mamembala njira zosiyanasiyana zopezera ndalama zawo.

Mikwamba anati thumba zambiri zimachita bwino misika ikakhala yabwino. Koma mayeso enieni ndi omwe amachitika misika ikayipa. Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund ili ndi njira yotetezera. Njirayi imateteza mamembala ku kusintha kwakukulu m'misika, mmwamba ndi pansi. Izi zikutanthauza kuti mamembala amapeza phindu lokhazikika kwa zaka zambiri. Ndicho chimene chimapangitsa Old Mutual kukhala yosiyana ndi thumba lina.

Mikwamba anati kampaniyo ipitiliza kugwira ntchito kuti ipereke phindu lokwera kuposa kukwera kwa mitengo. Kukwera kwa mitengo kumatanthauza kuti mitengo imakwera. Ngati phindu lipambana kukwera kwa mitengo, ndalama za mamembala zimakula ndikukhalabe zolimba. Kampaniyo ikunena kuti ipitiliza kuteteza ndalama zomwe mamembala amasunga mtsogolo.

en.title: MEPA lets Press Cane restart work after five months
en.body: The Malawi Environment Protection Authority (MEPA) has lifted a stop order on Press Cane Limited. The company makes ethanol and was shut down in January after waste water leaked into the land. MEPA checked the company's work in April and saw good progress. Press Cane paid 895 million kwacha to help the people who were hurt. The company can now start work again.
en.body_more:
MEPA gave the stop order on 26 January 2026 to stop more damage to the land. The company had to fix the waste water ponds, move dirt to make walls higher, and clean up the soil. The letter from MEPA boss Wilfred Kadewa on 18 May 2026 said Press Cane did good work. The company also built a plant to make the waste water clean and safe. No bad water will come out from making ethanol now.

Before Press Cane can work, MEPA says it must do more. The company must finish fixing all the waste water ponds. It must make the land good again so people can grow food there. The District Commissioner told MEPA which gardens need help. Press Cane must also pay for tests on the water in the area to show it is clean and safe.

Press Cane's boss, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, said the company is happy to start work again. He said they learned from what went wrong. "We will work hard to keep the land and water clean," he said. The company said it will follow all the rules and work with the people and the government to keep the area safe.

ny.title: MEPA yalola Press Cane kuyambanso kugwira ntchito patatha miyezi isanu
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Environment Protection Authority (MEPA) lachotsa lamulo loletsa ntchito ya Press Cane Limited. Kampaniyo imapanga ethanol ndipo inatsekedwa mu Januwale madzi atatayikira m'nthaka. MEPA inayang'ana ntchito ya kampaniyo mu Epulo ndipo inaona kuti ikupita patsogolo. Press Cane inalipira 895 miliyoni kwacha kuti ithandize anthu omwe anavulala. Kampaniyo tsopano ikhoza kuyambanso ntchito.
ny.body_more:
MEPA idapereka lamulo loletsa kuwononga nthaka pa 26 Januwale 2026. Kampaniyo idayenera kukonza maiwe amadzi otayira, kusuntha dothi kuti makoma akhale okwera, ndikuyeretsa nthaka. Kalata yochokera kwa bwana wa MEPA, Wilfred Kadewa, pa 18 Meyi 2026, idati Press Cane yachita bwino. Kampaniyo idamanganso fakitale kuti madzi otayira akhale oyera komanso otetezeka. Palibe madzi oipa omwe adzatuluke popanga ethanol tsopano.

Press Cane isanagwire ntchito, MEPA ikunena kuti iyenera kuchita zambiri. Kampaniyo iyenera kumaliza kukonza maiwe onse amadzi otayira. Iyenera kukonzanso malo kuti anthu athe kulima chakudya kumeneko. Mtsogoleri wa Chigawo adauza MEPA minda yomwe ikufunika thandizo. Press Cane iyeneranso kulipira mayeso pamadzi omwe ali m'derali kuti awonetse kuti ndi oyera komanso otetezeka.

Bwyson Mkhomaanthu, bwana wa Press Cane, adati kampaniyo ikusangalala kuyambanso ntchito. Anati adaphunzira kuchokera ku zomwe zidalakwika. "Tidzagwira ntchito molimbika kuti nthaka ndi madzi zikhale zoyera," adatero. Kampaniyo inati itsatira malamulo onse ndikugwira ntchito ndi anthu komanso boma kuti malowa akhale otetezeka.

en.title: Court Pauses Chihana's Suspension in Water Board Fight
en.body: The Supreme Court has stopped a High Court order that removed Yeremiah Chihana from his job at the Blantyre Water Board. The politician was appointed Chief Executive Officer on April 8, 2026. The board suspended him on April 26 after he made decisions without asking them first. A judge said Chihana went to the wrong court. The Supreme Court is now allowing him to stay in the job while the legal case continues.
en.body_more:
The High Court judge, Allan Muhome, ruled that Chihana's dispute is about his work contract. He said the Industrial Relations Court, not the High Court, should handle this case. Muhome wrote that the law made special courts for work problems. The High Court should not deal with these cases at first time, the judge said.

Chihana's lawyer, Michael Goba Chipeta, said they will take the case to the Supreme Court of Appeal. They want to change the High Court's decision. The board says Chihana did many things wrong. He told people the water board would give free water to sick people without asking the board first. He also said water kiosk operators did not need to pay old debts. The board says he had no right to say these things. Chihana also let someone who was not allowed to use his official car.

The case has caused big problems at the water board. The board is supposed to help many people in Malawi get clean water. But now the leaders are fighting in court instead of working. Chihana was told he could work until April 2029. The Supreme Court's choice means this fight will go on for more time.

ny.title: Khoti Layimitsa Kuyimitsidwa kwa Chihana pa Nkhondo ya Water Board
ny.body: Khoti Lalikulu laletsa lamulo la Khoti Lalikulu lomwe linachotsa Yeremiah Chihana pa ntchito yake ku Blantyre Water Board. Wandaleyu adasankhidwa kukhala Chief Executive Officer pa Epulo 8, 2026. Bungweli lidamuyimitsa ntchito pa Epulo 26 atapanga zisankho popanda kuzifunsa kaye. Woweruza adati Chihana adapita kukhoti lolakwika. Khoti Lalikulu tsopano likumulola kuti apitirize kugwira ntchitoyo pamene mlanduwo ukupitirira.
ny.body_more:
Woweruza wa Khoti Lalikulu, Allan Muhome, adagamula kuti mkangano wa Chihana ndi wokhudza pangano lake la ntchito. Anati Khoti Loona za Ubale wa Antchito, osati Khoti Lalikulu, ndilo liyenera kuyang'anira nkhaniyi. Muhome analemba kuti lamuloli lidapanga makhoti apadera a mavuto a ntchito. Khoti Lalikulu siliyenera kuthana ndi milandu iyi koyamba, woweruzayo adatero.

Loya wa Chihana, Michael Goba Chipeta, adati atenga nkhaniyi ku Khoti Lalikulu la Apilo. Akufuna kusintha chigamulo cha Khoti Lalikulu. Bungweli likuti Chihana adachita zinthu zambiri molakwika. Adauza anthu kuti bungwe la madzi lipereka madzi aulere kwa odwala popanda kufunsa bungweli kaye. Anatinso ogwira ntchito m'malo osungira madzi safunika kulipira ngongole zakale. Bungweli likuti analibe ufulu wonena izi. Chihana adalolanso munthu amene saloledwa kugwiritsa ntchito galimoto yake yovomerezeka.

Mlanduwu wabweretsa mavuto akulu ku bungwe la madzi. Bungweli likuyenera kuthandiza anthu ambiri ku Malawi kupeza madzi oyera. Koma tsopano atsogoleri akukangana kukhoti m'malo mogwira ntchito. Chihana adauzidwa kuti akhoza kugwira ntchito mpaka Epulo 2029. Kusankhidwa kwa Khoti Lalikulu kumatanthauza kuti nkhondoyi ipitilira kwa nthawi yochulukirapo.

en.title: New Malawi Firm Makes 50 Tonnes of Fertiliser Per Hour
en.body: A new company called Wait Holdings has started to make fertiliser in Blantyre. The plant makes 50 metric tonnes per hour. It is called MlimiFert. The company opened the plant in January 2026 at a place called Lunzu. The boss is Irene Mlundira. She said the plant can make 30,000 tonnes every month. The plant makes different types of fertiliser for different crops and soils.
en.body_more:
Mlundira said the company was made to stop Malawi from buying so much fertiliser from other countries. She said the plant has systems to blend the fertiliser, places to keep it, and ways to check if it is good. The plant also has a lab to test soil. This helps farmers know what fertiliser they need. It can lower waste and help soil stay good for many years.

The company will spend $5 million to make the plant bigger. They will also open new shops in Lilongwe and Mzuzu this year. Malawi needs between 450,000 and 475,000 tonnes of fertiliser every year. But the government has had trouble buying it from other lands because of money problems. The plant makes fertiliser for maize, tobacco, tea, beans, fruits and other crops.

One expert, Tamani Nkhono-Mvula, said this is good for Malawi. He said Malawi buys fertiliser from Russia and the Middle East, which is far away. This can cause big problems. Herbert Chagona, who leads a farm group, also said this is good. He said the plant shows that people want to put money into fertiliser work in Malawi.

The fertiliser made here fits the soil and crops of Malawi. This means farmers can use less and grow more. The plant will serve big farms, small farms, and groups that buy fertiliser. Wait Holdings is a Malawi-owned firm. It was made to help Malawi make its own fertiliser and add more value to farm work.

ny.title: Kampani Yatsopano ku Malawi Imapanga Matani 50 a Feteleza Pa Ola Lililonse
ny.body: Kampani yatsopano yotchedwa Wait Holdings yayamba kupanga feteleza ku Blantyre. Fakitaleyi imapanga matani 50 pa ola limodzi. Imatchedwa MlimiFert. Kampaniyo idatsegula fakitaleyi mu Januwale 2026 pamalo otchedwa Lunzu. Bwana wake ndi Irene Mlundira. Iye adati fakitaleyi imatha kupanga matani 30,000 pamwezi. Fakitaleyi imapanga mitundu yosiyanasiyana ya feteleza wa mbewu ndi nthaka zosiyanasiyana.
ny.body_more:
Mlundira anati kampaniyo idapangidwa kuti ilepheretse Malawi kugula feteleza wambiri kuchokera kumayiko ena. Iye anati fakitaleyi ili ndi njira zosakaniza feteleza, malo osungira, ndi njira zowunikira ngati ili yabwino. Kampaniyi ilinso ndi labotale yoyesera nthaka. Izi zimathandiza alimi kudziwa feteleza omwe amafunikira. Zingathandize kuchepetsa zinyalala ndikuthandizira nthaka kukhala yabwino kwa zaka zambiri.

Kampaniyo idzagwiritsa ntchito $5 miliyoni kuti chomeracho chikhale chachikulu. Adzatsegulanso masitolo atsopano ku Lilongwe ndi Mzuzu chaka chino. Malawi ikufunika matani pakati pa 450,000 ndi 475,000 a feteleza chaka chilichonse. Koma boma lakhala likuvutika kugula feteleza kuchokera kumayiko ena chifukwa cha mavuto azachuma. Kampaniyi imapanga feteleza wa chimanga, fodya, tiyi, nyemba, zipatso ndi mbewu zina.

Katswiri wina, Tamani Nkhono-Mvula, adati izi ndi zabwino kwa Malawi. Iye anati Malawi imagula feteleza kuchokera ku Russia ndi Middle East, komwe kuli kutali kwambiri. Izi zitha kuyambitsa mavuto akulu. Herbert Chagona, yemwe akutsogolera gulu la alimi, adatinso izi ndi zabwino. Iye anati chomerachi chikusonyeza kuti anthu akufuna kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama pochita feteleza ku Malawi.

Feteleza wopangidwa pano umagwirizana ndi nthaka ndi mbewu za ku Malawi. Izi zikutanthauza kuti alimi angagwiritse ntchito zochepa ndikulima zambiri. Chomerachi chidzatumikira minda ikuluikulu, minda ing'onoing'ono, ndi magulu omwe amagula feteleza. Wait Holdings ndi kampani ya ku Malawi. Inapangidwa kuti ithandize Malawi kupanga feteleza yakeyake ndikuwonjezera phindu pantchito zaulimi.

en.title: Police and army raid Msundwe trading centre, arrest 14
en.body: Police and army officers came to Msundwe trading centre early yesterday morning. They arrested 14 people. The officers were heavily armed. Some wore riot gear. They stopped cars at roadblocks. They searched homes and shops. They took ethanol, fake alcohol, gas bottles and other things. Msundwe is about 40 kilometres west of Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
The operation involved many police officers and soldiers. Residents woke up to see armed men in the streets. Some people said this was the biggest security action they have seen in many years. Two residents spoke about what they saw. Malita Kakudzala said she saw many police but did not know what was happening. Chikaiko Damson, a trader, said business went on but people were worried. He heard some people were arrested but was not sure what they did.

The 14 arrested people are 13 men and one woman. Police say they broke laws. They say the people obstructed police work. They had things that may have been stolen. They had gas bottles without permission. They made fake alcohol and fake trademark goods. Police took the goods but did not say how much they were worth. The arrested people are at Lilongwe Police Station now.

Police deputy spokesman Alfred Chimthere said the raid went as planned. He said there was no fighting. He said the area is calm and safe. He said police and the army are working together to make Msundwe safer. Some people on social media asked if this was about former president Lazarus Chakwera or if it was political trouble. Chimthere said he would not talk about this. He said the operation was about crime only.

Msundwe has a long history of crime and unrest. After the 2019 election, there was violence in the area. Jean Sendeza, a former member of parliament, said she saw more security in the area but was still getting details about what happened.

ny.title: Apolisi ndi asilikali alowa m'malo ogulitsira ku Msundwe, ndipo agwira anthu 14
ny.body: Apolisi ndi akuluakulu ankhondo anabwera ku malo ogulitsira ku Msundwe m'mawa kwambiri dzulo. Anagwira anthu 14. Apolisiwo anali ndi zida zambiri. Ena anavala zida zachiwawa. Anayimitsa magalimoto pamalo otsekeredwa magalimoto. Anafufuza m'nyumba ndi m'masitolo. Anatenga ethanol, mowa wabodza, mabotolo a gasi ndi zina. Msundwe ili pamtunda wa makilomita 40 kumadzulo kwa Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Ntchitoyi inakhudza apolisi ndi asilikali ambiri. Anthu okhala m'deralo anadzuka ndikuona amuna okhala ndi zida m'misewu. Anthu ena anati iyi inali njira yaikulu kwambiri yotetezera yomwe adaiwona m'zaka zambiri. Anthu awiri okhala m'deralo analankhula za zomwe adawona. Malita Kakudzala anati adawona apolisi ambiri koma sankadziwa zomwe zikuchitika. Chikaiko Damson, wamalonda, anati bizinesi idapitilira koma anthu anali ndi nkhawa. Anamva kuti anthu ena amangidwa koma sanadziwe zomwe adachita.

Anthu 14 omwe adamangidwa ndi amuna 13 ndi mkazi m'modzi. Apolisi akuti adaswa malamulo. Akuti anthuwa adaletsa ntchito ya apolisi. Anali ndi zinthu zomwe mwina zidabedwa. Anali ndi mabotolo a gasi popanda chilolezo. Amapanga mowa wabodza ndi zinthu zabodza. Apolisi adatenga katunduyo koma sananene kuti ndi wofunika bwanji. Anthu omwe adamangidwawo ali ku Lilongwe Police Station tsopano.

Wachiwiri kwa wolankhulira apolisi Alfred Chimthere adati kuukiraku kudachitika monga momwe adakonzera. Anati panalibe nkhondo. Anati derali ndi bata komanso lotetezeka. Anati apolisi ndi asilikali akugwira ntchito limodzi kuti Msundwe akhale otetezeka. Anthu ena pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti anafunsa ngati izi zinali zokhudza purezidenti wakale Lazarus Chakwera kapena ngati zinali zovuta zandale. Chimthere anati sakanalankhula za izi. Anati ntchitoyi inali yokhudza umbanda wokha.

Msundwe wakhala ndi mbiri yayitali ya umbanda ndi chisokonezo. Pambuyo pa chisankho cha 2019, panali chiwawa m'derali. Jean Sendeza, yemwe kale anali membala wa nyumba yamalamulo, anati akuona chitetezo chambiri m'derali koma akupezabe tsatanetsatane wa zomwe zinachitika.

en.title: PressCane pays K895m to farmers hit by chemical spill
en.body: PressCane Limited gave K895 million to Chikwawa District Council on Monday. The money will help farmers whose crops died in a chemical spill. The spill came from the company's waste ponds. It happened when crops were almost ready to pick. Many families lost a lot of money.
en.body_more:
The government told PressCane to pay this money. Two groups ordered the action: the Malawi Environmental Protection Authority and the National Water Resources Authority. They checked what went wrong for many months before this.

PressCane's leader, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, said the company is sorry. He said they obeyed the rules now. The company fixed the waste ponds and made them stronger. They added more space at the top of the ponds. They put in wells to check the water. They also closed some old ponds.

Chikwawa's government leader, Frank Mkandawire, got the money for the farmers. He said the wait is over. The spill came at the worst time—when the crops were about to be ready. It hurt many families very much. Now the families can get help and plant again next season.

The spill was one of the worst things that happened to Chikwawa farmers this year. It came when they needed their crops the most. The money will help them buy seeds and tools for next year.

ny.title: PressCane yalipira K895 miliyoni kwa alimi omwe akhudzidwa ndi kutayikira kwa mankhwala
ny.body: Kampani ya PressCane Limited yapereka K895 miliyoni ku Khonsolo ya Chikwawa District Council Lolemba. Ndalamazi zithandiza alimi omwe mbewu zawo zinafa chifukwa cha mankhwala omwe anatayikira. Kutayikiraku kunachokera m'madamu a zinyalala a kampaniyo. Izi zinachitika pamene mbewu zinali zitatsala pang'ono kukolola. Mabanja ambiri anataya ndalama zambiri.
ny.body_more:
Boma linauza PressCane kuti ilipire ndalamazi. Magulu awiri analamula kuti achite izi: Malawi Environmental Protection Authority ndi National Water Resources Authority. Anayang'ana zomwe zinalakwika kwa miyezi yambiri izi zisanachitike.

Mtsogoleri wa PressCane, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, anati kampaniyo yapepesa. Anati tsopano atsatira malamulo. Kampaniyo inakonza maiwe otayira zinyalala ndikuwapatsa mphamvu. Anawonjezera malo ambiri pamwamba pa maiwe. Anaika zitsime kuti ayang'anire madzi. Anatsekanso maiwe akale.

Mtsogoleri wa boma la Chikwawa, Frank Mkandawire, ndiye anapezera alimi ndalamazo. Anati kudikira kwatha. Kutayikiraku kunabwera panthawi yoyipa kwambiri—pamene mbewu zinali pafupi kukonzeka. Kunavulaza mabanja ambiri kwambiri. Tsopano mabanja akhoza kupeza thandizo ndikubzalanso nyengo yamawa.

Kutayikiraku kunali chimodzi mwa zinthu zoyipa kwambiri zomwe zinachitikira alimi a Chikwawa chaka chino. Kunabwera pamene anafunikira kwambiri mbewu zawo. Ndalamazo zidzawathandiza kugula mbewu ndi zida za chaka chamawa.

en.title: Parliament starts new probe into 2024 plane crash
en.body: A government committee has started a new inquiry into a plane crash that killed nine people in June 2024. The crash happened near Mzuzu in central Malawi. It killed former vice-president Saulos Chilima and eight others, including military workers and the former first lady Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri. The committee visited Kamuzu International Airport in Lilongwe on Monday to start its work.
en.body_more:
The committee is led by Walter Nyamilandu Manda. He said the team wants to find new information that earlier inquiries did not find. "There are certain areas that are gray," Nyamilandu said. "There are some inconsistencies and irregularities where things are not making any sense." He said the committee will dig deeper before asking witnesses to speak.

On Monday, committee members met with airport workers at Kamuzu International Airport. They visited the Air Traffic Control Tower where pilots file flight plans before they leave. They also met with people from the civil aviation office and airport companies. These visits help the committee understand how the airport works and what happened before the plane took off on June 10, 2024.

Yesterday, the committee visited the crash site in Nthungwa Forest in Nkhata Bay, where the Dornier aircraft went down. Many senior government workers went to the site, including the Inspector General of Police, the Secretary for Health, and military officials. The committee wants to see exactly where the plane crashed and understand the final moments of the flight.

Nyamilandu said the committee hopes to finish its work by August 2026. The team plans to visit Mzuzu Airport next and then go to Cobbe Barracks in Zomba, where parts of the aircraft are kept. An earlier inquiry by German experts said bad weather and low visibility caused the crash, but many people in Malawi want answers from a local investigation.

ny.title: Nyumba Yamalamulo Yayamba Kufufuza Kwatsopano Pa Ngozi Ya Ndege Ya 2024
ny.body: Komiti ya boma yayambitsa kafukufuku watsopano pa ngozi ya ndege yomwe inapha anthu asanu ndi anayi mu June 2024. Ngoziyi inachitikira pafupi ndi Mzuzu m'chigawo chapakati cha Malawi. Inapha wachiwiri kwa purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu, kuphatikizapo ogwira ntchito zankhondo komanso mayi woyamba wakale Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri. Komitiyi idapita ku bwalo la ndege la Kamuzu International Airport ku Lilongwe Lolemba kuti iyambe ntchito yake.
ny.body_more:
Komitiyi ikutsogoleredwa ndi Walter Nyamilandu Manda. Iye anati gululi likufuna kupeza mfundo zatsopano zomwe kafukufuku wakale sanapeze. "Pali madera ena omwe ndi olakwika," adatero Nyamilandu. "Pali kusagwirizana ndi zolakwika zina zomwe zinthu sizikumveka bwino." Iye anati komitiyi idzafufuza mozama asanapemphe mboni kuti zilankhule.

Lolemba, mamembala a komitiyi adakumana ndi ogwira ntchito ku eyapoti ku Kamuzu International Airport. Adapita ku Air Traffic Control Tower komwe oyendetsa ndege amalemba mapulani a ndege asananyamuke. Adakumananso ndi anthu ochokera ku ofesi ya ndege za anthu wamba ndi makampani aku eyapoti. Maulendo awa amathandiza komitiyi kumvetsetsa momwe eyapoti imagwirira ntchito komanso zomwe zinachitika ndegeyo isananyamuke pa June 10, 2024.

Dzulo, komitiyi idapita ku malo omwe ndege ya Dornier idagwera. Antchito ambiri aboma adapita kumalowa, kuphatikiza Inspector General wa apolisi, Secretary of Health, ndi akuluakulu ankhondo. Komitiyi ikufuna kuwona komwe ndegeyo idagwera ndikumvetsetsa mphindi zomaliza za ulendowo.

Nyamilandu adati komitiyi ikuyembekeza kumaliza ntchito yake pofika mu Ogasiti 2026. Gululi likukonzekera kupita ku eyapoti ya Mzuzu kenako kupita ku Cobbe Barracks ku Zomba, komwe kuli zida zina za ndegeyo. Kafukufuku wakale wa akatswiri aku Germany adati nyengo yoipa komanso kusawona bwino kwa ndegeyo ndi zomwe zidayambitsa ngoziyi, koma anthu ambiri ku Malawi akufuna mayankho kuchokera ku kafukufuku wakomweko.

en.title: Escom asks public to stop vandals stealing power equipment
en.body: The Electricity Supply Corporation of Malawi (Escom) has asked the public for help. Between October 2025 and March 2026, vandals damaged 40 transformers and cables. The damage costs Escom about K3 billion each year to fix. A single transformer costs K20 million. Escom says this is a big crisis. The parastatal has made 59 arrests and got 10 convictions so far.
en.body_more:
Escom's chief public relations officer Pilirani Phiri said the problem is very serious. He said that K3 billion is for the broken equipment only. It does not count the money lost when people have no power. Businesses close, machines stop, and hospitals cannot work well. He said this money could help poor areas get electricity instead. Phiri said Escom has started a 30-day plan to tell people about the problem and stop the thieves.

In April 2026 alone, five transformers were damaged. Three were in Lilongwe, one in Blantyre, and one in Chiradzulu. In May, one transformer was damaged in Zomba. Another was damaged on the Blantyre-Chikwawa Road. A security expert said the vandals must be people who work or worked at Escom. They know how the equipment works, he said.

Doctors say the broken transformers hurt hospitals. When there is no power, mothers cannot have babies safely. Babies are at risk. The cold boxes for vaccines break. Blood tests cannot happen. The new Electricity Act says vandals can go to jail for 30 years. There is no fine. Escom says it needs everyone to help—communities, leaders, and business people must protect the transformers. These are public things. They help all people.

ny.title: Escom yapempha anthu kuti asiye kuba zipangizo zamagetsi
ny.body: Kampani Yopereka Magetsi ku Malawi (Escom) yapempha thandizo kwa anthu onse. Pakati pa Okutobala 2025 ndi Marichi 2026, anthu owononga zinthu anawononga ma transformer ndi zingwe 40. Kuwonongeka kumeneku kumawononga ndalama zokwana K3 biliyoni chaka chilichonse kuti akonze. Transformer imodzi imawononga K20 miliyoni. Escom ikunena kuti vutoli ndi lalikulu. Bungwe la boma lamanga anthu 59 ndipo lapeza milandu 10 mpaka pano.
ny.body_more:
Mkulu wa bungwe la Escom, Pilirani Phiri, anati vutoli ndi lalikulu kwambiri. Anati K3 biliyoni ndi ya zipangizo zosweka zokha. Siziwerengera ndalama zomwe zatayika pamene anthu alibe magetsi. Mabizinesi amatseka, makina amasiya, ndipo zipatala sizingagwire ntchito bwino. Anati ndalamazi zingathandize madera osauka kupeza magetsi m'malo mwake. Phiri anati Escom yayamba dongosolo la masiku 30 louza anthu za vutoli ndikuletsa akuba.

Mu Epulo 2026 yokha, ma transformer asanu anawonongeka. Atatu anali ku Lilongwe, limodzi ku Blantyre, ndi limodzi ku Chiradzulu. Mu Meyi, transformer imodzi inawonongeka ku Zomba. Ina inawonongeka pa msewu wa Blantyre-Chikwawa. Katswiri wa zachitetezo anati owononga ayenera kukhala anthu ogwira ntchito kapena ogwira ntchito ku Escom. Amadziwa momwe zipangizozi zimagwirira ntchito, anatero.

Madokotala amati ma transformer osweka amavulaza zipatala. Pamene palibe magetsi, amayi sangabereke ana mosamala. Makanda ali pachiwopsezo. Mabokosi ozizira a katemera amasweka. Kuyezetsa magazi sikungachitike. Lamulo latsopano la Magetsi limati anthu owononga zinthu akhoza kumangidwa zaka 30. Palibe chindapusa. Escom ikunena kuti ikufunika aliyense kuti athandize—madera, atsogoleri, ndi amalonda ayenera kuteteza ma transformer. Izi ndi zinthu za anthu onse. Zimathandiza anthu onse.

en.title: Lake Malawi Water Rises. Homes and Resorts Are Flooded.
en.body: Lake Malawi is getting bigger. Water is now very high. It covers beaches, homes and tourist places. The water level is 476.41 metres above sea level. This is close to the highest ever level in 1980. Rain has fallen a lot for many seasons. Now people are worried. Families must leave their homes.
en.body_more:
In Mangochi, many resorts have water inside the rooms and buildings. The sandy beaches are gone. Some homes cannot be used. Two areas called Ngalamu and Mapila have lost many families. About 30 families live in one school now. They left their homes because of the water. In Karonga, homes, roads and schools near the lake are broken or will break soon. Fishing areas have no fish to catch. In Likoma Island, about 50 homes in three villages are gone under water.

Farmers are also hurt. Along the Shire River in Chikwawa, the water from the lake floods the fields. Crops are under water. Fishers cannot go to the lake. They cannot work. The National Water Resource Authority says this happens because rain came many times. The lake is very big, so water stays in it for a long time. When rain comes again the next year, the water does not go down.

The lake is now at a very high level. Even a small amount of rain will flood more areas. People try to move water out through the Kamuzu Barrage, but they cannot move too much. If they move too much water, it will flood other places downstream. Between 2012 and 2017, the water was much lower. But for five years now, it keeps going up. Visitors, farmers, fishers and families all wait and hope the water stops.

ny.title: Madzi a Nyanja ya Malawi Akukwera. Nyumba ndi Malo Ochitirako Maholide Zasefukira.
ny.body: Nyanja ya Malawi ikukulirakulira. Madzi tsopano ndi okwera kwambiri. Amaphimba magombe, nyumba ndi malo oyendera alendo. Madzi ali pamtunda wa mamita 476.41 pamwamba pa nyanja. Izi zili pafupi ndi mtunda wapamwamba kwambiri mu 1980. Mvula yagwa kwambiri kwa nyengo zambiri. Tsopano anthu akuda nkhawa. Mabanja ayenera kusiya nyumba zawo.
ny.body_more:
Ku Mangochi, malo ambiri opumulirako ali ndi madzi mkati mwa zipinda ndi nyumba. Magombe amchenga atha. Nyumba zina sizingagwiritsidwe ntchito. Madera awiri otchedwa Ngalamu ndi Mapila atayika mabanja ambiri. Mabanja pafupifupi 30 amakhala kusukulu imodzi tsopano. Achoka m'nyumba zawo chifukwa cha madzi. Ku Karonga, nyumba, misewu ndi masukulu pafupi ndi nyanja zasweka kapena zidzasweka posachedwa. Malo osodza nsomba alibe nsomba zoti agwire. Ku Likoma Island, nyumba pafupifupi 50 m'midzi itatu zathedwa madzi.

Alimi nawonso avulala. M'mphepete mwa Mtsinje wa Shire ku Chikwawa, madzi ochokera ku nyanjayi amasefukira m'minda. Mbewu zili pansi pa madzi. Asodzi sangathe kupita ku nyanjayi. Sangagwire ntchito. Bungwe la National Water Resource Authority limati izi zimachitika chifukwa mvula imabwera kangapo. Nyanjayi ndi yayikulu kwambiri, kotero madzi amakhala mmenemo kwa nthawi yayitali. Mvula ikabweranso chaka chamawa, madzi satha.

Nyanjayi tsopano ili pamlingo wapamwamba kwambiri. Ngakhale mvula yochepa idzasefukira m'madera ambiri. Anthu amayesa kusuntha madzi kudzera mu Kamuzu Barrage, koma sangathe kusuntha kwambiri. Ngati asuntha madzi ambiri, adzasefukira m'malo ena omwe ali pansi pa mtsinje. Pakati pa 2012 ndi 2017, madzi anali otsika kwambiri. Koma kwa zaka zisanu tsopano, akupitirira kukwera. Alendo, alimi, asodzi ndi mabanja onse akudikira ndipo akuyembekeza kuti madziwo asiye.

en.title: Sunbird wins beach soccer title with 4-1 win
en.body: Sunbird Nkopola from Mangochi has won the 2025-26 beach soccer championship. They beat Peninsula Stars of Karonga 4-1 in the final at Arnold Lakeshore Resort in Nkhotakota. The game took place on Saturday and Sunday. Sunbird got a trophy, gold medal, and K9 million in prize money. Isaac Jamu, Sandram Saddie, Martin Biliati and Achraf Saidi scored for Sunbird. Vincent Mwanyongo scored for Peninsula Stars.
en.body_more:
To reach the final, Sunbird beat hosts Vinthenga FC 8-4 in the semi-final. Peninsula Stars won their semi-final on penalties, beating Umodzi 4-3 after a 5-5 draw in normal time. Chintheche Beach Eagles, who were the old champions, lost in the group stage. They had won the title three times in a row before this year.

Prizes went to the top three teams. Peninsula Stars got a silver medal and K5 million. Umodzi of Salima came third and got K2.5 million. The best players also got money. Isaac Jamu of Sunbird was named Player of the Tournament. Mackson Mzembe of Peninsula Stars was Best Goalkeeper. Yusuf Issa of Umodzi and Isaac Jamu were joint top scorers with nine goals each. Each of these players got K50,000.

FAM Vice President Christopher Madalitso Kuyera said the tournament was very competitive and well run. He said he will ask FAM President Fleetwood Haiya for more money for the next tournament. Beach Soccer Chairperson False Ngwira said beach soccer is growing in Malawi. The group wants to bring the sport to more districts, like Likoma Island. Willy Kumilambe, coach of the national team, said the tournament helped him find new players for the Beach Soccer Africa Cup of Nations qualifiers in September.

ny.title: Sunbird yapambana chikho cha mpira wa m'mphepete mwa nyanja ndi chigonjetso cha 4-1
ny.body: Sunbird Nkopola wa ku Mangochi wapambana mpikisano wa mpira wa m'mphepete mwa nyanja wa 2025-26. Adagonjetsa Peninsula Stars ya ku Karonga 4-1 pa mpikisano womaliza ku Arnold Lakeshore Resort ku Nkhotakota. Masewerawa adachitika Loweruka ndi Lamlungu. Sunbird adapeza chikho, mendulo yagolide, ndi ndalama zokwana K9 miliyoni. Isaac Jamu, Sandram Saddie, Martin Biliati ndi Achraf Saidi adagoletsa Sunbird ndipo Vincent Mwanyongo adagoletsa Peninsula Stars.
ny.body_more:
Kuti afike kumapeto, Sunbird idagonjetsa Vinthenga FC yomwe ili ndi timu yake 8-4 mu semi-final. Peninsula Stars idapambana semi-final yawo ndi ma penalty, ndikugonjetsa Umodzi 4-3 pambuyo pa 5-5 motsatizana. Chintheche Beach Eagles, omwe anali akale opambana, adataya mu gawo lamagulu. Adapambana mutu katatu motsatizana chaka chino chisanafike.

Mphoto zidapita ku magulu atatu apamwamba. Peninsula Stars idapeza mendulo yasiliva ndi K5 miliyoni. Umodzi wa ku Salima adabwera pachitatu ndipo adapeza K2.5 miliyoni. Osewera abwino kwambiri adapezanso ndalama. Isaac Jamu wa ku Sunbird adasankhidwa kukhala Wosewera wa Mpikisano. Mackson Mzembe wa ku Peninsula Stars anali Woyang'anira Zigoli Wabwino Kwambiri. Yusuf Issa wa ku Umodzi ndi Isaac Jamu anali osewera otsogola omwe ali ndi zigoli zisanu ndi zinayi aliyense. Aliyense wa osewerawa adapeza K50,000.

Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wa FAM Christopher Madalitso Kuyera adati mpikisanowu unali wopikisana kwambiri komanso woyendetsedwa bwino. Anati adzapempha Purezidenti wa FAM Fleetwood Haiya ndalama zambiri pa mpikisano wotsatira. Wapampando wa Mpira wa M'nyanja False Ngwira anati mpira wa m'nyanja ukukula ku Malawi. Gululi likufuna kubweretsa masewerawa m'madera ambiri, monga ku Likoma Island. Willy Kumilambe, mphunzitsi wa timu ya dziko, anati mpikisanowu unamuthandiza kupeza osewera atsopano pamasewera oyenerera a Beach Soccer Africa Cup of Nations mu Seputembala.

en.title: Over 2,400 Malawians flee South Africa over attacks
en.body: More than 2,400 Malawians have come back home from South Africa in just a few weeks. They are leaving because people are attacking foreign workers there. At two border posts, officials counted 1,672 people at Dedza and 777 at Mwanza. This is much more than normal. The attacks are making people very afraid.
en.body_more:
William Kalanje, a person from the immigration office, said the numbers are not normal. "In December we see many people come home for the holidays. But now we see much more," he said. Sophie Chibenthu, another immigration worker, said Mwanza gets about 400 people each week in normal times. Now it gets more. Seven buses come each week with about 490 people sent back from South Africa.

Malawians went to South Africa to find work and make money. But now they are coming back because they are afraid. They face attacks and bad treatment there. One group working for peace in Malawi said the people who go home are doing the right thing. "Safety is more important than money," the group said. An expert on money and jobs said Malawi has big problems. "People have no money and no jobs here. The government must make new jobs if we want to stop people from leaving," he said.

Violence against foreign people in South Africa has been going on for many years. A group that watches these attacks said more than 660 people have died since 1994. More than 125,000 people have lost their homes. A movement called Operation Dudula says foreign workers take South African jobs. South Africa's leader, Cyril Ramaphosa, said he is against these attacks. But the violence keeps going. The Malawi government said it will help about 200 people who want to come back home from South Africa.

ny.title: Anthu opitilira 2,400 aku Malawi athawa ku South Africa chifukwa cha ziwawa
ny.body: Anthu aku Malawi opitilira 2,400 abwerera kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa m'masabata ochepa chabe. Akuchoka chifukwa anthu akuukira ogwira ntchito akunja kumeneko. Pamalire awiri, akuluakulu a boma anawerengera anthu 1,672 ku Dedza ndi 777 ku Mwanza. Izi ndi zachilendo kwambiri. Ziwopsezozi zikuchititsa anthu mantha kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
William Kalanje, munthu wochokera ku ofesi yoona za anthu olowa m'dziko, anati ziwerengerozi si zachilendo. "Mu Disembala timaona anthu ambiri akubwera kunyumba pa tchuthi. Koma tsopano tikuwona zambiri," adatero. Sophie Chibenthu, wogwira ntchito wina wolowa m'dziko, anati Mwanza imalandira anthu pafupifupi 400 sabata iliyonse nthawi zonse. Tsopano ikupeza ambiri. Mabasi asanu ndi awiri amabwera sabata iliyonse ndipo anthu pafupifupi 490 abwerera kuchokera ku South Africa.

Anthu aku Malawi adapita ku South Africa kukafunafuna ntchito ndikupeza ndalama. Koma tsopano akubweranso chifukwa akuopa. Amakumana ndi ziwawa ndi nkhanza kumeneko. Gulu lina logwira ntchito yolimbikitsa mtendere ku Malawi linati anthu omwe amapita kunyumba akuchita bwino. "Chitetezo n'chofunika kwambiri kuposa ndalama," adatero gululo. Katswiri wa ndalama ndi ntchito adati Malawi ili ndi mavuto akulu. "Anthu alibe ndalama ndipo alibe ntchito kuno. Boma liyenera kupanga ntchito zatsopano ngati tikufuna kuletsa anthu kuti asachoke," adatero.

Chiwawa chokhudza anthu akunja ku South Africa chakhala chikuchitika kwa zaka zambiri. Gulu lomwe limaonera ziwopsezo izi lati anthu opitilira 660 afa kuyambira mu 1994. Anthu opitilira 125,000 ataya nyumba zawo. Gulu lotchedwa Operation Dudula limati ogwira ntchito ochokera kumayiko ena akutenga ntchito ku South Africa. Mtsogoleri wa South Africa, Cyril Ramaphosa, adati akutsutsana ndi ziwopsezozi. Koma chiwawa chikupitirirabe. Boma la Malawi lati lithandiza anthu pafupifupi 200 omwe akufuna kubwerera kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa.

en.title: New team begins work on plane crash that killed vice-president
en.body: A group from Parliament started work today on a plane crash that killed former Vice-President Saulos Chilima and eight other people. The crash happened on June 10, 2024, near Nthungwa in Chikangawa Forest. A military plane left Kamuzu Airport in Lilongwe that morning. It was going to Mzuzu Airport but crashed. Everyone on the plane died. Now Parliament wants to learn what happened.
en.body_more:
The committee is led by Walter Nyamilandu Manda. Today the team visited Kamuzu Airport to look at records and ask questions. Over the next few days, they will go to the crash site, Mzuzu Airport, and Zomba, where the broken plane is kept. The committee wants to see everything with their own eyes and gather facts.

The nine people who died were Chilima, former first lady Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, and seven others. Some were soldiers. They were flying to a funeral for a former minister.
