Two earlier groups already looked at this crash. They said bad weather and the plane's use caused the crash. But many people still had questions. They wanted to know more about what happened.

Parliament gave the new committee 90 days to finish its work. The group has 13 members from different political parties. They opened a hotline so people can send in new information. Experts say this inquiry must be done well and fairly. One person said it is a test of how honest and open the government can be. The committee will also look at how the plane was kept in good condition and how the flight was run.

ny.title: Gulu latsopano layamba ntchito yofufuza ngozi ya ndege yomwe inapha wachiwiri kwa purezidenti
ny.body: Gulu la Nyumba Yamalamulo layamba ntchito lero pa ngozi ya ndege yomwe inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu. Ngoziyi inachitika pa June 10, 2024, pafupi ndi Nthungwa ku Nkhalango ya Chikangawa. Ndege yankhondo inachoka pa eyapoti ya Kamuzu ku Lilongwe m'mawa womwewo. Inkapita ku eyapoti ya Mzuzu koma inagwa. Aliyense amene anali mu ndegeyo anamwalira. Tsopano Nyumba Yamalamulo ikufuna kudziwa zomwe zinachitika.
ny.body_more:
Komitiyi ikutsogozedwa ndi Walter Nyamilandu Manda. Lero gululi lapita ku Kamuzu Airport kukayang'ana zolemba ndikufunsa mafunso. M'masiku angapo otsatira, apita ku Mzuzu Airport, ndi Zomba, komwe ndegeyo yasweka. Komitiyi ikufuna kuona chilichonse ndi maso awo ndikusonkhanitsa mfundo.

Anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe anamwalira anali Chilima, mayi woyamba wakale Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, ndi ena asanu ndi awiri. Ena anali asilikali. Anali kuuluka kupita ku maliro a nduna yakale. Magulu awiri oyambirira adayang'ana kale ngoziyi. Adati nyengo yoipa ndi kugwiritsidwa ntchito kwa ndegeyo ndi zomwe zidayambitsa ngoziyi. Koma anthu ambiri anali ndi mafunso. Amafuna kudziwa zambiri za zomwe zinachitika.

Nyumba yamalamulo idapatsa komiti yatsopano masiku 90 kuti amalize ntchito yake. Gululi lili ndi mamembala 13 ochokera m'magulu osiyanasiyana andale. Adatsegula foni yolumikizira anthu kuti anthu athe kutumiza zambiri zatsopano. Akatswiri amati kafukufukuyu ayenera kuchitidwa bwino komanso mwachilungamo. Munthu wina adati ndi mayeso a momwe boma lingakhalire loona mtima komanso lotseguka. Komitiyi idzawonanso momwe ndegeyo idasungidwira bwino komanso momwe ndegeyo idayendera.

en.title: Malawi marks World Hypertension Day, urges blood pressure tests
en.body: Malawi joined the world on May 17 to mark World Hypertension Day. Health experts warned that many people in Malawi have high blood pressure but do not know it. The theme was "Controlling Hypertension Together". Events were held in villages and towns. Experts said about 32 percent of Malawian adults have high blood pressure. Nearly half of these people do not know they are sick.
en.body_more:
High blood pressure is called the silent killer because it can cause no signs. People can have it for years and feel well. But it can damage the heart, blood vessels and kidneys. It can lead to stroke, heart disease and kidney failure. Health workers said many people only go to the hospital when they feel very sick, but by then it is too late.

Health experts said most causes of high blood pressure can be stopped. They said people can change their habits. Tobacco smoking, alcohol abuse, no physical activity and too much salt in food are the main causes. One man, Siveli Aliyeri, was 53 years old and had very high blood pressure from drinking and smoking. He changed his life. He stopped drinking and smoking. He ate less oily and salty food. His blood pressure became normal again. He said he could not even ride a bicycle for a short way without falling off. Now he is much better.

Health groups are working in villages to find people with high blood pressure early. Community volunteers check blood pressure and teach people. The government's health workers also help. Leaders in the villages support this work. One chief in Lilongwe said high blood pressure is a big problem in his area. Some chiefs have become very ill and had to go to the hospital.

Experts asked all people to check their blood pressure often, even if they feel well. The Ministry of Health said people must go to the doctor early if they have high blood pressure. Groups like the Kidney Foundation and the Stroke Foundation are helping. They said this work must continue and get stronger.

ny.title: Dziko la Malawi likukondwerera Tsiku la Matenda Othamanga Kwambiri Padziko Lonse, ndipo lalimbikitsa anthu kuti ayesere kuthamanga kwa magazi
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi linagwirizana ndi dziko lonse pa 17 Meyi kuti likumbukire Tsiku la Matenda Othamanga Kwambiri Padziko Lonse. Akatswiri azaumoyo anachenjeza kuti anthu ambiri ku Malawi ali ndi kuthamanga kwa magazi koma sakudziwa. Mutu wake unali wakuti "Kulamulira Matenda Othamanga Kwambiri Pamodzi". Zochitikazo zinachitika m'midzi ndi m'matauni. Akatswiri anati pafupifupi 32 peresenti ya akuluakulu aku Malawi ali ndi kuthamanga kwa magazi. Pafupifupi theka la anthuwa sadziwa kuti akudwala.
ny.body_more:
Kuthamanga kwa magazi kumatchedwa kuti ndi wakupha wachinsinsi chifukwa sikungayambitse zizindikiro zilizonse. Anthu amatha kukhala nako kwa zaka zambiri ndikumva bwino. Koma kumatha kuwononga mtima, mitsempha yamagazi ndi impso. Kungayambitse sitiroko, matenda amtima komanso kulephera kwa impso. Ogwira ntchito zachipatala anati anthu ambiri amapita kuchipatala akamadwala kwambiri, koma nthawi imeneyo zimakhala zitachedwa.

Akatswiri azaumoyo adati zifukwa zambiri za kuthamanga kwa magazi zimatha kuthetsedwa. Anati anthu amatha kusintha zizolowezi zawo. Kusuta fodya, kumwa mowa mopitirira muyeso, kusachita masewera olimbitsa thupi komanso mchere wambiri muzakudya ndiye zifukwa zazikulu. Mwamuna wina, Siveli Aliyeri, anali ndi zaka 53 ndipo anali ndi kuthamanga kwa magazi kwambiri chifukwa cha kumwa ndi kusuta. Anasintha moyo wake. Anasiya kumwa ndi kusuta. Anadya chakudya chochepa chamafuta ndi mchere. Kuthamanga kwa magazi kwake kunakhalanso bwino. Anati sakanatha ngakhale kukwera njinga kwa kanthawi kochepa osagwa. Tsopano ali bwino kwambiri.

Magulu azaumoyo akugwira ntchito m'midzi kuti apeze anthu omwe ali ndi kuthamanga kwa magazi msanga. Odzipereka ammudzi amayesa kuthamanga kwa magazi ndikuphunzitsa anthu. Ogwira ntchito zaumoyo aboma nawonso amathandiza. Atsogoleri m'midzi amathandizira ntchitoyi. Mtsogoleri wina ku Lilongwe anati kuthamanga kwa magazi ndi vuto lalikulu m'dera lake. Mafumu ena adwala kwambiri ndipo amayenera kupita kuchipatala.

Akatswiri adapempha anthu onse kuti aziyang'ana kuthamanga kwa magazi awo pafupipafupi, ngakhale atakhala bwino. Unduna wa Zaumoyo unati anthu ayenera kupita kwa dokotala msanga ngati ali ndi kuthamanga kwa magazi. Magulu monga Kidney Foundation ndi Stroke Foundation akuthandiza. Anati ntchitoyi iyenera kupitilira ndikukhala yolimba.

en.title: Parliament wants housing firm to build schools and houses
en.body: A group of lawmakers wants the government to give work to the Malawi Housing Corporation. The group is called the Public Accounts Committee. They met with the housing firm in Blantyre on Saturday. They want the firm to build schools and teachers' houses. The work will come from a new K5 billion building fund for each area. The committee says this will help the firm and give Malawi good buildings.
en.body_more:
The committee wants at least 10 percent of the K5 billion to go to the housing firm. Committee leader Steve Malondera asked why the firm does not get more work from the government. He said the firm builds cheap, strong, good buildings. "What are they failing to do?" he asked.

The housing firm has big money problems. It owes about K9 billion. It has only K6 billion in things it owns. The firm also has a problem. People who rent from it do not pay on time. Some renters are government offices. This has hurt the firm's money.

The housing firm's leader is Bob Chimkango. He said the firm is like a sleeping giant. He said it is being choked but can still grow. He said the firm does not want money from government. It only wants work. "Once we get the work, we will earn what we need," he said. In the last four years, the firm has lost about K8.1 billion.

The committee will now talk to two government offices. These are the Ministry of Lands, Housing and Urban Development and the Ministry of Local Government and Rural Development. They will talk about how to make this plan real. Parliament just made the building fund much bigger. It went up from K220 million to K5 billion for each area.

ny.title: Nyumba Yamalamulo ikufuna kampani yomanga nyumba kuti imange masukulu ndi nyumba
ny.body: Gulu la opanga malamulo likufuna kuti boma lipereke ntchito ku Malawi Housing Corporation. Gululi limatchedwa Public Accounts Committee. Anakumana ndi kampani yoona za nyumba ku Blantyre Loweruka. Akufuna kuti kampaniyo imange masukulu ndi nyumba za aphunzitsi. Ntchitoyi idzachokera ku ndalama zatsopano zomangira nyumba za K5 biliyoni m'dera lililonse. Komitiyi ikunena kuti izi zithandiza kampaniyo ndikupatsa Malawi nyumba zabwino.
ny.body_more:
Komitiyi ikufuna kuti osachepera 10 peresenti ya K5 biliyoni ipite ku kampani yogulitsa nyumba. Mtsogoleri wa komitiyi Steve Malondera adafunsa chifukwa chake kampaniyo siilandira ntchito zambiri kuchokera ku boma. Iye adati kampaniyo imamanga nyumba zotsika mtengo, zolimba, komanso zabwino. "Akulephera kuchita chiyani?" adafunsa.

Kampani yogulitsa nyumba ili ndi mavuto azachuma ambiri. Ili ndi ngongole ya pafupifupi K9 biliyoni. Ili ndi K6 biliyoni yokha pazinthu zomwe ili nazo. Kampaniyo ilinso ndi vuto. Anthu omwe amabwereka nyumba salipira pa nthawi yake. Ena obwereka nyumba ndi maofesi aboma. Izi zawononga ndalama za kampaniyo.

Mtsogoleri wa kampani yogulitsa nyumba ndi Bob Chimkango. Iye adati kampaniyo ili ngati chimphona chogona. Iye adati ikukankhidwa koma ikhoza kukula. Iye adati kampaniyo sikufuna ndalama kuchokera ku boma. Imangofuna ntchito. "Tikangopeza ntchitoyo, tidzapeza zomwe tikufuna," adatero. M'zaka zinayi zapitazi, kampaniyo yataya pafupifupi K8.1 biliyoni.

Komitiyi tsopano ilankhula ndi maofesi awiri aboma. Izi ndi Unduna wa Malo, Nyumba ndi Chitukuko cha Mizinda ndi Unduna wa Maboma Apafupi ndi Chitukuko cha Kumidzi. Adzakambirana momwe angapangire dongosololi kukhala lenileni. Nyumba Yamalamulo yangokulitsa ndalama zomangira nyumba. Ndalamazo zakwera kuchoka pa K220 miliyoni kufika pa K5 biliyoni pa dera lililonse.

en.title: Government must show where austerity savings have gone
en.body: People in Malawi are asking the government to say how much money it has saved through austerity measures. The measures started in November 2025. The government says there were "huge savings" by March 31, 2026. But the government has not told the public the exact numbers. Many people are unhappy about the lack of information.
en.body_more:
The austerity measures cut government spending in many ways. The government stopped buying new cars. It stopped paying for travel abroad for workers. It stopped hiring new workers, except in key jobs. Senior workers got less fuel money. But people say they have seen no real help from these savings.

Willy Kambwandira works for the Centre for Social Transparency and Accountability. He said the government must show real numbers. He said people cannot see how the savings have made their lives better. John Kapito, from the Consumers Association of Malawi, sent a letter asking for details. He asked how much money was saved from travel, workshops, and vehicle purchases. He asked where the money has gone. He asked if the money went to pay debts, help poor people, or fix services.

George Chaima is a person who studies government. He said the government must be open. He said people will not trust the government if it does not show them the facts. Williams Banda works for the Treasury. He said the savings came from stopping big purchases, less travel, and fewer new workers. He said the money went to pay down debt, not for new things. He said the measures have helped bring down inflation and interest rates.

The Finance Minister, Joseph Mwanamvekha, said the government will show all the numbers in September. He said they will publish a report on the 2026/27 budget at mid-year. Many people say September is too long to wait. They say this makes them think the government is hiding something.

ny.title: Boma liyenera kusonyeza komwe ndalama zosungira ndalama zochepetsera ndalama zapita
ny.body: Anthu ku Malawi akupempha boma kuti linene ndalama zomwe lasunga pogwiritsa ntchito njira zochepetsera ndalama. Njirazi zinayamba mu Novembala 2025. Boma lati panali "ndalama zambiri zosungidwa" pofika pa 31 Marichi, 2026. Koma boma silinauze anthu ziwerengero zenizeni. Anthu ambiri sakusangalala ndi kusowa kwa chidziwitso.
ny.body_more:
Ndondomeko zochepetsera ndalama zomwe boma limagwiritsa ntchito zinachepetsa ndalama zomwe boma limagwiritsa ntchito m'njira zambiri. Boma linasiya kugula magalimoto atsopano. Linasiya kulipira ndalama zoyendera kunja kwa antchito. Linasiya kulemba antchito atsopano, kupatulapo ntchito zofunika kwambiri. Ogwira ntchito akuluakulu amapeza ndalama zochepa zamafuta. Koma anthu amati sanawone thandizo lenileni kuchokera ku ndalama zomwe zasungidwazi.

Willy Kambwandira amagwira ntchito ku Centre for Social Transparency and Accountability. Anati boma liyenera kuwonetsa ziwerengero zenizeni. Anati anthu sangaone momwe ndalama zomwe zasungidwa zathandizira miyoyo yawo kukhala yabwino. John Kapito, wochokera ku Consumers Association of Malawi, adatumiza kalata yopempha tsatanetsatane. Anafunsa kuti ndalama zomwe zasungidwa kuchokera paulendo, ma workshop, ndi kugula magalimoto zapita kuti. Anafunsa kuti ndalamazo zapita kuti zilipire ngongole, kuthandiza anthu osauka, kapena kukonza mautumiki.

George Chaima ndi munthu amene amaphunzira za boma. Anati boma liyenera kukhala lotseguka. Anati anthu sadzakhulupirira boma ngati silidzawawonetsa zoona zake. Williams Banda amagwira ntchito ku Treasury. Anati ndalama zomwe zasungidwazo zinachokera ku kuletsa kugula kwakukulu, kuyenda kochepa, komanso antchito atsopano ochepa. Anati ndalamazo zinapita kuti zilipire ngongole, osati zinthu zatsopano. Iye anati njirazi zathandiza kuchepetsa kukwera kwa mitengo ndi chiwongola dzanja.

Nduna ya Zachuma, Joseph Mwanamvekakha, adati boma lidzawonetsa ziwerengero zonse mu Seputembala. Iye anati adzafalitsa lipoti la bajeti ya 2026/27 pakati pa chaka. Anthu ambiri amati Seputembala ndi yayitali kwambiri kuti adikire. Amati izi zimawapangitsa kuganiza kuti boma likubisa chinachake.

en.title: Wanderers face Mitundu in battle to return to winning ways
en.body: Mighty Wanderers play Luanar Mitundu FC today at Dedza Stadium. Wanderers lost 2-1 to Big Bullets last week. This is their first loss of the season. Coach Bob Mpinganjira said the team has prepared well. He said Mitundu is a tricky team and Wanderers must not underrate them. Wanderers are ninth on the table with three points.
en.body_more:
Mitundu are a new team in the top league. They finished second in the lower division last season. They lost 2-0 to Masters FC on Monday, so they also want to bounce back. Coach Oscar Kaunda said his players are not scared of Wanderers, even though Wanderers are the champions. Kaunda said his team will follow their game plan and not panic.

Wanderers have some problems before this match. Gaddie Chirwa cannot play because he got a red card in the last game. Alex Kambilinya is also hurt and may not play. Mitundu have two players who know Wanderers well. Oscar Kaunda coached Wanderers before. Vincent Nyangulu was a striker for Wanderers. But Wanderers have a much stronger team than Mitundu. Wanderers won the title last season with only one loss.

Today Wanderers are ninth with three points from two games. Mitundu are seventh with four points from three games. A loss or a draw for Wanderers is bad news. Masters FC are top of the table, six points ahead of Wanderers. Many other games also play today. Creck Sporting Club play Kamuzu Barracks. Chitipa United face Civil Service United. Ekhaya FC play Moyale Barracks and want their first win of the season.

ny.title: Wanderers ikukumana ndi Mitundu pankhondo kuti ibwererenso ku njira zopambana
ny.body: Mighty Wanderers isewera ndi Luanar Mitundu FC lero ku Dedza Stadium. Wanderers idagonja 2-1 ndi Big Bullets sabata yatha. Iyi ndi nthawi yawo yoyamba kugonja nyengo ino. Mphunzitsi Bob Mpinganjira adati timuyi yakonzekera bwino. Iye anati Mitundu ndi timu yovuta ndipo Wanderers sayenera kuwanyoza. Wanderers ili pa nambala 9 patebulo ndi mapointi atatu.
ny.body_more:
Mitundu ndi timu yatsopano mu ligi yapamwamba. Adamaliza pa nambala yachiwiri mu gawo lotsika nyengo yatha. Adataya 2-0 ndi Masters FC Lolemba, kotero akufunanso kubwerera m'mbuyo. Mphunzitsi Oscar Kaunda adati osewera ake saopa Wanderers, ngakhale Wanderers ndi omwe ali ndi zigoli. Kaunda adati timu yake itsatira dongosolo lawo lamasewera ndipo sadzaopa.

Wanderers ali ndi mavuto ena masewerawa asanachitike. Gaddie Chirwa sangasewere chifukwa adalandira khadi lofiira pamasewera apitawa. Alex Kambilinya nayenso wavulala ndipo mwina sangasewere. Mitundu ili ndi osewera awiri omwe amadziwa bwino Wanderers. Oscar Kaunda adaphunzitsa Wanderers kale. Vincent Nyangulu anali womenya wa Wanderers. Koma Wanderers ili ndi timu yamphamvu kwambiri kuposa Mitundu. Wanderers idapambana mpikisano nyengo yatha ndi kutayika kamodzi kokha.

Lero Wanderers ili pa nambala 9 ndi mapointi atatu kuchokera pamasewera awiri. Mitundu ili pa nambala 7 ndi mapointi anayi kuchokera pamasewera atatu. Kutayika kapena kutayika kwa Wanderers ndi nkhani yoipa. Masters FC ili pamwamba pa tebulo, mapointi asanu ndi limodzi patsogolo pa Wanderers. Masewera ena ambiri amaseweranso lero. Creck Sporting Club isewera ndi Kamuzu Barracks. Chitipa United isewera ndi Civil Service United. Ekhaya FC isewera ndi Moyale Barracks ndipo ikufuna kupambana koyamba nyengo ino.

en.title: Kwacha to stay strong, but problems hide below
en.body: The Malawi Kwacha will stay stable in the next few months. Tobacco farmers are selling crops and bringing in foreign money. But experts say this is not the full story. They warn that deep problems in the economy could make the currency weak very soon.
en.body_more:
The Reserve Bank of Malawi keeps the Kwacha steady by using money from tobacco sales. Tobacco is Malawi's biggest export and brings in most foreign cash. But this also means the Kwacha is not truly strong.

Experts say Malawi relies too much on one crop. The country does not make enough other things. There are not enough factories. Foreign money in the bank is low. Prices for things go up between 12% and 15% each year. This makes life hard for families and shops.

Business people need new ideas. They can make tobacco into finished products instead of selling raw crops. Other businesses like energy, food making, and digital work are growing fast. The government is helping small mines and farms with loans. Mobile money and digital banks are helping people in villages who cannot reach real banks.

Regional trade can also help. Malawi is between Zambia, Mozambique, and Tanzania. The African Continental Free Trade Area opens new markets. Business leaders should work with neighboring countries to sell more goods. The government must fix roads, fix power cuts, and make rules simpler. When all groups work together, Malawi can turn these problems into chances to grow.

ny.title: Kwacha kukhalabe olimba, koma mavuto amabisala pansipa
ny.body: Malawi Kwacha ikhalabe yokhazikika m'miyezi ingapo ikubwerayi. Alimi a fodya akugulitsa mbewu ndikubweretsa ndalama zakunja. Koma akatswiri akuti iyi si nkhani yonse. Akuchenjeza kuti mavuto akulu azachuma angapangitse ndalama kufooka posachedwa.
ny.body_more:
Banki Yaikulu ya Malawi imasunga Kwacha yokhazikika pogwiritsa ntchito ndalama zomwe zimagulitsidwa kuchokera ku malonda a fodya. Fodya ndiye chinthu chachikulu kwambiri chomwe Malawi imatumiza kunja ndipo chimabweretsa ndalama zambiri zakunja. Koma izi zikutanthauzanso kuti Kwacha siili yolimba kwenikweni.

Akatswiri amati Malawi imadalira kwambiri mbewu imodzi. Dzikoli silipanga zinthu zina zokwanira. Palibe mafakitale okwanira. Ndalama zakunja m'banki ndizotsika. Mitengo ya zinthu imakwera pakati pa 12% ndi 15% chaka chilichonse. Izi zimapangitsa moyo kukhala wovuta kwa mabanja ndi masitolo.

Amalonda amafunikira malingaliro atsopano. Angapange fodya kukhala zinthu zomalizidwa m'malo mogulitsa mbewu zosaphika. Mabizinesi ena monga mphamvu, kupanga chakudya, ndi ntchito zama digito akukula mwachangu. Boma likuthandiza migodi yaying'ono ndi minda ndi ngongole. Ndalama zam'manja ndi mabanki ama digito akuthandiza anthu m'midzi omwe sangathe kufikira mabanki enieni.

Malonda am'deralo angathandizenso. Malawi ili pakati pa Zambia, Mozambique, ndi Tanzania. Malo Ogulitsa Aulere a Africa Continental akutsegula misika yatsopano. Atsogoleri amabizinesi ayenera kugwira ntchito ndi mayiko oyandikana nawo kuti agulitse katundu wambiri. Boma liyenera kukonza misewu, kukonza kutsekedwa kwa magetsi, ndikupangitsa malamulo kukhala osavuta. Magulu onse akagwira ntchito limodzi, Malawi ikhoza kusintha mavutowa kukhala mwayi wokulira.

en.title: Democracy Group Asks Treasury Why Admarc Has No Maize Money
en.body: A group called Cdedi asked the Treasury to explain why it has not given money to Admarc for buying maize. Parliament passed the budget in April, but Admarc still has no funds. Cdedi said this breaks a promise made by the ruling party. The party said it would make Admarc strong so farmers get fair prices. Without money, farmers sell cheap to private traders instead.
en.body_more:
Cdedi wrote a letter to the Secretary to Treasury, Cliff Chiunda. The group said the delay is bad for farmers and for the country. When Treasury waits to give money, private traders buy maize cheaply and sell it high later. This means farmers lose money and poor people pay too much for food.

The ruling Democratic Progressive Party made promises in its manifesto. It said Admarc would be a strong business that helps farmers. It also said the country would keep 150,000 tonnes of maize in storage at all times. Cdedi said these promises have not happened yet.

Admarc's leader said the budget gave only 60 billion kwacha. But Admarc needs 144 billion kwacha to work well. This means Admarc must borrow 80 billion kwacha and pay 10 billion in interest. An expert called Hastings Sonjo agreed with Cdedi. He said quick funding is needed because slow action hurts food security. He also said importing maize is bad because it uses money the country needs for fuel, medicine and other goods. When farmers do not sell to Admarc, they sell to traders who take too much profit.

Cdedi said last year the country had to buy maize from other countries even though farmers grew it at home. The group called this embarrassing for a farm country. It asked Treasury to release the money now so Admarc can buy from farmers before private traders do.

ny.title: Gulu la Demokalase Likufunsa Zachuma Chifukwa Chake Admarc Ilibe Ndalama za Chimanga
ny.body: Gulu lotchedwa Cdedi linapempha Treasury kuti ifotokoze chifukwa chake silinapereke ndalama ku Admarc zogulira chimanga. Nyumba Yamalamulo idavomereza bajeti mu Epulo, koma Admarc ikadalibe ndalama. Cdedi adati izi zikuswa lonjezo lomwe chipani cholamulira chidapanga. Chipanicho chidati chipangitsa Admarc kukhala yolimba kuti alimi apeze mitengo yoyenera. Popanda ndalama, alimi amagulitsa zotsika mtengo kwa amalonda achinsinsi m'malo mwake.
ny.body_more:
Cdedi adalemba kalata kwa Mlembi wa Zachuma, Cliff Chiunda. Gululo linati kuchedwaku n’koipa kwa alimi komanso dzikolo. Pamene Zachuma zikudikira kupereka ndalama, amalonda achinsinsi amagula chimanga motchipa ndikuchigulitsa mokwera mtengo pambuyo pake. Izi zikutanthauza kuti alimi amataya ndalama ndipo anthu osauka amalipira ndalama zambiri pa chakudya.

Chipani cholamula cha Democratic Progressive Party chinalonjeza mu manifesto yake. Chinati Admarc idzakhala bizinesi yolimba yomwe imathandiza alimi. Chinatinso dzikolo lidzasunga matani 150,000 a chimanga nthawi zonse. Cdedi adati malonjezo awa sanachitikebe.

Mtsogoleri wa Admarc adati bajetiyo idapereka ma kwacha 60 biliyoni okha. Koma Admarc ikufunika ma kwacha 144 biliyoni kuti igwire ntchito bwino. Izi zikutanthauza kuti Admarc iyenera kubwereka ma kwacha 80 biliyoni ndikulipira chiwongola dzanja cha 10 biliyoni. Katswiri wotchedwa Hastings Sonjo adagwirizana ndi Cdedi. Anati ndalama mwachangu zikufunika chifukwa kuchitapo kanthu pang'onopang'ono kumawononga chitetezo cha chakudya. Anatinso kutumiza chimanga kunja n’koipa chifukwa kumagwiritsa ntchito ndalama zomwe dzikolo limafuna pamafuta, mankhwala ndi zinthu zina. Alimi akapanda kugulitsa ku Admarc, amagulitsa kwa amalonda omwe amapeza phindu lalikulu.

Cdedi adati chaka chatha dzikolo lidayenera kugula chimanga kuchokera kumayiko ena ngakhale alimi adachilima kwawo. Gululo linati izi ndi zochititsa manyazi kudziko laulimi. Linapempha Treasury kuti itulutse ndalamazo tsopano kuti Admarc ithe kugula kwa alimi asanayambe amalonda achinsinsi.

en.title: Dead bank asks for 1 trillion kwacha from Malawi
en.body: A bank that closed in 2005 is now asking Malawi for over 1 trillion kwacha. Finance Bank of Malawi stopped working after the central bank took away its licence. But a court said the central bank did this in the wrong way. Now the bank wants money for lost business and profits. A hearing will happen on May 25, 2026.
en.body_more:
The Finance Bank says it lost 551 million US dollars when the central bank closed it. It also wants 61.7 billion kwacha for other losses and interest. The bank says it could have been worth 352 million US dollars by now if it had stayed open.

The government says it will fight the claim. The Attorney General Frank Mbeta said his office will work with the central bank to say the claim is too big. A lawyer named Benedicto Kondowe said the court must think about what this costs ordinary people. He said the court must check if the bank's numbers are real and fair.

The case started in 2005 when the bank lost its licence. A court gave the bank money back in 2014, but a higher court just said the licence was taken away in the wrong way. The bank closed fully in January 2006. Money expert Mkuzo Kenneth Kuwani made the numbers. He said the bank made good money before it closed.

Many people are worried. A group called NACA said the money could hurt Malawi. People are already poor, food is expensive, and jobs are hard to find. If the court says the bank must get this money, it will make things harder for everyone.

ny.title: Banki ya Dead ikupempha Malawi kuti ipereke 1 trillion kwacha
ny.body: Banki yomwe inatseka mu 2005 tsopano ikupempha Malawi ndalama zoposa 1 thililiyoni kwacha. Banki Yachuma ya Malawi inasiya kugwira ntchito banki yayikulu italanda chilolezo chake. Koma khoti linati banki yayikulu idachita izi molakwika. Tsopano bankiyo ikufuna ndalama zoti iwononge bizinesi ndi phindu. Msonkhano udzachitika pa Meyi 25, 2026.
ny.body_more:
Banki Yachuma ikunena kuti idataya madola 551 miliyoni aku US pamene banki yayikulu idatseka. Ikufunanso ma kwacha 61.7 biliyoni chifukwa cha kutayika kwina ndi chiwongola dzanja. Bankiyo ikunena kuti ikanakhala ndi madola 352 miliyoni aku US pofika pano ikanakhalabe yotseguka.

Boma likunena kuti lidzalimbana ndi mlanduwu. Loya Wamkulu Frank Mbeta adati ofesi yake idzagwira ntchito ndi banki yayikulu kuti inene kuti mlanduwu ndi waukulu kwambiri. Loya wotchedwa Benedicto Kondowe adati khothi liyenera kuganizira za zomwe izi zimawonongera anthu wamba. Anati khothi liyenera kuwona ngati manambala a bankiyo ndi enieni komanso olungama.

Mlanduwu unayamba mu 2005 pomwe bankiyo idataya chilolezo chake. Khothi linabweza ndalama ku bankiyo mu 2014, koma khothi lalikulu linangonena kuti chilolezocho chinachotsedwa molakwika. Bankiyo idatseka kwathunthu mu Januwale 2006. Katswiri wazachuma Mkuzo Kenneth Kuwani ndiye adapanga manambalawo. Anati bankiyo idapeza ndalama zambiri isanatseke.

Anthu ambiri akuda nkhawa. Gulu lotchedwa NACA linati ndalamazo zitha kuvulaza Malawi. Anthu ndi osauka kale, chakudya n’chokwera mtengo, ndipo ntchito n’zovuta kupeza. Ngati khoti litanena kuti banki iyenera kupeza ndalama zimenezi, zinthu zidzavuta kwa aliyense.

en.title: Police use tear gas at Kamuzu Day as Chakwera kept away
en.body: Police fired tear gas on Thursday in Lilongwe during Kamuzu Day. Former president Lazarus Chakwera and his supporters wanted to lay flowers at the mausoleum. Police stopped them at roadblocks near the city centre. The government said Chakwera could not come to the main ceremony. The Malawi Congress Party held a separate event instead.
en.body_more:
The government said Ken Kandodo and the Kamuzu family made the choice to keep Chakwera away. But Kandodo would not say anything more about it. A government spokesman, Shadric Namalomba, said the government had nothing to do with it. He said the day was for all people, not for one party. President Peter Mutharika sent his culture minister, Alfred Gangata, to the official event.

When Chakwera's supporters tried to walk to the mausoleum, police blocked them with roadblocks. Hundreds of people pushed forward from the MCP office. Police then shot tear gas into the crowd. People ran away in panic. Chakwera's party said they had asked the museum office for permission. They said they did nothing wrong.

Namalomba said the MCP march was not legal. He said they did not ask the police or the city council. "No clearance means no legality," he said. "The era of impunity is over." But the MCP secretary general, Richard Chimwendo Banda, said the police action was bad for democracy. He said it was wrong on a day to honour the founding president. A political analyst, Ben Chakhame, warned that these rival events show Malawi is becoming more divided. He said holding separate ceremonies on national days harms the country.

ny.title: Apolisi agwiritsa ntchito utsi wokhetsa misozi pa tsiku la Kamuzu pomwe Chakwera adasungidwa kutali
ny.body: Apolisi anaponya utsi wokhetsa misozi Lachinayi ku Lilongwe pa tsiku la Kamuzu Day. Purezidenti wakale Lazarus Chakwera ndi omutsatira ake ankafuna kuyika maluwa pamanda a anthu akufa. Apolisi anawayimitsa pamalo otsekereza magalimoto pafupi ndi pakati pa mzinda. Boma linati Chakwera sangabwere ku mwambo waukulu. Chipani cha Malawi Congress chinachita mwambo wina m'malo mwake.
ny.body_more:
Boma linati Ken Kandodo ndi banja la Kamuzu adasankha kuti achotse Chakwera. Koma Kandodo sananene chilichonse chokhudza nkhaniyi. Mneneri wa boma, Shadric Namalomba, anati boma silinachitepo kanthu. Anati tsikuli linali la anthu onse, osati la chipani chimodzi. Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adatumiza nduna yake ya chikhalidwe, Alfred Gangata, ku mwambowu wovomerezeka.

Otsatira a Chakwera atayesa kuyenda kupita kumanda, apolisi adawatseka ndi zotchinga. Anthu mazana ambiri adathamangira patsogolo kuchokera ku ofesi ya MCP. Kenako apolisi adawombera utsi wokhetsa misozi m'khamulo. Anthu adathawa ndi mantha. Gulu la Chakwera linati lapempha chilolezo ku ofesi yosungiramo zinthu zakale. Iwo anati sanachite cholakwika chilichonse.

Namalomba adati ulendo wa MCP sunali wovomerezeka. Anati sanafunse apolisi kapena khonsolo ya mzinda. "Kupanda chilolezo kumatanthauza kuti palibe lamulo," adatero. "Nthawi yosalangidwa yatha." Koma mlembi wamkulu wa MCP, Richard Chimwendo Banda, adati zomwe apolisi adachita zinali zoyipa pa demokalase. Iye anati sikunali koyenera pa tsiku lolemekeza purezidenti woyambitsa. Katswiri wa ndale, Ben Chakhame, anachenjeza kuti zochitika zotsutsanazi zikusonyeza kuti dziko la Malawi likugawikana kwambiri. Iye anati kuchita miyambo yosiyana pa masiku a dziko kumavulaza dzikolo.

en.title: UK gives Malawi lab machines worth £1.2 million
en.body: The UK government gave the Malawi Bureau of Standards new lab machines. The machines cost £1.2 million. The UK sent them through the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office. TradeMark Africa helped. The machines test food and crops for bad things. Malawi can now test products at home.
en.body_more:
The new machines include ultra-high performance liquid chromatography, and two other machines that check for mycotoxins and pesticide residue. Mycotoxins are poisons found in food crops. Pesticide residue is poison left on plants. Before, Malawi had to send samples abroad for testing. This cost a lot of money and took a long time.

Now businesses in Malawi can test their products fast at home. This helps farmers and food makers sell more goods. They can sell to other countries in the region. Better testing helps companies get better prices in markets like COMESA. Small companies can now get fast tests for their crops and food. They can sell faster and sell more.

The new machines help many types of business. They can test food, medicine, and farm products. This helps keep bad products away from shops and people. Companies that use these new standards can sell to big buyers in other countries. The machines show that Malawi wants to make better products. This helps bring new business to the country.

The director of the Malawi Bureau of Standards said the machines help make services better. Companies can get tests fast and trust the answers. This is part of Malawi's plan to grow and make more things. Good quality control helps everyone in the country.

ny.title: UK yapereka makina a labu ku Malawi okwana £1.2 miliyoni
ny.body: Boma la UK linapatsa makina atsopano a labu ku Malawi Bureau of Standards. Makinawa adagula £1.2 miliyoni. UK idawatumiza kudzera ku Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office. TradeMark Africa idathandiza. Makinawa amayesa chakudya ndi mbewu kuti aone ngati zili zoipa. Malawi tsopano ikhoza kuyesa zinthu kunyumba.
ny.body_more:
Makina atsopanowa akuphatikizapo chromatography yamadzimadzi yogwira ntchito kwambiri, ndi makina ena awiri omwe amafufuza mycotoxins ndi zotsalira za mankhwala ophera tizilombo. Mycotoxins ndi poizoni wopezeka mu mbewu za chakudya. Zotsalira za mankhwala ophera tizilombo ndi poizoni wotsalira pa zomera. Kale, Malawi inkayenera kutumiza zitsanzo kunja kuti zikayesedwe. Izi zinkawononga ndalama zambiri ndipo zinkatenga nthawi yayitali.

Tsopano mabizinesi ku Malawi amatha kuyesa zinthu zawo mwachangu kunyumba. Izi zimathandiza alimi ndi opanga chakudya kugulitsa katundu wambiri. Amatha kugulitsa kumayiko ena m'chigawochi. Kuyesa bwino kumathandiza makampani kupeza mitengo yabwino m'misika ngati COMESA. Makampani ang'onoang'ono tsopano amatha kupeza mayeso achangu a mbewu zawo ndi chakudya. Amatha kugulitsa mwachangu ndikugulitsa zambiri.

Makina atsopanowa amathandiza mitundu yambiri ya mabizinesi. Amatha kuyesa chakudya, mankhwala, ndi zinthu zaulimi. Izi zimathandiza kusunga zinthu zoyipa kutali ndi masitolo ndi anthu. Makampani omwe amagwiritsa ntchito miyezo yatsopanoyi amatha kugulitsa kwa ogula akuluakulu m'maiko ena. Makinawa akuwonetsa kuti Malawi ikufuna kupanga zinthu zabwino. Izi zimathandiza kubweretsa mabizinesi atsopano mdzikolo.

Mkulu wa Malawi Bureau of Standards adati makinawa amathandiza kuti ntchito zikhale bwino. Makampani amatha kupeza mayeso mwachangu ndikudalira mayankho. Izi ndi gawo la dongosolo la Malawi lokulitsa ndi kupanga zinthu zambiri. Kulamulira bwino zinthu kumathandiza aliyense mdziko muno.

en.title: Eighth person dies in mob violence over false rumours
en.body: An eighth person has died in attacks linked to false stories about disappearing private parts in Chikwawa and Nsanje. The man, a phone charger seller from Tomali, died early on Kamuzu Day after a mob beat him on Sunday. More than 90 people have been arrested. Police have sent 300 officers to stop the violence.
en.body_more:
Police say the rumours are not true. "No one has lost their private parts," said Frank Kumukumu, the police chief for the South East Region. "These claims are false and dangerous." He said police will punish anyone who spreads lies that harm people.

The police have set up a camp in Ngabu, a town in Chikwawa. Officers patrol villages and markets every day. They tell people not to hurt others and to report crimes to police instead. The government sent Ben Phiri and Noel Kayira to visit people and tell them the stories are not real. "We have agreed that this foolishness and thuggery ends today," Phiri said.

About 60 people are now in Blantyre Central Prison. Police say they will face murder charges. Traditional leaders have been told to stop the violence and not help criminals hide. Human rights groups say this is now a serious crisis for people's safety.

This echoes a similar panic in 2017, when false stories about "blood suckers" led to at least nine deaths. Kumukumu warned that more arrests will come as the operation continues to restore peace and order in the two districts.

ny.title: Munthu wachisanu ndi chitatu wafa chifukwa cha chiwawa cha gulu la anthu chifukwa cha mphekesera zabodza
ny.body: Munthu wachisanu ndi chitatu wafa pa ziwawa zokhudzana ndi nkhani zabodza zokhudza kusowa kwa ziwalo zachinsinsi ku Chikwawa ndi Nsanje. Munthuyo, wogulitsa mafoni ochokera ku Tomali, anamwalira m'mawa kwambiri pa Kamuzu Day gulu la anthu litamumenya Lamlungu. Anthu opitilira 90 amangidwa. Apolisi atumiza apolisi 300 kuti athetse chiwawachi.
ny.body_more:
Apolisi akuti mphekeserazi si zoona. "Palibe amene wataya ziwalo zake zachinsinsi," adatero Frank Kumukumu, mkulu wa apolisi ku South East Region. "Zonena izi ndi zabodza komanso zoopsa." Anati apolisi adzalanga aliyense amene amafalitsa mabodza omwe amavulaza anthu.

Apolisi akhazikitsa msasa ku Ngabu, tawuni ku Chikwawa. Apolisi amayendayenda m'midzi ndi m'misika tsiku lililonse. Amauza anthu kuti asavulaze ena ndikupereka malipoti kwa apolisi m'malo mwake. Boma latumiza Ben Phiri ndi Noel Kayira kuti akachezere anthu ndikuwauza kuti nkhanizo si zenizeni. "Tagwirizana kuti kupusa ndi uchigawenga uku kumatha lero," adatero Phiri.

Anthu pafupifupi 60 tsopano ali ku Blantyre Central Prison. Apolisi akuti adzakumana ndi milandu yopha anthu. Atsogoleri achikhalidwe auzidwa kuti asiye chiwawa komanso asathandize zigawenga kubisala. Mabungwe omenyera ufulu wa anthu akuti tsopano ndi vuto lalikulu la chitetezo cha anthu.

Izi zikufanana ndi mantha omwewo mu 2017, pomwe nkhani zabodza zokhudza "oyamwa magazi" zidapangitsa kuti anthu osachepera asanu ndi anayi afe. Kumukumu anachenjeza kuti anthu ambiri adzamangidwa pamene ntchito yobwezeretsa mtendere ndi bata m'maboma awiriwa ikupitiliza.

en.title: Eight people die in Shire Valley mob attacks over false claims
en.body: Eight people have died in two weeks in Chikwawa and Nsanje districts. Men were killed by groups of people who believed false stories. The stories say men can steal other men's private parts. Police say this is not true. The government sent 300 police officers to stop the killings. Over 90 people have been arrested. One man died on May 14, Kamuzu Day.
en.body_more:
Police Commissioner Frank Kumukumu said detectives are working hard in the communities. He said the police will catch and punish anyone who spreads the false stories. He asked people to stop sending fake news. The police said they will keep safe anyone who gives them information to help stop the mob attacks.

On Wednesday, the Minister of Local Government came to Chikwawa. He said the stories about disappearing private parts are lies and "thuggery." He said beating and killing people is not right. He warned that chiefs who help the violence or hide the people who do it will lose their power. About 60 people arrested are waiting to face murder charges in court.

Groups of people in the area are now helping. Police work with local leaders and faith leaders to teach the community the truth. A human rights group is also helping spread awareness. The same false stories happened in Zambia and came from Mozambique. In 2017, stories about "blood suckers" killed at least nine people in six districts of Malawi. Police say no new attacks have happened since they came to the area.

ny.title: Anthu asanu ndi atatu afa ku ziwawa za anthu aku Shire Valley chifukwa cha mabodza
ny.body: Anthu asanu ndi atatu afa m'masabata awiri m'maboma a Chikwawa ndi Nsanje. Amuna aphedwa ndi magulu a anthu omwe amakhulupirira nkhani zabodza. Nkhanizo zimati amuna amatha kuba ziwalo za amuna ena. Apolisi akuti izi si zoona. Boma latumiza apolisi 300 kuti aletse kupha anthu. Anthu opitilira 90 amangidwa. Munthu m'modzi wamwalira pa Meyi 14, Kamuzu Day.
ny.body_more:
Komishinala wa apolisi Frank Kumukumu adati apolisi akugwira ntchito molimbika m'madera. Anati apolisi adzagwira ndi kulanga aliyense amene amafalitsa nkhani zabodza. Anapempha anthu kuti asiye kutumiza nkhani zabodza. Apolisi anati adzateteza aliyense amene angawapatse chidziwitso kuti athandize kuletsa ziwopsezo za zigawenga.

Lachitatu, Nduna ya Zaboma Zam'deralo inabwera ku Chikwawa. Anati nkhani zokhudza kutha kwa ziwalo zachinsinsi ndi mabodza komanso "zachifwamba." Anati kumenya ndi kupha anthu sikoyenera. Anachenjeza kuti mafumu omwe amathandiza chiwawa kapena kubisa anthu omwe amachita zimenezo adzataya mphamvu zawo. Anthu pafupifupi 60 omwe amangidwa akuyembekezera kuimbidwa mlandu wakupha kukhothi.

Magulu a anthu m'derali akuthandiza tsopano. Apolisi amagwira ntchito ndi atsogoleri am'deralo ndi atsogoleri achipembedzo kuti aphunzitse anthu ammudzi zoona. Gulu la ufulu wa anthu likuthandizanso kufalitsa chidziwitso. Nkhani zabodza zomwezo zinachitika ku Zambia ndipo zinachokera ku Mozambique. Mu 2017, nkhani zokhudza "oyamwa magazi" zinapha anthu osachepera asanu ndi anayi m'maboma asanu ndi limodzi a Malawi. Apolisi akuti palibe ziwawa zatsopano zomwe zachitika kuyambira pomwe adabwera m'derali.

en.title: ACB Staff Angry Over Unfair Pay Raises
en.body: Workers at the Anti-Corruption Bureau are upset. Senior staff got very big pay rises, but junior staff got small ones. The director got 18.5 percent more. But department heads got 45.9 percent more. Junior staff got only 16 percent. Workers say this is not fair. They feel the top people got too much.
en.body_more:
The ACB made new salary rules. The Director General got 18.5 percent more pay. The Deputy Director General got 33.8 percent more. But department heads in Grade 3 got the biggest raise: 45.9 percent. New deputy director jobs start at about K3.3 million each month.

One worker said the changes made problems for other grades too. "Grade 4 is new, so they made Grade 3 pay higher. This moved chiefs from Grade 4 to Grade 5. This also changed extra things they get," the worker said. Lower staff say their 16 percent raise does not match the cost of living. They work hard but do not get fair pay.

The ACB spokesperson, Jacqueline Ngongonda, did not answer questions about this. A lawyer, Benedicto Kondowe, said the bureau can change pay. But big gaps between workers can cause bad feelings. "Lower staff think the top people got too much. This makes them sad and angry. It makes them not want to work well," he said. Junior staff do important work too, he added.

At the national level, the government gave all civil servants 10 to 20 percent more pay in 2026/27. The ACB raises are much higher. A professor, Chrispine Mphande, said the ACB has not had enough money for a long time. The pay changes may help. But staff trust is now low inside the bureau.

ny.title: Antchito a ACB Akwiya Chifukwa cha Kukwezedwa kwa Malipiro Osalungama
ny.body: Ogwira ntchito ku Anti-Corruption Bureau akhumudwa. Ogwira ntchito akuluakulu akwezedwa malipiro ambiri, koma ogwira ntchito aang'ono akwezedwa ochepa. Wotsogolera ntchito akwezedwa ndi 18.5 peresenti. Koma akuluakulu a madipatimenti akwezedwa ndi 45.9 peresenti. Ogwira ntchito aang'ono akwezedwa ndi 16 peresenti yokha. Ogwira ntchito akunena kuti izi si chilungamo. Akuganiza kuti anthu apamwamba akwezedwa kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
ACB yapanga malamulo atsopano a malipiro. Director General adapeza malipiro owonjezera ndi 18.5 peresenti. Wachiwiri kwa Director General adapeza 33.8 peresenti yowonjezera. Koma atsogoleri a madipatimenti mu Giredi 3 adalandira kukwezedwa kwakukulu: 45.9 peresenti. Ntchito zatsopano za wachiwiri kwa director zimayamba pafupifupi K3.3 miliyoni mwezi uliwonse.

Wogwira ntchito wina adati kusinthaku kudabweretsa mavuto kwa magiredi ena. "Giredi 4 ndi yatsopano, kotero adapangitsa kuti malipiro a Giredi 3 akhale okwera. Izi zidasuntha atsogoleri kuchokera ku Giredi 4 kupita ku Giredi 5. Izi zidasinthanso zinthu zina zomwe amapeza," adatero wantchitoyo. Ogwira ntchito otsika akuti kukwezedwa kwawo ndi 16 peresenti sikukugwirizana ndi mtengo wa moyo. Amagwira ntchito molimbika koma salandira malipiro oyenera.

Wolankhulira ACB, Jacqueline Ngongonda, sanayankhe mafunso okhudza izi. Loya, Benedicto Kondowe, adati bungweli likhoza kusintha malipiro. Koma mipata yayikulu pakati pa antchito ingayambitse malingaliro oipa. "Ogwira ntchito otsika amaganiza kuti anthu apamwamba adalandira zambiri. Izi zimawapangitsa kukhala achisoni komanso okwiya. Zimawapangitsa kuti asafune kugwira ntchito bwino," adatero. Ogwira ntchito achichepere nawonso amachita ntchito yofunika, adawonjezera.

Pa mlingo wa dziko lonse, boma linapatsa antchito onse aboma malipiro owonjezera ndi 10 mpaka 20 peresenti mu 2026/27. Kukwezedwa kwa ACB kuli kokwera kwambiri. Pulofesa, Chrispine Mphande, anati ACB sinali ndi ndalama zokwanira kwa nthawi yayitali. Kusintha kwa malipiro kungathandize. Koma chidaliro cha ogwira ntchito tsopano chili chochepa mkati mwa bungweli.

en.title: MCP Plans Separate Prayer Service for Kamuzu Day
en.body: The Malawi Congress Party will hold its own prayers for Kamuzu Day after the government did not invite former president Lazarus Chakwera to the official ceremony on May 14. The MCP said the decision was unfair because Kamuzu Banda founded both the nation and the party. President Peter Mutharika will not attend the state event; instead, a minister will represent him. The MCP asked the government to change the decision but was told no.
en.body_more:
MCP Secretary General Richard Chimwendo Banda told the press that the government said Chakwera could not come because the President had sent a minister instead. He said this created a "protocol problem" that made it hard for the party leader to attend. Chimwendo Banda said Kamuzu Day has deep meaning for both the nation and the MCP. The party will hold prayers at its headquarters in Lilongwe after the official service ends. Chakwera will lay a wreath at Kamuzu's tomb to show respect.

The ceremony will take place at Kamuzu Mausoleum next to Parliament Building in Lilongwe at 10am. Organisers asked people to wear neutral clothes and not wear party colours. Ken Kandodo, who represents the Kamuzu family, said Kamuzu was "a man for all people" and believed in unity. Officials said the day should unite all Malawians, not divide them by politics.

Kamuzu Banda died on November 25, 1997, at age 99. He led Malawi for three decades after independence in 1964 and lost the first free elections in 1994. Activists said the day should help Malawians learn about both the good and bad parts of his rule. Some people feel the day brings back painful memories of arrests and hard times during his time as leader.

ny.title: MCP Yakonza Pemphero Lina la Tsiku la Kamuzu
ny.body: Chipani cha Malawi Congress Party chidzachita mapemphero ake a Kamuzu Day pambuyo poti boma silinaitane purezidenti wakale Lazarus Chakwera ku mwambo wovomerezeka pa Meyi 14. MCP inati chisankhocho sichinali cholungama chifukwa Kamuzu Banda ndiye anayambitsa dzikolo komanso chipanicho. Purezidenti Peter Mutharika sadzapezekapo pamwambo wa boma; m'malo mwake, nduna idzamuyimira. MCP idapempha boma kuti lisinthe chisankhocho koma idauzidwa kuti ayi.
ny.body_more:
Mlembi Wamkulu wa MCP, Richard Chimwendo Banda, adauza atolankhani kuti boma lati Chakwera sangabwere chifukwa Purezidenti adatumiza nduna m'malo mwake. Iye adati izi zabweretsa "vuto la protocol" lomwe lidapangitsa kuti mtsogoleri wa chipanicho asamapezekepo. Chimwendo Banda adati Tsiku la Kamuzu lili ndi tanthauzo lalikulu kwa dziko lonse komanso kwa MCP. Chipanichi chidzapemphera ku likulu lake ku Lilongwe mwambo ukatha. Chakwera adzaika nkhata pamanda a Kamuzu kuti asonyeze ulemu.

Mwambowu udzachitikira ku Kamuzu Mausoleum pafupi ndi Nyumba ya Malamulo ku Lilongwe nthawi ya 10 koloko m'mawa. Okonza mwambowu adapempha anthu kuti avale zovala zosalowerera ndale komanso kuti asavale mitundu ya chipani. Ken Kandodo, yemwe akuyimira banja la Kamuzu, adati Kamuzu anali "munthu wa anthu onse" ndipo amakhulupirira mgwirizano. Akuluakulu aboma adati tsikuli liyenera kulumikiza anthu onse aku Malawi, osati kuwagawa ndi ndale.

Kamuzu Banda anamwalira pa Novembala 25, 1997, ali ndi zaka 99. Anatsogolera dziko la Malawi kwa zaka makumi atatu pambuyo pa ufulu wodzilamulira mu 1964 ndipo anataya chisankho choyamba chaulere mu 1994. Anthu omenyera ufuluwo anati tsikuli liyenera kuthandiza anthu aku Malawi kuphunzira za zabwino ndi zoyipa za ulamuliro wake. Anthu ena amaona kuti tsikuli limabweretsa zokumbukira zowawa za kumangidwa ndi nthawi zovuta panthawi yake monga mtsogoleri.

en.title: Prophet Bushiri gives K300 million to clean Malawi plan
en.body: Prophet Shepherd Bushiri and his wife Mary gave K300 million to Beam Trust this week. Beam Trust works to clean and beautify Malawi. First Lady Gertrude Mutharika received the money at Kamuzu Palace in Lilongwe. Bushiri made a promise to give this money in February. The First Lady said thank you and said the money will help keep public places clean.
en.body_more:
The Beam Trust is working on a plan for 2026 to 2030. The plan focuses on health, sanitation, and schools. Bushiri said he and his wife want to help because they saw the good work Beam does in towns and villages. He said true development needs people and groups to put money into things that help people every day.

Mutharika called on all people in Malawi to help. She said everyone must keep their homes, schools, hospitals, and markets clean. She said this is not only the job of the government. Every person must help to make the country beautiful and clean.

Beam Trust has already adopted many hospitals across Malawi. These include Bwaila District Hospital in Lilongwe, Mzuzu Urban Community Hospital, and hospitals in Balaka, Thyolo, and Rumphi. The trust works to fix broken buildings in hospitals and give them clean water and toilets. Bushiri said his gift will help Beam make towns and villages cleaner and better for all people.

Many people say this is one of the biggest gifts to help Malawi become clean in recent months. Beam Trust works with schools, villages, and companies to plant trees and clean up towns.

ny.title: Mneneri Bushiri wapereka K300 miliyoni kuti athandize Malawi kukonza dongosolo lake
ny.body: Mneneri Shepherd Bushiri ndi mkazi wake Mary apereka K300 miliyoni ku Beam Trust sabata ino. Beam Trust imagwira ntchito yoyeretsa ndi kukongoletsa Malawi. Mkazi wa Pulezidenti Gertrude Mutharika adalandira ndalamazo ku Kamuzu Palace ku Lilongwe. Bushiri adalonjeza kupereka ndalamazi mu February. Mkazi wa Pulezidenti adati zikomo ndipo adati ndalamazo zithandiza kuti malo opezeka anthu ambiri akhale aukhondo.
ny.body_more:
Bungwe la Beam Trust likugwira ntchito pa dongosolo la 2026 mpaka 2030. Dongosololi likuyang'ana kwambiri zaumoyo, ukhondo, ndi masukulu. Bushiri adati iye ndi mkazi wake akufuna kuthandiza chifukwa adawona ntchito yabwino yomwe Beam imachita m'matauni ndi m'midzi. Iye adati chitukuko chenicheni chikufuna anthu ndi magulu kuti azigwiritsa ntchito ndalama pazinthu zomwe zimathandiza anthu tsiku lililonse.

Mutharika adapempha anthu onse ku Malawi kuti athandize. Iye adati aliyense ayenera kusunga nyumba zawo, masukulu, zipatala, ndi misika yawo kukhala yoyera. Iye adati iyi si ntchito ya boma yokha. Munthu aliyense ayenera kuthandiza kuti dzikolo likhale lokongola komanso loyera.

Beam Trust yayamba kale kugwiritsa ntchito zipatala zambiri ku Malawi konse. Izi zikuphatikizapo Chipatala cha Bwaila District ku Lilongwe, Chipatala cha Mzuzu Urban Community, ndi zipatala ku Balaka, Thyolo, ndi Rumphi. Bungweli limagwira ntchito yokonza nyumba zosweka m'zipatala ndikuwapatsa madzi oyera ndi zimbudzi. Bushiri adati mphatso yake ithandiza Beam kupanga matauni ndi midzi kukhala oyera komanso abwino kwa anthu onse.

Anthu ambiri amati iyi ndi imodzi mwa mphatso zazikulu zothandizira Malawi kukhala yoyera m'miyezi yaposachedwa. Beam Trust imagwira ntchito ndi masukulu, midzi, ndi makampani kubzala mitengo ndikuyeretsa matauni.

en.title: ACB officer interdicted over failed arrest of ex-minister
en.body: The Anti-Corruption Bureau has interdicted senior officer Robert Mkandawire over the failed arrest of former finance minister Simplex Chithyola Banda on May 8. Acting ACB director Gabriel Chembezi wrote that Mkandawire found Chithyola at his home but did not take him. The letter says Mkandawire allowed the suspect to leave. A court later set aside the arrest warrant and Chithyola now faces summons instead of jail.
en.body_more:
Mkandawire said the situation was dangerous. He told people the team found many people at Chithyola's house. They threatened the officers. Some people said the arrest was political. Police only came at 9 PM, he said. Mkandawire said his team's safety came first.

Inside sources tell a different story. They say Mkandawire asked the legal team for permission before moving. The legal director said yes at 4 PM. The sources say the plan was bad from the start. Four officers went alone to arrest a top opposition leader at night. No police came to help them. The sources say this was wrong. One source asked: how do you send officers into that kind of situation without backup?

When officers came to arrest him, Chithyola called his lawyer, George Kadzipatike. The lawyer asked them to wait. When they tried again, Chithyola was gone. Bouncers blocked the way. Some people at the house made threats and talked about a dead ACB officer, Issa Njaunju, who was killed doing his job. The officers were scared. They asked for police help.

Sources say Chembezi told Mkandawire to stay at the house for hours. At 11 PM they were told to leave. The next morning, sources say, Chembezi asked Mkandawire to write a news statement about the arrest. But Chithyola had not been arrested. Sources say this was not normal. Some people inside the bureau say Chembezi wanted to remove Mkandawire before this happened. They say he is now using the failed arrest as a reason to blame the officer.

ny.title: Wapolisi wa ACB waletsedwa kugwira ntchito chifukwa cholephera kumanga nduna yakale
ny.body: Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu laletsa mkulu wa apolisi Robert Mkandawire chifukwa cholephera kumangidwa kwa nduna yakale ya zachuma Simplex Chithyola Banda pa Meyi 8. Woyang'anira wa ACB, Gabriel Chembezi, analemba kuti Mkandawire adapeza Chithyola kunyumba kwake koma sanamutenge. Kalatayo imati Mkandawire adalola woganiziridwayo kuti achoke. Pambuyo pake khothi linachotsa chikalata chomumanga ndipo tsopano Chithyola akukumana ndi kuyitanidwa m'malo mopita kundende.
ny.body_more:
Mkandawire anati zinthu zinali zoopsa. Anauza anthu kuti gululo linapeza anthu ambiri kunyumba kwa Chithyola. Anaopseza apolisi. Anthu ena anati kumangidwako kunali kwandale. Apolisi anafika 9 koloko madzulo, anatero. Mkandawire anati chitetezo cha gulu lake chinali choyamba.

Magwero amkati akufotokoza nkhani yosiyana. Akuti Mkandawire anapempha chilolezo ku gulu la zamalamulo asanasamuke. Woyang'anira zamalamulo anati inde 4 koloko madzulo. Magwero akuti dongosololi linali loipa kuyambira pachiyambi. Apolisi anayi anapita okha kukagwira mtsogoleri wamkulu wotsutsa usiku. Palibe apolisi omwe anabwera kudzawathandiza. Magwero akuti izi zinali zolakwika. Gwero lina linafunsa kuti: mumatumiza bwanji apolisi ku mkhalidwe woterewu popanda wothandiza?

Apolisi atabwera kudzamugwira, Chithyola anaimbira loya wake, George Kadzipatike. Loyayo anawapempha kuti adikire. Atayesanso, Chithyola anali atapita. Otsutsa anatseka njira. Anthu ena panyumbapo anaopseza ndipo analankhula za wapolisi wa ACB wakufa, Issa Njaunju, yemwe anaphedwa akugwira ntchito yake. Apolisiwo anachita mantha. Anapempha thandizo la apolisi.

Magwero akuti Chembezi adauza Mkandawire kuti akhale panyumbapo kwa maola ambiri. Nthawi ya 11 koloko madzulo adauzidwa kuti achoke. Magwero akuti m'mawa wotsatira, Chembezi adapempha Mkandawire kuti alembe nkhani yokhudza kumangidwa kwa munthuyo. Koma Chithyola sanamangidwe. Magwero akuti izi sizinali zachilendo. Anthu ena mkati mwa ofesiyo akuti Chembezi ankafuna kuchotsa Mkandawire izi zisanachitike. Akuti tsopano akugwiritsa ntchito kumangidwa komwe sikunatheke ngati chifukwa chomuimba mlandu apolisiyo.

en.title: Chinese prisoner on trial for bribing jail and court workers
en.body: A High Court in Lilongwe started a corruption trial on May 13, 2026, against Lin Yinhua, a Chinese man jailed for wildlife crimes. Two witnesses told the court that while Lin was in Maula Prison, he left without prison clothes or handcuffs. One witness said in 2023, prison workers took Lin to a private house in Lilongwe. Another said in 2022, Lin went to buy maize seeds for his farm. Lin faces five charges, including trying to bribe prison and court workers.
en.body_more:
The State's lawyer, Peter Sambani from the Anti-Corruption Bureau, said the government has 15 to 19 witnesses ready to testify against Lin. He said the State is ready and is finishing paperwork before the next court date. Sambani told the court that at the next hearing, the State plans to call at least four witnesses each day. This will help finish the case faster. The judge, Redson Kapindu, stopped the trial and will set a new date soon.

Lin was arrested shortly after he got a Presidential pardon in 2025. The court said no to his bail request. He had been in prison for 14 years for wildlife crimes but served only four years before the pardon. Prosecutors say Lin paid prison workers to give him special favors and let him leave jail while he was still a prisoner.

Lin is also accused of trying to give a judge 30 million kwacha through a prison worker. Police say he did this to get a lighter punishment in his wildlife crime case. The trial will continue with more witness testimony in the coming weeks.

ny.title: Mkaidi waku China akuimbidwa mlandu wopereka ziphuphu ku ndende ndi ogwira ntchito kukhothi
ny.body: Khoti Lalikulu ku Lilongwe linayamba mlandu wa ziphuphu pa Meyi 13, 2026, motsutsana ndi Lin Yinhua, mwamuna waku China yemwe anamangidwa chifukwa cha milandu ya nyama zakuthengo. Mboni ziwiri zinauza khotilo kuti pamene Lin anali kundende ya Maula, anachoka wopanda zovala za m’ndende kapena unyolo. Mboni imodzi inati mu 2023, ogwira ntchito m’ndende adatengera Lin kunyumba yachinsinsi ku Lilongwe. Wina anati mu 2022, Lin anapita kukagula mbewu za chimanga pafamu yake. Lin akukumana ndi milandu isanu, kuphatikizapo kuyesa kupereka ziphuphu kwa ogwira ntchito m’ndende ndi m’khoti.
ny.body_more:
Loya wa Boma, Peter Sambani wochokera ku Anti-Corruption Bureau, anati boma lili ndi mboni 15 mpaka 19 zokonzeka kupereka umboni wotsutsa Lin. Iye anati Boma lakonzeka ndipo likumaliza mapepala asanafike tsiku lotsatira la khoti. Sambani anauza khoti kuti pamsonkhano wotsatira, Boma likukonzekera kuyitana mboni zosachepera zinayi tsiku lililonse. Izi zithandiza kumaliza mlanduwo mwachangu. Woweruza milandu, Redson Kapindu, anaimitsa mlanduwo ndipo adzakhazikitsa tsiku latsopano posachedwa.

Lin anamangidwa atangolandira chikhululukiro cha Purezidenti mu 2025. Khotilo linakana pempho lake la belo. Anali m'ndende kwa zaka 14 chifukwa cha milandu ya nyama zakuthengo koma anakhala zaka zinayi zokha chikhululukirocho chisanachitike. Otsutsa milandu akuti Lin analipira ogwira ntchito kundende kuti amupatse mphatso zapadera ndikumulola kutuluka m'ndende pamene anali mkaidi.

Lin akuimbidwanso mlandu woyesa kupatsa woweruza 30 miliyoni kwacha kudzera mwa wogwira ntchito kundende. Apolisi akuti adachita izi kuti alandire chilango chocheperako pa mlandu wake wa nyama zakuthengo. Mlanduwu upitirira ndi umboni wowonjezera wa mboni m'masabata akubwerawa.

en.title: Airtel Money Launches K600 Million Prize Promotion Across Malawi
en.body: Airtel Money has started a big reward program called the Tola Promotion. The program runs from May 13 to August 5, 2026. Airtel will give K600 million to lucky customers. The company says the promotion helps more people use digital money. Customers can win money by using Airtel Money for everyday tasks.
en.body_more:
The promotion has many prizes. Every day, some customers will win money. Three customers will win K5 million each week. Three hundred customers will get K50,000 each. One lucky customer will win K50 million. Customers who use the My Airtel app will have two chances to win instead of one.

To win, customers must do things like send money to other Airtel users (at least K5,000), pay bills, buy electricity, or buy things in shops. Joshua Sichinga works for Airtel Malawi. He said the promotion helps people use digital money more often. He said it shows that Airtel wants to help Malawi move to a cashless country.

But there is a warning for customers. The Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority says some people pretend to work for Airtel. These bad people send fake messages. They tell customers to send money first before they can get their prize. This is not true. Real Airtel promotions never ask customers to pay money to claim prizes. Muchanakhwaye Mpuluka from the Gaming Authority said people must be careful. He said scammers pressure people to send money fast by saying they have won.

Airtel Money told customers not to share their PIN or codes with people they do not know. Winners will only get messages from official Airtel channels. Airtel said customers must check all messages before they reply to them.

ny.title: Airtel Money Yayambitsa Kutsatsa Mphoto za K600 Million ku Malawi
ny.body: Airtel Money yayambitsa pulogalamu yopereka mphoto yayikulu yotchedwa Tola Promotion. Pulogalamuyi ikuchitika kuyambira pa 13 Meyi mpaka 5 Ogasiti, 2026. Airtel ipereka K600 miliyoni kwa makasitomala amwayi. Kampaniyo ikunena kuti kutsatsaku kumathandiza anthu ambiri kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama za digito. Makasitomala amatha kupambana ndalama pogwiritsa ntchito Airtel Money pa ntchito za tsiku ndi tsiku.
ny.body_more:
Kutsatsaku kuli ndi mphoto zambiri. Tsiku lililonse, makasitomala ena amapambana ndalama. Makasitomala atatu amapambana K5 miliyoni sabata iliyonse. Makasitomala mazana atatu amalandira K50,000 aliyense. Kasitomala m'modzi wamwayi amapambana K50 miliyoni. Makasitomala omwe amagwiritsa ntchito pulogalamu ya My Airtel amakhala ndi mwayi wopambana kawiri m'malo mwa umodzi.

Kuti apambane, makasitomala ayenera kuchita zinthu monga kutumiza ndalama kwa ogwiritsa ntchito ena a Airtel (osachepera K5,000), kulipira mabilu, kugula magetsi, kapena kugula zinthu m'masitolo. Joshua Sichinga amagwira ntchito ku Airtel Malawi. Anati kutsatsaku kumathandiza anthu kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama za digito pafupipafupi. Anati zikusonyeza kuti Airtel ikufuna kuthandiza Malawi kusamukira kudziko lopanda ndalama.

Koma pali chenjezo kwa makasitomala. Bungwe la Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority limati anthu ena amadzinenera kuti amagwira ntchito ku Airtel. Anthu oipawa amatumiza mauthenga abodza. Amauza makasitomala kuti atumize ndalama kaye asanalandire mphotho yawo. Izi sizowona. Kutsatsa kwenikweni kwa Airtel sikumapempha makasitomala kuti alipire ndalama kuti atenge mphoto. Muchanakhwe Mpuluka wochokera ku Gaming Authority anati anthu ayenera kusamala. Anati achinyengo amakakamiza anthu kuti atumize ndalama mwachangu ponena kuti apambana.

Airtel Money yauza makasitomala kuti asagawane ma PIN awo kapena ma code awo ndi anthu omwe sakuwadziwa. Opambana adzalandira mauthenga ochokera ku njira zovomerezeka za Airtel zokha. Airtel yati makasitomala ayenera kuyang'ana mauthenga onse asanawayankhe.

en.title: Airtel Money Starts K600 Million Prize Promotion
en.body: Airtel Money, a mobile money service in Malawi, has started a new promotion called 'Tola'. The promotion will run from May 13 to August 5, 2026. Airtel will give away K600 million in prizes to customers. People can win daily prizes, weekly prizes of K5 million, and one person will win K50 million.
en.body_more:
Customers who use the My Airtel App will have a better chance to win. They can win by sending money to other Airtel numbers with K5,000 or more, paying bills, buying electricity tokens, or making payments in shops. People who recharge their own phone with at least K500 can also join the draws.

Airtel Money Marketing Director Joshua Sichinga said this promotion helps people in Malawi use mobile money more often. He said the promotion is about giving hope and helping people and businesses do better. The company wants more Malawians to use mobile money every day instead of using cash.

But there is a danger. Fraudsters are sending fake messages to people. They say people have won money and ask them to send money first. The Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority (MAGLA) told people to be careful. Director General Muchanankhwaye Mpuluka said scammers use fake prize messages to trick people. He said people must never send money to claim a real prize. Airtel also warned customers not to share their PIN codes or bank details with unknown people. Winners will only be called by Airtel through official channels.

ny.title: Airtel Money Yayamba Kutsatsa Mphoto ya K600 Miliyoni
ny.body: Kampani ya Airtel Money, yomwe ndi kampani yopereka ndalama pafoni ku Malawi, yayamba kutsatsa kwatsopano kotchedwa 'Tola'. Kutsatsaku kudzayamba pa 13 Meyi mpaka 5 Ogasiti, 2026. Airtel ipereka mphoto zokwana K600 miliyoni kwa makasitomala. Anthu akhoza kupambana mphoto za tsiku ndi tsiku, mphoto za K5 miliyoni sabata iliyonse, ndipo munthu m'modzi adzapambana K50 miliyoni.
ny.body_more:
Makasitomala omwe amagwiritsa ntchito My Airtel App adzakhala ndi mwayi wopambana. Akhoza kupambana potumiza ndalama ku manambala ena a Airtel ndi K5,000 kapena kuposerapo, kulipira mabilu, kugula ma tokeni amagetsi, kapena kulipira m'masitolo. Anthu omwe amachaja mafoni awo ndi K500 akhozanso kulowa nawo mu mpikisanowu.

Woyang'anira Zamalonda wa Airtel Money, Joshua Sichinga, adati kutsatsa kumeneku kumathandiza anthu ku Malawi kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama zam'manja pafupipafupi. Iye anati kutsatsaku ndi kokhudza kupereka chiyembekezo ndi kuthandiza anthu ndi mabizinesi kuchita bwino. Kampaniyo ikufuna kuti anthu ambiri aku Malawi azigwiritsa ntchito ndalama zam'manja tsiku lililonse m'malo mogwiritsa ntchito ndalama.

Koma pali ngozi. Achinyengo akutumiza mauthenga abodza kwa anthu. Amati anthu apambana ndalama ndipo amawapempha kuti atumize ndalama kaye. Bungwe la Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority (MAGLA) lauza anthu kuti asamale. Mtsogoleri Wamkulu Muchanankhwaye Mpuluka adati achinyengo amagwiritsa ntchito mauthenga abodza a mphotho kuti anyenge anthu. Iye anati anthu sayenera kutumiza ndalama kuti apeze mphotho yeniyeni. Airtel idachenjezanso makasitomala kuti asagawane ma PIN code awo kapena zambiri za banki ndi anthu osadziwika. Opambana adzayimbidwa ndi Airtel kudzera m'njira zovomerezeka.

en.title: Chilima Mausoleum to Open in June 2026
en.body: A mausoleum for the late Vice President Saulos Chilima will open on June 13, 2026, at his home in Nsipe, Ntcheu. The date is three days after the second year mark of his death. Efrem Chilima, who is his close family member and heads the planning group, said the work is going well. Many people from all over Malawi are expected to come.
en.body_more:
Saulos Chilima died on June 10, 2024, in a plane crash. He was with eight other people. The plane came down at Thungwa in Chikangawa Forest. The group was on the way to a funeral for a lawyer named Ralph Kasambara in Nkhata Bay.

The opening day will start with a church service at 9 in the morning. The service will be at Nsipe Catholic grounds. After prayers, there will be talks, and then the mausoleum will be shown to the public. The family chose the church grounds because they expect a very large crowd of people.

Effrem Chilima said the family wants to honour the late Vice President's life and work. He said Saulos did many good things and helped many people. The family said thank you to God for giving them Saulos. They said they want to celebrate all the good he did.

The family will tell the public if there are any new plans for the event. The mausoleum opening is expected to be an important day for Malawi. Many people still remember Saulos as a strong and young leader. The ceremony will give Malawians a chance to think about him and say goodbye.

ny.title: Mausoleum a Chilima adzatsegulidwa mu June 2026
ny.body: Nyumba ya manda ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima idzatsegulidwa pa 13 June, 2026, kunyumba kwake ku Nsipe, Ntcheu. Tsikuli ndi masiku atatu kuchokera pamene anamwalira chaka chachiwiri. Efrem Chilima, yemwe ndi wachibale wake wapamtima komanso mtsogoleri wa gulu lokonzekera, anati ntchitoyi ikuyenda bwino. Anthu ambiri ochokera ku Malawi konse akuyembekezeka kubwera.
ny.body_more:
Saulos Chilima anamwalira pa June 10, 2024, pa ngozi ya ndege. Iye anali ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu. Ndegeyo inagwa ku Thungwa ku Chikangawa Forest. Gululo linali paulendo wopita ku maliro a loya dzina lake Ralph Kasambara ku Nkhata Bay.

Tsiku lotsegulira lidzayamba ndi mwambo wa tchalitchi nthawi ya 9 koloko m'mawa. Utumikiwo udzachitikira ku Nsipe Catholic grounds. Pambuyo pa mapemphero, padzakhala zokambirana, kenako manda adzawonetsedwa kwa anthu onse. Banjali linasankha malo a tchalitchi chifukwa likuyembekezera khamu lalikulu la anthu.

Effrem Chilima adati banjali likufuna kulemekeza moyo ndi ntchito ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti. Anati Saulos adachita zinthu zabwino zambiri ndipo adathandiza anthu ambiri. Banjali linati zikomo kwa Mulungu chifukwa chowapatsa Saulos. Anati akufuna kukondwerera zabwino zonse zomwe adachita.

Banjali lidzauza anthu ngati pali mapulani atsopano a mwambowu. Kutsegulira manda akuyembekezeka kukhala tsiku lofunika kwambiri ku Malawi. Anthu ambiri amakumbukirabe Saulos ngati mtsogoleri wamphamvu komanso wachinyamata. Mwambowu udzapatsa anthu aku Malawi mwayi woganizira za iye ndikutsanzikana naye.

en.title: Trade Fair to bring over 200 businesses together in May 2026
en.body: Malawi's chambers of commerce say they will bring together over 200 businesses for the 36th International Trade Fair in May 2026. The fair will run from May 21 to 29 in Blantyre. So far, 123 businesses have said they will come. The theme is about making more goods and growing Malawi's factories. The fair helps small and big companies meet and work together.
en.body_more:
Wezi Mungoni, who works for the chambers of commerce, said the fair is very important for Malawi's money and jobs. "The Trade Fair helps bring in money and makes our economy strong," she said. By focusing on making more things and building factories, the fair helps local businesses grow. It also helps Malawi use local things instead of buying from other countries.

Before the fair opens, the chambers will hold a golf game on May 20. This will help business people meet and talk to each other. Businesses will also be able to make new deals for buying and selling goods. During the fair, there will be many events. These include talks about business, a farm market, and a day for schools. Companies can meet face to face and find new partners.

Countries that want to come to the fair are South Africa, Tanzania, India, Zimbabwe and Mozambique. The fair brings companies from many types of work. These are factories, farms, and money businesses. Small and medium shops will have a special market to sell things they make. This helps them show what they can do and find big companies to buy from them.

The chambers say the trade fair helps Malawi in big ways. It makes companies work together. It helps new businesses start. And it brings money into the country from other places. The goal this year is to have more companies than last year.

ny.title: Chiwonetsero cha Zamalonda chidzagwirizanitsa mabizinesi oposa 200 mu Meyi 2026
ny.body: Mabungwe a zamalonda ku Malawi akuti adzasonkhanitsa mabizinesi opitilira 200 pa Chiwonetsero cha Zamalonda Chapadziko Lonse cha 36 mu Meyi 2026. Chiwonetserochi chidzachitika kuyambira pa Meyi 21 mpaka 29 ku Blantyre. Pakadali pano, mabizinesi 123 anena kuti abwera. Mutu wake ndi wokhudza kupanga katundu wambiri ndikukulitsa mafakitale aku Malawi. Chiwonetserochi chimathandiza makampani ang'onoang'ono ndi akuluakulu kukumana ndi kugwira ntchito limodzi.
ny.body_more:
Wezi Mungoni, yemwe amagwira ntchito ku nyumba zamalonda, anati chiwonetserochi n'chofunika kwambiri pa ndalama ndi ntchito za Malawi. "Chiwonetsero cha Zamalonda chimathandiza kubweretsa ndalama ndikulimbitsa chuma chathu," adatero. Mwa kuyang'ana kwambiri pakupanga zinthu zambiri ndikumanga mafakitale, chiwonetserochi chimathandiza mabizinesi am'deralo kukula. Chimathandizanso Malawi kugwiritsa ntchito zinthu zakomweko m'malo mogula kuchokera kumayiko ena.

Chiwonetserochi chisanatsegulidwe, nyumbazi zidzachita masewera a gofu pa Meyi 20. Izi zithandiza amalonda kukumana ndikukambirana. Mabizinesi azithanso kupanga mapangano atsopano ogulira ndi kugulitsa katundu. Pa chiwonetserochi, padzakhala zochitika zambiri. Izi zikuphatikizapo zokambirana za bizinesi, msika wa famu, ndi tsiku la masukulu. Makampani amatha kukumana maso ndi maso ndikupeza anzawo atsopano.

Mayiko omwe akufuna kubwera ku chiwonetserochi ndi South Africa, Tanzania, India, Zimbabwe ndi Mozambique. Chiwonetserochi chimabweretsa makampani ochokera kumitundu yosiyanasiyana ya ntchito. Awa ndi mafakitale, minda, ndi mabizinesi andalama. Masitolo ang'onoang'ono ndi apakatikati adzakhala ndi msika wapadera wogulitsa zinthu zomwe amapanga. Izi zimawathandiza kuwonetsa zomwe angachite ndikupeza makampani akuluakulu oti agule kwa iwo.

Mabungwe akuti chiwonetsero cha malonda chimathandiza Malawi m'njira zazikulu. Chimathandiza makampani kugwira ntchito limodzi. Chimathandiza mabizinesi atsopano kuyamba. Ndipo chimabweretsa ndalama mdziko muno kuchokera kumadera ena. Cholinga chaka chino ndikukhala ndi makampani ambiri kuposa chaka chatha.

en.title: Masters FC wins all three games, goes top of league
en.body: Masters FC beat Luanar Mitundu 2-0 on Monday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. This is Masters' third win in a row. The team now has nine points and sits at the top of the FDH Bank Premiership. FCB Nyasa Bullets are second with seven points. Masters bought a slot in the league this year and are new to the top division.
en.body_more:
Stain Davie scored the first goal for Masters in the 29th minute. Leo Jachikho scored an own goal in the 67th minute to make it 2-0. Davie was named the best player of the match. Masters have also beaten Karonga United 1-2 away and Chitipa United 1-0 at home in their first two games.

Coach Peter Mponda said he is happy with the win but does not want to make too much noise about it. He said they have only played three games and there is a long way to go in the season. He said his team was surprised to win all three matches because his plan was to win one game and draw two. But he added that their play was not perfect. The club's chief, Kevin Moyo, said the good form comes from the coach's skill and the help from the team's sponsor and people who run the club. He also said the league is long and hard, like a long run.

Mponda has a strong history at other teams. When he coached FCB Nyasa Bullets last season, the team came second. He won 21 games, drew three, and lost six. At Silver Strikers in 2024, he won the league title for the first time in 11 years. In total at these two teams, he won 40 games, drew 13, and lost only seven in 60 games. This means he has never lost his first three games at any club he has worked for.

Other new teams are also doing well this season. Red Lions are sixth with five points and have not lost a game. Mitundu are seventh with four points. Soccer expert Parry Chinyama said the new league system, called the NBS National Division League, helped these teams get ready. Last year, this league took the place of regional leagues. It finished on time, so teams could prepare well for the top league.

ny.title: Masters FC yapambana masewera onse atatu ndipo yakhala pamwamba pa ligi
ny.body: Masters FC yagonjetsa Luanar Mitundu 2-0 Lolemba pa Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Uku ndi kupambana kwachitatu kwa Masters motsatizana. Gululi tsopano lili ndi mapointi asanu ndi anayi ndipo lili pamwamba pa FDH Bank Premiership. FCB Nyasa Bullets ili pa nambala yachiwiri ndi mapointi asanu ndi awiri. Masters yagula malo mu ligi chaka chino ndipo ndi yatsopano mu gawo lapamwamba.
ny.body_more:
Stain Davie ndiye adagoletsa chigoli choyamba cha Masters mu mphindi ya 29. Leo Jachikho adagoletsa chigoli chake mu mphindi ya 67 zomwe zidapangitsa kuti zigoli zikhale 2-0. Davie adasankhidwa kukhala wosewera wabwino kwambiri pamasewerawa. Masters adagoletsanso Karonga United 1-2 kutali ndi Chitipa United 1-0 kunyumba m'masewera awo awiri oyamba.

Mphunzitsi Peter Mponda adati akusangalala ndi chigonjetso koma sakufuna kupanga phokoso kwambiri. Iye adati adasewera masewera atatu okha ndipo pali ulendo wautali woti apite munyengo ino. Iye adati timu yake idadabwa kupambana masewera onse atatu chifukwa cholinga chake chinali kupambana masewera amodzi ndikugoletsa awiri. Koma adawonjezera kuti masewera awo sanali angwiro. Mtsogoleri wa kilabu, Kevin Moyo, adati mawonekedwe abwino amachokera ku luso la mphunzitsi komanso thandizo kuchokera kwa othandizira a timuyi komanso anthu omwe amayendetsa kilabu. Anatinso ligi ndi yayitali komanso yovuta, ngati kuthamanga kwa nthawi yayitali.

Mponda ali ndi mbiri yabwino m'magulu ena. Pamene adaphunzitsa FCB Nyasa Bullets nyengo yatha, timuyi idabwera yachiwiri. Iye adapambana masewera 21, adagoletsa atatu, ndipo adataya asanu ndi limodzi. Ku Silver Strikers mu 2024, adapambana chikho cha ligi koyamba m'zaka 11. Ponseponse m'magulu awiriwa, adapambana masewera 40, adagoletsa 13, ndipo adataya asanu ndi awiri okha m'masewera 60. Izi zikutanthauza kuti sanatayepo masewera ake atatu oyamba ku kilabu iliyonse yomwe adagwirirapo ntchito.

Magulu ena atsopano akuchita bwino nyengo ino. Red Lions ili pa nambala 6 ndi mapointi asanu ndipo sanatayepo masewera aliwonse. Mitundu ili pa nambala 7 ndi mapointi anayi. Katswiri wa mpira Parry Chinyama adati njira yatsopano ya ligi, yotchedwa NBS National Division League, yathandiza magulu awa kukonzekera. Chaka chatha, ligi iyi idatenga malo a ligi zachigawo. Idatha pa nthawi yake, kuti magulu athe kukonzekera bwino ligi yapamwamba.

en.title: Court says MEC must move to Blantyre. Attorney General agrees.
en.body: A judge in Malawi said the government can move the election office from Lilongwe to Blantyre. Three people asked the court to stop the move. The judge said no. The Attorney General, Frank Mbeta, then said the office must go now. The President made this order in October 2025.
en.body_more:
The judge's name was Kenyatta Nyirenda. He works in the High Court. Three people asked him to stop the move and to check if the President had the right to do it. The judge said they did not have good reasons. He said busy people should not ask courts to do things that do not touch their own lives.
