Mbeta said the order is the law. He said the office must move fast. He said it is now too late to wait. Another judge, Simeon Mdeza, had already said no to the office in February. The office asked for time to think about the move. But the judge said it was late.

When asked about this, the office's person for news said they will talk later. Other people think the move is not good. Michael Kaiyatsa from a rights group said this long wait makes people feel bad and takes away the office's work on real elections. One expert said the people should sit down and talk.

The President also said Malawi Prison and one more office must go back to other towns. The election office came to Lilongwe in June 2023. The Prison moved in January 2024. An old President in 2006 moved many offices to the capital to make work easier.

ny.title: Khoti lati MEC iyenera kusamukira ku Blantyre. Loya Wamkulu akuvomereza.
ny.body: Woweruza milandu ku Malawi anati boma likhoza kusamutsa ofesi yosankha kuchokera ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre. Anthu atatu anapempha khoti kuti liletse kusamutsaku. Woweruzayo anati ayi. Loya Wamkulu, Frank Mbeta, kenako anati ofesiyo iyenera kuchotsedwa tsopano. Purezidenti anapereka lamuloli mu Okutobala 2025.
ny.body_more:
Dzina la woweruzayo linali Kenyatta Nyirenda. Amagwira ntchito ku Khoti Lalikulu. Anthu atatu adamupempha kuti asiye kusamukaku ndikuwona ngati Purezidenti ali ndi ufulu wochita izi. Woweruzayo adati alibe zifukwa zomveka. Anati anthu otanganidwa sayenera kupempha makhothi kuti achite zinthu zomwe sizikhudza miyoyo yawo.

Mbeta adati lamulo ndi lamulo. Anati ofesiyo iyenera kusuntha mwachangu. Anati tsopano yachedwa kwambiri kudikira. Woweruza wina, Simeon Mdeza, anali atakana kale ofesiyo mu February. Ofesiyo idapempha nthawi yoganizira za kusamukaku. Koma woweruzayo adati yachedwa.

Atafunsidwa za izi, munthu wodziwa nkhani za ofesiyo adati adzalankhula pambuyo pake. Anthu ena amaganiza kuti kusamukaku sikwabwino. Michael Kaiyatsa wochokera ku gulu la ufulu adati kudikira kwakanthawi kumeneku kumapangitsa anthu kumva chisoni ndikuchotsa ntchito ya ofesiyo pa zisankho zenizeni. Katswiri wina adati anthu ayenera kukhala pansi ndikukambirana.

Purezidenti adatinso ndende ya Malawi ndi ofesi ina iyenera kubwerera kumizinda ina. Ofesi ya zisankho inafika ku Lilongwe mu June 2023. Ndende inasamukira mu January 2024. Purezidenti wakale mu 2006 anasamukira maofesi ambiri ku likulu la dzikolo kuti ntchito ikhale yosavuta.

en.title: Court says MEC must move to Blantyre now
en.body: The Attorney General of Malawi says the Electoral Commission must move from Lilongwe to Blantyre. A High Court judge ruled on Monday that three people cannot stop this move. The President ordered the move in October 2025. The Attorney General said the move must happen now.
en.body_more:
Judge Kenyatta Nyirenda dismissed an application by three people who wanted the court to stop the move. They asked the court to review the President's order. The judge said they did not have good reasons for the court to help them.

The Attorney General, Frank Mbeta, said the order is still the law. He told the Electoral Commission it must relocate right now. He said the court made it clear that people cannot use the courts to stop orders that do not affect their own rights. The Electoral Commission said it will speak about this matter at the right time.

In February, another judge also rejected the Electoral Commission's request to review the order. The judge said the request came too late. The Electoral Commission said in April it would stay in Lilongwe until the law was clear.

The President also ordered other government offices to move. The Communications Authority and Housing Corporation must move to Blantyre. The Prison Service must move to Zomba. The President said this would help the government work better. All these offices had moved to Lilongwe between 2023 and 2024. Critics say moving these offices costs a lot of money and may cause problems for elections.

ny.title: Khoti Lati MEC Iyenera Kusamukira ku Blantyre Tsopano
ny.body: Loya Wamkulu wa Malawi akuti bungwe la zisankho liyenera kusamuka kuchoka ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre. Woweruza wa Khoti Lalikulu adagamula Lolemba kuti anthu atatu sangaletse kusamukaku. Purezidenti adalamula kusamukaku mu Okutobala 2025. Loya Wamkulu adati kusamukaku kuyenera kuchitika tsopano.
ny.body_more:
Woweruza milandu Kenyatta Nyirenda anakana pempho la anthu atatu omwe ankafuna kuti khothi liletse kusinthaku. Anapempha khothi kuti liwunikenso lamulo la Purezidenti. Woweruza milanduyo anati alibe zifukwa zomveka zoti khothi liwathandize.

Woyimira milandu, Frank Mbeta, anati lamuloli likadali lamulo. Anauza Khothi Loona za Chisankho kuti liyenera kusamuka pakali pano. Anati khothilo linanena momveka bwino kuti anthu sangagwiritse ntchito makhothi kuletsa malamulo omwe sakhudza ufulu wawo. Khothi Loona za Chisankho linati lidzalankhula za nkhaniyi panthawi yoyenera.

Mu February, woweruza wina anakananso pempho la Khothi Loona za Chisankho kuti liwunikenso lamuloli. Woweruzayo anati pempholi linabwera mochedwa kwambiri. Khothi Loona za Chisankho linati mu Epulo lidzakhala ku Lilongwe mpaka lamulo litamveka bwino.

Purezidenti analamulanso maofesi ena aboma kuti asamuke. Bungwe Loona za Mauthenga ndi Bungwe Loona za Nyumba ayenera kusamukira ku Blantyre. Bungwe Loona za Ndende liyenera kusamukira ku Zomba. Purezidenti anati izi zithandiza boma kugwira ntchito bwino. Maofesi onsewa anasamukira ku Lilongwe pakati pa 2023 ndi 2024. Otsutsa amati kusamutsa maofesiwa kumawononga ndalama zambiri ndipo kungayambitse mavuto pa zisankho.

en.title: Court cancels arrest warrant for opposition leader Chithyola Banda
en.body: A court in Lilongwe has stopped an arrest warrant for Simplex Chithyola Banda, the leader of the opposition in parliament and a former finance minister. The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) had the warrant made on 8 May 2026. The court ordered on 11 May that police must call Chithyola Banda to court by summons instead of arrest. His lawyer said it was wrong to arrest him when he was already on bail in another case.
en.body_more:
The ACB says Chithyola Banda misused his office as finance minister. They say he gave K29.4 billion in extra money to the Mega Farms Project without good reason. This money helped the Greenbelt Authority. The court agreed that the case should move forward by summons, not arrest. The judge said Chithyola Banda must follow the same bail rules as in his other case.

Chithyola Banda is also charged in a second case about a fertiliser deal. The ACB says that while he was finance minister, he approved a bad deal. Government money was used to buy fertiliser, but it was never delivered. This caused losses. The ACB says he broke the law by abusing his office and money laundering.

Meanwhile, the ACB faces problems inside. The director general suspended a senior officer named Robert Mkandawire. He was told to arrest Chithyola Banda on the night of 8 May but failed. The ACB says Mkandawire met Chithyola Banda at his home but did not take him to police. The ACB letter says Mkandawire did not follow orders and did not write a report. The court warned that if Chithyola Banda does not obey the new order, it is contempt of court.

ny.title: Khothi laletsa chikalata chomangira mtsogoleri wotsutsa Chithyola Banda
ny.body: Khothi ku Lilongwe laletsa chikalata chomangira Simplex Chithyola Banda, mtsogoleri wa chipani chotsutsa m'nyumba yamalamulo komanso nduna yakale ya zachuma. Bungwe lolimbana ndi ziphuphu (ACB) linapereka chikalatacho pa 8 Meyi 2026. Khothilo linalamula pa 11 Meyi kuti apolisi ayitane Chithyola Banda kukhothi pomuitana m'malo momumanga. Loya wake anati kunali kulakwitsa kumumanga pomwe anali kale pa belo pamlandu wina.
ny.body_more:
ACB ikunena kuti Chithyola Banda adagwiritsa ntchito molakwika udindo wake monga nduna ya zachuma. Akuti adapereka ndalama zowonjezera za K29.4 biliyoni ku Mega Farms Project popanda chifukwa chomveka. Ndalamazi zinathandiza Greenbelt Authority. Khotilo linavomereza kuti mlanduwu upitirire ndi kuitana, osati kumangidwa. Woweruza adati Chithyola Banda ayenera kutsatira malamulo omwewo a belo monga momwe zinalili pa mlandu wake wina.

Chithyola Banda akuimbidwanso mlandu pa mlandu wachiwiri wokhudza mgwirizano wa feteleza. ACB ikunena kuti pamene anali nduna ya zachuma, adavomereza mgwirizano woipa. Ndalama za boma zidagwiritsidwa ntchito kugula feteleza, koma sizinaperekedwe. Izi zidapangitsa kuti atayike ndalama. ACB ikunena kuti adaswa lamulo pogwiritsa ntchito molakwika ofesi yake komanso kutsuka ndalama.

Pakadali pano, ACB ikukumana ndi mavuto mkati. Director general adayimitsa wapolisi wamkulu dzina lake Robert Mkandawire. Adauzidwa kuti amange Chithyola Banda usiku wa pa 8 Meyi koma adalephera. ACB ikunena kuti Mkandawire adakumana ndi Chithyola Banda kunyumba kwake koma sanamutengere kupolisi. Kalata ya ACB ikunena kuti Mkandawire sanatsatire malamulo ndipo sanalembe lipoti. Khotilo linachenjeza kuti ngati Chithyola Banda satsatira lamulo latsopanoli, ndi kunyoza khoti.

en.title: Wildlife Trafficker's Corruption Trial Delayed Again Over Fuel Crisis
en.body: A court hearing for Chinese wildlife trafficker Lin Yunhua did not start on Tuesday in Lilongwe. Lin is facing charges of trying to bribe a judge and prison workers. A vehicle sent to bring him from Dedza Prison broke down on the way. Prison staff said there was not enough fuel. The court will try again on Wednesday.
en.body_more:
Lin is accused of offering money to prison officers to help him get a lighter sentence in his original wildlife case back in 2019. He was sent to jail for 14 years in September 2021 for selling rhino horns and ivory. He was released in July 2025 after serving only four years following a pardon by the President. The Anti-Corruption Bureau then arrested him again two months later.

Lin now faces seven charges of corruption and abuse of power while he was a prisoner. He is being held at Dedza Prison and waiting for his trial. He is accused of offering 30 million kwacha to Aaron Ganyavu Kaunda, an officer at Maula Prison, to help bribe the judge. The judge at that time was Violet Chipao. Lin wants to be released, but in November last year the court said no. The judge said Lin is a flight risk and the crimes are very serious.

The judge also warned Lin's lawyer, Powell Nkutabasa, not to ask for more delays. The judge said the court will not accept delays that waste time. The court has said the trial should take three days. The judge also told the defence team to find an interpreter so Lin can talk with his lawyers. This must happen within 14 days. The current head of the prosecution office, Festino Maere, used to be Lin's defence lawyer before he took his government job.

ny.title: Mlandu wa Katangale wa Wogulitsa Zinyama Zakuthengo Wayimitsidwanso Chifukwa cha Kusowa kwa Mafuta
ny.body: Mlandu wa khothi wokhudza wogulitsa nyama zakuthengo ku China, Lin Yunhua, sunayambe Lachiwiri ku Lilongwe. Lin akukumana ndi milandu yofuna kupereka ziphuphu kwa woweruza milandu ndi ogwira ntchito kundende. Galimoto yomwe inatumizidwa kuti imutenge kuchokera ku ndende ya Dedza inawonongeka panjira. Ogwira ntchito kundende anati mafuta anali ochepa. Khothi lidzayesanso mlandu Lachitatu.
ny.body_more:
Lin akuimbidwa mlandu wopereka ndalama kwa akuluakulu a ndende kuti amuthandize kupeza chilango chopepuka pa mlandu wake woyamba wa nyama zakuthengo mu 2019. Anatumizidwa kundende zaka 14 mu Seputembala 2021 chifukwa chogulitsa nyanga za chipembere ndi minyanga ya njovu. Anamasulidwa mu Julayi 2025 atagwira ntchito zaka zinayi zokha pambuyo pa chikhululukiro cha Purezidenti. Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu kenako linamumanganso miyezi iwiri pambuyo pake.

Lin tsopano akukumana ndi milandu isanu ndi iwiri ya ziphuphu ndi kugwiritsa ntchito mphamvu molakwika pamene anali mkaidi. Akusungidwa ku Dedza Prison ndipo akuyembekezera kuzengedwa mlandu. Akuimbidwa mlandu wopereka 30 miliyoni kwacha kwa Aaron Ganyavu Kaunda, mkulu wa ndende ya Maula, kuti athandize kupereka ziphuphu kwa woweruza. Woweruza panthawiyo anali Violet Chipao. Lin akufuna kumasulidwa, koma mu Novembala chaka chatha khotilo linati ayi. Woweruzayo anati Lin ndi pachiwopsezo chothawa ndipo milanduyi ndi yayikulu kwambiri.

Woweruzayo adachenjezanso loya wa Lin, Powell Nkutabasa, kuti asapemphe kuchedwa kwina. Woweruzayo adati khotilo silidzavomereza kuchedwa komwe kumawononga nthawi. Khotilo lati mlanduwo uyenera kutenga masiku atatu. Woweruza milandu adauzanso gulu loteteza milandu kuti lipeze womasulira kuti Lin athe kulankhula ndi maloya ake. Izi ziyenera kuchitika mkati mwa masiku 14. Mtsogoleri wa ofesi yotsutsa milandu, Festino Maere, anali loya wa Lin asanatenge ntchito yake m'boma.

en.title: Four Killed in Mob Attacks Over False Body Part Claims
en.body: Four people died in mob attacks in Chikwawa and Nsanje on 8 and 9 May 2026. People said, without proof, that some men were stealing male body parts through witchcraft. This is not true. Police and local groups said the killings are very wrong. They ask people to report crimes to police, not to kill.
en.body_more:
The Malawi Police Service arrested over 45 people for spreading false stories about the missing body parts. A police officer said that rumours are not proof. "No one should be hit or killed because of a story," the officer said. "Bring suspects to us, not to the grave." President Peter Mutharika told his office to say that these killings are bad and not right for today. The President told police to work hard to catch all the people who did the killings.

MASO, a group that works with migrants and communities, said Malawi has a big problem with wrong thinking. The group's leader, Dr. Harvey Chidoba Banda, said people must stop taking the law and making their own justice. He said the country has many cultures and beliefs, but all must be built on love, logic, and respect for human life. "Taking the law into your own hands is not culture. It is crime," Dr. Banda said. He told government and other groups to work together to teach people to think better.

This is not the first time Malawi has had mob killings over witch stories. Human rights people say these events show that many people do not trust the police. A leader from Nsanje said chiefs, churches, and government must all speak together against the killings. MASO said it will do more work in towns and villages to teach people about human rights and how to solve fights the right way, through courts and police.

ny.title: Anthu Anayi Aphedwa ndi Ziwopsezo za Gulu la Anthu Chifukwa cha Bodza Lonena za Ziwalo za Thupi
ny.body: Anthu anayi anamwalira pa ziwawa za anthu ku Chikwawa ndi Nsanje pa 8 ndi 9 Meyi 2026. Anthu anati, popanda umboni, kuti amuna ena akuba ziwalo za amuna pogwiritsa ntchito ufiti. Izi si zoona. Apolisi ndi magulu am'deralo anati kupha anthu kumeneku n'kolakwika kwambiri. Amapempha anthu kuti akanene milandu kwa apolisi, osati kupha.
ny.body_more:
Apolisi aku Malawi agwira anthu opitilira 45 chifukwa chofalitsa nkhani zabodza zokhudza ziwalo za thupi zomwe zasowa. Wapolisi wina anati mphekesera sizowona. "Palibe amene ayenera kumenyedwa kapena kuphedwa chifukwa cha nkhani," adatero wapolisiyo. "Tibweretseni okayikira, osati kumanda." Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adauza ofesi yake kuti anene kuti kupha kumeneku ndi koipa ndipo sikoyenera lero. Purezidenti adauza apolisi kuti agwire ntchito molimbika kuti agwire anthu onse omwe adachita kupha anthuwo.

MASO, gulu lomwe limagwira ntchito ndi anthu osamukira kumayiko ena ndi madera, lati Malawi ili ndi vuto lalikulu la kuganiza molakwika. Mtsogoleri wa gululo, Dr. Harvey Chidoba Banda, adati anthu ayenera kusiya kutenga malamulo ndikupanga chilungamo chawo. Iye adati dzikolo lili ndi zikhalidwe ndi zikhulupiriro zambiri, koma zonse ziyenera kumangidwa pa chikondi, malingaliro, ndi ulemu wa moyo wa munthu. "Kutenga malamulo m'manja mwanu si chikhalidwe. Ndi mlandu," adatero Dr. Banda. Adauza boma ndi magulu ena kuti agwire ntchito limodzi kuphunzitsa anthu kuganiza bwino.

Iyi si nthawi yoyamba Malawi kukhala ndi kupha anthu chifukwa cha nkhani za mfiti. Anthu omenyera ufulu wa anthu akuti zochitikazi zikusonyeza kuti anthu ambiri sakhulupirira apolisi. Mtsogoleri wochokera ku Nsanje anati mafumu, matchalitchi, ndi boma ayenera kulankhulana motsutsana ndi kupha anthu kumeneku. MASO yati ichita ntchito yambiri m'matauni ndi m'midzi kuphunzitsa anthu za ufulu wa anthu komanso momwe angathetsere mikangano moyenera, kudzera m'makhothi ndi apolisi.

en.title: Audit Finds K5.98 Billion Lost in Council Money Mix-ups
en.body: An audit report has found big money problems in Malawi's local councils. The Auditor General checked the money from 30 councils in 2023 and 2024. They found that K5.98 billion was spent in the wrong way. The money was not kept safe. Records were poor. Contracts were given out with no proper checks. The report says councils did not follow the law.
en.body_more:
The audit looked at whether councils used public money in the right way, followed the rules, and kept good records. Instead, auditors found many problems. Officers could not show where money went. They did not keep proper books. Councils gave out contracts without following the right steps. Some gave contracts to just one company when they should have asked many companies to bid. Some councils paid contractors who were not qualified. Some did not ask the right committees before paying money.

Auditors also found missing building materials, fuel that no one could account for, and money for boreholes that was used badly. Some councils spent money on projects like the Jali bus depot in ways that did not make sense. Members of Parliament ran some projects with Constituency Development Fund money but did not ask communities to help plan them. Cash books had numbers that did not match. Some councils had big debts they could not explain. Money amounts were wrong on the papers.

The Auditor General said councils broke three laws many times: the Public Finance Management Act, the Public Audit Act, and the Local Government Act. A man named Benedicto Kondowe said this shows deep problems in how councils work, not just small mistakes. He said the same bad things happen every year. The report asked for discipline against the officers who caused problems, and asked police to look into it. It also said councils must fix how they keep records and how they buy things.

Minister Ben Phiri said the government will stop money from being lost. He said they have made security tighter. But Kondowe and others said the same promise comes every year, and nothing changes. The Finance Ministry has sent the report to Parliament.

ny.title: Kafukufuku Wapeza Kuti K5.98 Biliyoni Yatayika Chifukwa cha Kusokonezeka kwa Ndalama za Bungwe
ny.body: Lipoti la kafukufuku lapeza mavuto azachuma ambiri m'makhonsolo am'deralo ku Malawi. Auditor General adayang'ana ndalama kuchokera ku makhonsolo 30 mu 2023 ndi 2024. Adapeza kuti K5.98 biliyoni idagwiritsidwa ntchito molakwika. Ndalamazo sizinasungidwe bwino. Zolemba zinali zoipa. Mapangano adaperekedwa popanda kufufuza koyenera. Lipotilo likuti makhonsolo sanatsatire lamulo.
ny.body_more:
Kafukufukuyu adayang'ana ngati makhansala adagwiritsa ntchito ndalama za boma moyenera, adatsatira malamulo, komanso adasunga zolemba zabwino. M'malo mwake, owerengera ndalama adapeza mavuto ambiri. Akuluakulu sanathe kuwonetsa komwe ndalama zidapita. Sanasunge mabuku oyenera. Makhansala adapereka mapangano osatsatira njira zoyenera. Ena adapereka mapangano ku kampani imodzi yokha pomwe adayenera kupempha makampani ambiri kuti apereke ma bid. Makhansala ena adalipira makontrakitala omwe sanali oyenerera. Ena sanafunse makomiti oyenera asanalipire ndalama.

Owerengera ndalama adapezanso zida zomangira zomwe zikusowa, mafuta omwe palibe amene angawafotokozere, ndi ndalama za mabowo omwe adagwiritsidwa ntchito molakwika. Makhansala ena adagwiritsa ntchito ndalama pama projekiti monga malo osungira mabasi a Jali m'njira zosamveka. Aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo adayendetsa mapulojekiti ena ndi ndalama za Constituency Development Fund koma sanapemphe madera kuti awathandize kukonzekera. Mabuku opezera ndalama anali ndi manambala omwe sankagwirizana. Makhansala ena anali ndi ngongole zazikulu zomwe sakanatha kuzifotokoza. Ndalama zomwe zinali zolakwika pamapepala.

Wowerengera Ndalama Wamkulu adati makhansala adaswa malamulo atatu kangapo: Public Finance Management Act, Public Audit Act, ndi Local Government Act. Munthu wina dzina lake Benedicto Kondowe anati izi zikusonyeza mavuto aakulu momwe makhonsolo amagwirira ntchito, osati zolakwa zazing'ono zokha. Anati zinthu zoyipa zomwezo zimachitika chaka chilichonse. Lipotilo linapempha chilango kwa apolisi omwe amayambitsa mavuto, ndipo linapempha apolisi kuti ayang'anenso. Linatinso makhonsolo ayenera kukonza momwe amasungira zolemba ndi momwe amagulira zinthu.

Nduna Ben Phiri anati boma liletsa kutayika kwa ndalama. Anati alimbitsa chitetezo. Koma Kondowe ndi ena anati lonjezo lomweli limabwera chaka chilichonse, ndipo palibe chomwe chimasintha. Unduna wa Zachuma watumiza lipotilo ku Nyumba Yamalamulo.

en.title: Derby match makes K90 million but leaders say money went missing
en.body: A big football match in Lilongwe on Sunday made K90.8 million. Mighty Wanderers played FCB Nyasa Big Bullets. Bullets won 2-1. But many people say the money is too low. They think people got in for free without paying. They say fake tickets and open gates lost the stadium millions of Kwacha.
en.body_more:
The match was at Bingu National Stadium. Before costs were paid, K42.3 million came from match day sales. K48.5 million came from tickets sold before the game. But people in football say this number is wrong. The same two teams played at the same place in April and made K108 million. In May at Kamuzu Stadium, they made K154 million. In October at the same place, they made K138.2 million. The Sunday match made much less money.

Sulom Vice General Secretary Donnex Chilonga said fake tickets were used. He said security guards opened gates that were not real gates. He said a group of people did this on purpose and made trouble to hide what they were doing. Sulom will now check what happened. Sulom may print all tickets itself in the future.

Bullets Chief Executive Officer Albert Chigoga said the club wants a full check. He said the game had bad management and bad security. The government said stakeholders must work together to stop money loss. Each club got K18.3 million after all costs and fees.

On the field, Blessings Mpokera and Gastin Phodo scored for Bullets. Promise Kamwendo scored for Wanderers. Bullets played well and won the match.

ny.title: Masewera a Derby apanga K90 miliyoni koma atsogoleri akuti ndalama zasowa
ny.body: Masewero akuluakulu a mpira ku Lilongwe Lamlungu adapeza K90.8 miliyoni. Mighty Wanderers idasewera ndi FCB Nyasa Big Bullets. Bullets idapambana 2-1. Koma anthu ambiri amati ndalamazo ndi zochepa kwambiri. Amaganiza kuti anthu adalowa kwaulere osalipira. Amati matikiti abodza ndipo zipata zotseguka zidataya ma Kwacha mamiliyoni ambiri pabwalo.
ny.body_more:
Masewerowa anali ku Bingu National Stadium. Ndalama zisanalipidwe, K42.3 miliyoni zinachokera ku malonda a tsiku la masewera. K48.5 miliyoni zinachokera ku matikiti omwe adagulitsidwa masewerawa asanayambe. Koma anthu a mpira amati nambala iyi ndi yolakwika. Magulu awiri omwewo adasewera pamalo amodzi mu Epulo ndipo adapeza K108 miliyoni. Mu Meyi ku Kamuzu Stadium, adapeza K154 miliyoni. Mu Okutobala pamalo omwewo, adapeza K138.2 miliyoni. Masewero a Lamlungu adapeza ndalama zochepa kwambiri.

Wachiwiri kwa Nduna Yaikulu ya Sulom, Donnex Chilonga, adati matikiti abodza adagwiritsidwa ntchito. Anati alonda adatsegula zipata zomwe sizinali zipata zenizeni. Anati gulu la anthu adachita izi dala ndipo adayambitsa mavuto kuti abise zomwe akuchita. Sulom tsopano iwona zomwe zidachitika. Sulom ikhoza kusindikiza matikiti onse mtsogolo.

Mkulu wa Bullets, Albert Chigoga, adati kalabuyo ikufuna cheke yonse. Anati masewerawa anali ndi kasamalidwe koyipa komanso chitetezo choyipa. Boma lati okhudzidwa ayenera kugwira ntchito limodzi kuti aletse kutayika kwa ndalama. Kalabu iliyonse idapeza K18.3 miliyoni pambuyo pa ndalama zonse ndi zolipiritsa.


Pabwalo, Blessings Mpokera ndi Gastin Phodo adagoletsa zigoli za Bullets. Promise Kamwendo adagoletsa zigoli za Wanderers. Bullets idasewera bwino ndipo idapambana masewerawa.

en.title: Fifteen Malawians brought home after bus crash in Zimbabwe
en.body: Fifteen coffins arrived at Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre on Monday night. The bodies were Malawians who died in a bus crash in Zimbabwe on May 5. The bus was travelling from South Africa to Malawi when it hit a lorry near Suswe. Families gathered at the stadium to receive their loved ones. The ceremony showed the pain and loss felt across the nation.
en.body_more:
Among the dead was an eight-month-old baby. There was also Unique Phiri Soko, a 46-year-old woman who was seven months pregnant. She left behind four children, including seven-year-old twins. Her brother-in-law James Gondwe said the family lost both the mother and the unborn child. Another victim was John Kanyenga, a 50-year-old man from Thyolo. He had worked in Cape Town for four years and was coming home to see his family. His elder brother Gracious Banda said Kanyenga was their main help and the loss has crushed the family.

According to the information given, six victims were women, five were men, and four were children. The victims came from five districts: six from Thyolo, four from Mzimba, two from Mangochi, two from Balaka, and one from Mulanje. Many of the passengers were leaving South Africa because of rising violence against foreigners. They escaped one danger but met another on the road.

Deputy Chief Secretary Stuart Ligomeka spoke for President Peter Mutharika. He said the victims were good people whose lives ended too soon. Most were young and had left Malawi to find work and help their families. The President gave K500,000 to each family to help pay for funerals. The government of Zimbabwe gave help after the crash, and Malawi said thank you for this support.

Many small children at the stadium stood beside coffins they did not understand. The crash shows how many Malawians take risky journeys in search of better lives because poverty is hard at home.

ny.title: Anthu 15 aku Malawi abwerera kwawo pambuyo pa ngozi ya basi ku Zimbabwe
ny.body: Mabokosi khumi ndi asanu afika ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre Lolemba usiku. Mitemboyo inali ya anthu aku Malawi omwe anamwalira pa ngozi ya basi ku Zimbabwe pa 5 Meyi. Basiyo inali paulendo wochokera ku South Africa kupita ku Malawi pamene inagunda lori pafupi ndi Suswe. Mabanja anasonkhana pa bwalo la masewera kuti alandire okondedwa awo. Mwambowu unasonyeza ululu ndi kutayika komwe kwamveka mdziko lonselo.
ny.body_more:
Pakati pa omwe adamwalira panali mwana wa miyezi isanu ndi itatu. Panalinso Unique Phiri Soko, mayi wazaka 46 yemwe anali ndi pakati pa miyezi isanu ndi iwiri. Iye anasiya ana anayi, kuphatikizapo mapasa azaka zisanu ndi ziwiri. Mlamu wake James Gondwe anati banjali linataya mayi ndi mwana wosabadwayo. Wozunzidwa wina anali John Kanyenga, mwamuna wazaka 50 wochokera ku Thyolo. Anagwira ntchito ku Cape Town kwa zaka zinayi ndipo anali kubwera kunyumba kudzaona banja lake. Mchimwene wake wamkulu Gracious Banda anati Kanyenga ndiye anali thandizo lawo lalikulu ndipo kutayikaku kwasokoneza banjali.

Malinga ndi zomwe zaperekedwa, anthu asanu ndi mmodzi omwe adazunzidwa anali akazi, asanu anali amuna, ndipo anayi anali ana. Anthu omwe adazunzidwawo adachokera m'maboma asanu: asanu ndi mmodzi ochokera ku Thyolo, anayi ochokera ku Mzimba, awiri ochokera ku Mangochi, awiri ochokera ku Balaka, ndi mmodzi wochokera ku Mulanje. Ambiri mwa okwera ndegewo anali kuchoka ku South Africa chifukwa cha chiwawa chomwe chinali chikukulirakulira kwa alendo. Anathawa ngozi imodzi koma anakumana ndi ina pamsewu.

Wachiwiri kwa Nduna Yaikulu Stuart Ligomeka analankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti Peter Mutharika. Iye anati anthu omwe anakhudzidwawo anali anthu abwino omwe miyoyo yawo inatha msanga. Ambiri anali achinyamata ndipo anachoka ku Malawi kukapeza ntchito ndi kuthandiza mabanja awo. Purezidenti anapereka K500,000 ku banja lililonse kuti lithandize kulipira maliro. Boma la Zimbabwe linapereka thandizo pambuyo pa ngoziyi, ndipo Malawi inati zikomo chifukwa cha thandizoli.

Ana ambiri aang'ono omwe anali pa bwalo lamasewera anaima pafupi ndi mabokosi omwe sanamvetse. Ngoziyi ikuwonetsa momwe anthu ambiri aku Malawi amayendera maulendo oopsa kufunafuna miyoyo yabwino chifukwa umphawi ndi wovuta kunyumba.

en.title: Fifteen Malawians come home in coffins after Zimbabwe bus crash
en.body: Fifteen coffins arrived at Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre on Monday night. Inside were the bodies of Malawians who died in a bus crash in Zimbabwe on May 5. The bus was travelling from South Africa to Malawi when it hit a lorry. Families came to the stadium to receive their dead. Police officers carried each coffin carefully. Many people cried. Children screamed. Mothers fell to the ground.
en.body_more:
Among the fifteen dead was an eight-month-old baby. Unique Phiri Soko was a forty-six-year-old woman who was seven months pregnant. She was going home to have her baby, but she died in the crash. Her baby died too. She left behind four children, including twins aged seven. Her brother-in-law James Gondwe said her husband is very sad.

John Kanyenga was fifty years old. He lived in Cape Town for four years and worked to send money home to his family. He was coming home to see them again. Instead, his body came home. His brother Gracious Banda said the family lost their helper. John gave money to their elderly mother and to everyone.

The bus had six women, five men and four children who died. Six came from Thyolo district. Four came from Mzimba. Two came from Mangochi. Two came from Balaka. One came from Mulanje. Many of the people on the bus were running away from trouble in South Africa. Bad things were happening to people from other countries there. But they met danger in Zimbabwe instead.

Deputy Chief Secretary Stuart Ligomeka spoke for President Peter Mutharika. He said these people were good citizens. Most were young. They left home because they had no work. The President gave five hundred thousand kwacha to each family to help pay for funerals. Zimbabwe's government helped bring the bodies home.

ny.title: Anthu 15 aku Malawi abwerera m'nyumba zawo atavala mabokosi pambuyo pa ngozi ya basi ku Zimbabwe
ny.body: Mabokosi 15 anafika ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre Lolemba usiku. Mkati mwake munali matupi a anthu aku Malawi omwe anamwalira pa ngozi ya basi ku Zimbabwe pa 5 Meyi. Basiyo inali kuyenda kuchokera ku South Africa kupita ku Malawi pamene inagunda lorry. Mabanja anabwera ku bwalo la masewera kudzalandira akufa awo. Apolisi ananyamula bokosi lililonse mosamala. Anthu ambiri analira. Ana anafuula. Amayi anagwa pansi.
ny.body_more:
Pakati pa anthu 15 omwe anamwalira panali mwana wa miyezi isanu ndi itatu. Phiri Soko, wachilendo, anali mayi wazaka 46 yemwe anali ndi pakati pa miyezi isanu ndi iwiri. Ankapita kunyumba kukabereka mwana wake, koma anamwalira pangoziyi. Mwana wake nayenso anamwalira. Anasiya ana anayi, kuphatikizapo mapasa azaka zisanu ndi ziwiri. Mlamu wake James Gondwe anati mwamuna wake ndi wachisoni kwambiri.

John Kanyenga anali ndi zaka 50. Anakhala ku Cape Town kwa zaka zinayi ndipo ankagwira ntchito yotumiza ndalama kunyumba kwa banja lake. Ankabweranso kunyumba kudzawaona. M'malo mwake, thupi lake linabwerera kunyumba. Mchimwene wake Gracious Banda anati banjali linataya wothandiza wawo. John anapereka ndalama kwa amayi awo okalamba komanso kwa aliyense.

Basiyo inali ndi akazi asanu ndi mmodzi, amuna asanu ndi ana anayi omwe anamwalira. Asanu ndi mmodzi anachokera ku chigawo cha Thyolo. Anayi anachokera ku Mzimba. Awiri anachokera ku Mangochi. Awiri anachokera ku Balaka. Mmodzi anachokera ku Mulanje. Anthu ambiri omwe anali m'basiyo anali kuthawa mavuto ku South Africa. Zinthu zoipa zinali kuchitika kwa anthu ochokera kumayiko ena kumeneko. Koma anakumana ndi zoopsa ku Zimbabwe m'malo mwake.

Wachiwiri kwa Nduna Wamkulu Stuart Ligomeka analankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti Peter Mutharika. Anati anthuwa anali nzika zabwino. Ambiri anali achinyamata. Anachoka kwawo chifukwa analibe ntchito. Purezidenti anapereka makwacha zikwi mazana asanu ku banja lililonse kuti lithandizire kulipira maliro. Boma la Zimbabwe linathandiza kubweretsa matupiwo kunyumba.

en.title: Lilongwe Area 2 wins FCB golf tournament in Blantyre
en.body: A team from Lilongwe Area 2 won a golf tournament on Saturday at Blantyre Sports Club. The four players were Nadim Omar, Jamir M'manga, Michael Khomani, and Bernard Ndau. They beat 88 other golfers to win the First Capital Bank Invitational Golf Tournament. The team scored 56 in the Mexican Scramble format. They won by two shots.
en.body_more:
The winning team played together with a combined handicap of nine. They each played their best shot each time, and the scores came together to make a strong result. After the win, team captain Omar said the team worked well as one. "We worked well together throughout the round, enjoyed the game, and that unity made the difference," he said.

Two other teams came close. The Dedza Branch Team came second with a net score of 58. The team had Frank Jomo, Boniface Chima, Lui Jiango, and Samuel Debwe. The Lilongwe Branch Team came third, with Akbar Bhana, Shabir Patel, Nazir Omar, and Tony Omar. Both of these teams also had a combined handicap of nine.

This tournament was the first time the Mexican Scramble format was played at Blantyre Sports Club. The event also marked the start of a new FCB Golf Tournament series. Blantyre Sports Club Chairperson Nathan Nkhata said he was happy with the turnout. He said the format made the game fast and fun.

First Capital Bank said it is happy to help the club. The bank's head of marketing, Twikale Chirwa, said the bank believes in helping communities where it does business. The bank has also built new bridges at the club.

ny.title: Lilongwe Area 2 yapambana mpikisano wa gofu wa FCB ku Blantyre
ny.body: Gulu la ku Lilongwe Area 2 linapambana mpikisano wa gofu Loweruka ku Blantyre Sports Club. Osewera anayiwa anali Nadim Omar, Jamir M'manga, Michael Khomani, ndi Bernard Ndau. Anagonjetsa osewera ena 88 a gofu kuti apambane mpikisano wa First Capital Bank Invitational Golf Tournament. Gululi linapeza zigoli 56 mu mtundu wa Mexican Scramble. Anapambana ndi zigoli ziwiri.
ny.body_more:
Gulu lopambana linasewera limodzi ndi anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe anali ndi vuto la kusowa kwa osewera. Aliyense anasewera bwino kwambiri nthawi iliyonse, ndipo zigoli zinagwirizana kuti zipange zotsatira zabwino. Pambuyo pa kupambana, kaputeni wa gululo Omar anati gululo linagwira ntchito bwino limodzi. "Tinagwira ntchito bwino limodzi nthawi yonseyi, tinasangalala ndi masewerawa, ndipo mgwirizano umenewo unapanga kusiyana," adatero.

Magulu ena awiri anayandikira. Gulu la Dedza Branch linabwera lachiwiri ndi zigoli 58. Gululo linali ndi Frank Jomo, Boniface Chima, Lui Jiango, ndi Samuel Debwe. Gulu la Lilongwe Branch linabwera lachitatu, ndi Akbar Bhana, Shabir Patel, Nazir Omar, ndi Tony Omar. Magulu onsewa analinso ndi anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe anali ndi vuto la kusowa kwa osewera.

Mpikisano uwu unali nthawi yoyamba kuti mtundu wa Mexican Scramble useweredwe ku Blantyre Sports Club. Chochitikachi chinasonyezanso kuyamba kwa mndandanda watsopano wa FCB Golf Tournament. Wapampando wa Blantyre Sports Club, Nathan Nkhata, anati anali wokondwa ndi anthu omwe anapezekapo. Anati mawonekedwe ake anapangitsa kuti masewerawa akhale ofulumira komanso osangalatsa.

First Capital Bank inati ikusangalala kuthandiza gululo. Mkulu wa zamalonda ku bankiyi, Twikale Chirwa, anati bankiyi imakhulupirira kuthandiza madera omwe imachita bizinesi. Bankiyi yamanganso milatho yatsopano ku kilabu.

en.title: Group Defends Pacific Limited Over Limbe Power Cut
en.body: A group called MaBLEM says Pacific Limited did not cause a blackout in Limbe during the rainy season. The group visited the site and checked what happened. Heavy rain damaged an ESCOM electricity wall. The wall fell and broke electricity poles. This caused the power to go down for one day. MaBLEM says the company did the work on its own land and followed all the rules.
en.body_more:
MaBLEM checked the area in Blantyre where the blackout happened. The group says heavy rains were the main cause of the problem. ESCOM poles and walls sit on high ground near Pacific Limited's new shopping building. The rain was very strong. Water and mud pushed the wall down. The poles fell and broke the power lines. This is what caused the blackout. MaBLEM also found that heavy rains damaged more than 28 ESCOM power installations across Malawi at the same time.

The group says Pacific Limited is being treated unfairly. Many people and businesses in Malawi blame the company for problems that happen. MaBLEM says this is not fair to local companies. Pacific Limited is owned by Malawians and creates many jobs. The company built Pacific Towers in Limbe. More than 200 black-owned shops now work there. The building gives cheap space to local business owners.

MaBLEM also says Pacific Limited fixed more than 10,000 boreholes across Malawi. These boreholes give clean water to people in villages and poor areas. The group says the company also built an underground tunnel under its own building. This tunnel helps ESCOM move power lines in the future. This shows that Pacific Limited works well with ESCOM. MaBLEM asked all media, leaders, and the public to report facts about local companies. They must be fair and not use angry words or guesses.

ny.title: Gululi Lateteza Pacific Limited Chifukwa cha Kudula Mphamvu kwa Limbe
ny.body: Gulu lotchedwa MaBLEM likuti Pacific Limited sinachititse kuti magetsi azimitse ku Limbe nthawi yamvula. Gululo linapita kumaloko ndi kukawona zomwe zinachitika. Mvula yamphamvu inawononga khoma lamagetsi la ESCOM. Khoma linagwa ndikuswa mitengo yamagetsi. Izi zinapangitsa kuti magetsi azimitse kwa tsiku limodzi. MaBLEM akuti kampaniyo idagwira ntchitoyo pamalo ake ndipo idatsatira malamulo onse.
ny.body_more:
MaBLEM adayang'ana dera lomwe magetsi adazimitsidwa. Gululi likuti mvula yamphamvu ndiyo idayambitsa vutoli. Mizati ndi makoma a ESCOM ali pamalo okwera pafupi ndi nyumba yatsopano yogulitsira ya Pacific Limited. Mvula inali yamphamvu kwambiri. Madzi ndi matope zidakankhira khoma pansi. Mizatiyo idagwa ndikuswa zingwe zamagetsi. Ichi ndi chomwe chidayambitsa magetsi kuzimitsidwa. MaBLEM adapezanso kuti mvula yamphamvu idawononga malo opitilira 28 amagetsi a ESCOM ku Malawi nthawi imodzi.

Gululi likuti Pacific Limited ikuchitiridwa zinthu mopanda chilungamo. Anthu ambiri ndi mabizinesi ku Malawi akuimba mlandu kampaniyo chifukwa cha mavuto omwe amachitika. MaBLEM akuti izi sizolungama kwa makampani am'deralo. Pacific Limited ndi ya anthu aku Malawi ndipo imapanga ntchito zambiri. Kampaniyo idamanga Pacific Towers ku Limbe. Masitolo opitilira 200 a anthu akuda tsopano akugwira ntchito kumeneko. Nyumbayi imapereka malo otsika mtengo kwa eni mabizinesi am'deralo.

MaBLEM ikunenanso kuti Pacific Limited idakonza mabowo opitilira 10,000 ku Malawi konse. Mabowo awa amapereka madzi oyera kwa anthu m'midzi ndi m'madera osauka. Gululo likunena kuti kampaniyo idamanganso ngalande yapansi panthaka pansi pa nyumba yake. Ngalande iyi imathandiza ESCOM kusuntha mawaya amagetsi mtsogolo. Izi zikusonyeza kuti Pacific Limited imagwira ntchito bwino ndi ESCOM. MaBLEM adapempha atolankhani onse, atsogoleri, ndi anthu onse kuti afotokoze zoona zokhudza makampani am'deralo. Ayenera kukhala achilungamo osagwiritsa ntchito mawu okwiya kapena kulosera.

en.title: Bullets beat Wanderers 2-1 in fierce derby match
en.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets won 2-1 against Mighty Wanderers on Sunday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. This was a big win for Bullets. They had lost four games in a row to Wanderers before this match. Blessings Mpokera scored first from a penalty in the fifth minute. Maxwell Gasten Phodo scored the second goal in the 58th minute. Promise Kamwendo scored one goal for Wanderers in the 61st minute.
en.body_more:
The match was very tense and had many angry moments. In the first half, players Ephraim Kondowe and Dan Kudonto were both given yellow cards after they fought each other. In the second half, things got worse. Wanderers played hard and hit the post three times. But Bullets scored their second goal when Phodo headed in from a corner kick. Three minutes later, Wanderers got a penalty and Kamwendo scored to make it 2-1.

The game became very rough near the end. A Bullets official named Noel Kaole was sent off in the 69th minute. Then Phodo and Gaddie Chirwa from Wanderers were both sent off after they fought each other on the field. Wanderers threw everything forward to try to score again, but Bullets' goalkeeper Innocent Nyasulu made good saves to keep the score at 2-1.

The match made a lot of money for the teams. The stadium collected K90.8 million in total. After all costs were paid, the teams shared K36.6 million equally. Each team got K18.3 million. Analyst Ibrahim Phiri said the money was lower than expected because some people may have got into the stadium without paying. Bullets now has seven points from three games and is top of the table. Wanderers has three points from two games.

ny.title: Bullets yagonjetsa Wanderers 2-1 pamasewera a derby
ny.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets idapambana 2-1 motsutsana ndi Mighty Wanderers Lamlungu pa Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Uku kunali kupambana kwakukulu kwa Bullets. Adataya masewera anayi motsatizana ndi Wanderers masewerawa asanafike. Blessings Mpokera adagoletsa chigoli choyamba kuchokera ku penalty mu mphindi yachisanu. Maxwell Gasten Phodo adagoletsa chigoli chachiwiri mu mphindi ya 58. Promise Kamwendo adagoletsa chigoli chimodzi cha Wanderers mu mphindi ya 61.
ny.body_more:
Masewerowa anali ovuta kwambiri ndipo anali ndi nthawi zambiri zokwiya. Mu theka loyamba, osewera Ephraim Kondowe ndi Dan Kudonto onse anapatsidwa makadi achikasu atamenyana. Mu theka lachiwiri, zinthu zinaipiraipira. Wanderers adasewera mwamphamvu ndipo adagunda positi katatu. Koma Bullets adapeza chigoli chachiwiri pomwe Phodo adatsogolera kuchokera ku kona. Patatha mphindi zitatu, Wanderers adapeza penalty ndipo Kamwendo adapeza chigoli chomwe chinapangitsa kuti masewerowa akhale 2-1.

Masewerowa adakhala ovuta kwambiri kumapeto. Woyang'anira wa Bullets dzina lake Noel Kaole adatulutsidwa mu mphindi ya 69. Kenako Phodo ndi Gaddie Chirwa ochokera ku Wanderers onse adatulutsidwa mu red atamenyana pabwalo. Wanderers adaponya chilichonse patsogolo kuti ayesere kupeza chigoli kachiwiri, koma chigoli cha Bullets, Innocent Nyasulu, adapulumutsa bwino kuti zigolizo zipitirire 2-1.

Masewerowa adapanga ndalama zambiri kwa maguluwa. Bwaloli lidapeza K90.8 miliyoni yonse. Ndalama zonse zitaperekedwa, maguluwa adagawana K36.6 miliyoni mofanana. Gulu lililonse lidapeza K18.3 miliyoni. Katswiri wa zamaganizo Ibrahim Phiri anati ndalamazo zinali zochepa kuposa momwe ankayembekezera chifukwa anthu ena mwina analowa m'bwalo lamasewera osalipira. Bullets tsopano ili ndi mapointi asanu ndi awiri kuchokera pamasewera atatu ndipo ili pamwamba pa tebulo. Wanderers ili ndi mapointi atatu kuchokera pamasewera awiri.

en.title: Malawi mining boom: who will benefit most?
en.body: Malawi is set to earn between $30 billion and $43 billion from mining over the next 14 years. The country has rare earth deposits at Kangankunde and rutile at Kasiya. These minerals are needed for electric cars and clean energy. But many people ask: who will really get the money and who will pay the cost?
en.body_more:
The Balaka mining project at Kangankunde is expected to make $114 million every year. But Balaka District Council gets nothing from this money. The council still has to run the district and pay for schools and roads. This is because the law does not give mining money to local councils.

Bertha Phiri heads the Malawi Economic Justice Network. She said the mining laws need to change. She wants district councils to get part of the money from mining. She also said people must know what mining companies agree to do. Mining expert Grain Malunga said Malawi forgot about local content. This means using local workers and local goods in mining work. He said district councils have not got ready for this.

Balaka District Commissioner Bibu Yusufu Mdala said the council gets only K586.6 million each year but needs to spend K55.8 billion. The council depends on money from the central government. Most mining money now goes to central government or to companies. Only when a company signs a Community Development Agreement does the local area get money—just 0.45 percent of yearly revenue.

Governance expert Henry Chingayipe said the country should use a Sovereign Wealth Fund for mining money. He said much of this money should go to towns and villages near mines, to research, and to build better roads and buildings. The Malawi Mining Regulatory Authority has not yet answered questions about these proposals.

ny.title: Kukwera kwa migodi ku Malawi: ndani adzapindule kwambiri?
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi likuyembekezeka kupeza ndalama zokwana $30 biliyoni ndi $43 biliyoni kuchokera ku migodi m'zaka 14 zikubwerazi. Dzikoli lili ndi malo osungiramo nthaka osowa ku Kangankunde ndi rutile ku Kasiya. Miyala iyi ikufunika pamagalimoto amagetsi ndi magetsi oyera. Koma anthu ambiri amafunsa kuti: ndani angapeze ndalamazo ndipo ndani adzalipira mtengo wake?
ny.body_more:
Ntchito yomanga migodi ya Balaka ku Kangankunde ikuyembekezeka kupanga $114 miliyoni chaka chilichonse. Koma Khonsolo ya Balaka District Council silandira chilichonse kuchokera ku ndalamazi. Khonsoloyo ikuyenerabe kuyendetsa chigawochi ndikulipira masukulu ndi misewu. Izi zili choncho chifukwa lamulo silipereka ndalama za migodi ku makhonsolo am'deralo.

Bertha Phiri akutsogolera Malawi Economic Justice Network. Iye anati malamulo a migodi ayenera kusintha. Akufuna kuti makhonsolo am'madera apeze gawo la ndalama kuchokera ku migodi. Anatinso anthu ayenera kudziwa zomwe makampani amigodi amavomereza kuchita. Katswiri wa migodi Grain Malunga adati Malawi yaiwala za zomwe zili m'deralo. Izi zikutanthauza kugwiritsa ntchito antchito am'deralo ndi katundu wakomweko pantchito ya migodi. Iye anati makhonsolo am'madera sanakonzekere izi.

Khonsolo ya Balaka District Commissioner Bibu Yusufu Mdala adati khonsoloyo imapeza K586.6 miliyoni yokha pachaka koma ikufunika kugwiritsa ntchito K55.8 biliyoni. Khonsoloyo imadalira ndalama kuchokera ku boma lalikulu. Ndalama zambiri za migodi tsopano zimapita ku boma lalikulu kapena ku makampani. Kampani ikasaina Pangano la Chitukuko cha Anthu am'deralo ndiye kuti dera la m'deralo limapeza ndalama - 0.45 peresenti yokha ya ndalama zomwe amapeza pachaka.

Katswiri wa zaulamuliro Henry Chingayipe adati dzikolo liyenera kugwiritsa ntchito Sovereign Wealth Fund popereka ndalama za migodi. Iye adati ndalama zambiri mwa izi ziyenera kupita ku matauni ndi midzi yapafupi ndi migodi, kukafufuza, ndikumanga misewu ndi nyumba zabwino. Bungwe loona za migodi ku Malawi silinayankhe mafunso okhudza malingaliro awa.

en.title: Malawi's mining boom: who gets the money?
en.body: Malawi is about to dig for rare minerals. The country has gold, rare earth metals and other minerals. Mining could bring $30 to $43 billion in fourteen years. But people ask: Who will benefit? Poor people who live near the mines may not get much. Laws say people must be told before mining starts. But in reality, many communities do not understand what is happening.
en.body_more:
The Kangankunde mine in Balaka District shows the problem. This mine will make $114 million each year. But Balaka District Council will get zero money. The council has very little money to run schools and fix roads. It must ask the central government for help.

The problem is that current law does not share mining money with local councils. Only if a mining company signs a special agreement with the community does the local area get 0.45 percent of the money. Bertha Phiri works for a justice group. She said the laws must change. She said district councils must get part of the mining money. She also said communities must see all mining contracts.

Grain Malunga is a mining expert. He said Malawi has not focused enough on using local workers and local materials in mines. "Local people must have jobs in mining," he said. But district councils are not ready to train workers or run mines well. They do not have enough money or skills.

Balaka District has a budget of K55.8 billion each year. But it can only collect K586.6 million itself. The rest comes from the government and donors. This means the district cannot do much without help from above. Mining money could change this. The government says it will think about these ideas. But the Mining Authority has not yet answered questions about sharing money fairly with local areas.

ny.title: Kukwera kwa migodi ku Malawi: ndani amapeza ndalama?
ny.body: Malawi yatsala pang'ono kukumba miyala yamtengo wapatali yosowa. Dzikoli lili ndi golide, zitsulo zamtengo wapatali ndi miyala ina yamtengo wapatali. Kukumba migodi kungabweretse madola 30 mpaka 43 biliyoni m'zaka khumi ndi zinayi. Koma anthu amafunsa kuti: Ndani adzapindula? Anthu osauka omwe amakhala pafupi ndi migodi sangapeze zambiri. Malamulo amanena kuti anthu ayenera kuuzidwa migodi isanayambe. Koma zoona zake n'zakuti, madera ambiri samvetsa zomwe zikuchitika.
ny.body_more:
Mgodi wa Kangankunde ku Balaka District ukuwonetsa vutoli. Mgodi uwu upanga $114 miliyoni chaka chilichonse. Koma Balaka District Council sidzalandira ndalama. Balaka ili ndi ndalama zochepa zoyendetsera masukulu ndi kukonza misewu. Iyenera kupempha thandizo ku boma lalikulu.

Vuto ndilakuti lamulo lomwe lilipo siligawana ndalama za migodi ndi makhonsolo am'deralo. Pokhapokha ngati kampani ya migodi yasaina mgwirizano wapadera ndi anthu ammudzi, dera la m'deralo limapeza 0.45 peresenti ya ndalamazo. Bertha Phiri amagwira ntchito ku gulu la chilungamo. Anati malamulo ayenera kusintha. Anati makhonsolo am'madera ayenera kupeza gawo la ndalama za migodi. Anatinso madera ayenera kuwona mapangano onse a migodi.

Grain Malunga ndi katswiri wa migodi. Anati Malawi sinayang'ane mokwanira kugwiritsa ntchito antchito am'deralo ndi zipangizo zam'deralo m'migodi. "Anthu am'deralo ayenera kukhala ndi ntchito za migodi," adatero. Koma makhonsolo am'madera sali okonzeka kuphunzitsa antchito kapena kuyendetsa bwino migodi. Alibe ndalama zokwanira kapena luso.

Balaka District ili ndi bajeti ya K55.8 biliyoni chaka chilichonse. Koma imatha kungosonkhanitsa K586.6 miliyoni yokha. Zotsalazo zimachokera ku boma ndi opereka ndalama. Izi zikutanthauza kuti chigawochi sichingachite zambiri popanda thandizo lochokera kwa akuluakulu. Ndalama za migodi zitha kusintha izi. Boma likuti lidzaganizira za malingaliro awa. Koma Boma la Migodi silinayankhebe mafunso okhudza kugawana ndalama mwachilungamo ndi madera am'deralo.

en.title: Former Finance Minister Banda Rejects Arrest Claims
en.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, a former Finance Minister, says he is not running from the law. He says reports that he is evading arrest are false and politically motivated. The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) has issued a warrant for his arrest. Banda's team says he left Lilongwe on Friday evening before he knew about the warrant. They say he will work with the ACB when they contact him properly.
en.body_more:
The ACB says Banda approved about K29.5 billion without proper approval. The money went to the Mega Farms Project and the Greenbelt Authority. An ACB spokesperson, Jacqueline Ngongonda, said the funds left government procedures the wrong way. A court in Lilongwe issued the warrant on May 8, 2026.

Banda's helpers say he did not get any letter from the ACB before he travelled. They say it is unusual for police to try to arrest someone at night without talking to them first. They say normal practice is to contact the person and let them come in themselves. Banda's team says he is calm and ready to work with investigators.

Banda has faced other legal troubles recently. On April 13, police arrested him over the East Bridge fertilizer deal. He was accused of abuse of office and money laundering in that case too. Banda's helpers say he presented himself to police before, and he will do the same now. They say the reports that Banda is running are not true and damage his name without fair process.

ny.title: Nduna yakale ya zachuma Banda akana zomwe akunena kuti wamangidwa
ny.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, yemwe kale anali Nduna ya Zachuma, akuti sakuthawa lamulo. Iye akuti malipoti oti akupewa kumangidwa ndi abodza komanso okhudzana ndi ndale. Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu (ACB) lapereka chikalata chomumanga. Gulu la Banda likuti adachoka ku Lilongwe Lachisanu madzulo asanadziwe za chikalatacho. Akuti adzagwira ntchito ndi ACB akalankhulana naye moyenera.
ny.body_more:
Bungwe la ACB likuti Banda adavomereza pafupifupi K29.5 biliyoni popanda chilolezo choyenera. Ndalamazo zidapita ku Mega Farms Project ndi Greenbelt Authority. Mneneri wa ACB, Jacqueline Ngongonda, adati ndalamazo zidasiya njira za boma molakwika. Khothi ku Lilongwe lidapereka chikalatacho pa Meyi 8, 2026.

Othandiza a Banda akuti sanalandire kalata iliyonse kuchokera ku ACB asanapite. Akuti sizachilendo kuti apolisi ayesere kumanga munthu usiku osalankhula naye kaye. Akuti chizolowezi chachizolowezi ndikulankhula ndi munthuyo ndikumulola kuti abwere okha. Gulu la Banda likuti ali bata ndipo ali wokonzeka kugwira ntchito ndi ofufuza.

Banda wakumana ndi mavuto ena azamalamulo posachedwapa. Pa Epulo 13, apolisi adamugwira chifukwa cha mgwirizano wa feteleza ku East Bridge. Anamuimbidwa mlandu wogwiritsa ntchito molakwika ofesi komanso kutsuka ndalama pankhaniyi. Othandiza a Banda akuti adadzipereka kale kwa apolisi, ndipo achita chimodzimodzi tsopano. Akuti malipoti akuti Banda akuyendetsa si oona ndipo amawononga dzina lake popanda njira yoyenera.

en.title: Ex-Finance Minister Says He Is Not Running From Police
en.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, a former Finance Minister, says he is not hiding from the law. The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) has said it has a warrant to arrest him. But Chithyola says the reports are false and wrong. He says he left the city on Friday evening before he knew about the warrant. He says he will help the ACB.
en.body_more:
The ACB says Chithyola took about 29.5 billion kwacha of government money. This money was for the Mega Farms Project, the ACB says. The warrant was signed by the Lilongwe court on May 8, 2026. ACB spokesperson Jacqueline Ngongonda said Chithyola gave money without good reason. The money went to the Greenbelt Authority, she said.

Chithyola's helpers say he did nothing wrong. They say he left Lilongwe on Friday night. The warrant came later that same night, they say. They also say the ACB did not call him first. They say this is not how the law works. They say suspects get a call before police come at night. "The ACB did not call him on Friday," his aide said. "We think the ACB will call him today."

Chithyola's team says he is not a man who runs away. They say when police came for him before, in April, he went to them. "He will come when you ask him," his aide said. "People who say he is hiding are wrong."

This is the second time in weeks that Chithyola faces arrest. On April 13, police from the Fiscal and Fraud section took him in over other charges. Those charges were about money and abuse of office. They link to a fertilizer deal called East Bridge. Chithyola says he did not break the law in that case either.

ny.title: Nduna Yakale ya Zachuma Yati Sakuthawa Apolisi
ny.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, yemwe kale anali Nduna ya Zachuma, akuti sakubisalira lamulo. Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu (ACB) lati lili ndi chikalata chomumanga. Koma Chithyola akuti malipotiwo ndi abodza komanso olakwika. Akuti adachoka mumzindawu Lachisanu madzulo asanadziwe za chikalatacho. Akuti athandiza ACB.
ny.body_more:
ACB ikunena kuti Chithyola adatenga ndalama zokwana 29.5 biliyoni za boma. Ndalamazi zinali za Mega Farms Project, malinga ndi ACB. Chilolezocho chidasainidwa ndi khothi la Lilongwe pa Meyi 8, 2026. Mneneri wa ACB, Jacqueline Ngongonda, adati Chithyola adapereka ndalama popanda chifukwa chomveka. Ndalamazo zidapita ku Greenbelt Authority, adatero.

Othandiza a Chithyola akuti sanalakwitse chilichonse. Akuti adachoka ku Lilongwe Lachisanu usiku. Chilolezocho chidabwera usiku womwewo, akutero. Akutinso ACB sanamuyimbire foni kaye. Akuti umu si momwe lamulo limagwirira ntchito. Akuti okayikiridwa amalandira foni apolisi asanabwere usiku. "A ACB sanamuyimbire foni Lachisanu," adatero wothandizira wake. "Tikuganiza kuti ACB imuyimbira foni lero."

Gulu la Chithyola likuti si munthu wothawa. Akuti apolisi atabwera kudzamutenga kale, mu Epulo, adapita kwa iwo. "Adzabwera mukamufunsa," adatero wothandizira wake. "Anthu omwe akunena kuti akubisala ndi olakwika."

Iyi ndi nthawi yachiwiri m'masabata angapo kuti Chithyola amangidwe. Pa Epulo 13, apolisi ochokera ku gawo la Zachuma ndi Zachinyengo adamugwira chifukwa cha milandu ina. Milandu imeneyo inali yokhudza ndalama ndi kugwiritsa ntchito molakwika udindo. Imakhudzana ndi mgwirizano wa feteleza wotchedwa East Bridge. Chithyola akunena kuti sanaphwanye lamulo pankhaniyi.

en.title: Artists ask for more help to grow the art industry
en.body: Visual artists in Malawi want more support and money for their work. They made this call at Art in the Park 2026, an event held at Four Seasons Gardens in Lilongwe from Friday to Sunday. Artists said there are not enough spaces to show their work. They also asked people to buy local art and help the creative industry grow.
en.body_more:
Artist Charles Levison said more platforms are needed so people can see and buy art. "If we have more spaces to show our work, more people will learn about art and want to buy it," he said. Another artist, Panji Tembo, said that visitors from other countries like Malawian art very much, but local people do not buy enough. He asked for more art events in cities like Blantyre to help grow the industry.

Standard Bank gave K20 million to help pay for Art in the Park. The bank's chief, Phillip Madinga, said growth is not only about money—it is also about art and culture. "We must invest in artists and help Malawi's talent grow here and in other countries," he said. Madinga told people to buy Malawian art and put it on their walls at home and in offices.

Artists also said people must buy art outside of events. Timothy Kamayi said: "Support must not stop after exhibitions. People should go to art galleries and buy works there." Mbanase Nyirenda agreed, saying Malawians should change how they think about art.

Nick Hall, who leads the Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi, said the art standards are getting better each year. At this year's event, 47 artists showed their work. Money from the sales will help pay for wildlife clubs in schools. The government also said it will set up a National Arts and Heritage Council to help the creative sector grow.

ny.title: Ojambula akupempha thandizo lowonjezera kuti akulitse bizinesi ya zaluso
ny.body: Ojambula zithunzi ku Malawi akufuna thandizo lochulukirapo komanso ndalama zogwirira ntchito yawo. Adapempha izi ku Art in the Park 2026, chochitika chomwe chidachitikira ku Four Seasons Gardens ku Lilongwe kuyambira Lachisanu mpaka Lamlungu. Ojambula adati palibe malo okwanira owonetsera ntchito zawo. Adapemphanso anthu kuti agule zaluso zakomweko ndikuthandizira kukula kwa makampani opanga zinthu zatsopano.
ny.body_more:
Wojambula Charles Levison anati pakufunika nsanja zambiri kuti anthu athe kuwona ndikugula zaluso. "Ngati tili ndi malo ambiri owonetsera ntchito zathu, anthu ambiri adzaphunzira za zaluso ndipo akufuna kuzigula," adatero. Wojambula wina, Panji Tembo, adati alendo ochokera kumayiko ena amakonda kwambiri zaluso zaku Malawi, koma anthu am'deralo sagula zokwanira. Anapempha zochitika zambiri zaluso m'mizinda ngati Blantyre kuti zithandize kukulitsa makampani.

Standard Bank idapereka K20 miliyoni kuti ithandize kulipira Art in the Park. Mkulu wa bankiyo, Phillip Madinga, adati kukula sikungokhudza ndalama zokha—komanso zaluso ndi chikhalidwe. "Tiyenera kuyika ndalama mu ojambula ndikuthandizira luso la Malawi kukula kuno ndi m'maiko ena," adatero. Madinga adauza anthu kuti agule zaluso zaku Malawi ndikuziyika pamakoma awo kunyumba ndi m'maofesi.

Ojambula adatinso anthu ayenera kugula zaluso kunja kwa zochitika. Timothy Kamayi adati: "Chithandizo sichiyenera kuyima pambuyo pa ziwonetsero. Anthu ayenera kupita ku malo owonetsera zaluso ndikugula ntchito kumeneko." Mbanase Nyirenda adavomereza, ponena kuti aku Malawi ayenera kusintha momwe amaganizira za zaluso.

Nick Hall, yemwe akutsogolera bungwe la Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi, anati miyezo ya zaluso ikukwera chaka chilichonse. Pa chochitika cha chaka chino, ojambula 47 adawonetsa ntchito zawo. Ndalama zomwe zachokera ku malondawa zithandiza kulipira makalabu a nyama zakuthengo m'masukulu. Boma lanenanso kuti likhazikitsa Bungwe la Zaluso ndi Cholowa cha Dziko Lonse kuti lithandize gawo la zaluso kukula.

en.title: Artists ask for more help to grow art in Malawi
en.body: Visual artists in Malawi have asked for more support and places to show their work. They made this call at Art in the Park 2026, a big art show that ended on Sunday in Lilongwe. The event ran from Friday to Sunday at Four Seasons Gardens. Artists said people in Malawi do not buy enough local art, but visitors from other countries do. They asked the government and companies to help the art industry grow.
en.body_more:
Artist Charles Levison said there are not enough spaces for artists to show what they make. He said people cannot appreciate art if they do not see it. Levison asked the government and private companies to give money like Standard Bank does. Another artist, Panji Tembo, said foreign visitors love Malawian art. He asked for the same kind of art shows to happen in other cities like Blantyre.

Charcoal artist Timothy Kamayi and painter Mbanase Nyirenda both said Malawians must buy art from galleries, not just at shows. They said this will help the industry grow all year long. Deputy Director of Arts MacDonald Maluwaya said he was happy with the art on show. He told the artists they are doing good work and some pieces sold for more than 2 million Kwacha.

Standard Bank gave 20 million Kwacha to help the event. The bank's leader, Phillip Madinga, said true growth means more than money. He said it means helping artists and keeping Malawi's culture alive. He asked people to put Malawian art on their walls. The Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi said 47 artists showed their work this year. The show had a theme about saving the environment. Money from the event will help make wildlife clubs in schools.

ny.title: Ojambula akupempha thandizo lowonjezera kuti akulitse luso lawo ku Malawi
ny.body: Ojambula zithunzi ku Malawi apempha thandizo lowonjezera ndi malo oti awonetse ntchito zawo. Adapempha izi ku Art in the Park 2026, chiwonetsero chachikulu cha zaluso chomwe chinatha Lamlungu ku Lilongwe. Chochitikachi chinachitika kuyambira Lachisanu mpaka Lamlungu ku Four Seasons Gardens. Ojambula adati anthu ku Malawi sagula zaluso zakomweko zokwanira, koma alendo ochokera kumayiko ena amagula. Adapempha boma ndi makampani kuti athandize makampani a zaluso kukula.
ny.body_more:
Wojambula Charles Levison anati palibe malo okwanira oti ojambula aziwonetsa zomwe amapanga. Anati anthu sangayamikire zaluso ngati sakuziona. Levison anapempha boma ndi makampani achinsinsi kuti apereke ndalama monga momwe Standard Bank imachitira. Wojambula wina, Panji Tembo, anati alendo ochokera kumayiko ena amakonda zaluso zaku Malawi. Anapempha kuti ziwonetsero zaluso zofananazo zichitike m'mizinda ina monga Blantyre.

Wojambula wa makala Timothy Kamayi ndi wojambula Mbanase Nyirenda onse anati anthu aku Malawi ayenera kugula zaluso kuchokera ku malo owonetsera zithunzi, osati pa ziwonetsero zokha. Anati izi zithandiza kuti makampani akule chaka chonse. Wachiwiri kwa Mtsogoleri wa Zaluso MacDonald Maluwaya anati akusangalala ndi zaluso zomwe zikuwonetsedwa. Anauza ojambula kuti akuchita ntchito yabwino ndipo zinthu zina zagulitsidwa ndi ndalama zoposa 2 miliyoni Kwacha.

Standard Bank inapereka 20 miliyoni Kwacha kuti ithandize chochitikachi. Mtsogoleri wa bankiyo, Phillip Madinga, anati kukula kwenikweni kumatanthauza zambiri kuposa ndalama. Anati zikutanthauza kuthandiza ojambula ndikusunga chikhalidwe cha Malawi. Anapempha anthu kuti aike zaluso zaku Malawi pamakoma awo. Bungwe la Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi linati ojambula 47 adawonetsa ntchito yawo chaka chino. Chiwonetserochi chinali ndi mutu wokhudza kupulumutsa chilengedwe. Ndalama zomwe zachokera ku chochitikachi zithandiza kupanga makalabu a nyama zakuthengo m'masukulu.

en.title: HIV cases rise in Nsanje District to 10.1 percent
en.body: HIV infections are rising in Nsanje District. The rate went up from 9 percent to 10.1 percent. McKnowledge Tembo is the HIV coordinator. He said this on Friday at an event. The church Word Alive Ministry held the event with help from Compassion International. It was at Mbeta village. Tembo said the district found 252 new infections in one year. About 25,000 people take HIV medicine now.
en.body_more:
Tembo said the rise is a worry. He said people must learn how to stop HIV. The district wants to reach the 95:95:95 target. This means 95 of 100 people know if they have HIV. Of those, 95 take medicine. Of those, 95 have the virus under control. Tembo said thank you to the church and Compassion International. They help give people the right facts about HIV.

Rex Mlawu is a police officer in Nsanje. He said the event was good. He said people got correct facts about how to stop HIV. The group village head also said thank you. He said many people got free health checks at the event.

Madalitso Benjamin works for Compassion International. He said the event is part of a bigger plan. The plan is called "Realising Children from Poverty in Jesus Christ". It helps young people. The plan gives money to help young people go to school and learn skills. Some young people learned to build with wood and brick. Some learned to make clothes. Some learned to fix lights and power.

The event tried to help people take their medicine. It also tried to stop new HIV cases. Malawi wants all people to reach the 95:95:95 target. New cases in some places are a problem for this work.

ny.title: Milandu ya kachilombo ka HIV yakwera m'boma la Nsanje kufika pa 10.1 peresenti
ny.body: Matenda a HIV akukwera m'boma la Nsanje. Chiwerengero cha anthu omwe akudwala matendawa chakwera kuchoka pa 9 peresenti kufika pa 10.1 peresenti. McKnowledge Tembo ndiye wogwirizanitsa za HIV. Iye wanena izi Lachisanu pa chochitika china. Mpingo wa Word Alive Ministry unachititsa mwambowu mothandizidwa ndi Compassion International. Unali kumudzi wa Mbeta. Tembo adati chigawochi chapeza matenda atsopano 252 m'chaka chimodzi. Anthu pafupifupi 25,000 akumwa mankhwala a HIV tsopano.
ny.body_more:
Tembo anati kukwera kwa chiwerengerochi n’kodetsa nkhawa. Anati anthu ayenera kuphunzira momwe angaletsere kachilombo ka HIV. Chigawochi chikufuna kukwaniritsa cholinga cha 95:95:95. Izi zikutanthauza kuti anthu 95 mwa 100 amadziwa ngati ali ndi kachilombo ka HIV. Mwa iwo, 95 akumwa mankhwala. Mwa iwo, 95 ali ndi kachilomboka. Tembo anati zikomo ku tchalitchi ndi Compassion International. Amathandiza anthu kudziwa zoona zenizeni zokhudza kachilombo ka HIV.

Rex Mlawu ndi wapolisi ku Nsanje. Anati chochitikachi chinali chabwino. Anati anthu adapeza zoona zenizeni za momwe angaletsere kachilombo ka HIV. Mtsogoleri wa gululo anatinso zikomo. Anati anthu ambiri adalandira mayeso aulere azaumoyo pa chochitikachi.

Madalitso Benjamin amagwira ntchito ku Compassion International. Anati chochitikachi ndi gawo la dongosolo lalikulu. Dongosololi limatchedwa "Kuzindikira Ana Kuchokera ku Umphawi mwa Yesu Khristu". Limathandiza achinyamata. Dongosololi limapereka ndalama zothandizira achinyamata kupita kusukulu ndikuphunzira maluso. Achinyamata ena adaphunzira kumanga ndi matabwa ndi njerwa. Ena adaphunzira kupanga zovala. Ena adaphunzira kukonza magetsi ndi magetsi.

Chochitikachi chinayesetsa kuthandiza anthu kumwa mankhwala awo. Chinayesetsanso kuletsa anthu atsopano omwe ali ndi kachilombo ka HIV. Malawi ikufuna kuti anthu onse afike pamlingo wa 95:95:95. Milandu yatsopano m'malo ena ndi vuto pa ntchitoyi.

en.title: Bullets Beat Wanderers 2-1 in Big Blantyre Derby
en.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets won 2-1 against Mighty Wanderers on Sunday at Bingu National Stadium. This was the 80th Blantyre derby — Malawi's biggest football game. Bullets had lost four times to Wanderers before. This win gives Bullets revenge and shows they are strong again.
en.body_more:
Bullets scored first in just four minutes. Ephraim Kondowe was knocked down inside the area. Blessings Mpokera took the penalty and scored. The ball went into the roof of the net. Wanderers' goalkeeper Chancy Mtete could not stop it.

Wanderers tried to fight back. Coach Bob Mpinganjira made changes to help his team. Clever Kalambo played well for Bullets on the wing. In the second half, Wanderers pushed hard for an equal goal. They hit the post three times. But in minute 58, Maxwell Gasten Phodo jumped high and scored from a corner. Wanderers got one goal back in minute 61. Promise Kamwendo scored from a penalty after a hand ball. This made the game very tight.

Things got ugly when Phodo and Gaddie Chirwa hit each other in minute 77. The referee showed them both red cards — they left the field. Wanderers pushed hard at the end but Bullets' goalkeeper Innocent Nyasulu made good saves. Nyasulu won the prize for best player of the match. After the game, Bullets' coach Gilbert Chirwa said this was perfect revenge. The win puts Bullets at the top of the league with seven points.

ny.title: Bullets yagonjetsa Wanderers 2-1 mu Big Blantyre Derby
ny.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets idapambana 2-1 motsutsana ndi Mighty Wanderers Lamlungu ku Bingu National Stadium. Uwu unali mpikisano wa 80 ku Blantyre — masewera akuluakulu a mpira ku Malawi. Bullets idataya kale nthawi zinayi ndi Wanderers. Kupambana kumeneku kubwezera Bullets ndipo kukuwonetsa kuti ndi olimba kachiwiri.
ny.body_more:
Bullets inapeza chigoli choyamba mu mphindi zinayi zokha. Ephraim Kondowe anagwetsedwa mkati mwa malo. Blessings Mpokera analandira penalty ndipo anagoletsa. Mpirawo unalowa padenga la ukonde. Golikipa wa Wanderers, Chancy Mtete, sanathe kuuletsa.

Wanderers anayesa kumenyana. Mphunzitsi Bob Mpinganjira adasintha kuti athandize timu yake. Clever Kalambo adasewera bwino ku Bullets pa wing. Mu theka lachiwiri, Wanderers adakankha mwamphamvu kuti apeze chigoli chofanana. Adagunda positi katatu. Koma mu mphindi 58, Maxwell Gasten Phodo adalumpha pamwamba ndikugoletsa kuchokera pakona. Wanderers adapeza chigoli chimodzi kumbuyo mu mphindi 61. Promise Kamwendo adagoletsa kuchokera pa penalty atagoletsa mpira ndi dzanja. Izi zidapangitsa kuti masewerawa akhale olimba kwambiri.

Zinthu zinaipa pamene Phodo ndi Gaddie Chirwa adagundana mu mphindi 77. Woweruza adawawonetsa makadi ofiira onse - adatuluka pabwalo. Wanderers adakankha mwamphamvu kumapeto koma golikipa wa Bullets, Innocent Nyasulu, adapulumutsa bwino. Nyasulu adapambana mphoto ya wosewera wabwino kwambiri pamasewerawa. Pambuyo pa masewerawa, mphunzitsi wa Bullets Gilbert Chirwa adati uku kunali kubwezera kwabwino kwambiri. Kupambana kumeneku kwapangitsa Bullets kukhala pamwamba pa ligi ndi mapointi asanu ndi awiri.

en.title: Bullets face Wanderers in big derby match Sunday
en.body: Mighty Wanderers and FCB Nyasa Bullets will play each other on Sunday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. This is the big Blantyre derby. Wanderers have won the last four derby games and did not lose any goal. Bullets want to stop this run. The match will be under lights at night.
en.body_more:
Bullets have lost four derby games in a row. Their coach Gilbert Chirwa said the team has worked hard to play better. In their first two games this season, Bullets drew 2-2 with Civil Service United and won 1-0 against Mafco. Chirwa said his players made many chances but did not score enough goals. He said they are working on this.

Wanderers played only one league game so far. They beat Creck Sporting Club 2-1. Their assistant coach McDonald Mtetemera said the team is ready and the fans will see good football. Wanderers' head coach Bob Mpinganjira just came back from the United Kingdom, so Mtetemera will run the team on Sunday.

One key player is Sama Thierry Tanjong from Wanderers. He is from Cameroon and has scored in all four recent derby wins. Bullets' captain Blessing Mpokera will try to stop him. Bullets also have new players. Clever Kalambo and George Chaomba scored in their first games. The match will be the official start of the FDH Bank Premiership season. The league said no glass bottles or drinks can be sold at the stadium.

ny.title: Bullets ikumana ndi Wanderers pamasewera a big derby Lamlungu
ny.body: Mighty Wanderers ndi FCB Nyasa Bullets adzasewera Lamlungu pa Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Iyi ndi derby yayikulu ya Blantyre. Wanderers yapambana masewera anayi a derby ndipo sanataye chigoli chilichonse. Bullets ikufuna kuyimitsa mpikisanowu. Masewerawa adzakhala oyaka usiku.
ny.body_more:
Bullets yataya masewera anayi otsatizana a derby. Mphunzitsi wawo Gilbert Chirwa anati timuyi yagwira ntchito molimbika kuti isewera bwino. M'masewera awo awiri oyamba nyengo ino, Bullets idasewera 2-2 ndi Civil Service United ndipo idapambana 1-0 motsutsana ndi Mafco. Chirwa adati osewera ake adapanga mipata yambiri koma sanagole zigoli zokwanira. Anati akugwira ntchito pa izi.

Wanderers adasewera masewera amodzi okha a ligi mpaka pano. Adagonjetsa Creck Sporting Club 2-1. Mphunzitsi wawo wothandizira McDonald Mtetemera adati timuyi yakonzeka ndipo mafani awona mpira wabwino. Mphunzitsi wamkulu wa Wanderers Bob Mpinganjira wangobwera kumene kuchokera ku United Kingdom, kotero Mtetemera adzatsogolera timuyi Lamlungu.

Mmodzi mwa osewera ofunikira ndi Sama Thierry Tanjong wochokera ku Wanderers. Ndi wochokera ku Cameroon ndipo wagolesa zigoli m'masewera onse anayi aposachedwa a derby. Kaputeni wa Bullets Blessing Mpokera adzayesa kumuletsa. Bullets ilinso ndi osewera atsopano. Clever Kalambo ndi George Chaomba adagolesa zigoli m'masewera awo oyamba. Masewerawa adzakhala chiyambi chovomerezeka cha nyengo ya FDH Bank Premiership. Bungwe la ligi linati mabotolo agalasi kapena zakumwa sizingagulitsidwe pa bwalo lamasewera.

en.title: Malawi loses to Nigeria, misses World Cup
en.body: Malawi's Under-20 women's football team lost to Nigeria 2-1 yesterday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. The loss ends Malawi's chance to go to the FIFA Under-20 Women's World Cup in Poland. Malawi had lost the first game 2-0 in Lagos one week before. Forward Faith Chinzimu scored two goals for Malawi, but Nigeria scored one goal in the second half to win the tie.
en.body_more:
Chinzimu, who could not play in the first game because of club work, scored just ten minutes into the match. She got the ball from a free-kick by Olivia Phikani and shot past Nigeria's goalkeeper Christiana Ijoma Uzoma. The home crowd was very happy. Malawi was ahead 1-0 at half-time after the goalie Emily Nkhwazi made two big saves.

Chinzimu scored again in the 55th minute. She took the ball from a Nigeria player and ran from halfway up the field into the box. She shot and scored. Malawi now led 2-0. But four minutes later, Nigeria's Precious Chimere Oscar scored to make it 2-1. This goal ended Malawi's hope.

Malawi coach Maggie Chombo-Sadik said her team made a bad mistake that let Nigeria score. She had two foreign players back for this game—Chinzimu, who plays in Sweden, and Rose Kabzere. Before this match, Malawi had won all four rounds to reach this final round. They beat Central African Republic, Sudan, South Sudan, and Guinea Bissau on aggregate. Nigeria's coach Moses Adukwu said both teams played well.

The World Cup will be held in Poland in September this year. Only Malawi and Nigeria were left in this part of the qualifiers for Africa. Nigeria only needed a draw to qualify, but Malawi needed to win by three goals. The Young Scorchers will now not play in the World Cup for the first time.

ny.title: Malawi yagonja ndi Nigeria, yalephera World Cup
ny.body: Timu ya mpira wa azimayi azaka zosakwana 20 ya Malawi idagonja ndi Nigeria 2-1 dzulo pa Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Kugonja kumeneku kwathetsa mwayi wa Malawi wopita ku FIFA Under-20 Women's World Cup ku Poland. Malawi idagonja 2-0 pamasewera oyamba ku Lagos sabata imodzi yapitayo. Wosewera Faith Chinzimu adagoletsa zigoli ziwiri ku Malawi, koma Nigeria idagoletsa chigoli chimodzi mu theka lachiwiri kuti ipambane mpikisanowu.
ny.body_more:
Chinzimu, yemwe sanathe kusewera pamasewera oyamba chifukwa cha ntchito ya kilabu, adagoletsa mphindi khumi zokha masewerawa atatha. Adalandira mpirawo kuchokera ku free-kick ya Olivia Phikani ndikumenya mpira kudutsa goli wa Nigeria Christiana Ijoma Uzoma. Anthu omwe adasewera kunyumba adasangalala kwambiri. Malawi inali patsogolo ndi 1-0 nthawi yopuma pambuyo poti goli Emily Nkhwazi adapulumutsa zigoli ziwiri zazikulu.

Chinzimu adagoletsanso mu mphindi 55. Adatenga mpirawo kuchokera kwa wosewera waku Nigeria ndikuthamanga kuchokera pakati pa bwalo kupita ku bokosi. Adagoletsa ndikugoletsa. Malawi tsopano idatsogolera 2-0. Koma patatha mphindi zinayi, Precious Chimere Oscar waku Nigeria adagoletsa kuti zigoli 2-1. Chigoli ichi chidathetsa chiyembekezo cha Malawi.

Mphunzitsi wa Malawi Maggie Chombo-Sadik adati timu yake idalakwitsa kwambiri zomwe zidalola Nigeria kuti igoletse. Adabweranso ndi osewera awiri akunja pamasewerawa—Chinzimu, yemwe amasewera ku Sweden, ndi Rose Kabzere. Masewerawa asanachitike, Malawi idapambana magawo onse anayi kuti ifike pagawo lomaliza lino. Iwo adagonjetsa Central African Republic, Sudan, South Sudan, ndi Guinea Bissau pamasewera onse. Mphunzitsi wa Nigeria Moses Adukwu adati magulu onse awiri adasewera bwino.

World Cup idzachitikira ku Poland mu Seputembala chaka chino. Malawi ndi Nigeria okha ndi omwe adatsala mu gawo ili la masewera oyeserera ku Africa. Nigeria idafunikira draw kuti iyenerere, koma Malawi idafunikira kupambana ndi zigoli zitatu. Young Scorchers tsopano sadzasewera mu World Cup koyamba.
